text.NRSV: The Lord summoned Moses and spoke to him from the tent of meeting, saying:
text.Rotherham: Then called he unto Moses,—and Yahweh spake unto him, out of the tent of meeting, saying:
text.KJV: And the Lord called unto Moses, and spake unto him out of the tabernacle of the congregation, saying,
text.NABRE: The Lord called Moses, and spoke to him from the tent of meeting:
text.DRB: And our Lord called Moyses, and spake to him out of the tabernacle of testimony, saying:

text.NRSV: Speak to the people of Israel and say to them: When any of you bring an offering of livestock to the Lord, you shall bring your offering from the herd or from the flock.
text.Rotherham: Speak thou unto the sons of Israel, and thou shalt say unto them When any man, would bring near from among you an oblation, unto Yahweh, from the beasts, from the herd or from the flock, shall ye bring near your oblation.
text.KJV: Speak unto the children of Israel, and say unto them, If any man of you bring an offering unto the Lord, ye shall bring your offering of the cattle, even of the herd, and of the flock.
text.NABRE: Speak to the Israelites and tell them: When any one of you brings an offering of livestock to the Lord, you shall bring your offering from the herd or from the flock.
text.DRB: Speak to the children of Israel, & thou shalt say to them: The man of you, that shall offer an host to our Lord, of beasts, that is of oxen & sheep, offering victims,

text.NRSV: If the offering is a burnt offering from the herd, you shall offer a male without blemish; you shall bring it to the entrance of the tent of meeting, for acceptance in your behalf before the Lord.
text.Rotherham: If an ascending-sacrifice, be his oblation—of the herd, a male without defect, shall he bring near,—unto the entrance of the tent of meeting, shall he bring it, for its acceptance, before Yahweh.
text.KJV: If his offering be a burnt sacrifice of the herd, let him offer a male without blemish: he shall offer it of his own voluntary will at the door of the tabernacle of the congregation before the Lord.
text.NABRE: If a person’s offering is a burnt offering from the herd, the offering must be a male without blemish. The individual shall bring it to the entrance of the tent of meeting to find favor with the Lord,
text.DRB: if his oblation be an holocaust, and of the herd, he shall offer a male, without spot, at the door of the tabernacle of testimony, to propitiate our Lord unto him:

text.NRSV: You shall lay your hand on the head of the burnt offering, and it shall be acceptable in your behalf as atonement for you.
text.Rotherham: Then shall he lean his hand, upon the head of the ascending-sacrifice,—and it shall be accepted for him to put a propitiatory covering over him;
text.KJV: And he shall put his hand upon the head of the burnt offering; and it shall be accepted for him to make atonement for him.
text.NABRE: and shall lay a hand on the head of the burnt offering, so that it may be acceptable to make atonement for the one who offers it.
text.DRB: and he shall put his hands upon the head of the host, and it shall be acceptable, and profitable to his expiation.

text.NRSV: The bull shall be slaughtered before the Lord; and Aaron’s sons the priests shall offer the blood, dashing the blood against all sides of the altar that is at the entrance of the tent of meeting.
text.Rotherham: and he shall slay the young bullock before Yahweh,—and the sons of Aaron, the priests, shall bring near the blood, and shall dash the blood against the altar round about, [even the altar] that is at the entrance of the tent of meeting.
text.KJV: And he shall kill the bullock before the Lord: and the priests, Aaron’s sons, shall bring the blood, and sprinkle the blood round about upon the altar that is by the door of the tabernacle of the congregation.
text.NABRE: The bull shall then be slaughtered before the Lord, and Aaron’s sons, the priests, shall offer its blood by splashing it on all the sides of the altar which is at the entrance of the tent of meeting.
text.DRB: And he shall immolate the calf before our Lord, and the children of Aaron the Priests shall offer the blood thereof, pouring it in the circuit of the altar, which is before the door of the tabernacle.

text.NRSV: The burnt offering shall be flayed and cut up into its parts.
text.Rotherham: Then shall he flay the ascending-sacrifice,—and cut it up into its pieces.
text.KJV: And he shall flay the burnt offering, and cut it into his pieces.
text.NABRE: Then the burnt offering shall be flayed and cut into pieces.
text.DRB: And the skin of the host being plucked off, the joints they shall cut into pieces,

text.NRSV: The sons of the priest Aaron shall put fire on the altar and arrange wood on the fire.
text.Rotherham: And the sons of Aaron the priest shall place fire upon the altar,—and arrange wood, upon the fire.
text.KJV: And the sons of Aaron the priest shall put fire upon the altar, and lay the wood in order upon the fire:
text.NABRE: After Aaron’s sons, the priests, have put burning embers on the altar and laid wood on them,
text.DRB: and shall put fire underneath in the altar, having before laid a pile of wood in order:

text.NRSV: Aaron’s sons the priests shall arrange the parts, with the head and the suet, on the wood that is on the fire on the altar;
text.Rotherham: And the sons of Aaron, the priests, shall arrange the pieces, the head and the fat,—upon the wood, that is on the fire, that is on the altar.
text.KJV: And the priests, Aaron’s sons, shall lay the parts, the head, and the fat, in order upon the wood that is on the fire which is upon the altar:
text.NABRE: they shall lay the pieces of meat, together with the head and the suet, on top of the wood and the embers on the altar;
text.DRB: and the joints that are cut out, laying in order thereupon, to wit, the head, & all things that cleave to the liver,

text.NRSV: but its entrails and its legs shall be washed with water. Then the priest shall turn the whole into smoke on the altar as a burnt offering, an offering by fire of pleasing odor to the Lord.
text.Rotherham: But its inwards and its legs, shall they bathe with water,—then shall the priest make, of the whole, a perfume at the altar, an ascending-sacrifice an altar-flame of a satisfying odour unto Yahweh.
text.KJV: But his inwards and his legs shall he wash in water: and the priest shall burn all on the altar, to be a burnt sacrifice, an offering made by fire, of a sweet savour unto the Lord.
text.NABRE: but the inner organs and the shanks shall be washed with water. The priest shall then burn all of it on the altar as a burnt offering, a sweet-smelling oblation to the Lord.
text.DRB: the entrails and feet being washed with water, and the Priest shall burn them upon the altar for an holocaust, and sweet savour to our Lord.

text.NRSV: If your gift for a burnt offering is from the flock, from the sheep or goats, you shall offer a male without blemish.
text.Rotherham: But, if of the flock, be his oblation,—of the sheep or of the goats, for an ascending-sacrifice, a male without defect, shall he bring near.
text.KJV: And if his offering be of the flocks, namely, of the sheep, or of the goats, for a burnt sacrifice; he shall bring it a male without blemish.
text.NABRE: If a person’s burnt offering is from the flock, that is, a sheep or a goat, the offering must be a male without blemish.
text.DRB: And if the oblation be of flocks, an holocaust of sheep or of goats, a lamb of a year old without spot shall he offer:

text.NRSV: It shall be slaughtered on the north side of the altar before the Lord, and Aaron’s sons the priests shall dash its blood against all sides of the altar.
text.Rotherham: And he shall slay it on the side of the altar northward, before Yahweh,—and the sons of Aaron the priests shall dash its blood against the altar round about.
text.KJV: And he shall kill it on the side of the altar northward before the Lord: and the priests, Aaron’s sons, shall sprinkle his blood round about upon the altar.
text.NABRE: It shall be slaughtered on the north side of the altar before the Lord, and Aaron’s sons, the priests, shall splash its blood on all the sides of the altar.
text.DRB: and he shall immolate it at the side of the altar that looketh to the North, before our Lord: but the blood thereof the sons of Aaron shall pour upon the Altar round about:

text.NRSV: It shall be cut up into its parts, with its head and its suet, and the priest shall arrange them on the wood that is on the fire on the altar;
text.Rotherham: And he shall cut it up into its pieces, and its head, and its fat,—and the priest shall arrange them upon the wood that is on the fire, that is on the altar,
text.KJV: And he shall cut it into his pieces, with his head and his fat: and the priest shall lay them in order on the wood that is on the fire which is upon the altar:
text.NABRE: When it has been cut into pieces, the priest shall lay these, together with the head and suet, on top of the wood and the embers on the altar;
text.DRB: And they shall divide the joints, the head, and all that cleave to the liver: and shall lay them upon the wood, under which the fire is to be put:

text.NRSV: but the entrails and the legs shall be washed with water. Then the priest shall offer the whole and turn it into smoke on the altar; it is a burnt offering, an offering by fire of pleasing odor to the Lord.
text.Rotherham: But the inwards and the legs, shall he bathe in water,—then shall the priest bring near the whole and make a perfume at the altar, an ascending-sacrifice, it is an altar-flame of a satisfying odour, unto Yahweh.
text.KJV: But he shall wash the inwards and the legs with water: and the priest shall bring it all, and burn it upon the altar: it is a burnt sacrifice, an offering made by fire, of a sweet savour unto the Lord.
text.NABRE: but the inner organs and the shanks shall be washed with water. The priest shall then offer all of it, burning it on the altar. It is a burnt offering, a sweet-smelling oblation to the Lord.
text.DRB: but the entrails & the feet they shall wash with water. And the whole the Priest shall offer and burn upon the altar for an holocaust, and most sweet savour to our Lord.

text.NRSV: If your offering to the Lord is a burnt offering of birds, you shall choose your offering from turtledoves or pigeons.
text.Rotherham: But, if, of birds, be the ascending-sacrifice of his oblation unto Yahweh, then shall he bring near of the turtle-doves or of the young pigeons, his oblation.
text.KJV: And if the burnt sacrifice for his offering to the Lord be of fowls, then he shall bring his offering of turtledoves, or of young pigeons.
text.NABRE: If a person offers a bird as a burnt offering to the Lord, the offering brought must be a turtledove or a pigeon.
text.DRB: But if the oblation of holocaust to our Lord be of birds, of turtles and young pigeons,

text.NRSV: The priest shall bring it to the altar and wring off its head, and turn it into smoke on the altar; and its blood shall be drained out against the side of the altar.
text.Rotherham: And the priest shall bring it near unto the altar shall nip off its head, and make a perfume at the altar,—and its blood shall be drained out upon the wall of the altar.
text.KJV: And the priest shall bring it unto the altar, and wring off his head, and burn it on the altar; and the blood thereof shall be wrung out at the side of the altar:
text.NABRE: Having brought it to the altar, the priest shall wring its head off and burn it on the altar. The blood shall be drained out against the side of the altar.
text.DRB: the Priest shall offer it at the altar: and writhing the head to the neck, and breaking the place of the wound, he shall make the blood to run down upon the brim of the altar:

text.NRSV: He shall remove its crop with its contents and throw it at the east side of the altar, in the place for ashes.
text.Rotherham: Then shall he take away its crop with its plumage,—and cast it beside the altar, eastward, into the place of fat ashes;
text.KJV: And he shall pluck away his crop with his feathers, and cast it beside the altar on the east part, by the place of the ashes:
text.NABRE: He shall remove its crissum by means of its feathers and throw it on the ash heap at the east side of the altar.
text.DRB: but the crop of the throat, and the feathers he shall cast nigh to the altar at the east side, in the place where the ashes are wont to be poured out,

text.NRSV: He shall tear it open by its wings without severing it. Then the priest shall turn it into smoke on the altar, on the wood that is on the fire; it is a burnt offering, an offering by fire of pleasing odor to the Lord.
text.Rotherham: and he shall cleave it in its wings—he shall not divide it asunder, so shall the priest make a perfume therewith, at the altar, upon the wood that is on the fire,—an ascending-sacrifice, it is, an altar-flame of a satisfying odour, unto Yahweh.
text.KJV: And he shall cleave it with the wings thereof, but shall not divide it asunder: and the priest shall burn it upon the altar, upon the wood that is upon the fire: it is a burnt sacrifice, an offering made by fire, of a sweet savour unto the Lord.
text.NABRE: Then, having torn the bird open by its wings without separating the halves, the priest shall burn it on the altar, on the wood and the embers. It is a burnt offering, a sweet-smelling oblation to the Lord.
text.DRB: & he shall break the pinions thereof, and shall not cut nor divide it with a knife, and shall burn it upon the altar, putting fire under the wood. It is an holocaust and oblation of most sweet savour to our Lord.

text.NRSV: When anyone presents a grain offering to the Lord, the offering shall be of choice flour; the worshiper shall pour oil on it, and add frankincense to it,
text.Rotherham: But, when any person would bring near as an oblation, a meal-offering, unto Yahweh, of fine meal, shall be his oblation,—and he shall pour out thereon oil, and place thereon, frankincense;
text.KJV: And when any will offer a meat offering unto the Lord, his offering shall be of fine flour; and he shall pour oil upon it, and put frankincense thereon:
text.NABRE: When anyone brings a grain offering to the Lord, the offering must consist of bran flour. The offerer shall pour oil on it and put frankincense over it,
text.DRB: When a soul shall offer an oblation of sacrifice to our Lord, fine flour shall be his oblation, and he shall pour oil upon it, and put frankincense,

text.NRSV: and bring it to Aaron’s sons the priests. After taking from it a handful of the choice flour and oil, with all its frankincense, the priest shall turn this token portion into smoke on the altar, an offering by fire of pleasing odor to the Lord.
text.Rotherham: so shall he bring it in unto the sons of Aaron, the priests, and he shall grasp therefrom a handful of the fine meal thereof and of the oil thereof, besides all the frankincense thereof,—and the priest shall make a perfume with the memorial thereof at the altar, an altar-flame of a satisfying odour unto Yahweh.
text.KJV: And he shall bring it to Aaron’s sons the priests: and he shall take thereout his handful of the flour thereof, and of the oil thereof, with all the frankincense thereof; and the priest shall burn the memorial of it upon the altar, to be an offering made by fire, of a sweet savour unto the Lord:
text.NABRE: and bring it to Aaron’s sons, the priests. A priest shall take a handful of the bran flour and oil, together with all the frankincense, and shall burn it on the altar as a token of the offering, a sweet-smelling oblation to the Lord.
text.DRB: and shall carry it to the sons of Aaron the Priests: of whom one shall take a handful of the flour, and the oil, and all the frankincense, and shall put it a memorial upon the Altar for a most sweet savour to our Lord.

text.NRSV: And what is left of the grain offering shall be for Aaron and his sons, a most holy part of the offerings by fire to the Lord.
text.Rotherham: But, the remainder of the meal-offering, pertaineth unto Aaron and unto his sons,—most holy, from among the altar-flames of Yahweh.
text.KJV: And the remnant of the meat offering shall be Aaron’s and his sons’: it is a thing most holy of the offerings of the Lord made by fire.
text.NABRE: The rest of the grain offering belongs to Aaron and his sons, a most holy portion from the oblations to the Lord.
text.DRB: And that which shall be left of the sacrifice, shall be Aaron's, & his sons', Holy of holies among the oblations of our Lord.

text.NRSV: When you present a grain offering baked in the oven, it shall be of choice flour: unleavened cakes mixed with oil, or unleavened wafers spread with oil.
text.Rotherham: Moreover, when thou wouldst bring near as an oblation of a meal-offering, something baked in an oven, round unleavened cakes of fine meal, overflowed with oil, [shall it be], or unleavened wafers anointed with oil.
text.KJV: And if thou bring an oblation of a meat offering baken in the oven, it shall be unleavened cakes of fine flour mingled with oil, or unleavened wafers anointed with oil.
text.NABRE: When you offer a grain offering baked in an oven, it must be in the form of unleavened cakes made of bran flour mixed with oil, or of unleavened wafers spread with oil.
text.DRB: But when thou offerest a sacrifice baked in the oven: of flour, to wit, loaves without leaven, tempered with oil, and wafers unleavened laid over with oil.

text.NRSV: If your offering is grain prepared on a griddle, it shall be an unleavened cake of choice flour mixed with oil.
text.Rotherham: But, if, a meal-offering, on a girdle, be thine oblation, of fine meal, overflowed with oil unleavened, shall it be;
text.KJV: And if thy oblation be a meat offering baken in a pan, it shall be of fine flour unleavened, mingled with oil.
text.NABRE: If your offering is a grain offering that is fried on a griddle, it must be of bran flour mixed with oil and unleavened.
text.DRB: If thine oblation be of the frying pan, of flour tempered with oil, and without leaven,

text.NRSV: Break it in pieces, and pour oil on it; it is a grain offering.
text.Rotherham: parting it into pieces, then shalt thou pour thereon oil,—a meal-offering, it is,
text.KJV: Thou shalt part it in pieces, and pour oil thereon: it is a meat offering.
text.NABRE: Break it into pieces, and pour oil over it. It is a grain offering.
text.DRB: thou shalt divide it into little pieces, and shalt pour oil upon it.

text.NRSV: If your offering is grain prepared in a pan, it shall be made of choice flour in oil.
text.Rotherham: But, if a meal-offering of the boiler, be thine oblation, of fine meal with oil, shall it be made.
text.KJV: And if thy oblation be a meat offering baken in the fryingpan, it shall be made of fine flour with oil.
text.NABRE: If your offering is a grain offering that is prepared in a pan, it must be made of bran flour, fried in oil.
text.DRB: And if the sacrifice be from the gridiron, in like manner the flour shall be tempered with oil,

text.NRSV: You shall bring to the Lord the grain offering that is prepared in any of these ways; and when it is presented to the priest, he shall take it to the altar.
text.Rotherham: Then shalt thou bring in the meal-offering that is made of these, unto Yahweh,—and one shall bring it near unto the priest, and he shall take it near unto the altar.
text.KJV: And thou shalt bring the meat offering that is made of these things unto the Lord: and when it is presented unto the priest, he shall bring it unto the altar.
text.NABRE: A grain offering that is made in any of these ways you shall bring to the Lord. It shall be presented to the priest, who shall take it to the altar.
text.DRB: which offering to our Lord, thou shalt deliver to the hands of the Priest.

text.NRSV: The priest shall remove from the grain offering its token portion and turn this into smoke on the altar, an offering by fire of pleasing odor to the Lord.
text.Rotherham: Then shall the priest uplift out of the meal-offering, a memorial thereof, and make a perfume at the altar,—an altar-flame of a satisfying odour unto Yahweh.
text.KJV: And the priest shall take from the meat offering a memorial thereof, and shall burn it upon the altar: it is an offering made by fire, of a sweet savour unto the Lord.
text.NABRE: The priest shall then remove from the grain offering a token and burn it on the altar as a sweet-smelling oblation to the Lord.
text.DRB: Who having offered it shall take a memory of the sacrifice, and burn it upon the altar for a sweet savour to our Lord,

text.NRSV: And what is left of the grain offering shall be for Aaron and his sons; it is a most holy part of the offerings by fire to the Lord.
text.Rotherham: But the remainder of the meal-offering pertaineth to Aaron and to his sons,—most holy from among the altar-flames of Yahweh.
text.KJV: And that which is left of the meat offering shall be Aaron’s and his sons’: it is a thing most holy of the offerings of the Lord made by fire.
text.NABRE: The rest of the grain offering belongs to Aaron and his sons, a most holy portion from the oblations to the Lord.
text.DRB: and whatsoever is left, shall be Aaron's, and his sons, Holy of holies among the oblations of our Lord.

text.NRSV: No grain offering that you bring to the Lord shall be made with leaven, for you must not turn any leaven or honey into smoke as an offering by fire to the Lord.
text.Rotherham: None of the meal-offerings which ye bring near unto Yahweh shall be made into anything leavened,—for, of no leaven and Of no syrup, may ye make a perfume, as an altar-flame unto Yahweh.
text.KJV: No meat offering, which ye shall bring unto the Lord, shall be made with leaven: for ye shall burn no leaven, nor any honey, in any offering of the Lord made by fire.
text.NABRE: Every grain offering that you present to the Lord shall be unleavened, for you shall not burn any leaven or honey as an oblation to the Lord.
text.DRB: Every oblation that is offered to our Lord, shall be made without leaven, neither shall any leaven and honey be burned in the sacrifice of our Lord.

text.NRSV: You may bring them to the Lord as an offering of choice products, but they shall not be offered on the altar for pleasing odor.
text.Rotherham: As for an oblation of first-fruits, ye shall bring them near unto Yahweh,—but unto the altar, they shall not take them up, as a satisfying odour.
text.KJV: As for the oblation of the firstfruits, ye shall offer them unto the Lord: but they shall not be burnt on the altar for a sweet savour.
text.NABRE: Such you may present to the Lord in the offering of the first produce that is processed, but they are not to be placed on the altar for a pleasing odor.
text.DRB: The first fruits only of them and the gifts you shall offer: but upon the altar they shall not be put, for a savour of sweetness.

text.NRSV: You shall not omit from your grain offerings the salt of the covenant with your God; with all your offerings you shall offer salt.
text.Rotherham: And, every meal-offering oblation of thine, with salt, shalt thou season, and thou shalt not suffer to be lacking the salt of the covenant of thy God, from upon thy meal-offering,—upon every oblation of thine, shalt thou offer salt.
text.KJV: And every oblation of thy meat offering shalt thou season with salt; neither shalt thou suffer the salt of the covenant of thy God to be lacking from thy meat offering: with all thine offerings thou shalt offer salt.
text.NABRE: You shall season all your grain offerings with salt. Do not let the salt of the covenant with your God be lacking from your grain offering. On every offering you shall offer salt.
text.DRB: What sacrifice soever thou offerest, thou shalt season it with salt, neither shalt thou take away the salt of the covenant of thy God from thy sacrifice. In every oblation thou shalt offer salt.

text.NRSV: If you bring a grain offering of first fruits to the Lord, you shall bring as the grain offering of your first fruits coarse new grain from fresh ears, parched with fire.
text.Rotherham: But, if thou wouldst bring near a meal-offering of first-ripe corn, unto Yahweh, of green ears roasted in fire crushed grain of garden-land, shalt thou bring near thy meal-offering of first-ripe corn.
text.KJV: And if thou offer a meat offering of thy firstfruits unto the Lord, thou shalt offer for the meat offering of thy firstfruits green ears of corn dried by the fire, even corn beaten out of full ears.
text.NABRE: If you offer a grain offering of first ripe fruits to the Lord, you shall offer it in the form of fresh early grain, roasted by fire and crushed as a grain offering of your first ripe fruits.
text.DRB: But if thou offer a gift of the first fruits of thy corn to our Lord, of the ears being yet green, thou shalt dry it at the fire, and bruise it in manner of meal, and so shalt thou offer thy first fruits to our Lord,

text.NRSV: You shall add oil to it and lay frankincense on it; it is a grain offering.
text.Rotherham: And thou shalt place thereon, oil and shalt put thereon, frankincense,—a meal-offering, it is.
text.KJV: And thou shalt put oil upon it, and lay frankincense thereon: it is a meat offering.
text.NABRE: You shall put oil on it and set frankincense on it. It is a grain offering.
text.DRB: pouring oil upon it, and putting on frankincense, because it is the oblation of our Lord.

text.NRSV: And the priest shall turn a token portion of it into smoke—some of the coarse grain and oil with all its frankincense; it is an offering by fire to the Lord.
text.Rotherham: Then shall the priest make a perfume, with the memorial thereof,—from the crushed grain thereof and from the oil thereof, besides all the frankincense thereof,—an altar-flame, unto Yahweh.
text.KJV: And the priest shall burn the memorial of it, part of the beaten corn thereof, and part of the oil thereof, with all the frankincense thereof: it is an offering made by fire unto the Lord.
text.NABRE: The priest shall then burn some of the groats and oil, together with all the frankincense, as a token of the offering, an oblation to the Lord.
text.DRB: Whereof the Priest shall burn for memory of the gift, part of the meal bruised, and of the oil, and all the frankincense.

text.NRSV: If the offering is a sacrifice of well-being, if you offer an animal of the herd, whether male or female, you shall offer one without blemish before the Lord.
text.Rotherham: But, if a peace-offering, be his oblation, if, of the herd, he himself, be bringing near,—whether male or female, without defect, shall he bring it near before Yahweh.
text.KJV: And if his oblation be a sacrifice of peace offering, if he offer it of the herd; whether it be a male or female, he shall offer it without blemish before the Lord.
text.NABRE: If a person’s offering is a communion sacrifice, if it is brought from the herd, be it a male or a female animal, it must be presented without blemish before the Lord.
text.DRB: And if his oblation be an host of pacifics, and he will offer of beeves, male or female, without spot shall he offer them before our Lord.

text.NRSV: You shall lay your hand on the head of the offering and slaughter it at the entrance of the tent of meeting; and Aaron’s sons the priests shall dash the blood against all sides of the altar.
text.Rotherham: Then shall he lean his hand upon the head of his oblation, and slay it at the entrance of the tent of meeting,—and the sons of Aaron the priests shall dash the blood against the altar round about,
text.KJV: And he shall lay his hand upon the head of his offering, and kill it at the door of the tabernacle of the congregation: and Aaron’s sons the priests shall sprinkle the blood upon the altar round about.
text.NABRE: The one offering it shall lay a hand on the head of the offering. It shall then be slaughtered at the entrance of the tent of meeting. Aaron’s sons, the priests, shall splash its blood on all the sides of the altar.
text.DRB: And shall lay his hand upon the head of his victim, which shall be immolated in the entire of the tabernacle of testimony, & the sons of Aaron the Priest shall pour the blood in the circuit of the altar.

text.NRSV: You shall offer from the sacrifice of well-being, as an offering by fire to the Lord, the fat that covers the entrails and all the fat that is around the entrails;
text.Rotherham: Then shall he bring near out of the peace-offering, an altar-flame unto Yahweh,—the fat that covereth the inwards, and all the fat that is upon the inwards;
text.KJV: And he shall offer of the sacrifice of the peace offering an offering made by fire unto the Lord; the fat that covereth the inwards, and all the fat that is upon the inwards,
text.NABRE: From the communion sacrifice the individual shall offer as an oblation to the Lord the fat that covers the inner organs, and all the fat that adheres to them,
text.DRB: And they shall offer of the host of pacifics, for an oblation to our Lord, the fat that covered the entrails, and whatsoever fat is within:

text.NRSV: the two kidneys with the fat that is on them at the loins, and the appendage of the liver, which he shall remove with the kidneys.
text.Rotherham: and the two kidneys, and the fat which is upon them, which is upon the loins,—and the caul upon the liver, upon the kidneys, shall he remove it.
text.KJV: And the two kidneys, and the fat that is on them, which is by the flanks, and the caul above the liver, with the kidneys, it shall he take away.
text.NABRE: as well as the two kidneys, with the fat on them near the loins, and the lobe of the liver, which is removed with the kidneys.
text.DRB: the two kidneys with the fat wherewith the guts are covered, and the caul of the liver with the two little kidneys.

text.NRSV: Then Aaron’s sons shall turn these into smoke on the altar, with the burnt offering that is on the wood on the fire, as an offering by fire of pleasing odor to the Lord.
text.Rotherham: Then shall the sons of Aaron make thereof a perfume at the altar, upon the ascending-sacrifice which is on the wood, which is on the fire,—an altar-flame of a satisfying odour unto Yahweh.
text.KJV: And Aaron’s sons shall burn it on the altar upon the burnt sacrifice, which is upon the wood that is on the fire: it is an offering made by fire, of a sweet savour unto the Lord.
text.NABRE: Aaron’s sons shall burn this on the altar with the burnt offering that is on the wood and the embers, as a sweet-smelling oblation to the Lord.
text.DRB: And they shall burn them upon the altar, for an holocaust, putting fire under the wood: for an oblation of most sweet savour to our Lord.

text.NRSV: If your offering for a sacrifice of well-being to the Lord is from the flock, you shall offer a male or female without blemish.
text.Rotherham: But, if out of the flock, be his oblation for a peace-offering unto Yahweh, whether male or female—without defect, shall he bring it near.
text.KJV: And if his offering for a sacrifice of peace offering unto the Lord be of the flock; male or female, he shall offer it without blemish.
text.NABRE: If the communion sacrifice one offers to the Lord is from the flock, be it a male or a female animal, it must be presented without blemish.
text.DRB: But if his oblation, and the host of pacifics be of flocks, whether he offer male, or female, they shall be without spot.

text.NRSV: If you present a sheep as your offering, you shall bring it before the Lord
text.Rotherham: If a young sheep, he himself, be bringing near as his oblation, then shall he bring it near, before Yahweh.
text.KJV: If he offer a lamb for his offering, then shall he offer it before the Lord.
text.NABRE: If one presents a lamb as an offering, that person shall bring it before the Lord,
text.DRB: If he offer a lamb before our Lord,

text.NRSV: and lay your hand on the head of the offering. It shall be slaughtered before the tent of meeting, and Aaron’s sons shall dash its blood against all sides of the altar.
text.Rotherham: And he shall lean his hand upon the head of his oblation, and shall slay it before the tent of meeting,—and the sons of Aaron shall dash the blood thereof against the altar round about.
text.KJV: And he shall lay his hand upon the head of his offering, and kill it before the tabernacle of the congregation: and Aaron’s sons shall sprinkle the blood thereof round about upon the altar.
text.NABRE: and after laying a hand on the head of the offering, it shall then be slaughtered before the tent of meeting. Aaron’s sons shall splash its blood on all the sides of the altar.
text.DRB: he shall put his hand upon the head of his victim; which shall be immolated in the entire of the tabernacle of testimony: and the sons of Aaron shall pour the blood thereof in the circuit of the altar.

text.NRSV: You shall present its fat from the sacrifice of well-being, as an offering by fire to the Lord: the whole fat tail, which shall be removed close to the spine, the fat that covers the entrails, and all the fat that is around the entrails;
text.Rotherham: Then shall he bring near out of the peace-offering, an altar-flame unto Yahweh, the fat thereof the fat-tail thereof entire close to the backbone, shall he remove it,—and the fat that covereth the inwards, and all the fat that is upon the inwards;
text.KJV: And he shall offer of the sacrifice of the peace offering an offering made by fire unto the Lord; the fat thereof, and the whole rump, it shall he take off hard by the backbone; and the fat that covereth the inwards, and all the fat that is upon the inwards,
text.NABRE: From the communion sacrifice the individual shall present as an oblation to the Lord its fat: the whole fatty tail, which is removed close to the spine, the fat that covers the inner organs, and all the fat that adheres to them,
text.DRB: And they shall offer of the host of pacifics, a sacrifice to our Lord: the fat and the whole rump,

text.NRSV: the two kidneys with the fat that is on them at the loins, and the appendage of the liver, which you shall remove with the kidneys.
text.Rotherham: and the two kidneys, with the fat that is on them, which is on the loins,—and the caul that is on the liver, on the kidneys, shall he remove it.
text.KJV: And the two kidneys, and the fat that is upon them, which is by the flanks, and the caul above the liver, with the kidneys, it shall he take away.
text.NABRE: as well as the two kidneys, with the fat on them near the loins, and the lobe of the liver, which is removed with the kidneys.
text.DRB: with the kidneys, and the fat that covereth the belly and all the vital parts, and both little kidneys, with the fat that is about the guts, and the caul of the liver with the two little kidneys.

text.NRSV: Then the priest shall turn these into smoke on the altar as a food offering by fire to the Lord.
text.Rotherham: Then shall the priest make a perfume, at the altar,—the food of an altar-flame, unto Yahweh.
text.KJV: And the priest shall burn it upon the altar: it is the food of the offering made by fire unto the Lord.
text.NABRE: The priest shall burn this on the altar as food, an oblation to the Lord.
text.DRB: And the Priest shall burn them upon the altar, to the food of the fire, and of the oblation of our Lord.

text.NRSV: If your offering is a goat, you shall bring it before the Lord
text.Rotherham: But if a goat, be his oblation, then shall he bring it near, before Yahweh.
text.KJV: And if his offering be a goat, then he shall offer it before the Lord.
text.NABRE: If a person’s offering is a goat, the individual shall bring it before the Lord,
text.DRB: If his oblation be a goat, and he offer it to our Lord,

text.NRSV: and lay your hand on its head; it shall be slaughtered before the tent of meeting; and the sons of Aaron shall dash its blood against all sides of the altar.
text.Rotherham: And he shall lean his hand upon its head, and shall slay it before the tent of meeting,—and the sons of Aaron shall dash its blood against the altar, round about.
text.KJV: And he shall lay his hand upon the head of it, and kill it before the tabernacle of the congregation: and the sons of Aaron shall sprinkle the blood thereof upon the altar round about.
text.NABRE: and after laying a hand on its head, it shall then be slaughtered before the tent of meeting. Aaron’s sons shall splash its blood on all the sides of the altar.
text.DRB: he shall put his hand upon the head thereof: and shall immolate it in the entire of the tabernacle of testimony. And the sons of Aaron shall pour the blood thereof in the circuit of the altar.

text.NRSV: You shall present as your offering from it, as an offering by fire to the Lord, the fat that covers the entrails, and all the fat that is around the entrails;
text.Rotherham: Then shall he bring near therefrom as his oblation an altar-flame unto Yahweh,—the fat that covereth the inwards, and all the fat that is upon the inwards;
text.KJV: And he shall offer thereof his offering, even an offering made by fire unto the Lord; the fat that covereth the inwards, and all the fat that is upon the inwards,
text.NABRE: From this the one sacrificing shall present an offering as an oblation to the Lord: the fat that covers the inner organs, and all the fat that adheres to them,
text.DRB: And they shall take of it to the food of our Lord's fire, the fat that covereth the belly, and that covereth all the vital parts:

text.NRSV: the two kidneys with the fat that is on them at the loins, and the appendage of the liver, which you shall remove with the kidneys.
text.Rotherham: and the two kidneys, and the fat that is on them which is on the loins,—the caul upon the liver, on the kidneys, shall he remove it.
text.KJV: And the two kidneys, and the fat that is upon them, which is by the flanks, and the caul above the liver, with the kidneys, it shall he take away.
text.NABRE: as well as the two kidneys, with the fat on them near the loins, and the lobe of the liver, which is removed with the kidneys.
text.DRB: the two little kidneys with the caul, that is upon them about the guts, and the tallow of the liver with the little kidneys:

text.NRSV: Then the priest shall turn these into smoke on the altar as a food offering by fire for a pleasing odor. All fat is the Lord’s.
text.Rotherham: Then shall the priest make a perfume at the altar,—the food of an altar-flame for a satisfying odour,—all the fat—unto Yahweh.
text.KJV: And the priest shall burn them upon the altar: it is the food of the offering made by fire for a sweet savour: all the fat is the Lord’s.
text.NABRE: The priest shall burn these on the altar as food, a sweet-smelling oblation. All the fat belongs to the Lord.
text.DRB: and the Priest shall burn them upon the altar, to the food of the fire, and of the most sweet savour. All the fat shall be our Lord's

text.NRSV: It shall be a perpetual statute throughout your generations, in all your settlements: you must not eat any fat or any blood.
text.Rotherham: An age-abiding statute to your generations, in all your dwellings,—none of the fat nor of the blood, shall ye eat.
text.KJV: It shall be a perpetual statute for your generations throughout all your dwellings, that ye eat neither fat nor blood.
text.NABRE: This shall be a perpetual ordinance for your descendants wherever they may dwell. You shall not eat any fat or any blood.
text.DRB: by a perpetual right in your generations, and in all your habitations: you shall eat no blood nor fat at all.

text.NRSV: The Lord spoke to Moses, saying:
text.Rotherham: And Yahweh spake, unto Moses, saying:
text.KJV: And the Lord spake unto Moses, saying,
text.NABRE: The Lord said to Moses:
text.DRB: And our Lord spake to Moyses, saying:

text.NRSV: Speak to the people of Israel, saying: When anyone sins unintentionally in any of the Lord’s commandments about things not to be done, and does any one of them:
text.Rotherham: Speak thou unto the sons of Israel, saying—When any person, shall sin by mistake, departing from any of the commandments of Yahweh, as to things which should not be done, and shall do any one of them,—
text.KJV: Speak unto the children of Israel, saying, If a soul shall sin through ignorance against any of the commandments of the Lord concerning things which ought not to be done, and shall do against any of them:
text.NABRE: Tell the Israelites: When a person inadvertently does wrong by violating any one of the Lord ’s prohibitions—
text.DRB: Speak to the children of Israel: The soul that sinneth by ignorance, & doth any thing of all the commandments of our Lord, which he commanded not to be done:

text.NRSV: If it is the anointed priest who sins, thus bringing guilt on the people, he shall offer for the sin that he has committed a bull of the herd without blemish as a sin offering to the Lord.
text.Rotherham: If, the anointed priest, shall sin, so as to bring guilt upon the people, then shall he bring near for his sin which he hath committed a choice young bullock without defect, unto Yahweh as a sin-bearer.
text.KJV: If the priest that is anointed do sin according to the sin of the people; then let him bring for his sin, which he hath sinned, a young bullock without blemish unto the Lord for a sin offering.
text.NABRE: For the Anointed Priest. If it is the anointed priest who thus does wrong and thereby makes the people guilty, he shall offer to the Lord an unblemished bull of the herd as a purification offering for the wrong he committed.
text.DRB: If the Priest that is anointed sin, making the people to offend, he shall offer for his sin a calf without spot to our Lord,

text.NRSV: He shall bring the bull to the entrance of the tent of meeting before the Lord and lay his hand on the head of the bull; the bull shall be slaughtered before the Lord.
text.Rotherham: And he shall bring in the bullock unto the entrance of the tent of meeting, before Yahweh,—and shall lean his hand upon the head of the bullock, and shall slay the bullock before Yahweh.
text.KJV: And he shall bring the bullock unto the door of the tabernacle of the congregation before the Lord; and shall lay his hand upon the bullock’s head, and kill the bullock before the Lord.
text.NABRE: Bringing the bull to the entrance of the tent of meeting, before the Lord, he shall lay his hand on its head and slaughter it before the Lord.
text.DRB: and he shall bring it to the door of testimony before our Lord, and shall put his hand upon the head thereof, and shall immolate it to our Lord.

text.NRSV: The anointed priest shall take some of the blood of the bull and bring it into the tent of meeting,
text.Rotherham: And the anointed priest shall take of the blood of the bullock,—and bring it into the tent of meeting;
text.KJV: And the priest that is anointed shall take of the bullock’s blood, and bring it to the tabernacle of the congregation:
text.NABRE: The anointed priest shall then take some of the bull’s blood and bring it into the tent of meeting,
text.DRB: He shall draw also of the blood of the calf, carrying it into the tabernacle of testimony.

text.NRSV: and the priest shall dip his finger in the blood and sprinkle some of the blood seven times before the Lord in front of the curtain of the sanctuary.
text.Rotherham: and the priest shall dip his finger in the blood,—and sprinkle of the blood seven times, before Yahweh, in front of the veil of the sanctuary.
text.KJV: And the priest shall dip his finger in the blood, and sprinkle of the blood seven times before the Lord, before the vail of the sanctuary.
text.NABRE: where, dipping his finger in the blood, he shall sprinkle some of it seven times before the Lord, toward the veil of the sanctuary.
text.DRB: And when he hath dipped his finger in the blood he shall sprinkle it seven times before our Lord, against the veil of the Sanctuary.

text.NRSV: The priest shall put some of the blood on the horns of the altar of fragrant incense that is in the tent of meeting before the Lord; and the rest of the blood of the bull he shall pour out at the base of the altar of burnt offering, which is at the entrance of the tent of meeting.
text.Rotherham: Then shall the priest put of the blood upon the horns of the altar of fragrant incense before Yahweh, which is in the tent of meeting,—and, all the [remainder of the] blood of the bullock, shall he pour out at the base of the altar of ascending-sacrifice, which is at the entrance of the tent of meeting,
text.KJV: And the priest shall put some of the blood upon the horns of the altar of sweet incense before the Lord, which is in the tabernacle of the congregation; and shall pour all the blood of the bullock at the bottom of the altar of the burnt offering, which is at the door of the tabernacle of the congregation.
text.NABRE: The priest shall also put some of the blood on the horns of the altar of fragrant incense which stands before the Lord in the tent of meeting. The rest of the bull’s blood he shall pour out at the base of the altar for burnt offerings which is at the entrance of the tent of meeting.
text.DRB: and of the same blood he shall put upon the horns of the altar of incense most acceptable to our Lord, which is in the tabernacle of testimony. And all the rest of the blood he shall pour at the foot of the altar of holocaust in the entry of the tabernacle.

text.NRSV: He shall remove all the fat from the bull of sin offering: the fat that covers the entrails and all the fat that is around the entrails;
text.Rotherham: And all the fat of the sin-bearing bullock, shall he heave up therefrom,—the fat that covereth over the inwards, and all the fat that is upon the inwards;
text.KJV: And he shall take off from it all the fat of the bullock for the sin offering; the fat that covereth the inwards, and all the fat that is upon the inwards,
text.NABRE: He shall remove all the fat of the bull of the purification offering: the fat that covers the inner organs, and all the fat that adheres to them,
text.DRB: And the fat of the calf he shall take away for the sin, as well that which covereth the entrails, as all the parts that are within.

text.NRSV: the two kidneys with the fat that is on them at the loins; and the appendage of the liver, which he shall remove with the kidneys,
text.Rotherham: and the two kidneys, and the fat that is upon them which is upon the loins,—and as for the caul upon the liver, upon the kidneys, shall he remove it:
text.KJV: And the two kidneys, and the fat that is upon them, which is by the flanks, and the caul above the liver, with the kidneys, it shall he take away,
text.NABRE: as well as the two kidneys, with the fat on them near the loins, and the lobe of the liver, which is removed with the kidneys,
text.DRB: The two little kidneys, and the caul that is upon them beside the guts, & the fat of the liver with the two little kidneys,

text.NRSV: just as these are removed from the ox of the sacrifice of well-being. The priest shall turn them into smoke upon the altar of burnt offering.
text.Rotherham: just as it is heaved up from the ox of the peace-offering,—and the priest shall make of them a perfume on the altar of ascending-sacrifice,
text.KJV: As it was taken off from the bullock of the sacrifice of peace offerings: and the priest shall burn them upon the altar of the burnt offering.
text.NABRE: just as the fat pieces are removed from the ox of the communion sacrifice. The priest shall burn these on the altar for burnt offerings.
text.DRB: as is taken away from the calf which is an host of pacifics, and he shall burn them upon the altar of holocaust.

text.NRSV: But the skin of the bull and all its flesh, as well as its head, its legs, its entrails, and its dung—
text.Rotherham: But, as for the skin of the bullock and all its flesh with its head and with its legs,—and its inwards, and its dung,
text.KJV: And the skin of the bullock, and all his flesh, with his head, and with his legs, and his inwards, and his dung,
text.NABRE: But the hide of the bull and its meat, with its head, shanks, inner organs and dung,
text.DRB: But the skin and all the flesh with the head and feet and bowels and dung,

text.NRSV: all the rest of the bull—he shall carry out to a clean place outside the camp, to the ash heap, and shall burn it on a wood fire; on the ash heap it shall be burned.
text.Rotherham: he shall take forth the entire bullock—unto the outside of the camp, unto a clean place unto the outpoured heap of fat-ashes, and shall burn it up on wood in the fire,—upon the outpoured heap of fat-ashes, shall it be burned up.
text.KJV: Even the whole bullock shall he carry forth without the camp unto a clean place, where the ashes are poured out, and burn him on the wood with fire: where the ashes are poured out shall he be burnt.
text.NABRE: that is, the whole bull, shall be brought outside the camp to a clean place where the ashes are deposited and there be burned in a wood fire. At the place of the ash heap, there it must be burned.
text.DRB: and the rest of the body he shall carry forth without the camp into a clean place, where the ashes are wont to be poured out: and he shall burn them upon a pile of wood, which shall be burnt in the place where the ashes are poured out.

text.NRSV: If the whole congregation of Israel errs unintentionally and the matter escapes the notice of the assembly, and they do any one of the things that by the Lord’s commandments ought not to be done and incur guilt;
text.Rotherham: And, if the whole assembly of Israel, shall make a mistake, and a matter be hidden from the eyes of the convocation,—and so they do something, whereby they depart from any of the commandments of Yahweh as to things which should not be done, and become guilty;
text.KJV: And if the whole congregation of Israel sin through ignorance, and the thing be hid from the eyes of the assembly, and they have done somewhat against any of the commandments of the Lord concerning things which should not be done, and are guilty;
text.NABRE: For the Community. If the whole community of Israel errs inadvertently and without even being aware of it violates any of the Lord ’s prohibitions, and thus are guilty,
text.DRB: And if all the multitude of Israel be ignorant, and through ignorance do that which is against the commandment of our Lord,

text.NRSV: when the sin that they have committed becomes known, the assembly shall offer a bull of the herd for a sin offering and bring it before the tent of meeting.
text.Rotherham: and the sin shall become known, which they have committed therein, then shall the convocation bring near a choice young bullock as a sin-bearer, yea they shall bring it in. before the tent of meeting;
text.KJV: When the sin, which they have sinned against it, is known, then the congregation shall offer a young bullock for the sin, and bring him before the tabernacle of the congregation.
text.NABRE: when the wrong that was committed becomes known, the community shall offer a bull of the herd as a purification offering. They shall bring it before the tent of meeting.
text.DRB: and afterwards understand their sin, they shall offer for their sin a calf, and shall bring it to the door of the tabernacle.

text.NRSV: The elders of the congregation shall lay their hands on the head of the bull before the Lord, and the bull shall be slaughtered before the Lord.
text.Rotherham: and the elders of the assembly shall lean their hands upon the head of the bullock before Yahweh,—and shall slay the bullock, before Yahweh;
text.KJV: And the elders of the congregation shall lay their hands upon the head of the bullock before the Lord: and the bullock shall be killed before the Lord.
text.NABRE: The elders of the community shall lay their hands on the bull’s head before the Lord. When the bull has been slaughtered before the Lord,
text.DRB: And the ancients of the people shall put their hands upon the head thereof before our Lord. And the calf being immolated in the sight of our Lord,

text.NRSV: The anointed priest shall bring some of the blood of the bull into the tent of meeting,
text.Rotherham: and the anointed priest shall bring in of the blood of the bullock,—into the tent of meeting;
text.KJV: And the priest that is anointed shall bring of the bullock’s blood to the tabernacle of the congregation:
text.NABRE: the anointed priest shall bring some of its blood into the tent of meeting,
text.DRB: the Priest that is anointed shall carry of the blood into the tabernacle of testimony,

text.NRSV: and the priest shall dip his finger in the blood and sprinkle it seven times before the Lord, in front of the curtain.
text.Rotherham: and the priest shall dip his finger in the blood,—and shall sprinkle of the blood seven times before Yahweh, upon the face of the veil;
text.KJV: And the priest shall dip his finger in some of the blood, and sprinkle it seven times before the Lord, even before the vail.
text.NABRE: and dipping his finger in the blood, he shall sprinkle it seven times before the Lord, toward the veil.
text.DRB: dipping his finger, & sprinkling seven times against the veil,

text.NRSV: He shall put some of the blood on the horns of the altar that is before the Lord in the tent of meeting; and the rest of the blood he shall pour out at the base of the altar of burnt offering that is at the entrance of the tent of meeting.
text.Rotherham: and, some of the blood, shall he put upon the horns of the altar which is before Yahweh, which is in the tent of meeting; and, all the [remainder of the] blood, shall he pour out at the base of the altar of ascending-sacrifice, which is at the entrance of the tent of meeting;
text.KJV: And he shall put some of the blood upon the horns of the altar which is before the Lord, that is in the tabernacle of the congregation, and shall pour out all the blood at the bottom of the altar of the burnt offering, which is at the door of the tabernacle of the congregation.
text.NABRE: He shall also put some of the blood on the horns of the altar which is before the Lord in the tent of meeting. The rest of the blood he shall pour out at the base of the altar for burnt offerings which is at the entrance of the tent of meeting.
text.DRB: and he shall put of the same blood on the horns of the altar, that is before our Lord, in the tabernacle of testimony: and the rest of the blood, he shall pour at the foot of the altar of holocaust, which is at the door of the tabernacle of testimony.

text.NRSV: He shall remove all its fat and turn it into smoke on the altar.
text.Rotherham: and, as for all the fat thereof, he shall heave up therefrom,—and make a perfume at the altar.
text.KJV: And he shall take all his fat from him, and burn it upon the altar.
text.NABRE: He shall remove all of its fat and burn it on the altar,
text.DRB: And all the fat thereof he shall take up, & shall burn it upon the altar:

text.NRSV: He shall do with the bull just as is done with the bull of sin offering; he shall do the same with this. The priest shall make atonement for them, and they shall be forgiven.
text.Rotherham: And he shall do to the bullock, as he did to the [first] sin-bearing bullock, so shall he do with this,—and the priest shall put a propitiatory-covering over them, and it shall be forgiven them.
text.KJV: And he shall do with the bullock as he did with the bullock for a sin offering, so shall he do with this: and the priest shall make an atonement for them, and it shall be forgiven them.
text.NABRE: doing with this bull just as he did with the other bull of the purification offering; he will do the same thing. Thus the priest shall make atonement on their behalf, that they may be forgiven.
text.DRB: doing so with this calf, as he did also before: & the Priest praying for them, our Lord will be propitious unto them.

text.NRSV: He shall carry the bull outside the camp, and burn it as he burned the first bull; it is the sin offering for the assembly.
text.Rotherham: And he shall take forth the bullock unto the outside of the camp, and burn it up, just as he burned up the first bullock,—the sin-bearer of the convocation, it is.
text.KJV: And he shall carry forth the bullock without the camp, and burn him as he burned the first bullock: it is a sin offering for the congregation.
text.NABRE: This bull shall also be brought outside the camp and burned, just as the first bull. It is a purification offering for the assembly.
text.DRB: But the calf itself he shall carry forth without the camp, and shall burn it like as the former calf: because it is for the sin of the multitude.

text.NRSV: When a ruler sins, doing unintentionally any one of all the things that by commandments of the Lord his God ought not to be done and incurs guilt,
text.Rotherham: When a prince, shall sin,—and shall do something, departing from any of the commandments of Yahweh his God as to things which should not be done, by mistake and shall become aware of his guilt;
text.KJV: When a ruler hath sinned, and done somewhat through ignorance against any of the commandments of the Lord his God concerning things which should not be done, and is guilty;
text.NABRE: For the Tribal Leader. Should a tribal leader do wrong inadvertently by violating any one of the prohibitions of the Lord his God, and thus be guilty,
text.DRB: If a Prince sin, and by ignorance do of many things one, that by the law of our Lord is forbidden:

text.NRSV: once the sin that he has committed is made known to him, he shall bring as his offering a male goat without blemish.
text.Rotherham: or his sin wherein he hath sinned be made known unto him, then shall he bring in. as his oblation a he-goat a male without defect;
text.KJV: Or if his sin, wherein he hath sinned, come to his knowledge; he shall bring his offering, a kid of the goats, a male without blemish:
text.NABRE: when he learns of the wrong he committed, he shall bring as his offering an unblemished male goat.
text.DRB: and afterwards understandeth his sin, he shall offer an host to our Lord, a buck of the goats without spot.

text.NRSV: He shall lay his hand on the head of the goat; it shall be slaughtered at the spot where the burnt offering is slaughtered before the Lord; it is a sin offering.
text.Rotherham: and shall lean his hand upon the head of the goat, and shall slay it in the place where they slay the ascending-sacrifice before Yahweh,—a sin-bearer, it is.
text.KJV: And he shall lay his hand upon the head of the goat, and kill it in the place where they kill the burnt offering before the Lord: it is a sin offering.
text.NABRE: He shall lay his hand on its head and it shall be slaughtered in the place where the burnt offering is slaughtered, before the Lord. It is a purification offering.
text.DRB: And he shall put his hand upon the head thereof: and when he hath immolated it in the place where holocaust is wont to be slain before our Lord, because it is for sin,

text.NRSV: The priest shall take some of the blood of the sin offering with his finger and put it on the horns of the altar of burnt offering, and pour out the rest of its blood at the base of the altar of burnt offering.
text.Rotherham: And the priest shall take of the blood of the sin-bearer with his finger, and put upon the horns of the altar of ascending-sacrifice; and, the [remainder of the] blood thereof, shall he pour out at the base of the altar of ascending-sacrifice;
text.KJV: And the priest shall take of the blood of the sin offering with his finger, and put it upon the horns of the altar of burnt offering, and shall pour out his blood at the bottom of the altar of burnt offering.
text.NABRE: The priest shall then take some of the blood of the purification offering on his finger and put it on the horns of the altar for burnt offerings. The rest of the blood he shall pour out at the base of the altar.
text.DRB: the Priest shall dip his fingers in the blood of the host for sin, touching the horns of the altar of holocaust, and the rest pouring at the foot thereof.

text.NRSV: All its fat he shall turn into smoke on the altar, like the fat of the sacrifice of well-being. Thus the priest shall make atonement on his behalf for his sin, and he shall be forgiven.
text.Rotherham: and with all the fat thereof, shall he make a perfume at the altar, as with the fat of the peace-offering,—so shall the priest put a propitiatory-covering over him because of his sin, and it shall be forgiven him.
text.KJV: And he shall burn all his fat upon the altar, as the fat of the sacrifice of peace offerings: and the priest shall make an atonement for him as concerning his sin, and it shall be forgiven him.
text.NABRE: All of its fat he shall burn on the altar like the fat of the communion sacrifice. Thus the priest shall make atonement on the tribal leader’s behalf for his wrong, that he may be forgiven.
text.DRB: But the fat he shall burn upon it, as is wont to be done in the victims of pacifics: and the Priest shall pray for him, and for his sin, and it shall be forgiven him.

text.NRSV: If anyone of the ordinary people among you sins unintentionally in doing any one of the things that by the Lord’s commandments ought not to be done and incurs guilt,
text.Rotherham: And, if, any person, shall sin, by mistake from among the people of the land,—by his doing anything departing from any of the commandments of Yahweh, as to things which should not be done, and shall become aware of his guilt;
text.KJV: And if any one of the common people sin through ignorance, while he doeth somewhat against any of the commandments of the Lord concerning things which ought not to be done, and be guilty;
text.NABRE: For the General Populace. If anyone of the general populace does wrong inadvertently by violating one of the Lord ’s prohibitions, and thus is guilty,
text.DRB: And if a soul of the people of the land shall sin through ignorance, doing any of those things that by the law of our Lord are forbidden, and offending,

text.NRSV: when the sin that you have committed is made known to you, you shall bring a female goat without blemish as your offering, for the sin that you have committed.
text.Rotherham: or his sin which he hath committed shall be made known unto, him, then shall he bring in, as his oblation, a kid of the goats a female, without defect, for his sin which he hath committed;
text.KJV: Or if his sin, which he hath sinned, come to his knowledge: then he shall bring his offering, a kid of the goats, a female without blemish, for his sin which he hath sinned.
text.NABRE: upon learning of the wrong committed, that person shall bring an unblemished she-goat as the offering for the wrong committed.
text.DRB: and knoweth his sin, he shall offer a she goat without spot.

text.NRSV: You shall lay your hand on the head of the sin offering; and the sin offering shall be slaughtered at the place of the burnt offering.
text.Rotherham: and shall lean his hand upon the head of the sin-bearer,—and shall slay the sin-bearer in the place of the ascending-sacrifice.
text.KJV: And he shall lay his hand upon the head of the sin offering, and slay the sin offering in the place of the burnt offering.
text.NABRE: The wrongdoer shall lay a hand on the head of the purification offering, and the purification offering shall be slaughtered at the place of the burnt offerings.
text.DRB: And he shall put his hand upon the head of the host that is for sin, and shall immolate it in the place of holocaust.

text.NRSV: The priest shall take some of its blood with his finger and put it on the horns of the altar of burnt offering, and he shall pour out the rest of its blood at the base of the altar.
text.Rotherham: Then shall the priest take of the blood thereof with his finger, and put upon the horns of the altar of ascending-sacrifice,—and all the [remaining] blood thereof, shall he pour out, at the base of the altar;
text.KJV: And the priest shall take of the blood thereof with his finger, and put it upon the horns of the altar of burnt offering, and shall pour out all the blood thereof at the bottom of the altar.
text.NABRE: The priest shall then take some of its blood on his finger and put it on the horns of the altar for burnt offerings. The rest of the blood he shall pour out at the base of the altar.
text.DRB: And the Priest shall take up of the blood with his finger: and touching the horns of the altar of holocaust, the rest he shall pour out at the foot thereof.

text.NRSV: He shall remove all its fat, as the fat is removed from the offering of well-being, and the priest shall turn it into smoke on the altar for a pleasing odor to the Lord. Thus the priest shall make atonement on your behalf, and you shall be forgiven.
text.Rotherham: and all the fat thereof, shall he remove as the fat from off the peace-offering was removed, and the priest shall make a perfume at the altar, for a satisfying odour unto Yahweh,—so shall the priest put a propitiatory-covering over him, and it shall be forgiven him.
text.KJV: And he shall take away all the fat thereof, as the fat is taken away from off the sacrifice of peace offerings; and the priest shall burn it upon the altar for a sweet savour unto the Lord; and the priest shall make an atonement for him, and it shall be forgiven him.
text.NABRE: He shall remove all the fat, just as the fat is removed from the communion sacrifice. The priest shall burn it on the altar for a sweet odor to the Lord. Thus the priest shall make atonement, so that the individual may be forgiven.
text.DRB: But taking away all the fat, as is wont to be taken away of the victims of pacifics, he shall burn it upon the altar, for a sweet savour to our Lord; and he shall pray for him, and it shall be forgiven him.

text.NRSV: If you bring a sheep as your offering for a sin offering, you shall bring a female without blemish,
text.Rotherham: And, if a lamb, he bring in. as his oblation for a sin-bearer, a female without defect, shall he bring in;
text.KJV: And if he bring a lamb for a sin offering, he shall bring it a female without blemish.
text.NABRE: If, however, a person brings a lamb as a purification offering, that person shall bring an unblemished female, and
text.DRB: But if he offer of the flock a victim for his sin, to wit, an ewe without spot,

text.NRSV: lay your hand on its head, slaughter it for a sin offering at the place where the burnt offering is slaughtered.
text.Rotherham: and shall lean his hand upon the head of the sin-bearer,—and shall slay it as a sin-bearer, in the place where they slay the ascending-sacrifice.
text.KJV: And he shall lay his hand upon the head of the sin offering, and slay it for a sin offering in the place where they kill the burnt offering.
text.NABRE: lay a hand on its head. It shall be slaughtered as a purification offering in the place where the burnt offering is slaughtered.
text.DRB: he shall put his hand upon the head thereof, and shall immolate it in the place where the hosts of holocausts are wont to be slain.

text.NRSV: The priest shall take some of the blood of the sin offering with his finger and put it on the horns of the altar of burnt offering, and pour out the rest of its blood at the base of the altar.
text.Rotherham: Then shall the priest take of the blood of the sin-bearer with his finger, and put upon the horns of the altar of ascending-sacrifice,—and all the [remaining] blood thereof, shall he pour out, at the base of the altar;
text.KJV: And the priest shall take of the blood of the sin offering with his finger, and put it upon the horns of the altar of burnt offering, and shall pour out all the blood thereof at the bottom of the altar:
text.NABRE: The priest shall then take some of the blood of the purification offering on his finger and put it on the horns of the altar for burnt offerings. The rest of the blood he shall pour out at the base of the altar.
text.DRB: And the Priest shall take of the blood thereof with his finger, and touching the horns of the altar of holocaust, the rest he shall pour at the foot thereof.

text.NRSV: You shall remove all its fat, as the fat of the sheep is removed from the sacrifice of well-being, and the priest shall turn it into smoke on the altar, on top of the offerings by fire to the Lord. Thus the priest shall make atonement on your behalf for the sin that you have committed, and you shall be forgiven.
text.Rotherham: and all the fat thereof, shall he remove as the fat of the lamb is removed, from the peace-offering, and the priest shall make a perfume therewith at the altar, upon the altar-flames of Yahweh,—so shall the priest put a propitiatory-covering over him on account of his sin which he hath committed, and it shall be forgiven him.
text.KJV: And he shall take away all the fat thereof, as the fat of the lamb is taken away from the sacrifice of the peace offerings; and the priest shall burn them upon the altar, according to the offerings made by fire unto the Lord: and the priest shall make an atonement for his sin that he hath committed, and it shall be forgiven him.
text.NABRE: He shall remove all its fat just as the fat is removed from the lamb of the communion sacrifice. The priest shall burn these on the altar with the other oblations for the Lord. Thus the priest shall make atonement on the person’s behalf for the wrong committed, that the individual may be forgiven.
text.DRB: All the fat also he shall take away, as the fat of the ram, that is offered for pacifics, is wont to be taken away: and shall burn it upon the altar, a burnt sacrifice of our Lord: and he shall pray for him, and for his sin, and it shall be forgiven him.

text.NRSV: When any of you sin in that you have heard a public adjuration to testify and—though able to testify as one who has seen or learned of the matter—do not give information, you are subject to punishment.
text.Rotherham: Any person, moreover, whensoever he shall sin in that, when he heareth a voice of swearing, he himself, being a witness either seeing or knowing,—if he do not tell and so hath to bear his iniquity:—
text.KJV: And if a soul sin, and hear the voice of swearing, and is a witness, whether he hath seen or known of it; if he do not utter it, then he shall bear his iniquity.
text.NABRE: If a person, either having seen or come to know something, does wrong by refusing as a witness under oath to give information, that individual shall bear the penalty;
text.DRB: If a soul sin, and hear the voice of one swearing, and be witness, because either he himself saw, or is privy to it: unless he utter it, he shall bear his iniquity.

text.NRSV: Or when any of you touch any unclean thing—whether the carcass of an unclean beast or the carcass of unclean livestock or the carcass of an unclean swarming thing—and are unaware of it, you have become unclean, and are guilty.
text.Rotherham: Or, any person, who toucheth anything unclean, whether the carcase of an unclean wild-beast or the carcase of an unclean tame-beast, or the carcase of an unclean creeping thing,—and it is hidden from him, he himself being unclean, and he becometh aware of his guilt:—
text.KJV: Or if a soul touch any unclean thing, whether it be a carcase of an unclean beast, or a carcase of unclean cattle, or the carcase of unclean creeping things, and if it be hidden from him; he also shall be unclean, and guilty.
text.NABRE: or if someone, without being aware of it, touches any unclean thing, such as the carcass of an unclean wild animal, or an unclean domestic animal, or an unclean swarming creature, and thus is unclean and guilty;
text.DRB: The soul that toucheth any unclean thing, either that which was killed of a beast, or died of itself, or any other thing that creepeth: and forgeteth his uncleanness, is guilty, and hath offended:

text.NRSV: Or when you touch human uncleanness—any uncleanness by which one can become unclean—and, when you learn of it, become guilty;
text.Rotherham: Or whensoever one shall touch the uncleanness of man, as regardeth any uncleanness of his, wherewith one may become unclean,—and it be hidden from him, and then he himself, getting to know it becometh aware of his guilt:—
text.KJV: Or if he touch the uncleanness of man, whatsoever uncleanness it be that a man shall be defiled withal, and it be hid from him; when he knoweth of it, then he shall be guilty.
text.NABRE: or if someone, without being aware of it, touches some human uncleanness, whatever kind of uncleanness this may be, and then subsequently becomes aware of guilt;
text.DRB: and if he touch any thing of the uncleanness of man, according to any impurity wherewith he is wont to be polluted, & having forgotten, do know it afterward, he shall be guilty of an offence.

text.NRSV: or when any of you utter aloud a rash oath for a bad or a good purpose, whatever people utter in an oath, and are unaware of it, when you come to know it, you shall in any of these be guilty.
text.Rotherham: Or, any person, whensoever he shall swear, speaking unadvisedly with the lips, to harm or to help, as regardeth anything wherein the son of earth may speak unadvisedly by way of oath, and it be hidden from him,—and then, he himself, getting to know it, becometh aware of his guilt as regardeth any one of these things.
text.KJV: Or if a soul swear, pronouncing with his lips to do evil, or to do good, whatsoever it be that a man shall pronounce with an oath, and it be hid from him; when he knoweth of it, then he shall be guilty in one of these.
text.NABRE: or if someone, without being aware of it, rashly utters an oath with bad or good intent, whatever kind of oath this may be, and then subsequently becomes aware of guilt in regard to any of these matters—
text.DRB: The soul that sweareth, and uttereth with his lips that he would do either ill or well, and bindeth the same with an oath, and his word, & having forgotten afterward understandeth his offence,

text.NRSV: When you realize your guilt in any of these, you shall confess the sin that you have committed.
text.Rotherham: Then shall it be, when he becometh aware of his guilt, as regardeth any one of these things, that he shall confess that wherein he hath sinned;
text.KJV: And it shall be, when he shall be guilty in one of these things, that he shall confess that he hath sinned in that thing:
text.NABRE: when someone is guilty in regard to any of these matters, that person shall confess the wrong committed,
text.DRB: let him do penance for his sin,

text.NRSV: And you shall bring to the Lord, as your penalty for the sin that you have committed, a female from the flock, a lamb or a goat, as a sin offering; and the priest shall make atonement on your behalf for your sin.
text.Rotherham: and shall bring in as his guilt-bearer unto Yahweh for his sin which he hath committed, a female from the flock—a lamb or a kid of the goats—as a sin-bearer,—so shall the priest put propitiatory-covering over him, because of his sin.
text.KJV: And he shall bring his trespass offering unto the Lord for his sin which he hath sinned, a female from the flock, a lamb or a kid of the goats, for a sin offering; and the priest shall make an atonement for him concerning his sin.
text.NABRE: and make reparation to the Lord for the wrong committed: a female animal from the flock, a ewe lamb or a she-goat, as a purification offering. Thus the priest shall make atonement on the individual’s behalf for the wrong.
text.DRB: and offer of the flocks an ewe lamb, or a she goat, and the Priest shall pray for him and for his sin:

text.NRSV: But if you cannot afford a sheep, you shall bring to the Lord, as your penalty for the sin that you have committed, two turtledoves or two pigeons, one for a sin offering and the other for a burnt offering.
text.Rotherham: And, if his hand cannot reach sufficient for a lamb, then shall he bring in to bear his guilt—for that he hath sinned—two turtle-doves, or two young pigeons unto Yahweh,—one for a sin-bearer, and one for an ascending-sacrifice.
text.KJV: And if he be not able to bring a lamb, then he shall bring for his trespass, which he hath committed, two turtledoves, or two young pigeons, unto the Lord; one for a sin offering, and the other for a burnt offering.
text.NABRE: If, however, the person cannot afford an animal of the flock, that person shall bring to the Lord as reparation for the wrong committed two turtledoves or two pigeons, one for a purification offering and the other for a burnt offering.
text.DRB: but if he be not able to offer a beast, let him offer two turtles, or two young pigeons to our Lord, one for sin, and the other for an holocaust,

text.NRSV: You shall bring them to the priest, who shall offer first the one for the sin offering, wringing its head at the nape without severing it.
text.Rotherham: And he shall take them in unto the priest, who shall bring near that which is for a sin-bearer first,—and shall nip through the neck close to the head but shall not divide it asunder.
text.KJV: And he shall bring them unto the priest, who shall offer that which is for the sin offering first, and wring off his head from his neck, but shall not divide it asunder:
text.NABRE: The guilty party shall bring them to the priest, who shall offer the one for the purification offering first. Wringing its head at the neck, yet without breaking it off,
text.DRB: and he shall give them to the Priest: who offering the first for sin, shall writhe back the head thereof to the little pinions, so that it stick to the neck, and be not altogether broken off.

text.NRSV: He shall sprinkle some of the blood of the sin offering on the side of the altar, while the rest of the blood shall be drained out at the base of the altar; it is a sin offering.
text.Rotherham: And he shall sprinkle of the blood of the sin-bearer upon the wall of the altar, and the remainder of the blood, shall he drain out at the base of the altar,—a, sin-bearer, it is.
text.KJV: And he shall sprinkle of the blood of the sin offering upon the side of the altar; and the rest of the blood shall be wrung out at the bottom of the altar: it is a sin offering.
text.NABRE: he shall sprinkle some of the blood of the purification offering against the side of the altar. The rest of the blood shall be drained out against the base of the altar. It is a purification offering.
text.DRB: And of the blood thereof he shall sprinkle the wall of the altar, and whatsoever is left, he shall make it distill to the bottom thereof, because it is for sin.

text.NRSV: The second he shall prepare for a burnt offering according to the regulation. Thus the priest shall make atonement on your behalf for the sin that you have committed, and you shall be forgiven.
text.Rotherham: And of the second, shall he make an ascending-sacrifice according to the regulation,—so shall the priest put a propitiatory-covering over him, because of his sin which he hath committed and it shall be forgiven him.
text.KJV: And he shall offer the second for a burnt offering, according to the manner: and the priest shall make an atonement for him for his sin which he hath sinned, and it shall be forgiven him.
text.NABRE: The other bird he shall offer as a burnt offering according to procedure. Thus the priest shall make atonement on the person’s behalf for the wrong committed, so that the individual may be forgiven.
text.DRB: And the other he shall burn for an holocaust, as is wont to be done: and the Priest shall pray for him, and for his sin, and it shall be forgiven him.

text.NRSV: But if you cannot afford two turtledoves or two pigeons, you shall bring as your offering for the sin that you have committed one-tenth of an ephah of choice flour for a sin offering; you shall not put oil on it or lay frankincense on it, for it is a sin offering.
