text.Rotherham: But if his hand cannot lay hold of two turtledove, or two young pigeons, then shall he bring in as his oblation—because he hath sinned—the tenth of an ephah of fine meal for bearing sin,—he shall not put thereon oil, neither shall he lay thereon frankincense, for a sin-bearer, it is.
text.KJV: But if he be not able to bring two turtledoves, or two young pigeons, then he that sinned shall bring for his offering the tenth part of an ephah of fine flour for a sin offering; he shall put no oil upon it, neither shall he put any frankincense thereon: for it is a sin offering.
text.NABRE: If the person is unable to afford even two turtledoves or two pigeons, that person shall bring as an offering for the wrong committed one tenth of an ephah of bran flour for a purification offering. The guilty party shall not put oil or place frankincense on it, because it is a purification offering.
text.DRB: And if his hand be not able to offer two turtles, or two young pigeons, he shall offer for his sin of flour the tenth part of an ephi. He shall not put oil upon it, nor cast any frankincense thereon, because it is for sin,

text.NRSV: You shall bring it to the priest, and the priest shall scoop up a handful of it as its memorial portion, and turn this into smoke on the altar, with the offerings by fire to the Lord; it is a sin offering.
text.Rotherham: And he shall bring it in unto the priest, and the priest shall take therefrom a handful, as the memorial thereof, and shall make a perfume at the altar, upon the altar-flames of Yahweh,—a sin-bearer, it is.
text.KJV: Then shall he bring it to the priest, and the priest shall take his handful of it, even a memorial thereof, and burn it on the altar, according to the offerings made by fire unto the Lord: it is a sin offering.
text.NABRE: The individual shall bring it to the priest, who shall take a handful as a token of the offering and burn it on the altar with the other oblations for the Lord. It is a purification offering.
text.DRB: and he shall deliver it to the Priest: who taking thereof a full handful, shall burn it upon the altar for a monument of him that did offer it,

text.NRSV: Thus the priest shall make atonement on your behalf for whichever of these sins you have committed, and you shall be forgiven. Like the grain offering, the rest shall be for the priest.
text.Rotherham: So shall the priest put a propitiatory-covering over him, on account of his sin which he hath committed, departing from some one of these things, and it shall be forgiven him,—then shall it be the priest’s, like the meal-offering.
text.KJV: And the priest shall make an atonement for him as touching his sin that he hath sinned in one of these, and it shall be forgiven him: and the remnant shall be the priest’s, as a meat offering.
text.NABRE: Thus the priest shall make atonement on the person’s behalf for the wrong committed in any of the above cases, so that the individual may be forgiven. The rest of the offering, like the grain offering, shall belong to the priest. Reparation Offerings.
text.DRB: praying for him & making expiation: but the part that is left, himself shall have for a gift.

text.NRSV: The Lord spoke to Moses, saying:
text.Rotherham: And Yahweh spake unto Moses, saying—
text.KJV: And the Lord spake unto Moses, saying,
text.NABRE: The Lord said to Moses:
text.DRB: And our Lord spake to Moyses, saying:

text.NRSV: When any of you commit a trespass and sins unintentionally in any of the holy things of the Lord, you shall bring, as your guilt offering to the Lord, a ram without blemish from the flock, convertible into silver by the sanctuary shekel; it is a guilt offering.
text.Rotherham: Whensoever, any person, shall commit a trespass, and shall take away by mistake, from the holy things of Yahweh, then shall he bring in as his guilt-bearer unto Yahweh, a ram without defect out of the flock, with thine estimate in silver by shekels, after the shekel of the sanctuary, for a guilt-bearer:
text.KJV: If a soul commit a trespass, and sin through ignorance, in the holy things of the Lord; then he shall bring for his trespass unto the Lord a ram without blemish out of the flocks, with thy estimation by shekels of silver, after the shekel of the sanctuary, for a trespass offering:
text.NABRE: When a person commits sacrilege by inadvertently misusing any of the Lord ’s sacred objects, the wrongdoer shall bring to the Lord as reparation an unblemished ram from the flock, at the established value in silver shekels according to the sanctuary shekel, as a reparation offering.
text.DRB: If a soul transgressing the ceremonies, by error shall sin in those things that are sanctified to our Lord, he shall offer for his offence a ram without spot out of the flocks, that may be bought for two sicles, according to the weight of the Sanctuary:

text.NRSV: And you shall make restitution for the holy thing in which you were remiss, and shall add one-fifth to it and give it to the priest. The priest shall make atonement on your behalf with the ram of the guilt offering, and you shall be forgiven.
text.Rotherham: that, moreover, which he took away from the sanctuary, shall he make good and the fifth part thereof, shall he add thereunto, and shall give it to the priest,—and, the priest, shall put a propitiatory-covering over him with the guilt-bearing ram and it shall be forgiven him.
text.KJV: And he shall make amends for the harm that he hath done in the holy thing, and shall add the fifth part thereto, and give it unto the priest: and the priest shall make an atonement for him with the ram of the trespass offering, and it shall be forgiven him.
text.NABRE: The wrongdoer shall also restore what has been misused of the sacred objects, adding a fifth of its value, and give this to the priest. Thus the priest shall make atonement for the person with the ram of the reparation offering, so that the individual may be forgiven.
text.DRB: and the damage itself which he did, he shall restore, and the fifth part he shall add besides, delivering it to the Priest, who shall pray for him, offering the ram, and it shall be forgiven him.

text.NRSV: If any of you sin without knowing it, doing any of the things that by the Lord’s commandments ought not to be done, you have incurred guilt, and are subject to punishment.
text.Rotherham: And if any person when he shall sin, and do something, departing from any of the commandments of Yahweh, as to things which should not be done,—though he knew it not, shall so become guilty, and shall bear his iniquity,
text.KJV: And if a soul sin, and commit any of these things which are forbidden to be done by the commandments of the Lord; though he wist it not, yet is he guilty, and shall bear his iniquity.
text.NABRE: If someone does wrong and violates one of the Lord ’s prohibitions without realizing it, that person is guilty and shall bear the penalty.
text.DRB: If a soul sin by ignorance, and do one of those things which by the law of the Lord are forbidden, and being guilty of sin, understand his iniquity,

text.NRSV: You shall bring to the priest a ram without blemish from the flock (or the equivalent) as a guilt offering; and the priest shall make atonement on your behalf for the error that you committed unintentionally, and you shall be forgiven.
text.Rotherham: then shall he bring in a ram without defect out of the flock by thine estimate as a guilt-bearer unto the priest,—and the priest shall put a propitiatory-covering over him, on account of his mistake which he made though he knew it not and it shall be forgiven him:
text.KJV: And he shall bring a ram without blemish out of the flock, with thy estimation, for a trespass offering, unto the priest: and the priest shall make an atonement for him concerning his ignorance wherein he erred and wist it not, and it shall be forgiven him.
text.NABRE: The individual shall bring to the priest an unblemished ram of the flock, at the established value, for a reparation offering. The priest shall then make atonement on the offerer’s behalf for the error inadvertently and unknowingly committed so that the individual may be forgiven.
text.DRB: he shall offer a ram without spot of the flocks to the Priest, according to the measure and estimation of the sin: who shall pray for him, because he did it unwitting: and it shall be forgiven him,

text.NRSV: It is a guilt offering; you have incurred guilt before the Lord.
text.Rotherham: a guilt-bearer, it is,—he was, verily guilty against Yahweh.
text.KJV: It is a trespass offering: he hath certainly trespassed against the Lord.
text.NABRE: It is a reparation offering. The individual must make reparation to the Lord.
text.DRB: because by error he offended against the Lord.

text.NRSV: The Lord spoke to Moses, saying:
text.Rotherham: And Yahweh spake unto Moses, saying—
text.KJV: And the Lord spake unto Moses, saying,
text.NABRE: B. Instructions for the Priests Chapter 6 The Daily Burnt Offering. The Lord said to Moses:
text.DRB: Our Lord spake to Moyses, saying:

text.NRSV: When any of you sin and commit a trespass against the Lord by deceiving a neighbor in a matter of a deposit or pledge, or by robbery, or if you have defrauded a neighbor,
text.Rotherham: When any person, shall sin, and shall commit a trespass against Yahweh,—and shall withhold something of the truth from his neighbour in respect of a deposit, or a pledge or anything plundered, or shall use extortion with his neighbour;
text.KJV: If a soul sin, and commit a trespass against the Lord, and lie unto his neighbour in that which was delivered him to keep, or in fellowship, or in a thing taken away by violence, or hath deceived his neighbour;
text.NABRE: Give Aaron and his sons the following command: This is the ritual for the burnt offering—the burnt offering that is to remain on the hearth of the altar all night until the next morning, while the fire is kept burning on the altar.
text.DRB: The soul that shall sin, and contemning the Lord, shall deny unto his neighbour the thing delivered to his custody, which was committed to his credit, or shall by force extort any thing, or do oppression,

text.NRSV: or have found something lost and lied about it—if you swear falsely regarding any of the various things that one may do and sin thereby—
text.Rotherham: or shall find something lost and shall withhold something of the truth therein, and shall swear to a falsehood,—as regardeth a single thing of all that a son of earth may do, to commit sin thereby;
text.KJV: Or have found that which was lost, and lieth concerning it, and sweareth falsely; in any of all these that a man doeth, sinning therein:
text.NABRE: The priest, clothed in his linen robe and wearing linen pants underneath, shall take away the ashes to which the fire has reduced the burnt offering on the altar, and lay them at the side of the altar.
text.DRB: or shall find a thing lost, and denying it, be also forsworn, and shall do any other thing of many, wherein men are wont to sin,

text.NRSV: when you have sinned and realize your guilt, and would restore what you took by robbery or by fraud or the deposit that was committed to you, or the lost thing that you found,
text.Rotherham: and so it shall come about that he shall commit sin and then become aware of his guilt, then shall he return the plunder which he had plundered, or the extortion which he had extorted, or the deposit that was deposited with him,—or the lost thing which he hath found:
text.KJV: Then it shall be, because he hath sinned, and is guilty, that he shall restore that which he took violently away, or the thing which he hath deceitfully gotten, or that which was delivered him to keep, or the lost thing which he found,
text.NABRE: Then, having taken off these garments and put on other garments, he shall carry the ashes to a clean place outside the camp.
text.DRB: being convicted of the offence,

text.NRSV: or anything else about which you have sworn falsely, you shall repay the principal amount and shall add one-fifth to it. You shall pay it to its owner when you realize your guilt.
text.Rotherham: or in anything as to which he hath been swearing to a falsehood, then shall he make it good in the principal thereof, and the fifth part thereof, shall he add thereunto,—to whomsoever it belongeth, to him, shall he give it in the day he becometh aware of his guilt;
text.KJV: Or all that about which he hath sworn falsely; he shall even restore it in the principal, and shall add the fifth part more thereto, and give it unto him to whom it appertaineth, in the day of his trespass offering.
text.NABRE: The fire on the altar is to be kept burning; it must not go out. Every morning the priest shall put firewood on it. On this he shall lay out the burnt offering and burn the fat of the communion offering.
text.DRB: he shall render all things which by fraud he would have obtained, whole, and the fifth part besides to the owner unto whom he did the damage.

text.NRSV: And you shall bring to the priest, as your guilt offering to the Lord, a ram without blemish from the flock, or its equivalent, for a guilt offering.
text.Rotherham: but, his guilt-bearer, itself, shall he bring in unto Yahweh,—a ram without defect out of the flock by thine estimate as a guilt-bearer, unto the priest.
text.KJV: And he shall bring his trespass offering unto the Lord, a ram without blemish out of the flock, with thy estimation, for a trespass offering, unto the priest:
text.NABRE: The fire is to be kept burning continuously on the altar; it must not go out. The Grain Offering.
text.DRB: But for his sin he shall offer a ram without spot out of the flock, and shall give it to the Priest, according to the estimation and measure of the offence:

text.NRSV: The priest shall make atonement on your behalf before the Lord, and you shall be forgiven for any of the things that one may do and incur guilt thereby.
text.Rotherham: So shall the priest put a propitiatory-covering over him before Yahweh, and it shall be forgiven him,—on account of any one thing, of all which one might do, so as to become guilty therein.
text.KJV: And the priest shall make an atonement for him before the Lord: and it shall be forgiven him for any thing of all that he hath done in trespassing therein.
text.NABRE: This is the ritual of the grain offering. Aaron’s sons shall offer it before the Lord, in front of the altar.
text.DRB: who shall pray for him before the Lord, and he shall have forgiveness for every thing that in doing he sinned.

text.NRSV: The Lord spoke to Moses, saying:
text.Rotherham: And Yahweh spake unto Moses saying:
text.KJV: And the Lord spake unto Moses, saying,
text.NABRE: A priest shall then take from the grain offering a handful of bran flour and oil, together with all the frankincense that is on it, and this he shall burn on the altar as a token of the offering, a sweet aroma to the Lord.
text.DRB: And our Lord spake to Moyses, saying:

text.NRSV: Command Aaron and his sons, saying: This is the ritual of the burnt offering. The burnt offering itself shall remain on the hearth upon the altar all night until the morning, while the fire on the altar shall be kept burning.
text.Rotherham: Command Aaron and his sons, saying, This is the law of the ascending-sacrifice,—the same, is the ascending-sacrifice, which is upon the hearth, upon the altar, all the night until the morning,—and the fire of the altar, shall be kept burning therein.
text.KJV: Command Aaron and his sons, saying, This is the law of the burnt offering: It is the burnt offering, because of the burning upon the altar all night unto the morning, and the fire of the altar shall be burning in it.
text.NABRE: The rest of it Aaron and his sons may eat; but it must be eaten unleavened in a sacred place: in the court of the tent of meeting they shall eat it.
text.DRB: Command Aaron and his sons: This is the Law of an holocaust: It shall be burnt upon the altar, all night until morning: the fire shall be upon the same altar.

text.NRSV: The priest shall put on his linen vestments after putting on his linen undergarments next to his body; and he shall take up the ashes to which the fire has reduced the burnt offering on the altar, and place them beside the altar.
text.Rotherham: So then the priest shall put on his upper garment of linen, and, breeches of linen, shall he put on over his flesh, then shall he take up the fat-ashes, whereto the fire consumeth the ascending-sacrifice on the altar,—and shall put them beside the altar.
text.KJV: And the priest shall put on his linen garment, and his linen breeches shall he put upon his flesh, and take up the ashes which the fire hath consumed with the burnt offering on the altar, and he shall put them beside the altar.
text.NABRE: It shall not be baked with leaven. I have given it to them as their portion from the oblations for the Lord; it is most holy, like the purification offering and the reparation offering.
text.DRB: The Priest shall be revested with the tunic and the linen femorals: and he shall take up the ashes, which the devouring fire burned, and putting them besides the altar,

text.NRSV: Then he shall take off his vestments and put on other garments, and carry the ashes out to a clean place outside the camp.
text.Rotherham: Then shall he put off his garments, and put on other garments,—and shall carry forth the fat-ashes unto the outside of the camp, unto a clean place,
text.KJV: And he shall put off his garments, and put on other garments, and carry forth the ashes without the camp unto a clean place.
text.NABRE: Every male of Aaron’s descendants may eat of it perpetually throughout your generations as their rightful due from the oblations for the Lord. Whatever touches the oblations becomes holy. High Priest’s Daily Grain Offering.
text.DRB: shall be unvested of his former vestments, and being clothed with others, shall carry them forth, without the camp, and in a most clean place shall cause them to be consumed unto dust.

text.NRSV: The fire on the altar shall be kept burning; it shall not go out. Every morning the priest shall add wood to it, lay out the burnt offering upon it, and turn into smoke the fat pieces of the offerings of well-being.
text.Rotherham: And, the fire on the altar, shall be kept burning therein, it shall not be suffered to go out, but the priest shall kindle up thereon pieces of wood, morning by morning,—and shall set in order thereupon the ascending-sacrifice, and shall make a perfume thereon with the fat-pieces of the peace-offerings:
text.KJV: And the fire upon the altar shall be burning in it; it shall not be put out: and the priest shall burn wood on it every morning, and lay the burnt offering in order upon it; and he shall burn thereon the fat of the peace offerings.
text.NABRE: The Lord said to Moses:
text.DRB: And the fire on the altar shall always burn, which the Priest shall nourish, putting wood underneath, in the morning every day, and laying on the holocaust, thereupon shall burn the fat of the pacifics.

text.NRSV: A perpetual fire shall be kept burning on the altar; it shall not go out.
text.Rotherham: fire, shall continually be kept burning on the altar, it shall not be suffered to go out.
text.KJV: The fire shall ever be burning upon the altar; it shall never go out.
text.NABRE: This is the offering that Aaron and his sons shall present to the Lord on the day he is anointed: one tenth of an ephah of bran flour for the regular grain offering, half of it in the morning and half of it in the evening.
text.DRB: This fire is continual which shall never fail on the altar.

text.NRSV: This is the ritual of the grain offering: The sons of Aaron shall offer it before the Lord, in front of the altar.
text.Rotherham: And, this, is the law of the meal-offering,—the sons of Aaron shall bring it near before Yahweh, unto the front of the altar.
text.KJV: And this is the law of the meat offering: the sons of Aaron shall offer it before the Lord, before the altar.
text.NABRE: You shall bring it well kneaded and fried in oil on a griddle. Having broken the offering into pieces, you shall present it as a sweet aroma to the Lord.
text.DRB: This is the Law of the sacrifice and libaments, which the children of Aaron shall offer before the Lord, and before the altar.

text.NRSV: You shall take from it a handful of the choice flour of the grain offering, with its oil and all the frankincense that is on the offering, and turn this token portion into smoke on the altar as a pleasing odor to the Lord.
text.Rotherham: Then shall one lift up therefrom a handful of the fine meal of the meal-offering, and of the oil thereof, and all the frankincense which is upon the meal-offering,—and shall make a perfume at the altar, an altar-flame of a satisfying odour, shall the memorial thereof be, unto Yahweh.
text.KJV: And he shall take of it his handful, of the flour of the meat offering, and of the oil thereof, and all the frankincense which is upon the meat offering, and shall burn it upon the altar for a sweet savour, even the memorial of it, unto the Lord.
text.NABRE: The anointed priest descended from Aaron who succeeds him shall do likewise. This is the Lord ’s due forever. The offering shall be wholly burned.
text.DRB: The Priest shall take up a handful of the flour, that is tempered with oil, and all the frankincense, that is put upon the flour: and he shall burn it on the altar for a monument of most sweet odour to the Lord:

text.NRSV: Aaron and his sons shall eat what is left of it; it shall be eaten as unleavened cakes in a holy place; they shall eat it in the court of the tent of meeting.
text.Rotherham: And the remainder thereof, shall Aaron and his sons eat,—as unleavened cakes, shall it be eaten, in a holy place, within the court of the tent of meeting, shall they eat it.
text.KJV: And the remainder thereof shall Aaron and his sons eat: with unleavened bread shall it be eaten in the holy place; in the court of the tabernacle of the congregation they shall eat it.
text.NABRE: Every grain offering of a priest shall be a whole offering; it may not be eaten. Purification Offerings.
text.DRB: and the part of the flour that is left, shall Aaron eat with his sons, without leaven: and he shall eat it in the Holy place of the court of the tabernacle.

text.NRSV: It shall not be baked with leaven. I have given it as their portion of my offerings by fire; it is most holy, like the sin offering and the guilt offering.
text.Rotherham: It shall not be baked into anything leavened, as their portion, have I given it, from among the altar-flames of Yahweh,—most holy, it is, like the sin-bearer, and like the guilt-bearer,
text.KJV: It shall not be baken with leaven. I have given it unto them for their portion of my offerings made by fire; it is most holy, as is the sin offering, and as the trespass offering.
text.NABRE: The Lord said to Moses:
text.DRB: And therefore it shall not be leavened, because part thereof is offered for the burnt sacrifice of the Lord. It shall be most Holy, as that for sin, and for offence.

text.NRSV: Every male among the descendants of Aaron shall eat of it, as their due for all time throughout your generations, from the Lord’s offerings by fire; anything that touches them shall become holy.
text.Rotherham: Any male among the sons of Aaron may eat it, an age-abiding statute to your generations from among the altar-flames of Yahweh,—every one that toucheth them shall be hallowed.
text.KJV: All the males among the children of Aaron shall eat of it. It shall be a statute for ever in your generations concerning the offerings of the Lord made by fire: every one that toucheth them shall be holy.
text.NABRE: Tell Aaron and his sons: This is the ritual for the purification offering. At the place where the burnt offering is slaughtered, there also, before the Lord, shall the purification offering be slaughtered. It is most holy.
text.DRB: The males only of Aaron's stock shall eat it. It shall be an ordinance and everlasting in your generations of the sacrifices of the Lord. Every one that toucheth them, shall be sanctified.

text.NRSV: The Lord spoke to Moses, saying:
text.Rotherham: And Yahweh spake unto Moses, saying—
text.KJV: And the Lord spake unto Moses, saying,
text.NABRE: The priest who offers the purification offering shall eat of it; it shall be eaten in a sacred place, in the court of the tent of meeting.
text.DRB: And our Lord spake to Moyses, saying:

text.NRSV: This is the offering that Aaron and his sons shall offer to the Lord on the day when he is anointed: one-tenth of an ephah of choice flour as a regular grain offering, half of it in the morning and half in the evening.
text.Rotherham: This, is the oblation of Aaron and his sons, which they shall bring near unto Yahweh in the day when he is anointed, The tenth of an ephah of fine meal, as a continual meal-offering,—half thereof in the morning, and half thereof in the evening;
text.KJV: This is the offering of Aaron and of his sons, which they shall offer unto the Lord in the day when he is anointed; the tenth part of an ephah of fine flour for a meat offering perpetual, half of it in the morning, and half thereof at night.
text.NABRE: Whatever touches its flesh becomes holy. If any of its blood spatters on a garment, the stained part must be washed in a sacred place.
text.DRB: This is the oblation of Aaron, and of his sons, which they must offer to the Lord, in the day of their unction. The tenth part of an ephi of flour shall they offer in a sacrifice for ever, half thereof in the morning, and half thereof at even:

text.NRSV: It shall be made with oil on a griddle; you shall bring it well soaked, as a grain offering of baked pieces, and you shall present it as a pleasing odor to the Lord.
text.Rotherham: on a pan, with oil, shall it be made when well mingled, shalt thou bring it in,—in baked portions, as a meal-offering in pieces, shalt thou bring it near as a satisfying odour unto Yahweh.
text.KJV: In a pan it shall be made with oil; and when it is baken, thou shalt bring it in: and the baken pieces of the meat offering shalt thou offer for a sweet savour unto the Lord.
text.NABRE: A clay vessel in which it has been boiled shall be broken; if it is boiled in a copper vessel, this shall be scoured afterward and rinsed with water.
text.DRB: which being tempered with oil shall be fried in a frying pan.

text.NRSV: And so the priest, anointed from among Aaron’s descendants as a successor, shall prepare it; it is the Lord’s—a perpetual due—to be turned entirely into smoke.
text.Rotherham: And the priest that is anointed in his stead from among his sons, shall prepare it,—[it is] an age-abiding statute, that unto Yahweh, shall a perfume, of the entire gift, be made:
text.KJV: And the priest of his sons that is anointed in his stead shall offer it: it is a statute for ever unto the Lord; it shall be wholly burnt.
text.NABRE: Every male of the priestly line may eat it. It is most holy.
text.DRB: And the Priest that by right succeedeth his father, shall offer it hot, for a most sweet odour to the Lord, and it shall wholly be burnt on the altar.

text.NRSV: Every grain offering of a priest shall be wholly burned; it shall not be eaten.
text.Rotherham: yea, every meal-offering of a priest, shall be, entire—it shall not be eaten.
text.KJV: For every meat offering for the priest shall be wholly burnt: it shall not be eaten.
text.NABRE: But no purification offering of which some blood has been brought into the tent of meeting to make atonement in the sanctuary shall be eaten; it must be burned with fire.
text.DRB: For every sacrifice of the Priest shall be consumed with fire, neither shall any man eat thereof.

text.NRSV: The Lord spoke to Moses, saying:
text.Rotherham: And Yahweh spake unto Moses, saying—
text.KJV: And the Lord spake unto Moses, saying,
text.DRB: And our Lord spake to Moyses, saying:

text.NRSV: Speak to Aaron and his sons, saying: This is the ritual of the sin offering. The sin offering shall be slaughtered before the Lord at the spot where the burnt offering is slaughtered; it is most holy.
text.Rotherham: Speak unto Aaron and unto his sons, saying, This, is the law of the sin-bearer. In the place where the ascending-sacrifice is slain, shall the sin-bearer be slain, before Yahweh, most holy, it is.
text.KJV: Speak unto Aaron and to his sons, saying, This is the law of the sin offering: In the place where the burnt offering is killed shall the sin offering be killed before the Lord: it is most holy.
text.DRB: Speak to Aaron and his sons: This is the law of the host for sin. In the place where the holocaust is offered, shall it be immolated before our Lord. It is Holy of holies.

text.NRSV: The priest who offers it as a sin offering shall eat of it; it shall be eaten in a holy place, in the court of the tent of meeting.
text.Rotherham: The priest who maketh it a sin-bearer, shall eat it,—in a holy place, shall it be eaten, in the court of the tent of meeting.
text.KJV: The priest that offereth it for sin shall eat it: in the holy place shall it be eaten, in the court of the tabernacle of the congregation.
text.DRB: The Priest that doth offer it, shall eat it in a holy place, in the court of the tabernacle.

text.NRSV: Whatever touches its flesh shall become holy; and when any of its blood is spattered on a garment, you shall wash the bespattered part in a holy place.
text.Rotherham: Every one who toucheth the flesh thereof, shall be hallowed; and when one sprinkleth some of the blood thereof upon a garment, that whereon it was sprinkled, shalt thou wash in a holy place.
text.KJV: Whatsoever shall touch the flesh thereof shall be holy: and when there is sprinkled of the blood thereof upon any garment, thou shalt wash that whereon it was sprinkled in the holy place.
text.DRB: Whatsoever shall touch the flesh thereof, shall be sanctified. If of the blood thereof a garment be sprinkled, it shall be washed in a holy place.

text.NRSV: An earthen vessel in which it is boiled shall be broken; but if it is boiled in a bronze vessel, that shall be scoured and rinsed in water.
text.Rotherham: But, the earthen vessel wherein it is boiled, shall be broken,—or, if, in a vessel of bronze, it hath been boiled, then shall the vessel be scoured and rinsed in water.
text.KJV: But the earthen vessel wherein it is sodden shall be broken: and if it be sodden in a brasen pot, it shall be both scoured, and rinsed in water.
text.DRB: And the earthen vessel, wherein it was sodden, shall be broken, but if the vessel be of brass, it shall be scoured, and washed with water.

text.NRSV: Every male among the priests shall eat of it; it is most holy.
text.Rotherham: Any male among the priests may eat thereof,—most holy, it is.
text.KJV: All the males among the priests shall eat thereof: it is most holy.
text.DRB: Every male of the priestly race shall eat of the flesh thereof, because it is Holy of holies.

text.NRSV: But no sin offering shall be eaten from which any blood is brought into the tent of meeting for making atonement in the holy place; it shall be burned with fire.
text.Rotherham: But, no sin-bearer whereof any of the blood is taken into the tent of meeting to make a propitiatory-covering in the sanctuary, shall be eaten,—with fire, shall it be consumed.
text.KJV: And no sin offering, whereof any of the blood is brought into the tabernacle of the congregation to reconcile withal in the holy place, shall be eaten: it shall be burnt in the fire.
text.DRB: For the host that is slain for sin, whose blood is carried into the tabernacle of testimony to make expiation in the Sanctuary, shall not be eaten, but shall be burnt with fire.

text.NRSV: This is the ritual of the guilt offering. It is most holy;
text.Rotherham: And this, is the law of the guilt-bearer,—most holy, it is!
text.KJV: Likewise this is the law of the trespass offering: it is most holy.
text.NABRE: This is the ritual for the reparation offering. It is most holy.
text.DRB: This also is the law of an host for an offence, the most Holy:

text.NRSV: at the spot where the burnt offering is slaughtered they shall slaughter the guilt offering, and its blood shall be dashed against all sides of the altar.
text.Rotherham: In the place where they slay the ascending-sacrifice, shall they slay the guilt-bearer; and, the blood thereof, shall one dash against the altar, round about;
text.KJV: In the place where they kill the burnt offering shall they kill the trespass offering: and the blood thereof shall he sprinkle round about upon the altar.
text.NABRE: At the place where the burnt offering is slaughtered, the reparation offering shall also be slaughtered. Its blood shall be splashed on all the sides of the altar.
text.DRB: therefore where the holocaust is immolated, the victim also for an offence shall be slain: the blood thereof shall be poured round about the altar.

text.NRSV: All its fat shall be offered: the broad tail, the fat that covers the entrails,
text.Rotherham: and, as for all the fat thereof, one shall bring near therefrom,—the fat-tail, and the fat that covereth the inwards;
text.KJV: And he shall offer of it all the fat thereof; the rump, and the fat that covereth the inwards,
text.NABRE: All of its fat shall be offered: the fatty tail, the fat that covers the inner organs, and all the fat that adheres to them,
text.DRB: They shall offer thereof the rump and the fat that covereth the entrails:

text.NRSV: the two kidneys with the fat that is on them at the loins, and the appendage of the liver, which shall be removed with the kidneys.
text.Rotherham: and the two kidneys, with the fat that is upon them, which is upon the loins,—and the caul upon the liver, up to the kidneys, shall he remove it.
text.KJV: And the two kidneys, and the fat that is on them, which is by the flanks, and the caul that is above the liver, with the kidneys, it shall he take away:
text.NABRE: as well as the two kidneys with the fat on them near the loins, and the lobe of the liver, which is removed with the kidneys.
text.DRB: the two little kidneys, and the fat that is beside the guts, and the caul of the liver with the two little kidneys.

text.NRSV: The priest shall turn them into smoke on the altar as an offering by fire to the Lord; it is a guilt offering.
text.Rotherham: And the priest shall make a perfume with them at the altar, an altar-flame unto Yahweh—a guilt-bearer, it is,
text.KJV: And the priest shall burn them upon the altar for an offering made by fire unto the Lord: it is a trespass offering.
text.NABRE: The priest shall burn these on the altar as an oblation to the Lord. It is a reparation offering.
text.DRB: And the Priest shall burn them upon the altar: it is the burnt sacrifice of our Lord for an offence.

text.NRSV: Every male among the priests shall eat of it; it shall be eaten in a holy place; it is most holy.
text.Rotherham: Any male among the priests, may eat thereof,—in a holy place, shall it be eaten, most holy, it is.
text.KJV: Every male among the priests shall eat thereof: it shall be eaten in the holy place: it is most holy.
text.NABRE: Every male of the priestly line may eat of it; but it must be eaten in a sacred place. It is most holy.
text.DRB: Every male of the Priest's stock, in a holy place shall eat this flesh, because it is most Holy.

text.NRSV: The guilt offering is like the sin offering, there is the same ritual for them; the priest who makes atonement with it shall have it.
text.Rotherham: As the sin-bearer, so the guilt-bearer,—one law, for them,—the priest who maketh a propitiatory-covering, therewith,—to him, shall it belong,
text.KJV: As the sin offering is, so is the trespass offering: there is one law for them: the priest that maketh atonement therewith shall have it.
text.NABRE: Because the purification offering and the reparation offering are alike, both have the same ritual. The reparation offering belongs to the priest who makes atonement with it.
text.DRB: As the host for sin is offered, so also that for an offence: the law of both hosts shall be one: to the Priest that offereth it, it shall pertain.

text.NRSV: So, too, the priest who offers anyone’s burnt offering shall keep the skin of the burnt offering that he offered.
text.Rotherham: And, as for the priest who bringeth near the ascending-sacrifice of any man, the skin of the ascending-sacrifice which he hath brought near, to the priest himself, shall it belong.
text.KJV: And the priest that offereth any man’s burnt offering, even the priest shall have to himself the skin of the burnt offering which he hath offered.
text.NABRE: As for the priest who offers someone’s burnt offering, to him belongs the hide of the burnt offering that is offered.
text.DRB: The Priest that offereth the victim of holocaust, shall have the skin thereof.

text.NRSV: And every grain offering baked in the oven, and all that is prepared in a pan or on a griddle, shall belong to the priest who offers it.
text.Rotherham: And as for any meal-offering that may be baked in an oven, or anything that hath been prepared in a boiler or on a girdle, to the priest that bringeth it near—to him, shall it belong,
text.KJV: And all the meat offering that is baken in the oven, and all that is dressed in the fryingpan, and in the pan, shall be the priest’s that offereth it.
text.NABRE: Also, every grain offering that is baked in an oven or made in a pan or on a griddle shall belong to the priest who offers it,
text.DRB: And every sacrifice of flour, that is baked in the oven, & whatsoever is prepared upon the gridiron, or in the frying pan, it shall be that Priest's by whom it is offered.

text.NRSV: But every other grain offering, mixed with oil or dry, shall belong to all the sons of Aaron equally.
text.Rotherham: And, as for any meal-offering overflowed with oil or dry,—to all the sons of Aaron, shall it belong, to one as much as to another.
text.KJV: And every meat offering, mingled with oil, and dry, shall all the sons of Aaron have, one as much as another.
text.NABRE: whereas all grain offerings that are mixed with oil or are dry shall belong to all of Aaron’s sons without distinction. Communion Sacrifices.
text.DRB: Whether they be tempered with oil, or dry, to all the sons of Aaron equal measure shall be divided to ever one.

text.NRSV: This is the ritual of the sacrifice of well-being that one may offer to the Lord.
text.Rotherham: And, this, is the law of the peace-offering which one may bring near unto Yahweh.
text.KJV: And this is the law of the sacrifice of peace offerings, which he shall offer unto the Lord.
text.NABRE: This is the ritual for the communion sacrifice that is offered to the Lord.
text.DRB: This is the law of the host of pacifics that is offered to our Lord.

text.NRSV: If you offer it for thanksgiving, you shall offer with the thank offering unleavened cakes mixed with oil, unleavened wafers spread with oil, and cakes of choice flour well soaked in oil.
text.Rotherham: If, for thanksgiving, he bring it near, then shall he bring near, with the thanksgiving sacrifice perforated cakes unleavened, overflowed with oil, and wafers unleavened anointed with oil,—and of fine flour moistened, perforated cakes overflowed with oil.
text.KJV: If he offer it for a thanksgiving, then he shall offer with the sacrifice of thanksgiving unleavened cakes mingled with oil, and unleavened wafers anointed with oil, and cakes mingled with oil, of fine flour, fried.
text.NABRE: If someone offers it for thanksgiving, that person shall offer it with unleavened cakes mixed with oil, unleavened wafers spread with oil, and cakes made of bran flour mixed with oil and well kneaded.
text.DRB: If the oblation be for thanks-giving, they shall offer loaves without leaven tempered with oil, and wafers unleavened laid over with oil, and fried flour, and manchets tempered with the mingling of oil:

text.NRSV: With your thanksgiving sacrifice of well-being you shall bring your offering with cakes of leavened bread.
text.Rotherham: With perforated cakes of leavened bread, shall he bring near his oblation, with his peace-offering of thanksgiving,
text.KJV: Besides the cakes, he shall offer for his offering leavened bread with the sacrifice of thanksgiving of his peace offerings.
text.NABRE: One shall present this offering together with loaves of leavened bread along with the thanksgiving communion sacrifice.
text.DRB: Loaves also leavened with the host of thanks, which is offered for pacifics:

text.NRSV: From this you shall offer one cake from each offering, as a gift to the Lord; it shall belong to the priest who dashes the blood of the offering of well-being.
text.Rotherham: Then shall he bring near therefrom one of each oblation, a heave-offering unto Yahweh,—to the priest who dasheth [against the altar] the blood of the peace-offering—to him, shall it belong.
text.KJV: And of it he shall offer one out of the whole oblation for an heave offering unto the Lord, and it shall be the priest’s that sprinkleth the blood of the peace offerings.
text.NABRE: From this the individual shall offer one bread of each type of offering as a contribution to the Lord; this shall belong to the priest who splashes the blood of the communion offering.
text.DRB: whereof one for first fruits shall be offered to our Lord, and shall be the Priest's that shall pour out the blood of the host.

text.NRSV: And the flesh of your thanksgiving sacrifice of well-being shall be eaten on the day it is offered; you shall not leave any of it until morning.
text.Rotherham: But, as for the flesh of his peace-offering of thanksgiving, on the day of bringing it near, shall it be eaten,—he shall not leave thereof until morning,
text.KJV: And the flesh of the sacrifice of his peace offerings for thanksgiving shall be eaten the same day that it is offered; he shall not leave any of it until the morning.
text.NABRE: The meat of the thanksgiving communion sacrifice shall be eaten on the day it is offered; none of it may be kept till the next morning.
text.DRB: the flesh whereof shall be eaten the same day, neither shall any of it remain until morning.

text.NRSV: But if the sacrifice you offer is a votive offering or a freewill offering, it shall be eaten on the day that you offer your sacrifice, and what is left of it shall be eaten the next day;
text.Rotherham: But, if, a vow or a freewill offering, be his sacrifice of oblation, in the day he bringeth near his sacrifice, shall it be eaten, and on the morrow, then may the remainder thereof, be eaten;
text.KJV: But if the sacrifice of his offering be a vow, or a voluntary offering, it shall be eaten the same day that he offereth his sacrifice: and on the morrow also the remainder of it shall be eaten:
text.NABRE: However, if the sacrifice offered is a votive or a voluntary offering, it shall be eaten on the day the sacrifice is offered, and on the next day what is left over may be eaten.
text.DRB: If any man by vow, or of his own accord offer an host, it shall in like manner be eaten the same day: but if ought remain until the morrow, it is lawful to eat it:

text.NRSV: but what is left of the flesh of the sacrifice shall be burned up on the third day.
text.Rotherham: but as for the remainder of the flesh of the sacrifice, on the third day—in fire, shall it be consumed.
text.KJV: But the remainder of the flesh of the sacrifice on the third day shall be burnt with fire.
text.NABRE: But what is left over of the meat of the sacrifice on the third day must be burned in the fire.
text.DRB: but whatsoever the third day shall find, fire shall consume it.

text.NRSV: If any of the flesh of your sacrifice of well-being is eaten on the third day, it shall not be acceptable, nor shall it be credited to the one who offers it; it shall be an abomination, and the one who eats of it shall incur guilt.
text.Rotherham: And, if any of the flesh of his peace-offering, be at all eaten, on the third day, he that brought it near [shall not be accepted] it shall not be reckoned to him, unclean, shall it be,—and the person who shall eat thereof, shall bear his iniquity.
text.KJV: And if any of the flesh of the sacrifice of his peace offerings be eaten at all on the third day, it shall not be accepted, neither shall it be imputed unto him that offereth it: it shall be an abomination, and the soul that eateth of it shall bear his iniquity.
text.NABRE: If indeed any of the flesh of the communion sacrifice is eaten on the third day, it shall not be accepted; it will not be reckoned to the credit of the one offering it. Rather it becomes a desecrated meat. Anyone who eats of it shall bear the penalty.
text.DRB: If any man eat of the flesh of the victim of pacifics the third day, the oblation shall be of none effect, neither shall it profit the offerer: yea rather whatsoever soul shall defile itself with such meat, shall be guilty of prevarication.

text.NRSV: Flesh that touches any unclean thing shall not be eaten; it shall be burned. As for other flesh, all who are clean may eat such flesh.
text.Rotherham: And, the flesh that toucheth anything unclean, shall not be eaten, in fire, shall it be consumed,—but, as for the [other] flesh, any one who is clean, may eat flesh.
text.KJV: And the flesh that toucheth any unclean thing shall not be eaten; it shall be burnt with fire: and as for the flesh, all that be clean shall eat thereof.
text.NABRE: Should the meat touch anything unclean, it may not be eaten, but shall be burned in the fire. As for other meat, all who are clean may eat of it.
text.DRB: The flesh that hath touched any unclean thing, shall not be eaten, but shall be burnt with fire: he that is clean, shall eat thereof.

text.NRSV: But those who eat flesh from the Lord’s sacrifice of well-being while in a state of uncleanness shall be cut off from their kin.
text.Rotherham: But as for the person who shall eat flesh from the peace-offering, which pertaineth unto Yahweh, while his uncleanness is on him, then shall that person be cut off from among his kinsfolk.
text.KJV: But the soul that eateth of the flesh of the sacrifice of peace offerings, that pertain unto the Lord, having his uncleanness upon him, even that soul shall be cut off from his people.
text.NABRE: If, however, someone in a state of uncleanness eats the meat of a communion sacrifice belonging to the Lord, that person shall be cut off from the people.
text.DRB: Asoul being polluted that eateth of the flesh of the host of pacifics, which is offered to our Lord, shall perish from his people.

text.NRSV: When any one of you touches any unclean thing—human uncleanness or an unclean animal or any unclean creature—and then eats flesh from the Lord’s sacrifice of well-being, you shall be cut off from your kin.
text.Rotherham: Yea, as for, any person, whensoever he shall touch anything unclean—the uncleanness of man or an unclean beast, or any unclean disgusting thing,—and then eateth of the flesh of a peace-offering that pertaineth unto Yahweh, then shall that person be cut off from among his kinsfolk.
text.KJV: Moreover the soul that shall touch any unclean thing, as the uncleanness of man, or any unclean beast, or any abominable unclean thing, and eat of the flesh of the sacrifice of peace offerings, which pertain unto the Lord, even that soul shall be cut off from his people.
text.NABRE: Likewise, if someone touches anything unclean, whether it be human uncleanness or an unclean animal or an unclean loathsome creature, and then eats the meat of the communion sacrifice belonging to the Lord, that person, too, shall be cut off from the people.
text.DRB: And that which hath touched the uncleanness of man, or of beast, or of any thing that can pollute, & eateth of such kind of flesh, shall perish from his people.

text.NRSV: The Lord spoke to Moses, saying:
text.Rotherham: And Yahweh spake unto Moses, saying—
text.KJV: And the Lord spake unto Moses, saying,
text.NABRE: Prohibition Against Blood and Fat. The Lord said to Moses:
text.DRB: And our Lord spake to Moyses, saying:

text.NRSV: Speak to the people of Israel, saying: You shall eat no fat of ox or sheep or goat.
text.Rotherham: Speak unto the sons of Israel, saying,—No fat, of ox or sheep or goat, shall ye eat:
text.KJV: Speak unto the children of Israel, saying, Ye shall eat no manner of fat, of ox, or of sheep, or of goat.
text.NABRE: Tell the Israelites: You shall not eat the fat of any ox or sheep or goat.
text.DRB: Speak to the children of Israel: The fat of a sheep, and of an oxe, and of a goat you shall not eat.

text.NRSV: The fat of an animal that died or was mangled by wild animals may be put to any use, but you must not eat it.
text.Rotherham: and though the fat of what dieth of itself and the fat of that which has been torn in pieces, may be used for any other service, yet shall ye, in nowise eat it.
text.KJV: And the fat of the beast that dieth of itself, and the fat of that which is torn with beasts, may be used in any other use: but ye shall in no wise eat of it.
text.NABRE: Although the fat of an animal that has died a natural death or has been killed by wild beasts may be put to any other use, you may not eat it.
text.DRB: The fat of the carcass of carrion, & of the beast that was caught of another beast, you shall have for diverse uses.

text.NRSV: If any one of you eats the fat from an animal of which an offering by fire may be made to the Lord, you who eat it shall be cut off from your kin.
text.Rotherham: Nay, as touching any one who eateth the fat of any beast, wherefrom he might bring near an altar-flame unto Yahweh, then shall the person that eateth be cut off from among his kinsfolk.
text.KJV: For whosoever eateth the fat of the beast, of which men offer an offering made by fire unto the Lord, even the soul that eateth it shall be cut off from his people.
text.NABRE: If anyone eats the fat of an animal from which an oblation is made to the Lord, that person shall be cut off from the people.
text.DRB: If any man eat the fat that should be offered for the burnt sacrifice of our Lord, he shall perish out of his people.

text.NRSV: You must not eat any blood whatever, either of bird or of animal, in any of your settlements.
text.Rotherham: And no manner of blood, shall ye eat, in any of your dwellings,—whether of bird or of beast:
text.KJV: Moreover ye shall eat no manner of blood, whether it be of fowl or of beast, in any of your dwellings.
text.NABRE: Wherever you dwell, you shall not eat any blood, whether of bird or of animal.
text.DRB: The blood also of whatsoever beast you shall not take in meat, as well of birds as of cattle.

text.NRSV: Any one of you who eats any blood shall be cut off from your kin.
text.Rotherham: as touching any person who eateth any manner of blood, then shall that person be cut off from among his kinsfolk.
text.KJV: Whatsoever soul it be that eateth any manner of blood, even that soul shall be cut off from his people.
text.NABRE: Every person who eats any blood shall be cut off from the people.
text.DRB: Every soul that eateth blood, shall perish out of his people.

text.NRSV: The Lord spoke to Moses, saying:
text.Rotherham: And Yahweh spake unto Moses, saying,
text.KJV: And the Lord spake unto Moses, saying,
text.NABRE: Portions from the Communion Sacrifice for Priests. The Lord said to Moses:
text.DRB: And our Lord spake to Moyses, saying:

text.NRSV: Speak to the people of Israel, saying: Any who offer a sacrifice of well-being to the Lord shall bring the offering to the Lord from the sacrifice of well-being.
text.Rotherham: Speak unto the sons of Israel saying,—He that offereth his peace-offering unto Yahweh, shall bring in his own oblation unto Yahweh out of his peace-offering:
text.KJV: Speak unto the children of Israel, saying, He that offereth the sacrifice of his peace offerings unto the Lord shall bring his oblation unto the Lord of the sacrifice of his peace offerings.
text.NABRE: Tell the Israelites: The person who offers a communion sacrifice to the Lord shall be the one to bring from it the offering to the Lord.
text.DRB: Speak to the children of Israel, saying: He that offereth a victim of pacifics to our Lord, let him offer therewith a sacrifice also, that is, the libaments thereof.

text.NRSV: The worshiper’s own hands shall bring the Lord’s offering by fire. You shall bring the fat with the breast, so that the breast may be raised as an elevation offering before the Lord.
text.Rotherham: his own hands shall bring in the altar-flames of Yahweh,—the fat with the breast, he shall bring it in, the breast, to wave it as a wave-offering before Yahweh.
text.KJV: His own hands shall bring the offerings of the Lord made by fire, the fat with the breast, it shall he bring, that the breast may be waved for a wave offering before the Lord.
text.NABRE: The offerer’s own hands shall carry the oblations for the Lord: the person shall bring the fat together with the brisket, which is to be raised as an elevated offering before the Lord.
text.DRB: He shall hold in his hands the fat of the host, and the breast: & when he hath offered and consecrated both to our Lord, he shall deliver them to the Priest,

text.NRSV: The priest shall turn the fat into smoke on the altar, but the breast shall belong to Aaron and his sons.
text.Rotherham: Then shall the priest make a perfume with the fat at the altar,—and the breast shall be for Aaron and for his sons.
text.KJV: And the priest shall burn the fat upon the altar: but the breast shall be Aaron’s and his sons’.
text.NABRE: The priest shall burn the fat on the altar, but the brisket belongs to Aaron and his sons.
text.DRB: who shall burn the fat upon the altar, but the breast shall be Aaron's, and his sons'.

text.NRSV: And the right thigh from your sacrifices of well-being you shall give to the priest as an offering;
text.Rotherham: But, the right shoulder, shall ye give as a heave-offering, unto the priest,—from among your peace-offerings
text.KJV: And the right shoulder shall ye give unto the priest for an heave offering of the sacrifices of your peace offerings.
text.NABRE: Moreover, from your communion sacrifices you shall give to the priest the right leg as a contribution.
text.DRB: The right shoulder also of the pacific hosts shall fall for first fruits of the Priest.

text.NRSV: the one among the sons of Aaron who offers the blood and fat of the offering of well-being shall have the right thigh for a portion.
text.Rotherham: he that bringeth near the blood of the peace-offerings and the fat from among the sons of Aaron, to him, shall belong the right shoulder for a portion.
text.KJV: He among the sons of Aaron, that offereth the blood of the peace offerings, and the fat, shall have the right shoulder for his part.
text.NABRE: The one among Aaron’s sons who offers the blood and the fat of the communion offering shall have the right leg as his portion,
text.DRB: He of the sons of Aaron, that offereth the blood, and the fat, himself shall have the right shoulder also for his portion.

text.NRSV: For I have taken the breast of the elevation offering, and the thigh that is offered, from the people of Israel, from their sacrifices of well-being, and have given them to Aaron the priest and to his sons, as a perpetual due from the people of Israel.
text.Rotherham: For, the wave-breast and the heave-shoulder, have I taken of the sons of Israel, out of their peace-offerings,—and have given them unto Aaron the priest and unto his sons as an age-abiding statute, from the sons of Israel.
text.KJV: For the wave breast and the heave shoulder have I taken of the children of Israel from off the sacrifices of their peace offerings, and have given them unto Aaron the priest and unto his sons by a statute for ever from among the children of Israel.
text.NABRE: for from the communion sacrifices of the Israelites I have taken the brisket that is elevated and the leg that is a contribution, and I have given them to Aaron, the priest, and to his sons as their due from the Israelites forever.
text.DRB: For the breast of elevation and the shoulder of separation I have taken of the children of Israel, from their pacific hosts, and have given them to Aaron the Priest, and to his sons, by a law for ever, of all the people of Israel.

text.NRSV: This is the portion allotted to Aaron and to his sons from the offerings made by fire to the Lord, once they have been brought forward to serve as priests to the Lord;
text.Rotherham: This, is what pertaineth to the anointing of Aaron and to the anointing of his sons out of the altar-flames of Yahweh,—[granted] in the day when he brought them near, to minister as priests unto Yahweh:
text.KJV: This is the portion of the anointing of Aaron, and of the anointing of his sons, out of the offerings of the Lord made by fire, in the day when he presented them to minister unto the Lord in the priest’s office;
text.NABRE: This is the priestly share from the oblations for the Lord, allotted to Aaron and his sons on the day they were brought forth to be the priests of the Lord,
text.DRB: This is the anointing of Aaron and his sons, in the ceremonies of our Lord, in the day when Moyses offered them, that they might do the function of priesthood,

text.NRSV: these the Lord commanded to be given them, when he anointed them, as a perpetual due from the people of Israel throughout their generations.
text.Rotherham: which Yahweh commanded to give them in the day when he anointed them, from among the sons of Israel,—an age-abiding statute to their generations.
text.KJV: Which the Lord commanded to be given them of the children of Israel, in the day that he anointed them, by a statute for ever throughout their generations.
text.NABRE: which the Lord ordered to be given them from the Israelites on the day they were anointed, as their due throughout their generations forever.
text.DRB: and the things that our Lord commanded to be given them of the children of Israel, by a perpetual religion in their generations.

text.NRSV: This is the ritual of the burnt offering, the grain offering, the sin offering, the guilt offering, the offering of ordination, and the sacrifice of well-being,
text.Rotherham: This is the law—for the ascending-sacrifice, for the meal-offering, and for the sin-bearer, and for the guilt-bearer,—and for the installation-offerings, and for the peace-offering:
text.KJV: This is the law of the burnt offering, of the meat offering, and of the sin offering, and of the trespass offering, and of the consecrations, and of the sacrifice of the peace offerings;
text.NABRE: Summary. This is the ritual for the burnt offering, the grain offering, the purification offering, the reparation offering, the ordination offering, and the communion sacrifice,
text.DRB: This is the law of holocaust, and of the sacrifice of sin, and for an offence, and for consecration, and the victims of pacifics:

text.NRSV: which the Lord commanded Moses on Mount Sinai, when he commanded the people of Israel to bring their offerings to the Lord, in the wilderness of Sinai.
text.Rotherham: which Yahweh commanded Moses in Mount Sinai,—in the day when he commanded the sons of Israel to bring near their oblations unto Yahweh in the desert of Sinai.
text.KJV: Which the Lord commanded Moses in mount Sinai, in the day that he commanded the children of Israel to offer their oblations unto the Lord, in the wilderness of Sinai.
text.NABRE: which the Lord enjoined on Moses at Mount Sinai at the time when he commanded the Israelites in the wilderness of Sinai to bring their offerings to the Lord.
text.DRB: Which our Lord appointed to Moyses in mount Sinai when he commanded the children of Israel, that they should offer their oblations to our Lord in the desert of Sinai.

text.NRSV: The Lord spoke to Moses, saying:
text.Rotherham: Then spake Yahweh unto Moses, saying:
text.KJV: And the Lord spake unto Moses, saying,
text.NABRE: The Lord said to Moses:
text.DRB: And Our Lord spake to Moyses, saying:

text.NRSV: Take Aaron and his sons with him, and the vestments, and the anointing oil, and the bull of sin offering, and the two rams, and the basket of unleavened bread;
text.Rotherham: Take thou Aaron and his sons with him, and the garments, and the anointing oil,—and the sin-bearing bullock, and the two rams, and the basket of unleavened cakes;
text.KJV: Take Aaron and his sons with him, and the garments, and the anointing oil, and a bullock for the sin offering, and two rams, and a basket of unleavened bread;
text.NABRE: Take Aaron along with his sons, the vestments, the anointing oil, the bull for a purification offering, the two rams, and the basket of unleavened bread,
text.DRB: Take Aaron with his sons, their vestments, and the oil of unction, a calf for sin, two Rams, a basket with azymes,

text.NRSV: and assemble the whole congregation at the entrance of the tent of meeting.
text.Rotherham: and all the assembly, call thou together,—unto the entrance of the tent of meeting.
text.KJV: And gather thou all the congregation together unto the door of the tabernacle of the congregation.
text.NABRE: then assemble the whole community at the entrance of the tent of meeting.
text.DRB: and thou shalt gather all the assembly to the door of the tabernacle.

text.NRSV: And Moses did as the Lord commanded him. When the congregation was assembled at the entrance of the tent of meeting,
text.Rotherham: And Moses did, as Yahweh commanded him,—and the assembly was called together, unto the entrance of the tent of meeting.
text.KJV: And Moses did as the Lord commanded him; and the assembly was gathered together unto the door of the tabernacle of the congregation.
text.NABRE: Moses did as the Lord had commanded. When the community had assembled at the entrance of the tent of meeting,
text.DRB: And Moyses did as our Lord had commanded. And all the multitude being gathered before the door of the tabernacle,

text.NRSV: Moses said to the congregation, “This is what the Lord has commanded to be done.”
text.Rotherham: Then said Moses unto the assembly,—This is the thing, which Yahweh hath commanded to be done.
text.KJV: And Moses said unto the congregation, This is the thing which the Lord commanded to be done.
text.NABRE: Moses told them: “This is what the Lord has ordered to be done.”
text.DRB: he said: This is the word, that our Lord hath commanded to be done.

text.NRSV: Then Moses brought Aaron and his sons forward, and washed them with water.
text.Rotherham: So Moses brought near Aaron and his sons,—and bathed them with water,
text.KJV: And Moses brought Aaron and his sons, and washed them with water.
text.NABRE: Bringing forward Aaron and his sons, Moses first washed them with water.
text.DRB: And immediately he offered Aaron & his sons: and when he had washed them,

text.NRSV: He put the tunic on him, fastened the sash around him, clothed him with the robe, and put the ephod on him; he then put the decorated band of the ephod around him, tying the ephod to him with it.
text.Rotherham: and put upon him the tunic and girded him with the band, and clothed him with the robe, and put upon him the ephod, and girded him with the curiously-woven band of the ephod, and bound it to him therewith;
text.KJV: And he put upon him the coat, and girded him with the girdle, and clothed him with the robe, and put the ephod upon him, and he girded him with the curious girdle of the ephod, and bound it unto him therewith.
text.NABRE: Then he put the tunic on Aaron, girded him with the sash, clothed him with the robe, placed the ephod on him, and girded him with the ephod’s embroidered belt, fastening the ephod on him with it.
text.DRB: he revested the high-Priest with the strait linen garment, girding him with a bawdrike, & revesting him with the tunic of hyacinth, and over it he put the Ephod,

text.NRSV: He placed the breastpiece on him, and in the breastpiece he put the Urim and the Thummim.
text.Rotherham: and he put upon him the breastpiece,—and placed, in the breastpiece, the Lights and the Perfections;
text.KJV: And he put the breastplate upon him: also he put in the breastplate the Urim and the Thummim.
text.NABRE: He then set the breastpiece on him, putting the Urim and Thummim in it.
text.DRB: which he straitening with the girdle, fitted it to the Rationale, wherein was Doctrine and Verity.

text.NRSV: And he set the turban on his head, and on the turban, in front, he set the golden ornament, the holy diadem, as the Lord commanded Moses.
text.Rotherham: and he put the turban upon his head,—and put upon the turban, on the forefront thereof the burnished plate of gold—the holy crown, As Yahweh commanded Moses.
text.KJV: And he put the mitre upon his head; also upon the mitre, even upon his forefront, did he put the golden plate, the holy crown; as the Lord commanded Moses.
text.NABRE: He put the turban on his head, attaching the gold medallion, the sacred headband, on the front of the turban, as the Lord had commanded Moses to do.
text.DRB: with the mitre also he covered his head: and upon it, against the forehead, he put the plate of gold consecrated in sanctification, as our Lord had commanded him.

text.NRSV: Then Moses took the anointing oil and anointed the tabernacle and all that was in it, and consecrated them.
text.Rotherham: And Moses took the anointing oil, and anointed the habitation and all that was therein,—and hallowed them;
text.KJV: And Moses took the anointing oil, and anointed the tabernacle and all that was therein, and sanctified them.
text.NABRE: Taking the anointing oil, Moses anointed and consecrated the tabernacle and all that was in it.
text.DRB: He took also the oil of unction, wherewith he anointed the tabernacle, with all the furniture thereof.

text.NRSV: He sprinkled some of it on the altar seven times, and anointed the altar and all its utensils, and the basin and its base, to consecrate them.
text.Rotherham: and he sprinkled thereof upon the altar seven times,—and anointed the altar, and all the utensils thereof and the laver and its stand to hallow them
text.KJV: And he sprinkled thereof upon the altar seven times, and anointed the altar and all his vessels, both the laver and his foot, to sanctify them.
text.NABRE: Then he sprinkled some of the oil seven times on the altar, and anointed the altar, with all its utensils, and the laver, with its base, to consecrate them.
text.DRB: And sanctifying them, and having sprinkled the altar seven times, he anointed it, and all the vessels thereof, and the laver with the foot thereof he sanctified with the oil.

text.NRSV: He poured some of the anointing oil on Aaron’s head and anointed him, to consecrate him.
text.Rotherham: and he poured of the anointing oil, upon the head of Aaron,—and anointed him, to hallow him.
text.KJV: And he poured of the anointing oil upon Aaron’s head, and anointed him, to sanctify him.
text.NABRE: He also poured some of the anointing oil on Aaron’s head and anointed him, to consecrate him.
text.DRB: The which pouring upon Aaron's head, he anointed, & consecrated him:

text.NRSV: And Moses brought forward Aaron’s sons, and clothed them with tunics, and fastened sashes around them, and tied headdresses on them, as the Lord commanded Moses.
text.Rotherham: And Moses brought near the sons of Aaron and clothed them with tunics and girded them with bands, and wrapped round for them turbans,—As Yahweh commanded Moses.
text.KJV: And Moses brought Aaron’s sons, and put coats upon them, and girded them with girdles, and put bonnets upon them; as the Lord commanded Moses.
text.NABRE: Moses likewise brought forward Aaron’s sons, clothed them with tunics, girded them with sashes, and put skullcaps on them, as the Lord had commanded him to do.
text.DRB: his sons also after he had offered them, he revested with linen tunics and girded them with bawdrikes, and put mitres on them, as our Lord had commanded.

text.NRSV: He led forward the bull of sin offering; and Aaron and his sons laid their hands upon the head of the bull of sin offering,
text.Rotherham: Then was led near the sin-bearing bullock,—and Aaron and his sons leaned their hands upon the head of the sin-bearing bullock,
text.KJV: And he brought the bullock for the sin offering: and Aaron and his sons laid their hands upon the head of the bullock for the sin offering.
text.NABRE: Ordination Sacrifices. He brought forward the bull for a purification offering, and Aaron and his sons laid their hands on its head.
text.DRB: He offered also the calf for sin: and when Aaron and his sons had put their hands upon the head thereof,

text.NRSV: and it was slaughtered. Moses took the blood and with his finger put some on each of the horns of the altar, purifying the altar; then he poured out the blood at the base of the altar. Thus he consecrated it, to make atonement for it.
text.Rotherham: Then was it slain, and Moses took the blood and put [thereof] upon the horns of the altar round about, with his finger, and cleansed the altar from sin,—and the [remainder of the] blood, poured he forth at the base of the altar, and hallowed it putting a propitiatory-covering thereupon.
text.KJV: And he slew it; and Moses took the blood, and put it upon the horns of the altar round about with his finger, and purified the altar, and poured the blood at the bottom of the altar, and sanctified it, to make reconciliation upon it.
text.NABRE: When it was slaughtered, Moses took the blood and with his finger he put it on the horns around the altar, thus purifying the altar. He poured out the rest of the blood at the base of the altar. Thus he consecrated it so that atonement could be made on it.
text.DRB: he did immolate it: drawing the blood, & dipping his finger, touched the horns of the altar round about. Which being expiated, and sanctified, he poured the rest of the blood at the bottom thereof.

text.NRSV: Moses took all the fat that was around the entrails, and the appendage of the liver, and the two kidneys with their fat, and turned them into smoke on the altar.
text.Rotherham: And he took all the fat that was on the inwards, and the caul of the liver, and the two kidneys and their fat,—and Moses made a perfume at the altar.
text.KJV: And he took all the fat that was upon the inwards, and the caul above the liver, and the two kidneys, and their fat, and Moses burned it upon the altar.
text.NABRE: Taking all the fat that was over the inner organs, as well as the lobe of the liver and the two kidneys with their fat, Moses burned them on the altar.
text.DRB: But the fat that was upon the entrails, and the caul of the liver, & the two little kidneys, with their little tallow he burnt upon the altar:

text.NRSV: But the bull itself, and its skin and flesh and its dung, he burned with fire outside the camp, as the Lord commanded Moses.
text.Rotherham: But the bullock itself, and its skin and its flesh and its dung, consumed he with fire, outside the camp,—As Yahweh commanded Moses,
text.KJV: But the bullock, and his hide, his flesh, and his dung, he burnt with fire without the camp; as the Lord commanded Moses.
text.NABRE: The bull, however, with its hide and flesh and dung he burned in the fire outside the camp, as the Lord had commanded Moses to do.
text.DRB: the calf with the skin, and the flesh, and the dung, he burnt without the camp, as our Lord had commanded.

text.NRSV: Then he presented the ram of burnt offering. Aaron and his sons laid their hands on the head of the ram,
text.Rotherham: Then was brought near the ram for the ascending-sacrifice,—and Aaron and his sons leaned their hands upon the head of the ram;
text.KJV: And he brought the ram for the burnt offering: and Aaron and his sons laid their hands upon the head of the ram.
text.NABRE: He next brought forward the ram of the burnt offering, and Aaron and his sons laid their hands on its head.
text.DRB: He offered also a ram for an holocaust: upon the head whereof when Aaron and his sons had put their hands,

text.NRSV: and it was slaughtered. Moses dashed the blood against all sides of the altar.
text.Rotherham: and it was slain,—and Moses dashed the blood against the altar round about;
text.KJV: And he killed it; and Moses sprinkled the blood upon the altar round about.
text.NABRE: When it was slaughtered, Moses splashed the blood on all sides of the altar.
text.DRB: he did immolate it, and poured the blood thereof in the circuit of the altar.

text.NRSV: The ram was cut into its parts, and Moses turned the head, the parts, and the suet into smoke on the altar;
text.Rotherham: and the ram itself, was cut up into its pieces,—and Moses made a perfume with the head and with the pieces and with the fat;
text.KJV: And he cut the ram into pieces; and Moses burnt the head, and the pieces, and the fat.
text.NABRE: After the ram was cut up into pieces, Moses burned the head, the cut-up pieces and the suet.
text.DRB: And cutting the ram itself into pieces, the head thereof, and the joints, and the fat he burnt with fire,

text.NRSV: but the entrails and the legs he washed with water. Moses turned the whole ram into smoke on the altar; it was a burnt offering for a pleasing odor, an offering by fire to the Lord, as the Lord commanded Moses.
text.Rotherham: but the inwards and the legs, were bathed in water,—and Moses made a perfume with the whole ram at the altar, an ascending-sacrifice, it was—for a satisfying odour an altar-flame, it was unto Yahweh, As Yahweh commanded Moses.
text.KJV: And he washed the inwards and the legs in water; and Moses burnt the whole ram upon the altar: it was a burnt sacrifice for a sweet savour, and an offering made by fire unto the Lord; as the Lord commanded Moses.
text.NABRE: After the inner organs and the shanks were washed with water, Moses burned these remaining parts of the ram on the altar. It was a burnt offering for a sweet aroma, an oblation to the Lord, as the Lord had commanded Moses.
text.DRB: having first washed the entrails, & the feet, & the whole ram together he burnt upon the altar, because it was an holocaust of most sweet odour to our Lord, as he had commanded him.

text.NRSV: Then he presented the second ram, the ram of ordination. Aaron and his sons laid their hands on the head of the ram,
text.Rotherham: Then was brought near the second ram, the ram of installation, and Aaron and his sons leaned their hands upon the head of the ram;
text.KJV: And he brought the other ram, the ram of consecration: and Aaron and his sons laid their hands upon the head of the ram.
text.NABRE: Then he brought forward the second ram, the ordination ram, and Aaron and his sons laid their hands on its head.
text.DRB: He offered also the second ram, for the consecration of Priests: and Aaron and his sons did put their hands upon the head thereof:

text.NRSV: and it was slaughtered. Moses took some of its blood and put it on the lobe of Aaron’s right ear and on the thumb of his right hand and on the big toe of his right foot.
text.Rotherham: and it was slain, and Moses took of the blood thereof, and put upon the tip of Aaron’s right ear,—and upon the thumb of his right hand, and upon the great toe of his right foot.
text.KJV: And he slew it; and Moses took of the blood of it, and put it upon the tip of Aaron’s right ear, and upon the thumb of his right hand, and upon the great toe of his right foot.
text.NABRE: When it was slaughtered, Moses took some of its blood and put it on the lobe of Aaron’s right ear, on the thumb of his right hand, and on the big toe of his right foot.
text.DRB: which when Moyses had immolated, taking of the blood thereof, he touched the tip of Aaron's right ear, and the thumb of his right hand, in like manner also of his foot.

text.NRSV: After Aaron’s sons were brought forward, Moses put some of the blood on the lobes of their right ears and on the thumbs of their right hands and on the big toes of their right feet; and Moses dashed the rest of the blood against all sides of the altar.
text.Rotherham: Then were brought near the sons of Aaron, and Moses put of the blood upon the tip of their right ear, and upon the thumb of their right hand, and upon the great toe of their right foot,—and Moses dashed the blood against the altar round about.
text.KJV: And he brought Aaron’s sons, and Moses put of the blood upon the tip of their right ear, and upon the thumbs of their right hands, and upon the great toes of their right feet: and Moses sprinkled the blood upon the altar round about.
text.NABRE: Moses had the sons of Aaron also come forward, and he put some of the blood on the lobes of their right ears, on the thumbs of their right hands, and on the big toes of their right feet. The rest of the blood he splashed on all the sides of the altar.
text.DRB: He offered also the sons of Aaron: and when of the blood of the ram, being immolated, he had touched the tip of the right ear of every one, and the thumbs of the right hand and foot, the rest he poured on the altar, round about:

text.NRSV: He took the fat—the broad tail, all the fat that was around the entrails, the appendage of the liver, and the two kidneys with their fat—and the right thigh.
text.Rotherham: And he took the fat and the fat-tail, and all the fat that was on the inwards, and the caul of the liver, and the two kidneys and their fat,—and the right shoulder;
text.KJV: And he took the fat, and the rump, and all the fat that was upon the inwards, and the caul above the liver, and the two kidneys, and their fat, and the right shoulder:
text.NABRE: He then took the fat: the fatty tail and all the fat over the inner organs, the lobe of the liver and the two kidneys with their fat, and likewise the right thigh;
text.DRB: but the fat, and the rump, and all the fat that covereth the entrails, and the caul of the liver, and the two kidneys with their fat, and with the right shoulder, he separated.

text.NRSV: From the basket of unleavened bread that was before the Lord, he took one cake of unleavened bread, one cake of bread with oil, and one wafer, and placed them on the fat and on the right thigh.
text.Rotherham: and out of the basket of unleavened-cakes that was before Yahweh, took he one round unleavened-cake and one round oil-cake, and one wafer, and put them upon the fat portions, and upon the right shoulder;
text.KJV: And out of the basket of unleavened bread, that was before the Lord, he took one unleavened cake, and a cake of oiled bread, and one wafer, and put them on the fat, and upon the right shoulder:
text.NABRE: from the basket of unleavened bread that was set before the Lord he took one unleavened cake, one loaf of bread made with oil, and one wafer; these he placed on top of the portions of fat and the right thigh.
text.DRB: And taking out of the basket of azymes, which was before our Lord, a loaf without leaven, and a manchet tempered with oil, and a wafer, he put them upon the fat, and the right shoulder,

text.NRSV: He placed all these on the palms of Aaron and on the palms of his sons, and raised them as an elevation offering before the Lord.
text.Rotherham: and placed the whole upon the opened palms of Aaron, and upon the opened palms of his sons,—and waved them as a wave-offering, before Yahweh.
text.KJV: And he put all upon Aaron’s hands, and upon his sons’ hands, and waved them for a wave offering before the Lord.
text.NABRE: He then put all these things upon the palms of Aaron and his sons, whom he had raise them as an elevated offering before the Lord.
text.DRB: delivering all to Aaron, and to his sons, Who having lifted them up before our Lord,

text.NRSV: Then Moses took them from their hands and turned them into smoke on the altar with the burnt offering. This was an ordination offering for a pleasing odor, an offering by fire to the Lord.
