text.Rotherham: And Moses took them from off their opened palms, and made a perfume at the altar upon the ascending-sacrifice,—an installation, they were for a satisfying odour, an altar-flame, it was unto Yahweh.
text.KJV: And Moses took them from off their hands, and burnt them on the altar upon the burnt offering: they were consecrations for a sweet savour: it is an offering made by fire unto the Lord.
text.NABRE: When Moses had removed them from their palms, he burned them on the altar with the burnt offering. They were an ordination offering for a sweet aroma, an oblation to the Lord.
text.DRB: he took them again of their hands, and burnt them upon the altar of holocaust, because it was the oblation of consecration, for a sweet odour, of the sacrifice to our Lord.

text.NRSV: Moses took the breast and elevated it as an elevation offering before the Lord; it was Moses’ portion of the ram of ordination, as the Lord commanded Moses.
text.Rotherham: And Moses took the breast, and waved it as a wave-offering, before Yahweh,—of the ram of installation, unto Moses it belonged, as a portion, As Yahweh commanded Moses.
text.KJV: And Moses took the breast, and waved it for a wave offering before the Lord: for of the ram of consecration it was Moses’ part; as the Lord commanded Moses.
text.NABRE: He then took the brisket and raised it as an elevated offering before the Lord; this was Moses’ own portion of the ordination ram, as the Lord had commanded Moses.
text.DRB: And he took of the ram of consecration, the breast for his portion, elevating it before our Lord, as our Lord had commanded him.

text.NRSV: Then Moses took some of the anointing oil and some of the blood that was on the altar and sprinkled them on Aaron and his vestments, and also on his sons and their vestments. Thus he consecrated Aaron and his vestments, and also his sons and their vestments.
text.Rotherham: And Moses took of the anointing oil and of the blood that was upon the altar, and sprinkled upon Aaron, upon his garments, and upon his sons and upon the garments of his sons with him,—and hallowed Aaron, his garments, and his sons and the garments of his sons with him.
text.KJV: And Moses took of the anointing oil, and of the blood which was upon the altar, and sprinkled it upon Aaron, and upon his garments, and upon his sons, and upon his sons’ garments with him; and sanctified Aaron, and his garments, and his sons, and his sons’ garments with him.
text.NABRE: Taking some of the anointing oil and some of the blood that was on the altar, Moses sprinkled it upon Aaron and his vestments, as well as his sons and their vestments, thus consecrating both Aaron and his vestments and his sons and their vestments.
text.DRB: And taking the ointment, and the blood that was upon the altar, he sprinkled it upon Aaron, and his vestments, & upon his sons and their vestments.

text.NRSV: And Moses said to Aaron and his sons, “Boil the flesh at the entrance of the tent of meeting, and eat it there with the bread that is in the basket of ordination offerings, as I was commanded, ‘Aaron and his sons shall eat it’;
text.Rotherham: And Moses said unto Aaron and unto his sons Boil ye the flesh, at the entrance of the tent of meeting, and there, shall ye eat it, and the bread which is in the basket of installation,—as I have been commanded, saying, Aaron and his sons, shall eat it.
text.KJV: And Moses said unto Aaron and to his sons, Boil the flesh at the door of the tabernacle of the congregation: and there eat it with the bread that is in the basket of consecrations, as I commanded, saying, Aaron and his sons shall eat it.
text.NABRE: Moses said to Aaron and his sons, “Boil the meat at the entrance of the tent of meeting, and there eat it with the bread that is in the basket of the ordination offering, in keeping with the command I have received: ‘Aaron and his sons shall eat of it.’
text.DRB: And when he had sanctified them in their vestments, he commanded them, saying: Boil the flesh before the door of the tabernacle, and there eat it. Eat ye also the loaves of consecration, that are laid in the basket, as our Lord commanded me, saying: Aaron and his sons shall eat them:

text.NRSV: and what remains of the flesh and the bread you shall burn with fire.
text.Rotherham: But, that which is left of the flesh, and of the bread, with fire, shall ye consume.
text.KJV: And that which remaineth of the flesh and of the bread shall ye burn with fire.
text.NABRE: What is left over of the meat and the bread you shall burn in the fire.
text.DRB: and whatsoever shall be left of the flesh, and the loaves, fire shall consume.

text.NRSV: You shall not go outside the entrance of the tent of meeting for seven days, until the day when your period of ordination is completed. For it will take seven days to ordain you;
text.Rotherham: And from the entrance of the tent of meeting, shall ye not go forth for seven days, until the day that filleth up the days of your installation,—because for seven days, will he install you:
text.KJV: And ye shall not go out of the door of the tabernacle of the congregation in seven days, until the days of your consecration be at an end: for seven days shall he consecrate you.
text.NABRE: Moreover, you are not to depart from the entrance of the tent of meeting for seven days, until the days of your ordination are completed; for your ordination is to last for seven days.
text.DRB: Out of the door also of the tabernacle you shall not go forth seven days, until the day wherein the time of your consecration shall be expired, for in seven days the consecration is finished:

text.NRSV: as has been done today, the Lord has commanded to be done to make atonement for you.
text.Rotherham: as hath been done this day, [so] hath Yahweh commanded to be done, to put a propitiatory-covering over you.
text.KJV: As he hath done this day, so the Lord hath commanded to do, to make an atonement for you.
text.NABRE: What has been done today the Lord has commanded be done, to make atonement for you.
text.DRB: as at this present it hath been done, that the rite of the sacrifice might be accomplished.

text.NRSV: You shall remain at the entrance of the tent of meeting day and night for seven days, keeping the Lord’s charge so that you do not die; for so I am commanded.”
text.Rotherham: Even at the entrance of the tent of meeting, shall ye abide day and night for seven days, and shall keep the watch of Yahweh and shall not die,—for, so, am I commanded.
text.KJV: Therefore shall ye abide at the door of the tabernacle of the congregation day and night seven days, and keep the charge of the Lord, that ye die not: for so I am commanded.
text.NABRE: You must remain at the entrance of the tent of meeting day and night for seven days, carrying out the prescriptions of the Lord, so that you do not die, for this is the command I have received.”
text.DRB: Day & night shall you tarry in the tabernacle observing the watches of our Lord, lest you die: for so it hath been commanded me.

text.NRSV: Aaron and his sons did all the things that the Lord commanded through Moses.
text.Rotherham: And Aaron and his sons did all the things which Yahweh had commanded through the mediation of Moses.
text.KJV: So Aaron and his sons did all things which the Lord commanded by the hand of Moses.
text.NABRE: So Aaron and his sons did all that the Lord had commanded through Moses.
text.DRB: And Aaron, and his sons did all things which our Lord spake by the hand of Moyses.

text.NRSV: On the eighth day Moses summoned Aaron and his sons and the elders of Israel.
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass on the eighth day, that Moses called for Aaron, and for his sons,—and for the elders of Israel;
text.KJV: And it came to pass on the eighth day, that Moses called Aaron and his sons, and the elders of Israel;
text.NABRE: On the eighth day Moses summoned Aaron and his sons, together with the elders of Israel,
text.DRB: And when the eighth day was come, Moyses called Aaron and his sons, and the ancients of Israel, and said to Aaron.

text.NRSV: He said to Aaron, “Take a bull calf for a sin offering and a ram for a burnt offering, without blemish, and offer them before the Lord.
text.Rotherham: and said unto Aaron—Take for thyself a choice calf, as a sin-bearer, and a ram for an ascending-sacrifice, each without defect,—and bring them near before Yahweh;
text.KJV: And he said unto Aaron, Take thee a young calf for a sin offering, and a ram for a burnt offering, without blemish, and offer them before the Lord.
text.NABRE: and said to Aaron, “Take a calf of the herd for a purification offering and a ram for a burnt offering, both without blemish, and offer them before the Lord.
text.DRB: Take of the herd a calf for sin, and a ram for an holocaust, both without spot, and offer them before our Lord.

text.NRSV: And say to the people of Israel, ‘Take a male goat for a sin offering; a calf and a lamb, yearlings without blemish, for a burnt offering;
text.Rotherham: and unto the sons of Israel, shalt thou speak, saying,—Take ye a he-goat, as a sin-bearer, and a calf and a young sheep, each a year old, without defect for an ascending-sacrifice;
text.KJV: And unto the children of Israel thou shalt speak, saying, Take ye a kid of the goats for a sin offering; and a calf and a lamb, both of the first year, without blemish, for a burnt offering;
text.NABRE: Tell the Israelites, too: Take a he-goat for a purification offering, a calf and a lamb, both unblemished yearlings, for a burnt offering,
text.DRB: And to the children of Israel thou shalt speak: Take ye a buck goat for sin, and a calf, and a lamb, of a year old, & without spot for an holocaust,

text.NRSV: and an ox and a ram for an offering of well-being to sacrifice before the Lord; and a grain offering mixed with oil. For today the Lord will appear to you.’”
text.Rotherham: and an ox and a ram for peace-offerings, to sacrifice before Yahweh, and a meal-offering overflowed with oil,—for, to-day, doth, Yahweh, appear unto you.
text.KJV: Also a bullock and a ram for peace offerings, to sacrifice before the Lord; and a meat offering mingled with oil: for to day the Lord will appear unto you.
text.NABRE: and an ox and a ram for a communion sacrifice, to sacrifice before the Lord, along with a grain offering mixed with oil; for today the Lord will appear to you.”
text.DRB: an ox and a ram for pacifics: and immolate them before our Lord, offering in the sacrifice of every one flour tempered with oil; for today our Lord will appear to you.

text.NRSV: They brought what Moses commanded to the front of the tent of meeting; and the whole congregation drew near and stood before the Lord.
text.Rotherham: So they brought that which Moses commanded before the tent of meeting,—and all the assembly drew near, and stood before Yahweh.
text.KJV: And they brought that which Moses commanded before the tabernacle of the congregation: and all the congregation drew near and stood before the Lord.
text.NABRE: So they brought what Moses had ordered before the tent of meeting. When the whole community had come forward and stood before the Lord,
text.DRB: They took therefore all things that Moyses had commanded before the door of the tabernacle: where when all the multitude stood,

text.NRSV: And Moses said, “This is the thing that the Lord commanded you to do, so that the glory of the Lord may appear to you.”
text.Rotherham: And Moses said—This, is the thing which Yahweh hath commanded that ye shall do,—that the glory of Yahweh may appear unto you.
text.KJV: And Moses said, This is the thing which the Lord commanded that ye should do: and the glory of the Lord shall appear unto you.
text.NABRE: Moses said, “This is what the Lord orders you to do, that the glory of the Lord may appear to you.
text.DRB: Moyses said: This is the word, which our Lord hath commanded: do it, and his glory will appear to you.

text.NRSV: Then Moses said to Aaron, “Draw near to the altar and sacrifice your sin offering and your burnt offering, and make atonement for yourself and for the people; and sacrifice the offering of the people, and make atonement for them; as the Lord has commanded.”
text.Rotherham: and Moses said unto Aaron—Draw near unto the altar, and offer thy sin-bearer and thine ascending-sacrifice, and put a propitiatory-covering about thyself and about the people,—and offer the oblation of the people and put a propitiatory-covering about them, As Yahweh hath commanded.
text.KJV: And Moses said unto Aaron, Go unto the altar, and offer thy sin offering, and thy burnt offering, and make an atonement for thyself, and for the people: and offer the offering of the people, and make an atonement for them; as the Lord commanded.
text.NABRE: Approach the altar,” Moses then told Aaron, “and make your purification offering and your burnt offering in atonement for yourself and for your household; then make the offering of the people in atonement for them, as the Lord has commanded.”
text.DRB: And he said to Aaron: Approach to the altar, & immolate for thy sin: offer the holocaust, and pray for thyself and for the people, and when thou hast slain the people's host, pray for them, as our Lord hath commanded.

text.NRSV: Aaron drew near to the altar, and slaughtered the calf of the sin offering, which was for himself.
text.Rotherham: So Aaron drew near unto the altar,—and slew the sin-bearing calf which was for himself;
text.KJV: Aaron therefore went unto the altar, and slew the calf of the sin offering, which was for himself.
text.NABRE: Approaching the altar, Aaron first slaughtered the calf of the purification offering that was his own offering.
text.DRB: And forthwith Aaron approaching to the altar, immolated the calf for his sin:

text.NRSV: The sons of Aaron presented the blood to him, and he dipped his finger in the blood and put it on the horns of the altar; and the rest of the blood he poured out at the base of the altar.
text.Rotherham: and the sons of Aaron brought the blood unto him, and he dipped his finger in the blood, and put [thereof] upon the horns of the altar,—and the [remainder of the] blood, poured he out at the base of the altar;
text.KJV: And the sons of Aaron brought the blood unto him: and he dipped his finger in the blood, and put it upon the horns of the altar, and poured out the blood at the bottom of the altar:
text.NABRE: When his sons presented the blood to him, he dipped his finger in the blood and put it on the horns of the altar. The rest of the blood he poured out at the base of the altar.
text.DRB: the blood whereof his sons brought to him: wherein dipping his finger, he touched the horns of the altar, and poured the rest at the foot thereof.

text.NRSV: But the fat, the kidneys, and the appendage of the liver from the sin offering he turned into smoke on the altar, as the Lord commanded Moses;
text.Rotherham: and with the fat and the kidneys and the caul a from the liver, of the sin-bearer, made he a perfume at the altar,—As Yahweh commanded Moses.
text.KJV: But the fat, and the kidneys, and the caul above the liver of the sin offering, he burnt upon the altar; as the Lord commanded Moses.
text.NABRE: He then burned on the altar the fat, the kidneys and the lobe of the liver from the purification offering, as the Lord had commanded Moses;
text.DRB: And the fat, and the little kidneys, and the caul of the liver, which are for sin, he burnt upon the altar, as our Lord had commanded Moyses:

text.NRSV: and the flesh and the skin he burned with fire outside the camp.
text.Rotherham: But, the flesh and the skin, consumed he with fire, outside the camp.
text.KJV: And the flesh and the hide he burnt with fire without the camp.
text.NABRE: but the flesh and the hide he burned in the fire outside the camp.
text.DRB: but the flesh and skin thereof he burnt with fire without the camp.

text.NRSV: Then he slaughtered the burnt offering. Aaron’s sons brought him the blood, and he dashed it against all sides of the altar.
text.Rotherham: Then slew he the ascending-sacrifice,—and the sons of Aaron presented unto him the blood, and he dashed it against the altar round about.
text.KJV: And he slew the burnt offering; and Aaron’s sons presented unto him the blood, which he sprinkled round about upon the altar.
text.NABRE: Then Aaron slaughtered the burnt offering. When his sons brought him the blood, he splashed it on all sides of the altar.
text.DRB: He immolated also the victim of holocaust: and his sons brought him the blood thereof, which he poured in the circuit of the altar.

text.NRSV: And they brought him the burnt offering piece by piece, and the head, which he turned into smoke on the altar.
text.Rotherham: And the ascending-sacrifice, presented they unto him piece by piece thereof also the head,—and he made a perfume upon the altar:
text.KJV: And they presented the burnt offering unto him, with the pieces thereof, and the head: and he burnt them upon the altar.
text.NABRE: They then brought him the pieces and the head of the burnt offering, and he burned them on the altar.
text.DRB: The host also it-self being cut into pieces, they brought with the head and every member. All which he burnt with fire upon the altar,

text.NRSV: He washed the entrails and the legs and, with the burnt offering, turned them into smoke on the altar.
text.Rotherham: and he bathed the inwards and the legs,—and made a perfume upon the ascending-sacrifice at the altar.
text.KJV: And he did wash the inwards and the legs, and burnt them upon the burnt offering on the altar.
text.NABRE: Having washed the inner organs and the shanks, he burned these also with the burnt offering on the altar.
text.DRB: having first washed the entrails and the feet with water.

text.NRSV: Then he presented the people’s offering. He took the goat of the sin offering that was for the people, and slaughtered it, and presented it as a sin offering like the first one.
text.Rotherham: Then brought he near the oblation of the people,—and took the sin-bearing goat which pertained to the people, and slew it and made therewith a sin-bearer, like the first.
text.KJV: And he brought the people’s offering, and took the goat, which was the sin offering for the people, and slew it, and offered it for sin, as the first.
text.NABRE: Then he had the people’s offering brought. Taking the goat that was for the people’s purification offering, he slaughtered it and offered it as a purification offering as before.
text.DRB: And offering for the sin of the people, he slew the buck goat: and expiating the altar:

text.NRSV: He presented the burnt offering, and sacrificed it according to the regulation.
text.Rotherham: Then brought he near the ascending-sacrifice,—and offered it, according to the regulation,
text.KJV: And he brought the burnt offering, and offered it according to the manner.
text.NABRE: Then he brought forward the burnt offering and offered it according to procedure.
text.DRB: he made the holocaust,

text.NRSV: Next he presented the grain offering, took a handful of it, and turned it into smoke on the altar, in addition to the burnt offering of the morning.
text.Rotherham: Then brought he near the meal-offering, and filled his hand therefrom, and made a perfume upon the altar,—in addition to the ascending-sacrifice of the morning.
text.KJV: And he brought the meat offering, and took an handful thereof, and burnt it upon the altar, beside the burnt sacrifice of the morning.
text.NABRE: He then presented the grain offering; taking a handful of it, he burned it on the altar, in addition to the morning burnt offering.
text.DRB: adding in the sacrifice the libaments, which are offered withal, and burning them upon the altar, beside the ceremonies of the morning holocaust.

text.NRSV: He slaughtered the ox and the ram as a sacrifice of well-being for the people. Aaron’s sons brought him the blood, which he dashed against all sides of the altar,
text.Rotherham: Then slew he the ox, and the ram, as a peace-offering, which pertained unto the people,—and the sons of Aaron presented the blood unto him, and he dashed it against the altar, round about;
text.KJV: He slew also the bullock and the ram for a sacrifice of peace offerings, which was for the people: and Aaron’s sons presented unto him the blood, which he sprinkled upon the altar round about,
text.NABRE: Finally he slaughtered the ox and the ram, the communion sacrifice of the people. When his sons brought him the blood, Aaron splashed it on all sides of the altar.
text.DRB: He immolated also the ox, and the ram, the pacific hosts of the people: and his sons brought him the blood, which he poured upon the altar round about.

text.NRSV: and the fat from the ox and from the ram—the broad tail, the fat that covers the entrails, the two kidneys and the fat on them, and the appendage of the liver.
text.Rotherham: and the portions of fat from the ox,—and from the ram, the fat-tail, and the covering, and the kidneys, and the caul of the liver;
text.KJV: And the fat of the bullock and of the ram, the rump, and that which covereth the inwards, and the kidneys, and the caul above the liver:
text.NABRE: The portions of fat from the ox and from the ram, the fatty tail, the covering fat, the kidneys, and the lobe of the liver
text.DRB: The fat also of the ox, and the rump of the ram, and the two little kidneys with their fat, and the caul of the liver,

text.NRSV: They first laid the fat on the breasts, and the fat was turned into smoke on the altar;
text.Rotherham: and they put the portions of fat upon the breasts,—and he made a perfume with the fat at the altar;
text.KJV: And they put the fat upon the breasts, and he burnt the fat upon the altar:
text.NABRE: they placed on top of the briskets. Aaron burned the fat pieces on the altar,
text.DRB: they put upon the breasts, & after the fat was burnt upon the altar,

text.NRSV: and Aaron raised the breasts and the right thigh as an elevation offering before the Lord, as Moses had commanded.
text.Rotherham: but the breasts and the right shoulder, did Aaron wave as a wave-offering, before Yahweh,—as Moses commanded,
text.KJV: And the breasts and the right shoulder Aaron waved for a wave offering before the Lord; as Moses commanded.
text.NABRE: but the briskets and the right thigh he raised as an elevated offering before the Lord, as the Lord had commanded Moses.
text.DRB: their breasts, & the right shoulders, Aaron did separate, elevating them before our Lord, as Moyses had commanded.

text.NRSV: Aaron lifted his hands toward the people and blessed them; and he came down after sacrificing the sin offering, the burnt offering, and the offering of well-being.
text.Rotherham: Then Aaron lifted up his hands towards the people, and blessed them,—and he came down from offering the sin-bearer, and the ascending-sacrifice and the peace-offering.
text.KJV: And Aaron lifted up his hand toward the people, and blessed them, and came down from offering of the sin offering, and the burnt offering, and peace offerings.
text.NABRE: Revelation of the Lord’s Glory. Aaron then raised his hands over the people and blessed them. When he came down from offering the purification offering, the burnt offering, and the communion offering,
text.DRB: And stretching forth his hand to the people, he blessed them. And so the hosts for sin, and the holocausts, and the pacifics being finished, he descended.

text.NRSV: Moses and Aaron entered the tent of meeting, and then came out and blessed the people; and the glory of the Lord appeared to all the people.
text.Rotherham: And when Moses and Aaron had entered into the tent of meeting,—and had come forth, and blessed the people, then appeared the glory of Yahweh, unto all the people;
text.KJV: And Moses and Aaron went into the tabernacle of the congregation, and came out, and blessed the people: and the glory of the Lord appeared unto all the people.
text.NABRE: Moses and Aaron went into the tent of meeting. On coming out they blessed the people. Then the glory of the Lord appeared to all the people.
text.DRB: And Moyses and Aaron going into the tabernacle of testimony, & afterward coming forth, blessed the people. And the glory of our Lord appeared to all the multitude:

text.NRSV: Fire came out from the Lord and consumed the burnt offering and the fat pieces on the altar; and when all the people saw it, they shouted and fell on their faces.
text.Rotherham: yea, there came forth fire from before Yahweh, and consumed upon the altar, the ascending-sacrifice and the portions of fat,—and all the people beheld and shouted, and fell down upon their faces.
text.KJV: And there came a fire out from before the Lord, and consumed upon the altar the burnt offering and the fat: which when all the people saw, they shouted, and fell on their faces.
text.NABRE: Fire came forth from the Lord ’s presence and consumed the burnt offering and the fat on the altar. Seeing this, all the people shouted with joy and fell prostrate.
text.DRB: & behold a fire coming forth from our Lord, devoured the holocaust, and the fat that was upon the altar: Which thing, when the multitude had seen, they praised our Lord, falling on their faces.

text.NRSV: Now Aaron’s sons Nadab and Abihu each took his censer, put fire in it, and laid incense on it; they offered unholy fire before the Lord, such as he had not commanded them.
text.Rotherham: Then Aaron’s sons, Nadab and Abihu, took each man his censer and placed therein fire, and put thereon incense,—and brought near before Yahweh strange fire, which he had not commanded them.
text.KJV: And Nadab and Abihu, the sons of Aaron, took either of them his censer, and put fire therein, and put incense thereon, and offered strange fire before the Lord, which he commanded them not.
text.NABRE: Aaron’s sons Nadab and Abihu took their censers and, putting incense on the fire they had set in them, they offered before the Lord unauthorized fire, such as he had not commanded.
text.DRB: And Nadab and Abiu the sons of Aaron catching censers, did put in fire, and incense thereupon, offering before our Lord strange fire, which was not commanded them.

text.NRSV: And fire came out from the presence of the Lord and consumed them, and they died before the Lord.
text.Rotherham: Then came there forth fire from before Yahweh, and consumed them,—and they died before Yahweh.
text.KJV: And there went out fire from the Lord, and devoured them, and they died before the Lord.
text.NABRE: Fire therefore came forth from the Lord ’s presence and consumed them, so that they died in the Lord ’s presence.
text.DRB: And fire coming forth from our Lord, devoured them, & they died before our Lord.

text.NRSV: Then Moses said to Aaron, “This is what the Lord meant when he said, ‘Through those who are near me I will show myself holy, and before all the people I will be glorified.’” And Aaron fell silent.
text.Rotherham: Then said Moses unto Aaron—The very thing, that Yahweh spake, saying—In them that draw near to me, must I be hallowed, And before the faces of all the people, must I get myself honour,—And Aaron, was dumb.
text.KJV: Then Moses said unto Aaron, This is it that the Lord spake, saying, I will be sanctified in them that come nigh me, and before all the people I will be glorified. And Aaron held his peace.
text.NABRE: Moses then said to Aaron, “This is as the Lord said: Through those near to me I will be sanctified; in the sight of all the people I will obtain glory.” But Aaron said nothing.
text.DRB: And Moyses said to Aaron: This is it which our Lord hath spoken: I will be sanctified in them, that approach to me, and in the sight of all the people I will be glorified. Which Aaron hearing held his peace.

text.NRSV: Moses summoned Mishael and Elzaphan, sons of Uzziel the uncle of Aaron, and said to them, “Come forward, and carry your kinsmen away from the front of the sanctuary to a place outside the camp.”
text.Rotherham: Then called Moses unto Mishael, and unto Elzaphan, sons of Uzziel, uncle of Aaron,—and said unto them—Draw near bear away your brethren from before the sanctuary, unto the outside of the camp.
text.KJV: And Moses called Mishael and Elzaphan, the sons of Uzziel the uncle of Aaron, and said unto them, Come near, carry your brethren from before the sanctuary out of the camp.
text.NABRE: Then Moses summoned Mishael and Elzaphan, the sons of Aaron’s uncle Uzziel, with the order, “Come, carry your kinsmen from before the sanctuary to a place outside the camp.”
text.DRB: And Moyses calling Misael and Elisaphan, the sons of Oziel, the uncle of Aaron, said to them: Go and take away your brethren from the sight of the Sanctuary, and carry them without the camp.

text.NRSV: They came forward and carried them by their tunics out of the camp, as Moses had ordered.
text.Rotherham: So they drew near and bare them away, in their tunics, unto the outside of the camp,—as spake Moses.
text.KJV: So they went near, and carried them in their coats out of the camp; as Moses had said.
text.NABRE: So they drew near and carried them by means of their tunics outside the camp, as Moses had commanded.
text.DRB: And going forthwith they took them as they lay, revested with linen tunics, and did cast them forth, as it had been commanded them.

text.NRSV: And Moses said to Aaron and to his sons Eleazar and Ithamar, “Do not bare your heads or tear your clothes, or you will die and wrath will strike all the congregation; but your kindred, the whole house of Israel, shall bewail the burning that the Lord has kindled.
text.Rotherham: Then said Moses unto Aaron and to Eleazar and to Ithamar, his sons—Your heads, ye may not bare and your garments, ye shall not rend so shall ye not die, neither against all the assembly, will he be wroth,—but let, your brethren the whole house of Israel, bewail the consuming fire wherewith Yahweh hath consumed.
text.KJV: And Moses said unto Aaron, and unto Eleazar and unto Ithamar, his sons, Uncover not your heads, neither rend your clothes; lest ye die, and lest wrath come upon all the people: but let your brethren, the whole house of Israel, bewail the burning which the Lord hath kindled.
text.NABRE: Conduct of the Priests. Moses said to Aaron and his sons Eleazar and Ithamar, “Do not dishevel your hair or tear your garments, lest you die and bring God’s wrath also on the whole community. While your kindred, the rest of the house of Israel, may mourn for those whom the Lord ’s fire has burned up,
text.DRB: And Moyses spake to Aaron, & to Eleazar, and Ithamar his sons: Uncover not your heads, and rent not your vestments, lest perhaps you die, & indignation come upon all the assembly. Let your brethren, & all the house of Israel, lament the burning that our Lord hath raised,

text.NRSV: You shall not go outside the entrance of the tent of meeting, or you will die; for the anointing oil of the Lord is on you.” And they did as Moses had ordered.
text.Rotherham: But, from the entrance of the tent of meeting, shall ye not go forth, lest ye die, for, the anointing oil of Yahweh is upon you. And they did according to the word of Moses.
text.KJV: And ye shall not go out from the door of the tabernacle of the congregation, lest ye die: for the anointing oil of the Lord is upon you. And they did according to the word of Moses.
text.NABRE: you shall not go beyond the entrance of the tent of meeting, else you shall die; for the anointing oil of the Lord is upon you.” So they did as Moses told them.
text.DRB: and yourselves shall not go out of the door of the tabernacle, otherwise you shall perish: for the oil of holy unction is upon you. Who did all things according to the precept of Moyses.

text.NRSV: And the Lord spoke to Aaron:
text.Rotherham: Then spake Yahweh unto Aaron, saying:
text.KJV: And the Lord spake unto Aaron, saying,
text.NABRE: The Lord said to Aaron:
text.DRB: Our Lord also said to Aaron:

text.NRSV: Drink no wine or strong drink, neither you nor your sons, when you enter the tent of meeting, that you may not die; it is a statute forever throughout your generations.
text.Rotherham: Wine and strong drink, thou mayest not drink,—thou nor thy sons with thee when ye enter into the tent of meeting, so shall ye not die,—an age-abiding statute to your generations;
text.KJV: Do not drink wine nor strong drink, thou, nor thy sons with thee, when ye go into the tabernacle of the congregation, lest ye die: it shall be a statute for ever throughout your generations:
text.NABRE: When you are to go to the tent of meeting, you and your sons are forbidden, by a perpetual statute throughout your generations, to drink any wine or strong drink, lest you die.
text.DRB: Wine, and any thing that may make drunk, you shall not drink, thou and thy sons, when you enter into the tabernacle of testimony, lest you die: because it is an everlasting precept through your generations.

text.NRSV: You are to distinguish between the holy and the common, and between the unclean and the clean;
text.Rotherham: That ye may make a difference, between the sacred and the common,—and between the unclean and the clean;
text.KJV: And that ye may put difference between holy and unholy, and between unclean and clean;
text.NABRE: You must be able to distinguish between what is sacred and what is profane, and between what is clean and what is unclean;
text.DRB: And that you may have knowledge to discern between the holy and profane, between the polluted and clean:

text.NRSV: and you are to teach the people of Israel all the statutes that the Lord has spoken to them through Moses.
text.Rotherham: And may teach the sons of Israel,—all the statutes which Yahweh hath spoken unto them, by the mediation of Moses.
text.KJV: And that ye may teach the children of Israel all the statutes which the Lord hath spoken unto them by the hand of Moses.
text.NABRE: and you must be able to teach the Israelites all the statutes that the Lord has given them through Moses.
text.DRB: and may teach the children of Israel all my ordinances, which the Lord hath spoken to them by the hand of Moyses.

text.NRSV: Moses spoke to Aaron and to his remaining sons, Eleazar and Ithamar: “Take the grain offering that is left from the Lord’s offerings by fire, and eat it unleavened beside the altar, for it is most holy;
text.Rotherham: Then spake Moses unto Aaron and unto Eleazar and unto Ithamar his sons that were left, Take ye the meal-offering that is left of the altar-flames of Yahweh, and eat it unleavened beside the altar,—for, most holy, it is;
text.KJV: And Moses spake unto Aaron, and unto Eleazar and unto Ithamar, his sons that were left, Take the meat offering that remaineth of the offerings of the Lord made by fire, and eat it without leaven beside the altar: for it is most holy:
text.NABRE: The Eating of the Priestly Portions. Moses said to Aaron and his surviving sons, Eleazar and Ithamar, “Take the grain offering left over from the oblations to the Lord, and eat it beside the altar in the form of unleavened cakes, since it is most holy.
text.DRB: And Moyses spake to Aaron and to Eleazar, and Ithamar his sons, that were left: Take the sacrifice, that is remaining of the oblation of our Lord, and eat it without leaven beside the altar, because it is Holy of holies.

text.NRSV: you shall eat it in a holy place, because it is your due and your sons’ due, from the offerings by fire to the Lord; for so I am commanded.
text.Rotherham: therefore shall ye eat it in a holy place, for, thine by statute, and thy sons’ by statute, it is, from among the altar-flames of Yahweh,—for, so, am I commanded.
text.KJV: And ye shall eat it in the holy place, because it is thy due, and thy sons’ due, of the sacrifices of the Lord made by fire: for so I am commanded.
text.NABRE: You must eat it in a sacred place because it is your and your sons’ due from the oblations to the Lord; such is the command I have received.
text.DRB: And you shall eat it in a holy place: which is given to thee and thy sons of the oblations of our Lord, as it hath been commanded me.

text.NRSV: But the breast that is elevated and the thigh that is raised, you and your sons and daughters as well may eat in any clean place; for they have been assigned to you and your children from the sacrifices of the offerings of well-being of the people of Israel.
text.Rotherham: And the wave-breast, and the heave-shoulder, shall ye eat in clean place, thou, and thy sons and thy daughters with thee,—for as thine by statute and thy sons’ by statute, have they been given, from among the peace-offerings of the sons of Israel.
text.KJV: And the wave breast and heave shoulder shall ye eat in a clean place; thou, and thy sons, and thy daughters with thee: for they be thy due, and thy sons’ due, which are given out of the sacrifices of peace offerings of the children of Israel.
text.NABRE: The brisket of the elevated offering and the leg of the contribution, however, you and your sons and daughters may eat, in a clean place; for these have been assigned to you and your children as your due from the communion sacrifices of the Israelites.
text.DRB: The breast also that is offered, and the shoulder that is separated, you shall eat in a most clean place, thou and thy sons, and thy daughters with thee. For they are laid apart for thee and thy children, of the healthful hosts of the children of Israel:

text.NRSV: The thigh that is raised and the breast that is elevated they shall bring, together with the offerings by fire of the fat, to raise for an elevation offering before the Lord; they are to be your due and that of your children forever, as the Lord has commanded.
text.Rotherham: The heave-shoulder and the wave-breast upon the altar-flames of the fat portions, shall they bring in, to wave as a wave-offering, before Yahweh,—so shall they be thine, and thy sons’ with thee, by an age-abiding statute, As Yahweh hath commanded.
text.KJV: The heave shoulder and the wave breast shall they bring with the offerings made by fire of the fat, to wave it for a wave offering before the Lord; and it shall be thine, and thy sons’ with thee, by a statute for ever; as the Lord hath commanded.
text.NABRE: The leg of the contribution and the brisket of the elevated offering shall be brought in with the oblations of fat to be raised as an elevated offering before the Lord. They shall belong to you and your children as your due forever, as the Lord has commanded.”
text.DRB: because the shoulder and the breast, and the fat that is burnt on the altar, they have elevated before our Lord, and they pertain to thee, and to thy sons by a perpetual law, as our Lord hath commanded.

text.NRSV: Then Moses made inquiry about the goat of the sin offering, and—it had already been burned! He was angry with Eleazar and Ithamar, Aaron’s remaining sons, and said,
text.Rotherham: Now, as for the sin-bearing goat, Moses diligently sought it but lo! it had been burnt up,—then was he wroth against Eleazar and against Ithamar, the sons of Aaron that were left, saying:
text.KJV: And Moses diligently sought the goat of the sin offering, and, behold, it was burnt: and he was angry with Eleazar and Ithamar, the sons of Aaron which were left alive, saying,
text.NABRE: Moses inquired closely about the goat of the purification offering and discovered that it had all been burned. So he was angry with the surviving sons of Aaron, Eleazar and Ithamar, and said,
text.DRB: Among these things, when Moyses sought for the buck goat, that had been offered for sin, he found it burnt: and being angry against Eleazar, and Ithamar the sons of Aaron that remained, he said:

text.NRSV: “Why did you not eat the sin offering in the sacred area? For it is most holy, and God has given it to you that you may remove the guilt of the congregation, to make atonement on their behalf before the Lord.
text.Rotherham: Wherefore did ye not eat the sin-bearer in the holy place? for most, holy, it is,—and thee same, hath he given you that ye may bear the iniquity of the assembly, to put a propitiatory-covering over them before Yahweh.
text.KJV: Wherefore have ye not eaten the sin offering in the holy place, seeing it is most holy, and God hath given it you to bear the iniquity of the congregation, to make atonement for them before the Lord?
text.NABRE: “Why did you not eat the purification offering in the sacred place, since it is most holy? It has been given to you that you might remove the guilt of the community and make atonement for them before the Lord.
text.DRB: Why did you not eat the host for sin, in a holy place, which is most Holy, and given to you, that you may bear the iniquity of the multitude, and may pray for it in the sight of our Lord,

text.NRSV: Its blood was not brought into the inner part of the sanctuary. You should certainly have eaten it in the sanctuary, as I commanded.”
text.Rotherham: Lo! the blood thereof had not been taken into the holy place, within,—ye should have, indeed eaten, it, in a holy place, as I commanded.
text.KJV: Behold, the blood of it was not brought in within the holy place: ye should indeed have eaten it in the holy place, as I commanded.
text.NABRE: Since its blood was not brought inside the sanctuary, you should certainly have eaten the offering in the sanctuary, as I was commanded.”
text.DRB: especially whereas of the blood thereof, there hath not been carried within the holy places, and you ought to have eaten it in the Sanctuary, as it was commanded me?

text.NRSV: And Aaron spoke to Moses, “See, today they offered their sin offering and their burnt offering before the Lord; and yet such things as these have befallen me! If I had eaten the sin offering today, would it have been acceptable to the Lord?”
text.Rotherham: Then spake Aaron unto Moses—Lo! this very day, when they had brought near their own sin-bearer, and their own ascending-sacrifice, there befell me such things as these,—if, then, I had eaten of the sin-bearer this day, would it have been well-pleasing in the eyes of Yahweh?
text.KJV: And Aaron said unto Moses, Behold, this day have they offered their sin offering and their burnt offering before the Lord; and such things have befallen me: and if I had eaten the sin offering to day, should it have been accepted in the sight of the Lord?
text.NABRE: Aaron answered Moses, “Even though they presented their purification offering and burnt offering before the Lord today, still this misfortune has befallen me. Had I then eaten of the purification offering today, would it have been pleasing to the Lord?”
text.DRB: Aaron answered: This day hath been offered the victim for sin, and the holocaust before our Lord: and to me that is chanced which thou seest, how could I eat it, or please our Lord in ceremonies having a sorrowful heart?

text.NRSV: And when Moses heard that, he agreed.
text.Rotherham: And, when Moses heard that, then was it well-pleasing in his eyes.
text.KJV: And when Moses heard that, he was content.
text.NABRE: On hearing this, Moses was satisfied.
text.DRB: Which when Moyses had heard, he was satisfied with his answer.

text.NRSV: The Lord spoke to Moses and Aaron:
text.Rotherham: And Yahweh spake unto Moses and unto Aaron, saying unto them:
text.KJV: And the Lord spake unto Moses and to Aaron, saying unto them,
text.NABRE: The Lord said to Moses and Aaron:
text.DRB: And our Lord spake to Moyses & Aaron, saying:

text.NRSV: Speak to the people of Israel, saying: From among all the land animals these are the creatures that you may eat.
text.Rotherham: Speak ye unto the sons of Israel saying,—These are the living things which ye may eat, of all the beasts which are upon the earth:
text.KJV: Speak unto the children of Israel, saying, These are the beasts which ye shall eat among all the beasts that are on the earth.
text.NABRE: Speak to the Israelites and tell them: Of all land animals these are the ones you may eat:
text.DRB: Say to the children of Israel: these are the beasts which you ought to eat of all the living things of the earth.

text.NRSV: Any animal that has divided hoofs and is cleft-footed and chews the cud—such you may eat.
text.Rotherham: Whatsoever parteth the hoof and is cloven-footed, and cheweth the cud among beasts, that, may ye eat.
text.KJV: Whatsoever parteth the hoof, and is clovenfooted, and cheweth the cud, among the beasts, that shall ye eat.
text.NABRE: Any animal that has hoofs you may eat, provided it is cloven-footed and chews the cud.
text.DRB: Every one that hath the hoof divided, and cheweth the cud among the cattle, you shall eat.

text.NRSV: But among those that chew the cud or have divided hoofs, you shall not eat the following: the camel, for even though it chews the cud, it does not have divided hoofs; it is unclean for you.
text.Rotherham: Nevertheless, these, shall ye not eat, of them that chew the cud, and of them that part the hoof,—the camel, because though he, cheweth the cud, yet, the hoof, he parteth not, unclean, he is to you;
text.KJV: Nevertheless these shall ye not eat of them that chew the cud, or of them that divide the hoof: as the camel, because he cheweth the cud, but divideth not the hoof; he is unclean unto you.
text.NABRE: But you shall not eat any of the following from among those that only chew the cud or only have hoofs: the camel, which indeed chews the cud, but does not have hoofs and is therefore unclean for you;
text.DRB: But whatsoever indeed cheweth the cud, and hath an hoof, but divideth it not, as the camel, and others, that you shall not eat, and among the unclean you shall repute it.

text.NRSV: The rock badger, for even though it chews the cud, it does not have divided hoofs; it is unclean for you.
text.Rotherham: And, the coney, because, though he cheweth the cud, yet, the hoof, he parteth not,—unclean, he is to you;
text.KJV: And the coney, because he cheweth the cud, but divideth not the hoof; he is unclean unto you.
text.NABRE: the rock hyrax, which indeed chews the cud, but does not have hoofs and is therefore unclean for you;
text.DRB: Cherogril which cheweth the cud, and divideth not the hoof, is unclean.

text.NRSV: The hare, for even though it chews the cud, it does not have divided hoofs; it is unclean for you.
text.Rotherham: And the hare, because though she cheweth the cud, yet the hoof, she parteth not,—unclean, she is to you;
text.KJV: And the hare, because he cheweth the cud, but divideth not the hoof; he is unclean unto you.
text.NABRE: the hare, which indeed chews the cud, but does not have hoofs and is therefore unclean for you;
text.DRB: The hare also: for that also cheweth the cud, but divideth not the hoof.

text.NRSV: The pig, for even though it has divided hoofs and is cleft-footed, it does not chew the cud; it is unclean for you.
text.Rotherham: And, the swine, because though he parteth the hoof, and is cloven-footed, yet, the cud, he cheweth not,—unclean, he is to you;
text.KJV: And the swine, though he divide the hoof, and be clovenfooted, yet he cheweth not the cud; he is unclean to you.
text.NABRE: and the pig, which does indeed have hoofs and is cloven-footed, but does not chew the cud and is therefore unclean for you.
text.DRB: And the swine: which though it divideth the hoof, cheweth not the cud.

text.NRSV: Of their flesh you shall not eat, and their carcasses you shall not touch; they are unclean for you.
text.Rotherham: Of their flesh, shall ye not eat, and, their carcase, shall ye not touch,—unclean, they are to you.
text.KJV: Of their flesh shall ye not eat, and their carcase shall ye not touch; they are unclean to you.
text.NABRE: You shall not eat their meat, and you shall not touch their carcasses; they are unclean for you.
text.DRB: The flesh of these you shall not eat, nor touch their carcasses, because they are unclean to you.

text.NRSV: These you may eat, of all that are in the waters. Everything in the waters that has fins and scales, whether in the seas or in the streams—such you may eat.
text.Rotherham: these, may ye eat, of all that are in the waters,—all that have fins and scales in the waters, in the seas and in the rivers, them, may ye eat.
text.KJV: These shall ye eat of all that are in the waters: whatsoever hath fins and scales in the waters, in the seas, and in the rivers, them shall ye eat.
text.NABRE: Of the various creatures that live in the water, you may eat the following: whatever in the seas or in river waters that has both fins and scales you may eat.
text.DRB: These are the things that breed in the waters, and which it is lawful to eat. All that hath fins, and scales, as well in the sea, as in the rivers, and the pools, you shall eat.

text.NRSV: But anything in the seas or the streams that does not have fins and scales, of the swarming creatures in the waters and among all the other living creatures that are in the waters—they are detestable to you
text.Rotherham: But, all that have not fins and scales, in the seas and in the rivers, of all that swarm in the waters, and of all the living souls that are in the waters, an abomination, they are unto you;
text.KJV: And all that have not fins and scales in the seas, and in the rivers, of all that move in the waters, and of any living thing which is in the waters, they shall be an abomination unto you:
text.NABRE: But of the creatures that swarm in the water or of animals that otherwise live in the water, whether in the sea or in the rivers, all those that lack either fins or scales are loathsome for you,
text.DRB: But whatsoever hath not fins and scales, of those that move and live in the waters, shall be unto you abominable,

text.NRSV: and detestable they shall remain. Of their flesh you shall not eat, and their carcasses you shall regard as detestable.
text.Rotherham: and an abomination, shall they remain to you,—of their flesh, ye shall not eat, and their carcases, shall ye abhor.
text.KJV: They shall be even an abomination unto you; ye shall not eat of their flesh, but ye shall have their carcases in abomination.
text.NABRE: and shall always be loathsome to you. Their meat you shall not eat, and their carcasses you shall loathe.
text.DRB: and execrable, their flesh you shall not eat, and their carcasses you shall avoid.

text.NRSV: Everything in the waters that does not have fins and scales is detestable to you.
text.Rotherham: Whatsoever hath not fins and scales in the waters, an abomination, it is unto you.
text.KJV: Whatsoever hath no fins nor scales in the waters, that shall be an abomination unto you.
text.NABRE: Every water creature that lacks fins or scales is loathsome for you.
text.DRB: All that have not fins and scales in the waters, shall be polluted.

text.NRSV: These you shall regard as detestable among the birds. They shall not be eaten; they are an abomination: the eagle, the vulture, the osprey,
text.Rotherham: And, these, shall ye abhor of birds, they shall not be eaten, an abomination, they are,—the eagle, and the ossifrage, and the osprey;
text.KJV: And these are they which ye shall have in abomination among the fowls; they shall not be eaten, they are an abomination: the eagle, and the ossifrage, and the ospray,
text.NABRE: Of the birds, these you shall loathe; they shall not be eaten, they are loathsome: the griffon vulture, the bearded vulture, the black vulture,
text.DRB: Of birds these are they which you must not eat, and are to be avoided of you: The Eagle, and the griffon, and the osprey,

text.NRSV: the buzzard, the kite of any kind;
text.Rotherham: and the vulture, and the falcon, after its kind;
text.KJV: And the vulture, and the kite after his kind;
text.NABRE: the kite, the various species of falcons,
text.DRB: and the kite, and the vulture according to his kind,

text.NRSV: every raven of any kind;
text.Rotherham: every raven, after its kind;
text.KJV: Every raven after his kind;
text.NABRE: the various species of crows,
text.DRB: and every one of the raven's kind, according to their similitude,

text.NRSV: the ostrich, the nighthawk, the sea gull, the hawk of any kind;
text.Rotherham: and the female ostrich, and the male ostrich, and the sea-gull,—and the hawk after its kind;
text.KJV: And the owl, and the night hawk, and the cuckow, and the hawk after his kind,
text.NABRE: the eagle owl, the kestrel, the long-eared owl, the various species of hawks,
text.DRB: the ostrich, and the owl, and the stern, and the hawk according to his kind,

text.NRSV: the little owl, the cormorant, the great owl,
text.Rotherham: and the pelican and the gannet, and the bittern;
text.KJV: And the little owl, and the cormorant, and the great owl,
text.NABRE: the little owl, the cormorant, the screech owl,
text.DRB: the screech-owl, & the diver, and the stork,

text.NRSV: the water hen, the desert owl, the carrion vulture,
text.Rotherham: and the swan and the vomiting pelican and the little vulture;
text.KJV: And the swan, and the pelican, and the gier eagle,
text.NABRE: the barn owl, the horned owl, the osprey,
text.DRB: and the swan, and the onocratal, and the porphirion,

text.NRSV: the stork, the heron of any kind, the hoopoe, and the bat.
text.Rotherham: and the stork, and the parrot, after its kind,—and the mountain-cock and the bat.
text.KJV: And the stork, the heron after her kind, and the lapwing, and the bat.
text.NABRE: the stork, the various species of herons, the hoopoe, and the bat.
text.DRB: the herodian, & the charadrion according to his kind, the lapwing also, and the bat.

text.NRSV: All winged insects that walk upon all fours are detestable to you.
text.Rotherham: Every creeping thing that flieth, that goeth on all-fours, an abomination, it is unto you.
text.KJV: All fowls that creep, going upon all four, shall be an abomination unto you.
text.NABRE: The various winged insects that walk on all fours are loathsome for you.
text.DRB: Of fowls every one that goeth upon four feet, shall be abominable to you.

text.NRSV: But among the winged insects that walk on all fours you may eat those that have jointed legs above their feet, with which to leap on the ground.
text.Rotherham: Nevertheless, these, may ye eat, of all creeping things that fly, that go on all-fours,—such as have legs above their feet, to leap therewith, upon the earth—
text.KJV: Yet these may ye eat of every flying creeping thing that goeth upon all four, which have legs above their feet, to leap withal upon the earth;
text.NABRE: But of the various winged insects that walk on all fours you may eat those that have legs jointed above their feet for leaping on the ground;
text.DRB: And whatsoever walketh upon four feet, but hath the legs behind longer, wherewith he hoppeth upon the earth,

text.NRSV: Of them you may eat: the locust according to its kind, the bald locust according to its kind, the cricket according to its kind, and the grasshopper according to its kind.
text.Rotherham: these of them, may ye eat: the swarming-locust after its kind, and the devouring locust after its kind,—and the chargol-locust after its kind, and the chagab-locust after its kind.
text.KJV: Even these of them ye may eat; the locust after his kind, and the bald locust after his kind, and the beetle after his kind, and the grasshopper after his kind.
text.NABRE: hence of these you may eat the following: the various kinds of locusts, the various kinds of bald locusts, the various kinds of crickets, and the various kinds of grasshoppers.
text.DRB: that you shall eat, as is the bruke in his kind, the attake, and the ophiomach, and the locust, every one according to their kind.

text.NRSV: But all other winged insects that have four feet are detestable to you.
text.Rotherham: But every [other] creeping thing that flieth, which hath four feet, an abomination, it is unto you;
text.KJV: But all other flying creeping things, which have four feet, shall be an abomination unto you.
text.NABRE: All other winged insects that have four legs are loathsome for you.
text.DRB: But of fowl whatsoever hath four feet only, shall be execrable to you:

text.NRSV: By these you shall become unclean; whoever touches their carcass shall be unclean until the evening,
text.Rotherham: and, for these, shall ye count yourselves unclean—whosoever toucheth the carcase of them shall be unclean until the evening;
text.KJV: And for these ye shall be unclean: whosoever toucheth the carcase of them shall be unclean until the even.
text.NABRE: You become unclean by the following—anyone who touches their carcasses shall be unclean until evening,
text.DRB: and whatsoever shall touch the carcasses of them, shall be polluted, and shall be unclean until even:

text.NRSV: and whoever carries any part of their carcass shall wash his clothes and be unclean until the evening.
text.Rotherham: and whosoever beareth away aught of the carcase of them, shall wash his clothes and be unclean until the evening.
text.KJV: And whosoever beareth ought of the carcase of them shall wash his clothes, and be unclean until the even.
text.NABRE: and anyone who carries any part of their carcasses shall wash his garments and be unclean until evening—
text.DRB: and if it be necessary that he carry any of these that be dead, he shall wash his cloths, and shall be unclean until sun set.

text.NRSV: Every animal that has divided hoofs but is not cleft-footed or does not chew the cud is unclean for you; everyone who touches them shall be unclean.
text.Rotherham: As for every kind of beast which, though it parteth the hoof, yet is not cloven-footed nor cheweth the cud, unclean, they are unto you,—every one who toucheth them shall be unclean.
text.KJV: The carcases of every beast which divideth the hoof, and is not clovenfooted, nor cheweth the cud, are unclean unto you: every one that toucheth them shall be unclean.
text.NABRE: by all hoofed animals that are not cloven-footed or do not chew the cud; they are unclean for you; anyone who touches them becomes unclean.
text.DRB: Every beast that hath a hoof, but divideth it not, neither cheweth the cud, shall be unclean: and whatsoever toucheth it, shall be defiled.

text.NRSV: All that walk on their paws, among the animals that walk on all fours, are unclean for you; whoever touches their carcass shall be unclean until the evening,
text.Rotherham: And, all that go upon their paws, among all the living things that go on all-fours, unclean, they are unto you,—whoso toucheth the carcase of them, shall be unclean until the evening.
text.KJV: And whatsoever goeth upon his paws, among all manner of beasts that go on all four, those are unclean unto you: whoso toucheth their carcase shall be unclean until the even.
text.NABRE: Also by the various quadrupeds that walk on paws; they are unclean for you; anyone who touches their carcasses shall be unclean until evening,
text.DRB: That which walketh upon hands of all beasts, which go on four feet, shall be unclean: he that toucheth their carcases shall be polluted until even.

text.NRSV: and the one who carries their carcass shall wash his clothes and be unclean until the evening; they are unclean for you.
text.Rotherham: And, he that beareth away the carcase of them shall wash his clothes, and shall be unclean until the evening,—unclean, they are unto you.
text.KJV: And he that beareth the carcase of them shall wash his clothes, and be unclean until the even: they are unclean unto you.
text.NABRE: and anyone who carries their carcasses shall wash his garments and be unclean until evening. They are unclean for you.
text.DRB: And he that carrieth such carcasses, shall wash his cloths, and shall be unclean until even: because all these things are unclean to you.

text.NRSV: These are unclean for you among the creatures that swarm upon the earth: the weasel, the mouse, the great lizard according to its kind,
text.Rotherham: And, these, unto you, shall be unclean, among the creeping things that creep upon the earth,—the weasel and the mouse, and the lizard after its kind;
text.KJV: These also shall be unclean unto you among the creeping things that creep upon the earth; the weasel, and the mouse, and the tortoise after his kind,
text.NABRE: Of the creatures that swarm on the ground, the following are unclean for you: the rat, the mouse, the various kinds of lizards,
text.DRB: These also shall be reputed among polluted things, of all that move upon the earth, the weasel and the mouse and the crocodile, every one according to their kind,

text.NRSV: the gecko, the land crocodile, the lizard, the sand lizard, and the chameleon.
text.Rotherham: and the ferret and the chameleon and the wall-lizard,—and the winding lizard, and the mole.
text.KJV: And the ferret, and the chameleon, and the lizard, and the snail, and the mole.
text.NABRE: the gecko, the spotted lizard, the agama, the skink, and the chameleon.
text.DRB: the mygale, and the chameleon, and the stellion, and the lizard, and the mole:

text.NRSV: These are unclean for you among all that swarm; whoever touches them when they are dead shall be unclean until the evening.
text.Rotherham: These, are they which are unclean to you among all that creep,—whosoever toucheth them when they are dead shall be unclean until the evening;
text.KJV: These are unclean to you among all that creep: whosoever doth touch them, when they be dead, shall be unclean until the even.
text.NABRE: Among the various swarming creatures, these are unclean for you. Everyone who touches them when they are dead shall be unclean until evening.
text.DRB: all these are unclean. He that toucheth their carcasses, shall be unclean until even:

text.NRSV: And anything upon which any of them falls when they are dead shall be unclean, whether an article of wood or cloth or skin or sacking, any article that is used for any purpose; it shall be dipped into water, and it shall be unclean until the evening, and then it shall be clean.
text.Rotherham: and, everything whereon any of them shall fall when they are dead shall be unclean—of any articles of wood, or cloth, or skin, or sackcloth, any article wherewith any work is done,—shall be put in water and shall be unclean until the evening, and then be clean,
text.KJV: And upon whatsoever any of them, when they are dead, doth fall, it shall be unclean; whether it be any vessel of wood, or raiment, or skin, or sack, whatsoever vessel it be, wherein any work is done, it must be put into water, and it shall be unclean until the even; so it shall be cleansed.
text.NABRE: Everything on which one of them falls when dead becomes unclean, including any article of wood, cloth, leather or goat hair—any article of which use can be made. It must be immersed in water and remain unclean until evening, when it again becomes clean.
text.DRB: and that whereupon any thing of their carcasses falleth, shall be polluted, as well vessel of wood and raiment, as skins and hair cloths: and in whatsoever vessel any work is done, they shall be dipped in water, and shall be polluted until even, and so afterward shall be clean.

text.NRSV: And if any of them falls into any earthen vessel, all that is in it shall be unclean, and you shall break the vessel.
text.Rotherham: And, as for any earthen vessel whereinto any of them may fall, everything therein shall be unclean, and, the vessel itself, shall ye break in pieces.
text.KJV: And every earthen vessel, whereinto any of them falleth, whatsoever is in it shall be unclean; and ye shall break it.
text.NABRE: Should any of these creatures fall into a clay vessel, everything in it becomes unclean, and the vessel itself you must break.
text.DRB: But the earthen vessel, whereinto any of these falleth within it, shall be polluted, and therefore is to be broken.

text.NRSV: Any food in it which may be eaten, upon which water falls, shall be unclean; all drink that may be drunk from any such vessel shall be unclean.
text.Rotherham: Of all the food that may be eaten, that whereon shall come water, shall be unclean,—and, all drink which might be drunk in any [such] vessel, shall be unclean.
text.KJV: Of all meat which may be eaten, that on which such water cometh shall be unclean: and all drink that may be drunk in every such vessel shall be unclean.
text.NABRE: Any food that can be eaten which makes contact with water, and any liquid that may be drunk, in any such vessel become unclean.
text.DRB: All meat, which you shall eat, if the water be poured upon it, shall be unclean; and all liquor that is drunk of all vessel, shall be unclean.

text.NRSV: Anything on which any part of the carcass falls shall be unclean; whether an oven or stove, it shall be broken in pieces; they are unclean, and shall remain unclean for you.
text.Rotherham: And, everything whereon shall fall any part of the carcase of them shall be unclean, oven or fire-range, it shall be destroyed, unclean, they are,—and, unclean, Shall they remain to you.
text.KJV: And every thing whereupon any part of their carcase falleth shall be unclean; whether it be oven, or ranges for pots, they shall be broken down: for they are unclean, and shall be unclean unto you.
text.NABRE: Any object on which any part of their carcasses falls becomes unclean; if it is an oven or stove, this must be broken to pieces; they are unclean and shall always be unclean to you.
text.DRB: And upon whatsoever ought of such carcasses falleth, it shall be unclean: whether ovens, or pots with feet, they shall be destroyed, and shall be unclean.

text.NRSV: But a spring or a cistern holding water shall be clean, while whatever touches the carcass in it shall be unclean.
text.Rotherham: Notwithstanding, a fountain or cistern wherein is a gathering of waters shall be clean,—but, he that toucheth the carcase of them shall be unclean.
text.KJV: Nevertheless a fountain or pit, wherein there is plenty of water, shall be clean: but that which toucheth their carcase shall be unclean.
text.NABRE: However, a spring or a cistern for collecting water remains clean; but whoever touches such an animal’s carcass becomes unclean.
text.DRB: But the fountains and the cisterns, and all collection of waters shall be clean. He that toucheth their carcass, shall be polluted.

text.NRSV: If any part of their carcass falls upon any seed set aside for sowing, it is clean;
text.Rotherham: And when any part of the carcase of them shall fall upon seed for sowing, which is to be sown, the same is, clean.
text.KJV: And if any part of their carcase fall upon any sowing seed which is to be sown, it shall be clean.
text.NABRE: If any part of their carcasses falls on any sort of grain that is to be sown, it remains clean;
text.DRB: If it fall upon seed corn it shall not pollute it.

text.NRSV: but if water is put on the seed and any part of their carcass falls on it, it is unclean for you.
text.Rotherham: But, when water shall be put upon seed, and there shall fall thereon any part of the carcase of them, unclean, it is to you.
text.KJV: But if any water be put upon the seed, and any part of their carcase fall thereon, it shall be unclean unto you.
text.NABRE: but if the grain has become moistened, it becomes unclean to you when any part of their carcasses falls on it.
text.DRB: But if any man pour water upon the seed, and afterward it be touched with the carcasses, it shall be forthwith polluted.

text.NRSV: If any animal of which you may eat dies, anyone who touches its carcass shall be unclean until the evening.
text.Rotherham: And, when any of the beasts which are yours for food shall die, he that toucheth the carcase of it shall be unclean until the evening.
text.KJV: And if any beast, of which ye may eat, die; he that toucheth the carcase thereof shall be unclean until the even.
text.NABRE: When one of the animals that you could otherwise eat dies of itself, anyone who touches its carcass shall be unclean until evening;
text.DRB: If a beast be dead, of which it is lawful for you to eat, he that toucheth the carcass thereof, shall be unclean until even:

text.NRSV: Those who eat of its carcass shall wash their clothes and be unclean until the evening; those who carry the carcass shall wash their clothes and be unclean until the evening.
text.Rotherham: And, he that eateth of the carcase of it, shall wash his clothes, and be unclean until the evening,—he also that carrieth away the carcase thereof shall wash his clothes, and be unclean until the evening.
text.KJV: And he that eateth of the carcase of it shall wash his clothes, and be unclean until the even: he also that beareth the carcase of it shall wash his clothes, and be unclean until the even.
text.NABRE: and anyone who eats any part of its carcass shall wash his garments and be unclean until evening; so also, anyone who carries its carcass shall wash his garments and be unclean until evening.
text.DRB: and he that eateth or carrieth any thing thereof shall wash his cloths, & shall be unclean until even.

text.NRSV: All creatures that swarm upon the earth are detestable; they shall not be eaten.
text.Rotherham: And, any creeping thing that creepeth upon the earth, an abomination, it is—it shall not be eaten.
text.KJV: And every creeping thing that creepeth upon the earth shall be an abomination; it shall not be eaten.
text.NABRE: All the creatures that swarm on the ground are loathsome and shall not be eaten.
text.DRB: All that creepeth upon the earth, shall be abominable, neither shall it be taken for meat.

text.NRSV: Whatever moves on its belly, and whatever moves on all fours, or whatever has many feet, all the creatures that swarm upon the earth, you shall not eat; for they are detestable.
text.Rotherham: Everything that goeth upon the belly, and everything that goeth upon all-fours, even to everything having many feet, as regardeth any creeping thing that creepeth upon the earth, ye shall not eat them for, an abomination, they are.
text.KJV: Whatsoever goeth upon the belly, and whatsoever goeth upon all four, or whatsoever hath more feet among all creeping things that creep upon the earth, them ye shall not eat; for they are an abomination.
text.NABRE: Whether it crawls on its belly, goes on all fours, or has many legs—any creature that swarms on the earth—you shall not eat them; they are loathsome.
text.DRB: Whatsoever goeth upon the breast on four feet, & hath many feet, or traileth on the earth, you shall not eat, because it is abominable.

text.NRSV: You shall not make yourselves detestable with any creature that swarms; you shall not defile yourselves with them, and so become unclean.
text.Rotherham: Do not make your persons abominable, with any creeping thing that creepeth,—neither shall ye make yourselves unclean with them, that ye should become unclean thereby.
text.KJV: Ye shall not make yourselves abominable with any creeping thing that creepeth, neither shall ye make yourselves unclean with them, that ye should be defiled thereby.
text.NABRE: Do not make yourselves loathsome by any swarming creature nor defile yourselves with them and so become unclean by them.
text.DRB: Do not contaminate your souls, nor touch ought thereof, lest you be unclean.

text.NRSV: For I am the Lord your God; sanctify yourselves therefore, and be holy, for I am holy. You shall not defile yourselves with any swarming creature that moves on the earth.
text.Rotherham: For, I—Yahweh, am your God, therefore shall ye hallow yourselves and remain holy; for, holy, am, I,—therefore shall ye not make your persons unclean, with any creeping thing that crawleth upon the earth;
text.KJV: For I am the Lord your God: ye shall therefore sanctify yourselves, and ye shall be holy; for I am holy: neither shall ye defile yourselves with any manner of creeping thing that creepeth upon the earth.
text.NABRE: For I, the Lord, am your God. You shall make and keep yourselves holy, because I am holy. You shall not make yourselves unclean, then, by any swarming creature that crawls on the ground.
text.DRB: For I am the Lord your God: be holy, because I am holy. Pollute not your souls in any creeping beast, that moveth upon the earth.

text.NRSV: For I am the Lord who brought you up from the land of Egypt, to be your God; you shall be holy, for I am holy.
text.Rotherham: for, I—Yahweh, am he that brought you up out of the land of Egypt, that I might become your God,—therefore shall ye be holy, for, holy, am, I.
text.KJV: For I am the Lord that bringeth you up out of the land of Egypt, to be your God: ye shall therefore be holy, for I am holy.
text.NABRE: Since I, the Lord, am the one who brought you up from the land of Egypt that I might be your God, you shall be holy, because I am holy.
text.DRB: For I am the Lord, that brought you out of the Land of Ægypt, that I might be your God. You shall be holy because I am holy.

text.NRSV: This is the law of animals and birds and all living creatures that move in the waters and all creatures that swarm on the ground,
text.Rotherham: This, is the law of beast and of bird, and of every living soul that moveth in the waters,—and as to every soul that creepeth upon the earth:
text.KJV: This is the law of the beasts, and of the fowl, and of every living creature that moveth in the waters, and of every creature that creepeth upon the earth:
text.NABRE: This is the instruction for land animals, birds, and all the creatures that move about in the water, as well as any animal that swarms on the ground,
text.DRB: This is the law of beasts and fowls, and of every living soul, that moveth in the waters, and creepeth on the earth,

text.NRSV: to make a distinction between the unclean and the clean, and between the living creature that may be eaten and the living creature that may not be eaten.
text.Rotherham: That ye may make a difference—between the unclean and the clean,—and between the living thing that may be eaten, and the living thing which may not be eaten.
text.KJV: To make a difference between the unclean and the clean, and between the beast that may be eaten and the beast that may not be eaten.
text.NABRE: that you may distinguish between the clean and the unclean, and between creatures that may be eaten and those that may not be eaten.
text.DRB: that you may know the differences of the clean, and the unclean, and know what you ought to eat, and what to refuse.

text.NRSV: The Lord spoke to Moses, saying:
text.Rotherham: And Yahweh spake unto Moses, saying:
text.KJV: And the Lord spake unto Moses, saying,
text.NABRE: The Lord said to Moses:
text.DRB: And our Lord spake to Moyses, saying:

text.NRSV: Speak to the people of Israel, saying: If a woman conceives and bears a male child, she shall be ceremonially unclean seven days; as at the time of her menstruation, she shall be unclean.
text.Rotherham: Speak unto the sons of Israel, saying, A woman when she conceiveth seed and giveth birth to a male child, then shall she be unclean seven days, according to the days of her removal in her sickness shall she be unclean.
text.KJV: Speak unto the children of Israel, saying, If a woman have conceived seed, and born a man child: then she shall be unclean seven days; according to the days of the separation for her infirmity shall she be unclean.
text.NABRE: Tell the Israelites: When a woman has a child, giving birth to a boy, she shall be unclean for seven days, with the same uncleanness as during her menstrual period.
text.DRB: Speak to the children of Israel, and thou shalt say to them: A woman if receiving seed she bear a man child, shall be unclean seven days, according to the days of the separation of her flowers.

text.NRSV: On the eighth day the flesh of his foreskin shall be circumcised.
text.Rotherham: And, on the eighth day, shall the flesh of his foreskin be circumcised.
text.KJV: And in the eighth day the flesh of his foreskin shall be circumcised.
text.NABRE: On the eighth day, the flesh of the boy’s foreskin shall be circumcised,
text.DRB: And the eighth day the little infant shall be circumcised:

text.NRSV: Her time of blood purification shall be thirty-three days; she shall not touch any holy thing, or come into the sanctuary, until the days of her purification are completed.
text.Rotherham: And, for thirty-three days, shall she continue in the blood of purification,—no hallowed thing, shall she touch, and, into the sanctuary, shall she not enter, until her days of purification are fulfilled.
text.KJV: And she shall then continue in the blood of her purifying three and thirty days; she shall touch no hallowed thing, nor come into the sanctuary, until the days of her purifying be fulfilled.
text.NABRE: and then she shall spend thirty-three days more in a state of blood purity; she shall not touch anything sacred nor enter the sanctuary till the days of her purification are fulfilled.
text.DRB: but herself shall remain three & thirty days in the blood of her purification: No holy thing shall she touch, neither shall she enter into the Sanctuary, until the days of her purification be expired.

text.NRSV: If she bears a female child, she shall be unclean two weeks, as in her menstruation; her time of blood purification shall be sixty-six days.
text.Rotherham: But if, a female child, she bear, then shall she be unclean two weeks as in her removal,—and, for sixty-six days, shall she continue in the blood of purification.
text.KJV: But if she bear a maid child, then she shall be unclean two weeks, as in her separation: and she shall continue in the blood of her purifying threescore and six days.
text.NABRE: If she gives birth to a girl, for fourteen days she shall be as unclean as during her menstrual period, after which she shall spend sixty-six days in a state of blood purity.
text.DRB: But if she bear a woman child, she shall be unclean two weeks, according to the custom of the flux of her flowers, and sixty six days she shall remain in the blood of her purification.

text.NRSV: When the days of her purification are completed, whether for a son or for a daughter, she shall bring to the priest at the entrance of the tent of meeting a lamb in its first year for a burnt offering, and a pigeon or a turtledove for a sin offering.
text.Rotherham: And, when the days of her purification are fulfilled, whether for a son or for a daughter, she shall bring in a lamb, the choice of its year, as an ascending-sacrifice, and a young pigeon or a turtle-dove, as a sin-bearer, unto the entrance of the tent of meeting, unto the priest;
text.KJV: And when the days of her purifying are fulfilled, for a son, or for a daughter, she shall bring a lamb of the first year for a burnt offering, and a young pigeon, or a turtledove, for a sin offering, unto the door of the tabernacle of the congregation, unto the priest:
text.NABRE: When the days of her purification for a son or for a daughter are fulfilled, she shall bring to the priest at the entrance of the tent of meeting a yearling lamb for a burnt offering and a pigeon or a turtledove for a purification offering.
text.DRB: And when the days of her purification are expired, for a son, or for a daughter, she shall bring a lamb of a year old for an holocaust, and a young pigeon or a turtle for sin, to the door of the tabernacle of testimony, and shall deliver them to the Priest,

text.NRSV: He shall offer it before the Lord, and make atonement on her behalf; then she shall be clean from her flow of blood. This is the law for her who bears a child, male or female.
text.Rotherham: and he shall bring it near before Yahweh so shall the priest put a propitiatory-covering over her, and she shall be clean from her fountain of blood. This, is the law for her that hath given birth, to a male child or to a female.
text.KJV: Who shall offer it before the Lord, and make an atonement for her; and she shall be cleansed from the issue of her blood. This is the law for her that hath born a male or a female.
