text.NABRE: The priest shall offer them before the Lord to make atonement for her, and thus she will be clean again after her flow of blood. Such is the ritual for the woman who gives birth to a child, male or female.
text.DRB: who shall offer them before our Lord, and shall pray for her, and so she shall be cleansed from the flux of her blood, this is the law for her that beareth a man child or a woman child.

text.NRSV: If she cannot afford a sheep, she shall take two turtledoves or two pigeons, one for a burnt offering and the other for a sin offering; and the priest shall make atonement on her behalf, and she shall be clean.
text.Rotherham: But, if her hand findeth not sufficiency for a lamb, then shall she take two turtle doves or two young pigeons, one for an ascending-sacrifice and one for a sin-bearer,—so shall the priest put a propitiatory-covering over her, and she shall be clean.
text.KJV: And if she be not able to bring a lamb, then she shall bring two turtles, or two young pigeons; the one for the burnt offering, and the other for a sin offering: and the priest shall make an atonement for her, and she shall be clean.
text.NABRE: If, however, she cannot afford a lamb, she may take two turtledoves or two pigeons, the one for a burnt offering and the other for a purification offering. The priest shall make atonement for her, and thus she will again be clean.
text.DRB: And if her hand find not, neither is able to offer a lamb, she shall take two turtles, or two young pigeons, one for an holocaust, & another for sin, and the Priest shall pray for her, and so she shall be cleansed.

text.NRSV: The Lord spoke to Moses and Aaron, saying:
text.Rotherham: And Yahweh spake unto Moses and unto Aaron, saying—
text.KJV: And the Lord spake unto Moses and Aaron, saying,
text.NABRE: The Lord said to Moses and Aaron:
text.DRB: And our Lord spake to Moyses and Aaron, saying:

text.NRSV: When a person has on the skin of his body a swelling or an eruption or a spot, and it turns into a leprous disease on the skin of his body, he shall be brought to Aaron the priest or to one of his sons the priests.
text.Rotherham: When, any man, shall have—in the skin of his flesh—a rising, or a scab, or a bright spot, and it shall become in the skin of his flesh the plague-spot of leprosy, then shall he be brought in unto Aaron the priest, or unto one of his sons the priests;
text.KJV: When a man shall have in the skin of his flesh a rising, a scab, or bright spot, and it be in the skin of his flesh like the plague of leprosy; then he shall be brought unto Aaron the priest, or unto one of his sons the priests:
text.NABRE: When someone has on the skin a mark, lesion, or blotch which appears to develop into a scaly infection, the person shall be brought to Aaron, the priest, or to one of the priests among his sons.
text.DRB: The man, in whose skin and flesh shall arise a diverse colour or a blister, or any thing as it were shining, that is to say the plague of the leprosy, shall be brought to Aaron the Priest, or any one of his sons.

text.NRSV: The priest shall examine the disease on the skin of his body, and if the hair in the diseased area has turned white and the disease appears to be deeper than the skin of his body, it is a leprous disease; after the priest has examined him he shall pronounce him ceremonially unclean.
text.Rotherham: and the priest shall view the spot in the skin of his flesh—if, the hair in the plague, have turned white and the appearance of the spot be deeper than the skin of his flesh, the plague-spot of leprosy, it is,—so the priest shall view him and pronounce him unclean.
text.KJV: And the priest shall look on the plague in the skin of the flesh: and when the hair in the plague is turned white, and the plague in sight be deeper than the skin of his flesh, it is a plague of leprosy: and the priest shall look on him, and pronounce him unclean.
text.NABRE: If the priest, upon examination of the skin’s infection, finds that the hair on the infection has turned white and the infection itself appears to be deeper than the skin, it is indeed a scaly infection; the priest, on seeing this, shall declare the person unclean.
text.DRB: Who seeing the leprosy in his skin, & the hair turned into a white colour, and the form it-self of the leprosy lower than the skin, and the other flesh: it is the plague of the leprosy, and at his arbitrement he shall be separated.

text.NRSV: But if the spot is white in the skin of his body, and appears no deeper than the skin, and the hair in it has not turned white, the priest shall confine the diseased person for seven days.
text.Rotherham: But, if the bright spot, though white in the skin of his flesh, is not deeper in appearance than the skin, and, the hair, hath not turned white, then shall the priest shut up the plagued one, seven days.
text.KJV: If the bright spot be white in the skin of his flesh, and in sight be not deeper than the skin, and the hair thereof be not turned white; then the priest shall shut up him that hath the plague seven days:
text.NABRE: If, however, the blotch on the skin is white, but does not seem to be deeper than the skin, nor has the hair turned white, the priest shall quarantine the afflicted person for seven days.
text.DRB: But if there be a shining whiteness in the skin, and not lower than the other flesh, and the hair of the old colour, the Priest shall shut him up seven days,

text.NRSV: The priest shall examine him on the seventh day, and if in his eyes the disease is checked and the disease has not spread in the skin, the priest shall confine him seven days more.
text.Rotherham: And the priest shall view him on the seventh day, and lo! if the spot hath stayed to his sight, and the spot hath not spread in the skin, then shall the priest shut him up seven days more,
text.KJV: And the priest shall look on him the seventh day: and, behold, if the plague in his sight be at a stay, and the plague spread not in the skin; then the priest shall shut him up seven days more:
text.NABRE: Should the priest, upon examination on the seventh day, find that the infection has remained unchanged in color and has not spread on the skin, the priest shall quarantine the person for another seven days.
text.DRB: and the seventh day he shall view him: and if the leprosy be grown no farther, nor hath passed the former limits in the skin, again he shall shut him up other seven days.

text.NRSV: The priest shall examine him again on the seventh day, and if the disease has abated and the disease has not spread in the skin, the priest shall pronounce him clean; it is only an eruption; and he shall wash his clothes, and be clean.
text.Rotherham: Then shall the priest view him on the seventh day, a second time, and lo! if the spot is, faint, and the spot hath not spread in the skin, then shall the priest pronounce him clean—it is, a scab, and he shall wash his clothes, and be clean.
text.KJV: And the priest shall look on him again the seventh day: and, behold, if the plague be somewhat dark, and the plague spread not in the skin, the priest shall pronounce him clean: it is but a scab: and he shall wash his clothes, and be clean.
text.NABRE: Should the priest, upon examination again on the seventh day, find that the infection is now faded and has not spread on the skin, the priest shall declare the person clean; it was merely a scab. The person shall wash his garments and so become clean.
text.DRB: And the seventh day he shall behold: if the leprosy be somewhat obscure, and not grown in the skin, he shall cleanse him, because it is a scab: and the man shall wash his cloths, and shall be clean.

text.NRSV: But if the eruption spreads in the skin after he has shown himself to the priest for his cleansing, he shall appear again before the priest.
text.Rotherham: But if the scab shall have, verily spread, in the skin, since he was shown to the priest that he might be cleansed, then shall he shew himself again unto the priest;
text.KJV: But if the scab spread much abroad in the skin, after that he hath been seen of the priest for his cleansing, he shall be seen of the priest again:
text.NABRE: But if, after the person was examined by the priest and declared clean, the scab spreads at all on the skin, the person shall once more be examined by the priest.
text.DRB: And if the leprosy grow again, after that he was seen of the Priest and restored to cleanness, he shall be brought unto him,

text.NRSV: The priest shall make an examination, and if the eruption has spread in the skin, the priest shall pronounce him unclean; it is a leprous disease.
text.Rotherham: and the priest shall take a view, and lo! if the scab hath spread in the skin, then shall the priest pronounce him unclean—leprosy, it is.
text.KJV: And if the priest see that, behold, the scab spreadeth in the skin, then the priest shall pronounce him unclean: it is a leprosy.
text.NABRE: Should the priest, upon examination, find that the scab has indeed spread on the skin, he shall declare the person unclean; it is a scaly infection.
text.DRB: and shall be condemned of uncleanness.

text.NRSV: When a person has a leprous disease, he shall be brought to the priest.
text.Rotherham: When, the plague of leprosy, cometh to be in any human being, then shall he be brought in unto the priest;
text.KJV: When the plague of leprosy is in a man, then he shall be brought unto the priest;
text.NABRE: When someone is afflicted with a scaly infection, that person shall be brought to the priest.
text.DRB: If the plague of the leprosy be in a man, he shall be brought to the Priest,

text.NRSV: The priest shall make an examination, and if there is a white swelling in the skin that has turned the hair white, and there is quick raw flesh in the swelling,
text.Rotherham: and the priest shall take a view, and lo! if there is a white rising in the skin and, the same, hath turned the hair white,—and there be a wound of raw flesh in the rising,
text.KJV: And the priest shall see him: and, behold, if the rising be white in the skin, and it have turned the hair white, and there be quick raw flesh in the rising;
text.NABRE: Should the priest, upon examination, find that there is a white mark on the skin which has turned the hair white and that there is raw flesh in it,
text.DRB: and he shall view him. And when there is a white colour in the skin, and hath changed the look of the hair, and the flesh also it-self appear quick:

text.NRSV: it is a chronic leprous disease in the skin of his body. The priest shall pronounce him unclean; he shall not confine him, for he is unclean.
text.Rotherham: an old leprosy, it is in the skin of his flesh, and the priest shall pronounce him unclean,—he shall not shut him up, for unclean, he is.
text.KJV: It is an old leprosy in the skin of his flesh, and the priest shall pronounce him unclean, and shall not shut him up: for he is unclean.
text.NABRE: it is a chronic scaly infection on the skin. The priest shall declare the person unclean without quarantine, since the individual is certainly unclean.
text.DRB: it shall be judged a very old leprosy, and grown into the skin. The Priest therefore shall contaminate him, and shall not shut him up, because he is unclean evidently.

text.NRSV: But if the disease breaks out in the skin, so that it covers all the skin of the diseased person from head to foot, so far as the priest can see,
text.Rotherham: But, if the leprosy, cometh quite out, in the skin, and the leprosy covereth all the skin of him that, is plagued, from his head even unto his feet,—so far as appeareth to the eyes of the priest,
text.KJV: And if a leprosy break out abroad in the skin, and the leprosy cover all the skin of him that hath the plague from his head even to his foot, wheresoever the priest looketh;
text.NABRE: If the scaly infection breaks out on the skin and, as far as the priest can see, covers all the skin of the afflicted person from head to foot,
text.DRB: But if the leprosy spring forth running about in the skin, and cover all the flesh from the head to the feet, whatsoever falleth under the sight of the eyes,

text.NRSV: the priest shall make an examination, and if the disease has covered all his body, he shall pronounce him clean of the disease; since it has all turned white, he is clean.
text.Rotherham: then shall the priest takes view and lo! if the leprosy hath covered all his flesh, then shall he pronounce clean him that was plagued,—all of it, hath turned white, clean, he is.
text.KJV: Then the priest shall consider: and, behold, if the leprosy have covered all his flesh, he shall pronounce him clean that hath the plague: it is all turned white: he is clean.
text.NABRE: should the priest then, upon examination, find that the scaly infection does cover the whole body, he shall declare the afflicted person clean; since the person has turned completely white; that individual is clean.
text.DRB: the Priest shall view him, and shall judge that he is taken with a most clean leprosy: for that it is all turned into whiteness, and therefore the man shall be clean.

text.NRSV: But if raw flesh ever appears on him, he shall be unclean;
text.Rotherham: But, the very day there appeareth in him raw flesh, he shall be unclean;
text.KJV: But when raw flesh appeareth in him, he shall be unclean.
text.NABRE: But as soon as raw flesh appears, the individual is unclean;
text.DRB: But when there shall appear in him quick flesh,

text.NRSV: the priest shall examine the raw flesh and pronounce him unclean. Raw flesh is unclean, for it is a leprous disease.
text.Rotherham: so then the priest shall view the raw flesh, and pronounce him unclean,—as for the raw flesh, unclean, it is, leprosy, it is.
text.KJV: And the priest shall see the raw flesh, and pronounce him to be unclean: for the raw flesh is unclean: it is a leprosy.
text.NABRE: on observing the raw flesh, the priest shall declare the person unclean, because raw flesh is unclean; it is a scaly infection.
text.DRB: then by the judgment of the Priest he shall be polluted, and shall be reputed among the unclean, for quick flesh if it be spotted with leprosy, is unclean.

text.NRSV: But if the raw flesh again turns white, he shall come to the priest;
text.Rotherham: Or, if the raw flesh turn again and be changed to white, then shall he come in unto the priest;
text.KJV: Or if the raw flesh turn again, and be changed unto white, he shall come unto the priest;
text.NABRE: If, however, the raw flesh again turns white, the person shall return to the priest;
text.DRB: And if again it be turned into whiteness, and cover all the man,

text.NRSV: the priest shall examine him, and if the disease has turned white, the priest shall pronounce the diseased person clean. He is clean.
text.Rotherham: and the priest shall view him, and lo! if the spot hath changed to white, then shall the priest pronounce clean him that was plagued—clean, he is.
text.KJV: And the priest shall see him: and, behold, if the plague be turned into white; then the priest shall pronounce him clean that hath the plague: he is clean.
text.NABRE: should the latter, upon examination, find that the infection has indeed turned white, he shall declare the afflicted person clean; the individual is clean.
text.DRB: the Priest shall view him, and shall judge him to be clean.

text.NRSV: When a boil appears on the skin of one’s body and it heals,
text.Rotherham: And, when, any one’s flesh, hath, in the skin thereof, a boil,—and then it is healed;
text.KJV: The flesh also, in which, even in the skin thereof, was a boil, and is healed,
text.NABRE: If a boil appeared on a person’s skin which later healed,
text.DRB: And the flesh and the skin wherein a boil is risen, an healed,

text.NRSV: and in the place of the boil there appears a white swelling or a reddish-white spot, it shall be shown to the priest.
text.Rotherham: but in the place of the boil, is a white rising, or a bright spot, reddish white, then shall it be shown unto the priest.
text.KJV: And in the place of the boil there be a white rising, or a bright spot, white, and somewhat reddish, and it be shewed to the priest;
text.NABRE: should now in the place of the boil a white mark or a reddish white blotch develop, the person shall be examined by the priest.
text.DRB: and in the place of the boil there appear a white scar, or somewhat red, the man shall be brought to the Priest:

text.NRSV: The priest shall make an examination, and if it appears deeper than the skin and its hair has turned white, the priest shall pronounce him unclean; this is a leprous disease, broken out in the boil.
text.Rotherham: And the priest shall take a view and lo! if the appearance thereof, is lower than the skin, and, the hair thereof, hath turned white, then shall the priest pronounce him unclean—the plague-spot of leprosy, it, is, in the boil, broken out.
text.KJV: And if, when the priest seeth it, behold, it be in sight lower than the skin, and the hair thereof be turned white; the priest shall pronounce him unclean: it is a plague of leprosy broken out of the boil.
text.NABRE: If the latter, upon examination, finds that it is deeper than the skin and that the hair has turned white, he shall declare the person unclean; it is a scaly infection that has broken out in the boil.
text.DRB: and when he shall see the place of the leprosy lower than the other flesh; and the hair turned into whiteness, he shall contaminate him: for the plague of leprosy is risen in the boil.

text.NRSV: But if the priest examines it and the hair on it is not white, nor is it deeper than the skin but has abated, the priest shall confine him seven days.
text.Rotherham: But, if the priest shall view it and lo! there is no white hair therein, and it is not deeper than the skin, and, in itself, is faint, then shall the priest shut him up seven days;
text.KJV: But if the priest look on it, and, behold, there be no white hairs therein, and if it be not lower than the skin, but be somewhat dark; then the priest shall shut him up seven days:
text.NABRE: But if the priest, upon examination, finds that there is no white hair in it and that it is not deeper than the skin and is faded, the priest shall quarantine the person for seven days.
text.DRB: But if the hair be of the old colour, and the scar somewhat obscure, & be not lower than next flesh, he shall shut him up seven days.

text.NRSV: If it spreads in the skin, the priest shall pronounce him unclean; it is diseased.
text.Rotherham: and, if it, clearly spreadeth, in the skin, then shall the priest pronounce him unclean—a plague-spot, it is.
text.KJV: And if it spread much abroad in the skin, then the priest shall pronounce him unclean: it is a plague.
text.NABRE: If it has then spread on the skin, the priest shall declare the person unclean; it is an infection.
text.DRB: And if it be grown farther; he shall judge him to have the leprosy:

text.NRSV: But if the spot remains in one place and does not spread, it is the scar of the boil; the priest shall pronounce him clean.
text.Rotherham: But if in its place the bright spot stayeth hath not spread, a boil, it is,—and the priest shall pronounce him clean.
text.KJV: But if the bright spot stay in his place, and spread not, it is a burning boil; and the priest shall pronounce him clean.
text.NABRE: But if the blotch remains the same without spreading, it is merely the scar of the boil; the priest shall therefore declare the person clean.
text.DRB: but if it stay in his place, it is the scar of a boil, and the man shall be clean.

text.NRSV: Or, when the body has a burn on the skin and the raw flesh of the burn becomes a spot, reddish-white or white,
text.Rotherham: Or, when, any one’s flesh, hath in the skin thereof a fiery burning,—and the burning wound becometh a bright spot reddish white, or white,
text.KJV: Or if there be any flesh, in the skin whereof there is a hot burning, and the quick flesh that burneth have a white bright spot, somewhat reddish, or white;
text.NABRE: If there was a burn on a person’s skin, and the burned area now becomes a reddish white or a white blotch,
text.DRB: And the flesh and skin, that fire hath burnt, and being healed hath a white or a red scar,

text.NRSV: the priest shall examine it. If the hair in the spot has turned white and it appears deeper than the skin, it is a leprous disease; it has broken out in the burn, and the priest shall pronounce him unclean. This is a leprous disease.
text.Rotherham: then shall the priest view it—and lo! if the hair is turned white in the bright spot and the appearance thereof is deeper than the skin, leprosy, it is, broken out, in the burning,—so the priest shall pronounce him unclean, the plague-spot of leprosy, it is.
text.KJV: Then the priest shall look upon it: and, behold, if the hair in the bright spot be turned white, and it be in sight deeper than the skin; it is a leprosy broken out of the burning: wherefore the priest shall pronounce him unclean: it is the plague of leprosy.
text.NABRE: when the priest, upon examination, finds that the hair has turned white in the blotch and this seems to be deeper than the skin, it is a scaly infection that has broken out in the burn; the priest shall therefore declare the person unclean; it is a scaly infection.
text.DRB: the Priest shall consider it, and lo it is turned into whiteness, and the place thereof is lower than the other skin: he shall contaminate him, because the plague of leprosy is risen in the scar.

text.NRSV: But if the priest examines it and the hair on the spot is not white, and it is no deeper than the skin but has abated, the priest shall confine him seven days.
text.Rotherham: But, if the priest shall view it and lo! there is not, in the bright spot white hair, and it is not deeper than the skin but, itself, is faint, then shall the priest shut him up seven days;
text.KJV: But if the priest look on it, and, behold, there be no white hair in the bright spot, and it be no lower than the other skin, but be somewhat dark; then the priest shall shut him up seven days:
text.NABRE: But if the priest, upon examination, finds that there is no white hair in the blotch and that this is not deeper than the skin and is faded, the priest shall quarantine the person for seven days.
text.DRB: But if the colour of the hair be not changed, nor the blemish lower than the other flesh, and the form it-self of the leprosy be somewhat obscure, he shall shut him up seven days,

text.NRSV: The priest shall examine him the seventh day; if it is spreading in the skin, the priest shall pronounce him unclean. This is a leprous disease.
text.Rotherham: and the priest shall view him on the seventh day,—if it, hath plainly spread in the skin, then shall the priest pronounce him unclean, the plague-spot of leprosy, it is.
text.KJV: And the priest shall look upon him the seventh day: and if it be spread much abroad in the skin, then the priest shall pronounce him unclean: it is the plague of leprosy.
text.NABRE: Should the priest, upon examination on the seventh day, find that it has spread at all on the skin, he shall declare the person unclean; it is a scaly infection.
text.DRB: & the seventh day he shall behold him: if the leprosy be grown farther in the skin, he shall contaminate him,

text.NRSV: But if the spot remains in one place and does not spread in the skin but has abated, it is a swelling from the burn, and the priest shall pronounce him clean; for it is the scar of the burn.
text.Rotherham: But, if, in its place the bright spot hath stayed, and hath not spread in the skin, but, itself, is faint, the rising of a burning, it is,—and the priest shall pronounce him clean, for only the inflaming of the burning, it is.
text.KJV: And if the bright spot stay in his place, and spread not in the skin, but it be somewhat dark; it is a rising of the burning, and the priest shall pronounce him clean: for it is an inflammation of the burning.
text.NABRE: But if the blotch remains the same without spreading on the skin and is faded, it is merely the spot of the burn; the priest shall therefore declare the person clean, since it is only the scar of the burn.
text.DRB: but if the whiteness stay in his place, not very clear, it is the plague of a burning, and therefore he shall be cleansed, because it is the scar of a burning.

text.NRSV: When a man or woman has a disease on the head or in the beard,
text.Rotherham: And, when there cometh to be in, any man or woman, a spot,—in the head or in the beard,
text.KJV: If a man or woman have a plague upon the head or the beard;
text.NABRE: When a man or a woman has an infection on the head or in the beard,
text.DRB: Man, or woman, in whose head or beard the leprosy riseth, the Priest shall see them.

text.NRSV: the priest shall examine the disease. If it appears deeper than the skin and the hair in it is yellow and thin, the priest shall pronounce him unclean; it is a scall, a leprous disease of the head or the beard.
text.Rotherham: then shall the priest view the spot and lo! if, the appearance thereof, is deeper than the skin, and, therein, is yellow, thin hair, then shall the priest pronounce him unclean a scall, it is, a leprosy of the head or of the beard, it is.
text.KJV: Then the priest shall see the plague: and, behold, if it be in sight deeper than the skin; and there be in it a yellow thin hair; then the priest shall pronounce him unclean: it is a dry scall, even a leprosy upon the head or beard.
text.NABRE: should the priest, upon examination, find that the infection appears to be deeper than the skin and that there is fine yellow hair in it, the priest shall declare the person unclean; it is a scall. It is a scaly infection of the head or beard.
text.DRB: and if the place be lower than the other flesh, and the hair yellow, and thinner than it was wont; he shall contaminate them, because it is the leprosy of the head and the beard.

text.NRSV: But if the priest examines the scaly disease, and it does not appear deeper than the skin and there is no black hair in it, the priest shall confine the person with the scaly disease for seven days.
text.Rotherham: But when the priest vieweth the spot, and lo! there is, no appearance, of it deeper than the skin, and, no dark hair, is therein, then shall the priest shut up him that hath the plague-spot of scall, seven days;
text.KJV: And if the priest look on the plague of the scall, and, behold, it be not in sight deeper than the skin, and that there is no black hair in it; then the priest shall shut up him that hath the plague of the scall seven days:
text.NABRE: But if the priest, upon examining the scall infection, finds that it does not appear to be deeper than the skin, though the hair in it may not be black, the priest shall quarantine the scall-stricken person for seven days.
text.DRB: But if he perceive the place of the spot equal with the flesh near unto it, and the hair black: he shall shut him up seven days,

text.NRSV: On the seventh day the priest shall examine the itch; if the scall has not spread, and there is no yellow hair in it, and the scall does not appear deeper than the skin,
text.Rotherham: and the priest shall view the spot on the seventh day, and lo! if the scall hath not spread, and there hath not come to be therein yellow hair,—and, the appearance of the scall, is not deeper than the skin,
text.KJV: And in the seventh day the priest shall look on the plague: and, behold, if the scall spread not, and there be in it no yellow hair, and the scall be not in sight deeper than the skin;
text.NABRE: Should the priest, upon examining the infection on the seventh day find that the scall has not spread and has no yellow hair in it and does not seem to be deeper than the skin,
text.DRB: and in the seventh day he shall look upon it. If the spot be not grown, and the hair be of his own colour, and the place of the plague even with the other flesh:

text.NRSV: he shall shave, but the scall he shall not shave. The priest shall confine the person with the scall for seven days more.
text.Rotherham: then shall he shave himself, but the scall, shall he not shave, and the priest shall shut up him who hath the scall seven days, more;
text.KJV: He shall be shaven, but the scall shall he not shave; and the priest shall shut up him that hath the scall seven days more:
text.NABRE: the person shall shave, but not the scall spot. Then the priest shall quarantine the scall-diseased person for another seven days.
text.DRB: the man shall be shaven saving the place of the spot, and shall be shut up other seven days.

text.NRSV: On the seventh day the priest shall examine the scall; if the scall has not spread in the skin and it appears no deeper than the skin, the priest shall pronounce him clean; he shall wash his clothes, and be clean.
text.Rotherham: then shall the priest view the scall, on the seventh day, and lo! if the scall hath not spread in the skin, and, the appearance thereof, is not deeper than the skin, then shall the priest pronounce him clean, and he shall wash his clothes, and be clean.
text.KJV: And in the seventh day the priest shall look on the scall: and, behold, if the scall be not spread in the skin, nor be in sight deeper than the skin; then the priest shall pronounce him clean: and he shall wash his clothes, and be clean.
text.NABRE: If the priest, upon examining the scall on the seventh day, finds that it has not spread on the skin and that it does not appear to be deeper than the skin, he shall declare the person clean; the latter shall wash his garments, and will thus be clean.
text.DRB: If the seventh day the plague seem to have stayed in his place, & not lower than the other flesh, he shall cleanse him, and his cloths being washed he shall be clean.

text.NRSV: But if the scall spreads in the skin after he has been pronounced clean,
text.Rotherham: But, if the scall do indeed spread in the skin,—after he hath been pronounced clean,
text.KJV: But if the scall spread much in the skin after his cleansing;
text.NABRE: But if the scall spreads at all on the skin after the person has been declared clean—
text.DRB: But if after his cleansing the spot be grown again in the skin,

text.NRSV: the priest shall examine him. If the scall has spread in the skin, the priest need not seek for yellow hair; he is unclean.
text.Rotherham: then shall the priest take a view, and lo! if the scall hath spread in the skin, the priest shall not search for the yellow hair—unclean, he is.
text.KJV: Then the priest shall look on him: and, behold, if the scall be spread in the skin, the priest shall not seek for yellow hair; he is unclean.
text.NABRE: should the priest, upon examination, find that the scall has indeed spread on the skin, he need not look for yellow hair; the individual is unclean.
text.DRB: he shall no more seek whether the hair be changed into a yellow colour, because he is evidently unclean.

text.NRSV: If in his eyes the scall is unchanged and black hair has grown in it, the scall is healed; he is clean; and the priest shall pronounce him clean.
text.Rotherham: But, if in his eyes, the scall is at a stay, and dark hair hath grown up therein, the scall is healed, clean, he is,—and the priest shall pronounce him clean.
text.KJV: But if the scall be in his sight at a stay, and that there is black hair grown up therein; the scall is healed, he is clean: and the priest shall pronounce him clean.
text.NABRE: If, however, the scall has remained unchanged in color and black hair has grown in it, the disease has been healed; the person is clean, and the priest shall declare the individual clean.
text.DRB: Moreover if the spot be stayed, & the hair be black, let him know that the man is healed, and let him boldly pronounce him clean.

text.NRSV: When a man or a woman has spots on the skin of the body, white spots,
text.Rotherham: And when either, man or woman, hath in the skin of their flesh bright spots,—bright spots that are white,
text.KJV: If a man also or a woman have in the skin of their flesh bright spots, even white bright spots;
text.NABRE: When the skin of a man or a woman is spotted with several white blotches,
text.DRB: Man, or woman, in whose skin appeareth whiteness,

text.NRSV: the priest shall make an examination, and if the spots on the skin of the body are of a dull white, it is a rash that has broken out on the skin; he is clean.
text.Rotherham: then shall the priest take a view and lo! if, in the skin of their flesh, are bright spots that are dull white, dead white spot, it is, that hath broken through in the skin—clean, he is.
text.KJV: Then the priest shall look: and, behold, if the bright spots in the skin of their flesh be darkish white; it is a freckled spot that groweth in the skin; he is clean.
text.NABRE: if the priest, upon examination, finds that the blotches on the skin are pale white, it is only tetter that has broken out on the skin, and the person therefore is clean.
text.DRB: the Priest shall view them. If he find that whiteness somewhat obscure shineth in the skin, let him know that it is not the leprosy, but a spot of white colour and that the man is clean.

text.NRSV: If anyone loses the hair from his head, he is bald but he is clean.
text.Rotherham: And, when, any man’s, head loseth its hair, though, bald, he is, clean.
text.KJV: And the man whose hair is fallen off his head, he is bald; yet is he clean.
text.NABRE: When a man loses the hair of his head, he is simply bald on the crown and not unclean.
text.DRB: The man whose hair falleth off from his head, is bald and clean:

text.NRSV: If he loses the hair from his forehead and temples, he has baldness of the forehead but he is clean.
text.Rotherham: And if, in front, his head loseth its hair, though bald in the forehead, he is, clean.
text.KJV: And he that hath his hair fallen off from the part of his head toward his face, he is forehead bald: yet is he clean.
text.NABRE: So too, if he loses the hair on the front of his head, he is simply bald on the forehead and not unclean.
text.DRB: and if the hair fall from his forehead, he is bald before and clean.

text.NRSV: But if there is on the bald head or the bald forehead a reddish-white diseased spot, it is a leprous disease breaking out on his bald head or his bald forehead.
text.Rotherham: But, should there be, in the baldness behind, or in the baldness in front, a spot that is reddish white, leprosy broken out, it is, in his baldness behind, or in his baldness in front.
text.KJV: And if there be in the bald head, or bald forehead, a white reddish sore; it is a leprosy sprung up in his bald head, or his bald forehead.
text.NABRE: But when there is a reddish white infection on his bald crown or bald forehead, it is a scaly infection that is breaking out there.
text.DRB: But if in the baldness or in the baldness before there be risen a white or reddish colour,

text.NRSV: The priest shall examine him; if the diseased swelling is reddish-white on his bald head or on his bald forehead, which resembles a leprous disease in the skin of the body,
text.Rotherham: So the priest shall view it, and lo! if, the rising-spot, be reddish white in his baldness behind or in his baldness in front,—like the appearance of leprosy in the skin of the flesh,
text.KJV: Then the priest shall look upon it: and, behold, if the rising of the sore be white reddish in his bald head, or in his bald forehead, as the leprosy appeareth in the skin of the flesh;
text.NABRE: If the priest, upon examination, finds that the infection spot on the bald area on the crown or forehead has the same reddish white appearance as that of a scaly infection of the skin,
text.DRB: and the Priest perceive this, he shall condemn him undoubtedly of leprosy, which is risen in the baldness.

text.NRSV: he is leprous, he is unclean. The priest shall pronounce him unclean; the disease is on his head.
text.Rotherham: a leprous man, is he, unclean, he is,—unclean, shall the priest pronounce him in his head, is his plague.
text.KJV: He is a leprous man, he is unclean: the priest shall pronounce him utterly unclean; his plague is in his head.
text.NABRE: the man has a scaly infection and is unclean. The priest shall declare him unclean; his infection is on his head.
text.DRB: Whosoever therefore shall be defiled with leprosy & is separated at the arbitrement of the Priest,

text.NRSV: The person who has the leprous disease shall wear torn clothes and let the hair of his head be disheveled; and he shall cover his upper lip and cry out, “Unclean, unclean.”
text.Rotherham: Now, as for the leper in whom is the plague, His clothes, shall be rent, And, his head, shall be bare, And, his beard, shall he cover,—And, Unclean! Unclean! shall he cry.
text.KJV: And the leper in whom the plague is, his clothes shall be rent, and his head bare, and he shall put a covering upon his upper lip, and shall cry, Unclean, unclean.
text.NABRE: The garments of one afflicted with a scaly infection shall be rent and the hair disheveled, and the mustache covered. The individual shall cry out, “Unclean, unclean!”
text.DRB: shall have his cloths hanging loose, his head bare, his mouth covered with a cloth, he shall cry himself polluted and unclean.

text.NRSV: He shall remain unclean as long as he has the disease; he is unclean. He shall live alone; his dwelling shall be outside the camp.
text.Rotherham: All the days that the plague is in him, shall he continue unclean, Unclean, he is,—Alone, shall he remain, Outside the camp, shall be his dwelling.
text.KJV: All the days wherein the plague shall be in him he shall be defiled; he is unclean: he shall dwell alone; without the camp shall his habitation be.
text.NABRE: As long as the infection is present, the person shall be unclean. Being unclean, that individual shall dwell apart, taking up residence outside the camp.
text.DRB: All the time that he is a leper & unclean, he shall dwell alone without the camp.

text.NRSV: When a garment has a mark of leprosy in it, whether it is a woolen or a linen garment,
text.Rotherham: And, when, in a garment, there is a plague-spot of leprosy,—whether in a garment of wool, or a garment of flax;
text.KJV: The garment also that the plague of leprosy is in, whether it be a woollen garment, or a linen garment;
text.NABRE: Fungal Infection of Fabrics and Leather. When a fungal infection is on a garment of wool or of linen,
text.DRB: Awoollen or linen garment, that shall have the leprosy

text.NRSV: in warp or woof of linen or wool, or in skin or in anything made of skin,
text.Rotherham: either in warp or in weft, made with flax, or with wool,—or in a skin, or in anything wrought of skin;
text.KJV: Whether it be in the warp, or woof; of linen, or of woollen; whether in a skin, or in any thing made of skin;
text.NABRE: or on the warp and woof of linen or wool, or on a hide or anything made of leather,
text.DRB: in the warp, and the woof, or else a skin, or whatsoever is made of a skin,

text.NRSV: if the disease shows greenish or reddish in the garment, whether in warp or woof or in skin or in anything made of skin, it is a leprous disease and shall be shown to the priest.
text.Rotherham: and the spot cometh to be of a greenish yellow or reddish, in the garment or in the skin, whether in warp or in weft, or in any utensil of skin, the plague-spot of leprosy, it is,—and shall be shown unto the priest;
text.KJV: And if the plague be greenish or reddish in the garment, or in the skin, either in the warp, or in the woof, or in any thing of skin; it is a plague of leprosy, and shall be shewed unto the priest:
text.NABRE: if the infection on the garment or hide, or on the warp or woof, or on any leather article is greenish or reddish, the thing is indeed a fungal infection and must be examined by the priest.
text.DRB: if it be a white or reddish spot, it shall be reputed infected with leprosy, and shall be shewed to the Priest.

text.NRSV: The priest shall examine the disease, and isolate the disease seven days.
text.Rotherham: and the priest shall view the spot,—and shall shut up him that is plagued seven days;
text.KJV: And the priest shall look upon the plague, and shut up it that hath the plague seven days:
text.NABRE: Having examined the infection, the priest shall quarantine the infected article for seven days.
text.DRB: Who having considered it shall shut it up seven days:

text.NRSV: If the disease has spread in the garment, in warp or woof, or in the skin, whatever be the use of the skin, this is a spreading leprous disease; it is unclean.
text.Rotherham: then shall he view the spot, on the seventh day if the spot hath spread in the garment whether in warp or in weft, or in the skin, or anything which may be made of skin for service, the spot is a fretting leprosy, unclean, it is.
text.KJV: And he shall look on the plague on the seventh day: if the plague be spread in the garment, either in the warp, or in the woof, or in a skin, or in any work that is made of skin; the plague is a fretting leprosy; it is unclean.
text.NABRE: If the priest, upon inspecting the infection on the seventh day, finds that it has spread on the garment, or on the warp or woof, or on the leather, whatever be its use, the infection is a harmful fungus; the article is unclean.
text.DRB: and the seventh day again beholding it, if he find that it is grown, it is a leprosy that continueth: he shall judge the garment polluted, and every thing wherein it shall be found,

text.NRSV: He shall burn the garment, whether diseased in warp or woof, woolen or linen, or anything of skin, for it is a spreading leprous disease; it shall be burned in fire.
text.Rotherham: Then shall he burn up the garment whether it be in the warp or the weft in wool or in flax, or any utensil of skin, wherein shall be the plague-spot,—for, a fretting leprosy, it is, in fire, shall it be burnt up.
text.KJV: He shall therefore burn that garment, whether warp or woof, in woollen or in linen, or any thing of skin, wherein the plague is: for it is a fretting leprosy; it shall be burnt in the fire.
text.NABRE: He shall therefore burn up the garment, or the warp or woof, be it of wool or linen, or any leather article which is infected; since it is a harmful fungus, it must be destroyed by fire.
text.DRB: and therefore it shall be burnt with fire.

text.NRSV: If the priest makes an examination, and the disease has not spread in the garment, in warp or woof or in anything of skin,
text.Rotherham: But if the priest shall take a view, and lo! the spot has not spread, in the garment, either in warp or in weft,—or in any utensil of skin,
text.KJV: And if the priest shall look, and, behold, the plague be not spread in the garment, either in the warp, or in the woof, or in any thing of skin;
text.NABRE: But if the priest, upon examination, finds that it has not spread on the garment, or on the warp or woof, or on the leather article,
text.DRB: But if he see that it is not grown,

text.NRSV: the priest shall command that the diseased article be washed, and he shall isolate it seven days more.
text.Rotherham: then shall the priest give command, and they shall wash that wherein is the spot,—and he shall shut it up seven days more;
text.KJV: Then the priest shall command that they wash the thing wherein the plague is, and he shall shut it up seven days more:
text.NABRE: he shall give orders to have the infected article washed and then quarantined for another seven days.
text.DRB: he shall command, and they shall wash that, wherein the leprosy is, and he shall shut it up other seven days.

text.NRSV: Then the priest shall examine the diseased article after it has been washed. If the spot has not changed color, though the disease has not spread, it is unclean; you shall burn it in fire, whether the leprous spot is on the inside or on the outside.
text.Rotherham: then shall the priest take a view after the plagued garment hath been washed and lo! if the spot hath not changed its look, then though the spot hath not spread, yet unclean, it is, in the fire, shalt thou burn it up,—a sunken spot, it is, in the back thereof, or in the front thereof.
text.KJV: And the priest shall look on the plague, after that it is washed: and, behold, if the plague have not changed his colour, and the plague be not spread; it is unclean; thou shalt burn it in the fire; it is fret inward, whether it be bare within or without.
text.NABRE: If the priest, upon examination after the infection was washed, finds that it has not changed its color, even though it may not have spread, the article is unclean. You shall burn it with fire. It is a fray, be it on its inner or outer side.
text.DRB: And when he shall see that the old shape is not returned, neither yet that the leprosy is grown farther, he shall judge it unclean, and burn it with fire, for that the leprosy is spread in the outside of the garment or through the whole.

text.NRSV: If the priest makes an examination, and the disease has abated after it is washed, he shall tear the spot out of the garment, in skin, or in warp or woof.
text.Rotherham: And, if the priest hath taken a view, and lo! the spot is, faint, since it hath been washed, then shall he rend it out of the garment, or out of the skin, whether out of the warp or out of the weft;
text.KJV: And if the priest look, and, behold, the plague be somewhat dark after the washing of it; then he shall rend it out of the garment, or out of the skin, or out of the warp, or out of the woof:
text.NABRE: But if the priest, upon examination, finds that the infection has faded after the washing, he shall cut it out of the garment, or the leather, or the warp or woof.
text.DRB: But if the place of the leprosy be somewhat obscure, after that the garment is washed, he shall break it off, and divide it from that which is found.

text.NRSV: If it appears again in the garment, in warp or woof, or in anything of skin, it is spreading; you shall burn with fire that in which is the disease.
text.Rotherham: and, if it appear still in the garment—either in the warp or in the weft, or in any utensil of skin, a breaking out, it is,—in the fire, shalt thou burn up that wherein is the plague.
text.KJV: And if it appear still in the garment, either in the warp, or in the woof, or in any thing of skin; it is a spreading plague: thou shalt burn that wherein the plague is with fire.
text.NABRE: If, however, the infection again appears on the garment, or on the warp or woof, or on the leather article, it is still virulent and you shall burn the thing infected with fire.
text.DRB: And if besides in those places, that before were without spot, there appear a flying and wandering leprosy: it must be burnt with fire:

text.NRSV: But the garment, warp or woof, or anything of skin, from which the disease disappears when you have washed it, shall then be washed a second time, and it shall be clean.
text.Rotherham: But, as for the garment—whether the warp or the weft or any utensil of skin which thou shalt wash, and the plague shall depart therefrom, then shall it be washed a second time, and shall be clean.
text.KJV: And the garment, either warp, or woof, or whatsoever thing of skin it be, which thou shalt wash, if the plague be departed from them, then it shall be washed the second time, and shall be clean.
text.NABRE: But if, after the washing, the infection has disappeared from the garment, or the warp or woof, or the leather article, the thing shall be washed a second time, and thus it will be clean.
text.DRB: if it be ceased, he shall wash with water the parts that be pure, the second time, and they shall be clean.

text.NRSV: This is the rule for leprous diseases in a garment of wool or linen, either in warp or woof, or in anything of skin, for pronouncing it clean or unclean.
text.Rotherham: This, is the law as to the plague-spot of leprosy, in a garment of wool or of flax, whether in the warp or the weft, or in any utensil of skin,—To pronounce it clean, or to pronounce it unclean.
text.KJV: This is the law of the plague of leprosy in a garment of woollen or linen, either in the warp, or woof, or any thing of skins, to pronounce it clean, or to pronounce it unclean.
text.NABRE: This is the instruction for a fungal infection on a garment of wool or linen, or on a warp or woof, or on any leather article, to determine whether it is clean or unclean.
text.DRB: This is the law of the leprosy of a woollen and linen garment, of the warp and the woof, and of all stuff of skins, how it ought to be cleansed or contaminated.

text.NRSV: The Lord spoke to Moses, saying:
text.Rotherham: And Yahweh spake unto Moses, saying—
text.KJV: And the Lord spake unto Moses, saying,
text.NABRE: The Lord said to Moses:
text.DRB: And our Lord spake to Moyses, saying:

text.NRSV: This shall be the ritual for the leprous person at the time of his cleansing: He shall be brought to the priest;
text.Rotherham: This, shall be the law of the leper, in the day when he is declared clean,—he shall be brought in unto the priest;
text.KJV: This shall be the law of the leper in the day of his cleansing: He shall be brought unto the priest:
text.NABRE: This is the ritual for someone that had a scaly infection at the time of that person’s purification. The individual shall be brought to the priest,
text.DRB: This is the rite of a leper, when he is to be cleansed.

text.NRSV: the priest shall go out of the camp, and the priest shall make an examination. If the disease is healed in the leprous person,
text.Rotherham: then shall the priest go forth unto the outside of the camp,—and the priest shall take a view, and lo! if the plague of leprosy hath been healed out of the leper,;
text.KJV: And the priest shall go forth out of the camp; and the priest shall look, and, behold, if the plague of leprosy be healed in the leper;
text.NABRE: who is to go outside the camp. If the priest, upon inspection, finds that the scaly infection has healed in the afflicted person,
text.DRB: He shall be brought to the Priest: who going out of the camp, when he shall find that the leprosy is cleansed,

text.NRSV: the priest shall command that two living clean birds and cedarwood and crimson yarn and hyssop be brought for the one who is to be cleansed.
text.Rotherham: then shall the priest give command,—and there shall be taken—for him that is to be cleansed—two living clean birds,—and cedar wood, and crimson am hyssop;
text.KJV: Then shall the priest command to take for him that is to be cleansed two birds alive and clean, and cedar wood, and scarlet, and hyssop:
text.NABRE: he shall order that two live, clean birds, as well as some cedar wood, scarlet yarn, and hyssop be obtained for the one who is to be purified.
text.DRB: he shall command him that is purified, that he offer for himself two live sparrows, which it is lawful to eat, and cedar-wood, and scarlet & hyssop,

text.NRSV: The priest shall command that one of the birds be slaughtered over fresh water in an earthen vessel.
text.Rotherham: and the priest shall give command, and one bird shall be slain,—within an earthen vessel over living water:
text.KJV: And the priest shall command that one of the birds be killed in an earthen vessel over running water:
text.NABRE: The priest shall then order that one of the birds be slaughtered over an earthen vessel with fresh water in it.
text.DRB: and he shall command that one of the sparrows be immolated in an earthen vessel over living water:

text.NRSV: He shall take the living bird with the cedarwood and the crimson yarn and the hyssop, and dip them and the living bird in the blood of the bird that was slaughtered over the fresh water.
text.Rotherham: as for the living bird, he shall take, it, and the cedar wood and the crimson, and the hyssop,—and shall dip them and the living bird, in the blood of the bird that hath been slain over the living water;
text.KJV: As for the living bird, he shall take it, and the cedar wood, and the scarlet, and the hyssop, and shall dip them and the living bird in the blood of the bird that was killed over the running water:
text.NABRE: Taking the living bird with the cedar wood, the scarlet yarn and the hyssop, the priest shall dip them, including the live bird, in the blood of the bird that was slaughtered over the fresh water,
text.DRB: but the other being alive, with the cedar-wood, and scarlet and the hyssop, he shall dip in the blood of the sparrow that is immolated,

text.NRSV: He shall sprinkle it seven times upon the one who is to be cleansed of the leprous disease; then he shall pronounce him clean, and he shall let the living bird go into the open field.
text.Rotherham: and shall sprinkle upon him that is to be cleansed from the leprosy, seven times,—and shall pronounce him clean, and shall let go the living bird over the face of the field.
text.KJV: And he shall sprinkle upon him that is to be cleansed from the leprosy seven times, and shall pronounce him clean, and shall let the living bird loose into the open field.
text.NABRE: and then sprinkle seven times on the person to be purified from the scaly infection. When he has thus purified that person, he shall let the living bird fly away over the countryside.
text.DRB: wherewith he shall sprinkle him, that is to be cleansed, seven times, that he may be rightly purged: and he shall let go the live sparrow, that it fly into the field.

text.NRSV: The one who is to be cleansed shall wash his clothes, and shave off all his hair, and bathe himself in water, and he shall be clean. After that he shall come into the camp, but shall live outside his tent seven days.
text.Rotherham: And he that is to be declared clean shall wash his clothes and shave off all his hair, and bathe in water, and be clean, and, afterwards, shall he come into the camp,—and dwell outside his tent, seven days;
text.KJV: And he that is to be cleansed shall wash his clothes, and shave off all his hair, and wash himself in water, that he may be clean: and after that he shall come into the camp, and shall tarry abroad out of his tent seven days.
text.NABRE: The person being purified shall then wash his garments, shave off all hair, and bathe in water, and so become clean. After this the person may come inside the camp, but shall still remain outside his or her tent for seven days.
text.DRB: And when the man hath washed his cloths, he shall shave all the hair of his body, and shall be washed with water: & being purified he shall enter into the camp, yet so for all that, that he tarry without his own tent seven days,

text.NRSV: On the seventh day he shall shave all his hair: of head, beard, eyebrows; he shall shave all his hair. He shall wash his clothes and bathe his body in water, and he shall be clean.
text.Rotherham: and it shall be on the seventh day, that he shall shave off all the hair of his head, and his beard, and his eyebrows, even all his hair, shall he shave off,—and shall wash his clothes and bathe his flesh in water so shall he be clean.
text.KJV: But it shall be on the seventh day, that he shall shave all his hair off his head and his beard and his eyebrows, even all his hair he shall shave off: and he shall wash his clothes, also he shall wash his flesh in water, and he shall be clean.
text.NABRE: On the seventh day this individual shall again shave off all hair, of the head, beard, and eyebrows—all hair must be shaved—and also wash his garments and bathe the body in water, and so become clean.
text.DRB: & the seventh day he shall shave the hair of his head, & his beard & eye-brows, and the hair of his whole body. And having washed again his cloths, and his body,

text.NRSV: On the eighth day he shall take two male lambs without blemish, and one ewe lamb in its first year without blemish, and a grain offering of three-tenths of an ephah of choice flour mixed with oil, and one log of oil.
text.Rotherham: And, on the eighth day, he shall take two he-lambs, without defect, and one ewe-lamb, the choice of its year without defect,—and three-tenths of fine meal for a meal-offering, overflowed with oil, and one log of oil.
text.KJV: And on the eighth day he shall take two he lambs without blemish, and one ewe lamb of the first year without blemish, and three tenth deals of fine flour for a meat offering, mingled with oil, and one log of oil.
text.NABRE: On the eighth day the individual shall take two unblemished male lambs, one unblemished yearling ewe lamb, three tenths of an ephah of bran flour mixed with oil for a grain offering, and one log of oil.
text.DRB: the eighth day he shall take two lambs without spot, and an ewe of a year old without spot, and three tenths of flour tempered with oil for a sacrifice, and a sextary of oil apart.

text.NRSV: The priest who cleanses shall set the person to be cleansed, along with these things, before the Lord, at the entrance of the tent of meeting.
text.Rotherham: And the priest that is cleansing him shall cause the man that is to be cleansed, and those things to stand before Yahweh, at the opening of the tent of meeting.
text.KJV: And the priest that maketh him clean shall present the man that is to be made clean, and those things, before the Lord, at the door of the tabernacle of the congregation:
text.NABRE: The priest who performs the purification ceremony shall place the person who is being purified, as well as all these offerings, before the Lord at the entrance of the tent of meeting.
text.DRB: And when the Priest that purifieth the man, hath set him and all these things before the Lord in the door of the tabernacle of testimony,

text.NRSV: The priest shall take one of the male lambs, and offer it as a guilt offering, along with the log of oil, and raise them as an elevation offering before the Lord.
text.Rotherham: And the priest shall take one he-lamb, and bring him near as a guilt-bearer and the log of oil,—and shall wave them as a wave-offering, before Yahweh;
text.KJV: And the priest shall take one he lamb, and offer him for a trespass offering, and the log of oil, and wave them for a wave offering before the Lord:
text.NABRE: Taking one of the male lambs, the priest shall present it as a reparation offering, along with the log of oil, raising them as an elevated offering before the Lord.
text.DRB: he shall take a lamb, and offer it for offence, & the sextary of oil, and having offered all before the Lord,

text.NRSV: He shall slaughter the male lamb in the place where the sin offering and the burnt offering are slaughtered in the holy place; for the guilt offering, like the sin offering, belongs to the priest: it is most holy.
text.Rotherham: and shall slay the lamb in the place where the sin-bearer and the ascending-sacrifice are slain, in the holy place,—for like the sin-bearer, the guilt-bearer, is the priest’s, most holy, it is.
text.KJV: And he shall slay the lamb in the place where he shall kill the sin offering and the burnt offering, in the holy place: for as the sin offering is the priest’s, so is the trespass offering: it is most holy:
text.NABRE: This lamb shall be slaughtered in the sacred place where the purification offering and the burnt offering are slaughtered, because the reparation offering is like the purification offering; it belongs to the priest and is most holy.
text.DRB: he shall immolate the lamb, where the host for sin is wont to be immolated, and the holocaust, that is, in a holy place. For as for sin, so also the host for offence pertaineth to the Priest: it is Holy of holies.

text.NRSV: The priest shall take some of the blood of the guilt offering and put it on the lobe of the right ear of the one to be cleansed, and on the thumb of the right hand, and on the big toe of the right foot.
text.Rotherham: Then shall the priest take of the blood of the guilt-bearer, and the priest shall put it upon the tip of the right ear of him that is to be cleansed,—and upon the thumb of his right hand, and upon the great toe of his right foot;
text.KJV: And the priest shall take some of the blood of the trespass offering, and the priest shall put it upon the tip of the right ear of him that is to be cleansed, and upon the thumb of his right hand, and upon the great toe of his right foot:
text.NABRE: Then the priest shall take some of the blood of the reparation offering and put it on the lobe of the right ear, the thumb of the right hand, and the big toe of the right foot of the person being purified.
text.DRB: And the Priest taking of the blood of the host, that was immolated for offence, shall put it upon the tip of the right ear of him that is cleansed, and upon the thumbs of his right hand and foot:

text.NRSV: The priest shall take some of the log of oil and pour it into the palm of his own left hand,
text.Rotherham: and the priest shall take of the log of oil,—and shall pour it upon the palm of the priest’s left hand,
text.KJV: And the priest shall take some of the log of oil, and pour it into the palm of his own left hand:
text.NABRE: The priest shall also take the log of oil and pour some of it into the palm of his own left hand;
text.DRB: and of the sextary of oil he shall pour into his own left hand,

text.NRSV: and dip his right finger in the oil that is in his left hand and sprinkle some oil with his finger seven times before the Lord.
text.Rotherham: and the priest shall dip his right finger [and take] of the oil that is on the palm of his left hand,—and shall sprinkle of the oil with his finger seven times before Yahweh:
text.KJV: And the priest shall dip his right finger in the oil that is in his left hand, and shall sprinkle of the oil with his finger seven times before the Lord:
text.NABRE: then, dipping his right finger in the oil on his left palm, he shall sprinkle some of it with his finger seven times before the Lord.
text.DRB: and shall dip his right finger in it and shall sprinkle before the Lord seven times.

text.NRSV: Some of the oil that remains in his hand the priest shall put on the lobe of the right ear of the one to be cleansed, and on the thumb of the right hand, and on the big toe of the right foot, on top of the blood of the guilt offering.
text.Rotherham: and of the remainder of the oil which is on the palm of his hand, shall the priest put upon the tip of the right ear of him that is to be cleansed, and upon the thumb of his right hand, and upon the great toe of his right foot,—upon the blood of the guilt-bearer;
text.KJV: And of the rest of the oil that is in his hand shall the priest put upon the tip of the right ear of him that is to be cleansed, and upon the thumb of his right hand, and upon the great toe of his right foot, upon the blood of the trespass offering:
text.NABRE: Of the oil left in his hand the priest shall put some on the lobe of the right ear, the thumb of the right hand, and the big toe of the right foot of the person being purified, over the blood of the reparation offering.
text.DRB: and the residue of oil in his left hand, he shall pour upon the tip of the right ear of him that is cleansed, and upon the thumbs of his right hand and foot, and upon the blood that was shed for offence,

text.NRSV: The rest of the oil that is in the priest’s hand he shall put on the head of the one to be cleansed. Then the priest shall make atonement on his behalf before the Lord:
text.Rotherham: and that which remaineth of the oil that is on the palm of the priest’s hand, he shall put upon the head of him that is to be cleansed,—so shall the priest put a propitiatory-covering over him, before Yahweh.
text.KJV: And the remnant of the oil that is in the priest’s hand he shall pour upon the head of him that is to be cleansed: and the priest shall make an atonement for him before the Lord.
text.NABRE: The rest of the oil in his hand the priest shall put on the head of the one being purified. Thus shall the priest make atonement for the individual before the Lord.
text.DRB: and upon his head.

text.NRSV: the priest shall offer the sin offering, to make atonement for the one to be cleansed from his uncleanness. Afterward he shall slaughter the burnt offering;
text.Rotherham: Then shall the priest offer the sin-bearer, and shall put a propitiatory-covering over him that is to be cleansed because of his uncleanness,—and, afterwards, shall he slay the ascending-sacrifice.
text.KJV: And the priest shall offer the sin offering, and make an atonement for him that is to be cleansed from his uncleanness; and afterward he shall kill the burnt offering:
text.NABRE: The priest shall next offer the purification offering, thus making atonement on behalf of the one being purified from the uncleanness. After this the burnt offering shall be slaughtered.
text.DRB: And he shall pray for him before the Lord, and shall make the sacrifice for sin, then shall he immolate the holocaust,

text.NRSV: and the priest shall offer the burnt offering and the grain offering on the altar. Thus the priest shall make atonement on his behalf and he shall be clean.
text.Rotherham: And the priest shall cause the ascending-sacrifice, and the meal-offering to ascend at the altar,—so shall the priest put a propitiatory-covering over him, and he shall be clean.
text.KJV: And the priest shall offer the burnt offering and the meat offering upon the altar: and the priest shall make an atonement for him, and he shall be clean.
text.NABRE: The priest shall offer the burnt offering and the grain offering on the altar before the Lord. Thus shall the priest make atonement for the person, and the individual will become clean.
text.DRB: and put it on the altar with the libaments thereof, and the man shall orderly be cleansed.

text.NRSV: But if he is poor and cannot afford so much, he shall take one male lamb for a guilt offering to be elevated, to make atonement on his behalf, and one-tenth of an ephah of choice flour mixed with oil for a grain offering and a log of oil;
text.Rotherham: But if he be poor and his hand hath not enough, then shall he take one he-lamb as a guilt-bearer to be waved to put a propitiatory-covering over him,—and one-tenth of fine meal, overflowed with oil for a meal-offering, and a log of oil,
text.KJV: And if he be poor, and cannot get so much; then he shall take one lamb for a trespass offering to be waved, to make an atonement for him, and one tenth deal of fine flour mingled with oil for a meat offering, and a log of oil;
text.NABRE: Poor Person’s Sacrifices. If a person is poor and cannot afford so much, that person shall take one male lamb for a reparation offering, to be used as an elevated offering in atonement, one tenth of an ephah of bran flour mixed with oil for a grain offering, a log of oil,
text.DRB: But if he be poor, and his hand cannot find the things aforesaid, for offence he shall take a lamb for an oblation, that the Priest may pray for him, and a tenth part of flour tempered with oil for a sacrifice, and of oil a sextary,

text.NRSV: also two turtledoves or two pigeons, such as he can afford, one for a sin offering and the other for a burnt offering.
text.Rotherham: and two turtle-doves or two young pigeons, for which his hand hath enough,—so shall one be a sin-bearer, and the other an ascending-sacrifice.
text.KJV: And two turtledoves, or two young pigeons, such as he is able to get; and the one shall be a sin offering, and the other a burnt offering.
text.NABRE: and two turtledoves or pigeons, which the individual can more easily afford, the one as a purification offering and the other as a burnt offering.
text.DRB: and two turtles or two young pigeons, of the which let one be for sin, & the other for an holocaust:

text.NRSV: On the eighth day he shall bring them for his cleansing to the priest, to the entrance of the tent of meeting, before the Lord;
text.Rotherham: And he shall bring them in on the eighth day, for his cleansing, unto the priest,—unto the entrance of the tent of meeting, before Yahweh.
text.KJV: And he shall bring them on the eighth day for his cleansing unto the priest, unto the door of the tabernacle of the congregation, before the Lord.
text.NABRE: On the eighth day of purification the person shall bring them to the priest, at the entrance of the tent of meeting before the Lord.
text.DRB: & he shall offer them the eighth day of his purification to the Priest, at the door of the tabernacle of testimony before the Lord:

text.NRSV: and the priest shall take the lamb of the guilt offering and the log of oil, and the priest shall raise them as an elevation offering before the Lord.
text.Rotherham: Then shall the priest take the guilt-bearing lamb and the log of oil,—and the priest shall wave them as a wave-offering, before Yahweh.
text.KJV: And the priest shall take the lamb of the trespass offering, and the log of oil, and the priest shall wave them for a wave offering before the Lord:
text.NABRE: Taking the lamb of the reparation offering, along with the log of oil, the priest shall raise them as an elevated offering before the Lord.
text.DRB: who receiving the lamb for offence, and the sextary of oil, shall elevate them together:

text.NRSV: He shall slaughter the lamb of the guilt offering; and the priest shall take some of the blood of the guilt offering, and put it on the lobe of the right ear of the one to be cleansed, and on the thumb of his right hand, and on the big toe of his right foot.
text.Rotherham: Then shall the guilt-bearing lamb be slain, and the priest shall take of the blood of the guilt-bearer, and put upon the tip of the right ear of him that is to be cleansed,—and upon the thumb of his right hand, and upon the great toe of his right foot;
text.KJV: And he shall kill the lamb of the trespass offering, and the priest shall take some of the blood of the trespass offering, and put it upon the tip of the right ear of him that is to be cleansed, and upon the thumb of his right hand, and upon the great toe of his right foot:
text.NABRE: When the lamb of the reparation offering has been slaughtered, the priest shall take some of its blood, and put it on the lobe of the right ear, on the thumb of the right hand, and on the big toe of the right foot of the person being purified.
text.DRB: and the lamb being immolated, of the blood thereof he shall put upon the tip of the right ear of him that is cleansed, and upon the thumbs of his right hand and foot:

text.NRSV: The priest shall pour some of the oil into the palm of his own left hand,
text.Rotherham: also of the oil, shall the priest pour out,—on the palm of his own left hand;
text.KJV: And the priest shall pour of the oil into the palm of his own left hand:
text.NABRE: The priest shall then pour some of the oil into the palm of his own left hand
text.DRB: but part of the oil he shall pour into his own left hand,

text.NRSV: and shall sprinkle with his right finger some of the oil that is in his left hand seven times before the Lord.
text.Rotherham: and the priest shall sprinkle with his right finger, of the oil that is on the palm of his left hand,—seven times, before Yahweh;
text.KJV: And the priest shall sprinkle with his right finger some of the oil that is in his left hand seven times before the Lord:
text.NABRE: and with his right finger sprinkle some of the oil in his left palm seven times before the Lord.
text.DRB: wherein dipping the finger of his right hand, he shall sprinkle it seven times before the Lord:

text.NRSV: The priest shall put some of the oil that is in his hand on the lobe of the right ear of the one to be cleansed, and on the thumb of the right hand, and the big toe of the right foot, where the blood of the guilt offering was placed.
text.Rotherham: then shall the priest put, of the oil that is on the palm of his hand, upon the tip of the right ear of him that is to be cleansed, and upon the thumb of his right hand, and upon the great toe of his right foot,—upon the place of the blood of the guilt-offering;
text.KJV: And the priest shall put of the oil that is in his hand upon the tip of the right ear of him that is to be cleansed, and upon the thumb of his right hand, and upon the great toe of his right foot, upon the place of the blood of the trespass offering:
text.NABRE: Some of the oil in his hand the priest shall also put on the lobe of the right ear, the thumb of the right hand, and the big toe of the right foot of the person being purified, where he had sprinkled the blood of the reparation offering.
text.DRB: and he shall touch the tip of the right ear of him that is cleansed, and the thumbs of his right hand & foot, in the place of the blood that was shed for offence:

text.NRSV: The rest of the oil that is in the priest’s hand he shall put on the head of the one to be cleansed, to make atonement on his behalf before the Lord.
text.Rotherham: and that which remaineth of the oil which is on the palm of the priest’s hand, shall he put upon the head of him that is to be cleansed,—to put a propitiatory-covering over him before Yahweh.
text.KJV: And the rest of the oil that is in the priest’s hand he shall put upon the head of him that is to be cleansed, to make an atonement for him before the Lord.
text.NABRE: The rest of the oil in his hand the priest shall put on the head of the one being purified. Thus shall he make atonement for the individual before the Lord.
text.DRB: & the other part of the oil, that is in his left hand, he shall pour upon the head of the purified person, that he may propitiate the Lord for him,

text.NRSV: And he shall offer, of the turtledoves or pigeons such as he can afford,
text.Rotherham: Then shall he offer one of the turtle-doves, or of the young pigeons,—of that for which his hand hath enough;
text.KJV: And he shall offer the one of the turtledoves, or of the young pigeons, such as he can get;
text.NABRE: Then, of the turtledoves or pigeons, such as the person can afford,
text.DRB: & a turtle, or young pigeon he shall offer,

text.NRSV: one for a sin offering and the other for a burnt offering, along with a grain offering; and the priest shall make atonement before the Lord on behalf of the one being cleansed.
text.Rotherham: even that for which his hand hath enough,—the one as a sin-bearer and the other as an ascending-sacrifice, upon the meal-offering,—so shall the priest put a propitiatory-covering over him that is to be cleansed before Yahweh.
text.KJV: Even such as he is able to get, the one for a sin offering, and the other for a burnt offering, with the meat offering: and the priest shall make an atonement for him that is to be cleansed before the Lord.
text.NABRE: the priest shall offer one as a purification offering and the other as a burnt offering, along with the grain offering. Thus shall the priest make atonement before the Lord for the person who is being purified.
text.DRB: one for offence, & the other for an holocaust, with their libaments.

text.NRSV: This is the ritual for the one who has a leprous disease, who cannot afford the offerings for his cleansing.
text.Rotherham: This is the law for him in whom hath been the plague of leprosy, whose hand hath not enough for that which pertaineth to his cleansing.
text.KJV: This is the law of him in whom is the plague of leprosy, whose hand is not able to get that which pertaineth to his cleansing.
text.NABRE: This is the ritual for one afflicted with a scaly infection who has insufficient means for purification.
text.DRB: This is the sacrifice of a leper, that is not able to have all things for the cleansing of himself.

text.NRSV: The Lord spoke to Moses and Aaron, saying:
text.Rotherham: And Yahweh spake unto Moses and unto Aaron saying:
text.KJV: And the Lord spake unto Moses and unto Aaron, saying,
text.NABRE: Fungal Infection of Houses. The Lord said to Moses and Aaron:
text.DRB: And our Lord spake to Moyses and Aaron, saying:

text.NRSV: When you come into the land of Canaan, which I give you for a possession, and I put a leprous disease in a house in the land of your possession,
text.Rotherham: When ye enter into the land of Canaan which I am about to give you for a possession,—and I put a plague-mark of leprosy in a house, of the land of your possession,
text.KJV: When ye be come into the land of Canaan, which I give to you for a possession, and I put the plague of leprosy in a house of the land of your possession;
text.NABRE: When you come into the land of Canaan, which I am giving you to possess, if I put a fungal infection in any house of the land you occupy,
text.DRB: When you shall be entered into the Land of Chanaan, which I will give you in possession, if there be the plague of leprosy in a house,

text.NRSV: the owner of the house shall come and tell the priest, saying, “There seems to me to be some sort of disease in my house.”
text.Rotherham: then shall he that owneth the house come in, and tell the priest, saying,—A kind of plague-mark, appeareth to me in the house;
text.KJV: And he that owneth the house shall come and tell the priest, saying, It seemeth to me there is as it were a plague in the house:
text.NABRE: the owner of the house shall come and report to the priest, “Something like an infection has appeared in my house.”
text.DRB: he whose house it is, shall go and tell the Priest, saying: It seemeth to me, that there is as it were the plague of leprosy in my house,

text.NRSV: The priest shall command that they empty the house before the priest goes to examine the disease, or all that is in the house will become unclean; and afterward the priest shall go in to inspect the house.
text.Rotherham: and the priest shall give command, and they shall empty the house, ere yet the priest cometh in to view the mark, so that he do not pronounce unclean all that is in the house,—and, after this, shall the priest come in to view the house:
text.KJV: Then the priest shall command that they empty the house, before the priest go into it to see the plague, that all that is in the house be not made unclean: and afterward the priest shall go in to see the house:
text.NABRE: The priest shall then order the house to be cleared out before he goes in to examine the infection, lest everything in the house become unclean. Only after this is he to go in to examine the house.
text.DRB: But he shall command that they carry forth all things out of the house, before he go into it, and see whether it have the leprosy, lest all things become unclean that are in the house. And afterward he shall go in to consider the leprosy of the house:

text.NRSV: He shall examine the disease; if the disease is in the walls of the house with greenish or reddish spots, and if it appears to be deeper than the surface,
text.Rotherham: then shall he view the mark and lo! if the mark is in the walls of the house, with sunken places greenish yellow or reddish,—and they appear to be lower than the [surface of the] wall,
text.KJV: And he shall look on the plague, and, behold, if the plague be in the walls of the house with hollow strakes, greenish or reddish, which in sight are lower than the wall;
text.NABRE: If the priest, upon inspection, finds that the infection on the walls of the house consists of greenish or reddish spots which seem to go deeper than the surface of the wall,
text.DRB: & if he see in the walls thereof as it were little dints, disfigured with paleness or redness, and lower than all the rest,

text.NRSV: the priest shall go outside to the door of the house and shut up the house seven days.
text.Rotherham: then shall the priest come forth out of the house, unto the entrance of the house,—and shall shut up the house seven days;
text.KJV: Then the priest shall go out of the house to the door of the house, and shut up the house seven days:
text.NABRE: he shall go out of the house to the doorway and quarantine the house for seven days.
text.DRB: he shall go out of the door of the house, and forthwith shut it seven days.
