text.KJV: For it is the life of all flesh; the blood of it is for the life thereof: therefore I said unto the children of Israel, Ye shall eat the blood of no manner of flesh: for the life of all flesh is the blood thereof: whosoever eateth it shall be cut off.
text.NABRE: since the life of all flesh is its blood. I have told the Israelites: You shall not consume the blood of any flesh. Since the life of all flesh is its blood, anyone who consumes it shall be cut off.
text.DRB: For the life of all flesh is in the blood: whereupon I said to the children of Israel: The blood of no flesh shall you eat, because the life of the flesh is in the blood: and whosoever eateth it shall die.

text.NRSV: All persons, citizens or aliens, who eat what dies of itself or what has been torn by wild animals, shall wash their clothes, and bathe themselves in water, and be unclean until the evening; then they shall be clean.
text.Rotherham: And, in the case of any person who eateth that which died of itself or was torn in pieces, whether he be home-born, or a sojourner, then shall he wash his clothes, and bathe in water, and be unclean until the evening, and then be clean.
text.KJV: And every soul that eateth that which died of itself, or that which was torn with beasts, whether it be one of your own country, or a stranger, he shall both wash his clothes, and bathe himself in water, and be unclean until the even: then shall he be clean.
text.NABRE: Everyone, whether a native or an alien, who eats of an animal that died of itself or was killed by a wild beast, shall wash his garments, bathe in water, and be unclean until evening, and then become clean.
text.DRB: The soul that eateth carrion, or that which is taken of a beast, as well of them of the same country as of strangers, shall wash his cloths, and himself with water, and shall be contaminated until even: and in this order he shall be made clean.

text.NRSV: But if they do not wash themselves or bathe their body, they shall bear their guilt.
text.Rotherham: But, if he wash them not, and his flesh, he do not bathe, then shall he bear his iniquity.
text.KJV: But if he wash them not, nor bathe his flesh; then he shall bear his iniquity.
text.NABRE: If one does not wash his garments and bathe, that person shall bear the penalty.
text.DRB: And if he do not wash his cloths, and his body, he shall bear his iniquity.

text.NRSV: The Lord spoke to Moses, saying:
text.Rotherham: And Yahweh spake unto Moses, saying:
text.KJV: And the Lord spake unto Moses, saying,
text.NABRE: The Lord said to Moses:
text.DRB: And our Lord spake to Moyses, saying:

text.NRSV: Speak to the people of Israel and say to them: I am the Lord your God.
text.Rotherham: Speak unto the sons of Israel, and thou shalt say unto them,—I—Yahweh, am your God:—
text.KJV: Speak unto the children of Israel, and say unto them, I am the Lord your God.
text.NABRE: Speak to the Israelites and tell them: I, the Lord, am your God.
text.DRB: Speak to the children of Israel, and thou shalt say to them: I the Lord your God,

text.NRSV: You shall not do as they do in the land of Egypt, where you lived, and you shall not do as they do in the land of Canaan, to which I am bringing you. You shall not follow their statutes.
text.Rotherham: After the doings of the land of Egypt wherein ye dwelt, shall ye not do,—And after the doings of the land of Canaan whither, I, am bringing you in, shall ye not do, And, in their statutes, shall ye not walk:
text.KJV: After the doings of the land of Egypt, wherein ye dwelt, shall ye not do: and after the doings of the land of Canaan, whither I bring you, shall ye not do: neither shall ye walk in their ordinances.
text.NABRE: You shall not do as they do in the land of Egypt, where you once lived, nor shall you do as they do in the land of Canaan, where I am bringing you; do not conform to their customs.
text.DRB: according to the custom of the Land of Ægypt, wherein you have dwelt, you shall not do: and according to the manner of the Country of Chanaan, into the which I will bring you, you shall not do, nor walk in their ordinances.

text.NRSV: My ordinances you shall observe and my statutes you shall keep, following them: I am the Lord your God.
text.Rotherham: My regulations, shall ye do, And, my statutes, shall ye observe to walk therein,—I—Yahweh, am your God.
text.KJV: Ye shall do my judgments, and keep mine ordinances, to walk therein: I am the Lord your God.
text.NABRE: My decrees you shall carry out, and my statutes you shall take care to follow. I, the Lord, am your God.
text.DRB: You shall do my judgments, and shall observe my precepts, and shall walk in them. I the Lord your God.

text.NRSV: You shall keep my statutes and my ordinances; by doing so one shall live: I am the Lord.
text.Rotherham: Therefore shall ye observe my statutes, and my regulations, Which if the son of earth shall do them, Then shall he live in them,—I, am Yahweh.
text.KJV: Ye shall therefore keep my statutes, and my judgments: which if a man do, he shall live in them: I am the Lord.
text.NABRE: Keep, then, my statutes and decrees, for the person who carries them out will find life through them. I am the Lord.
text.DRB: Keep my laws and judgments, which a man doing, shall live in them. I the Lord.

text.NRSV: None of you shall approach anyone near of kin to uncover nakedness: I am the Lord.
text.Rotherham: No person whatsoever, unto any of the near kin of his own flesh, shall approach to uncover the parts of shame,—I, am Yahweh.
text.KJV: None of you shall approach to any that is near of kin to him, to uncover their nakedness: I am the Lord.
text.NABRE: None of you shall approach a close relative to have sexual intercourse. I am the Lord.
text.DRB: No man shall approach to her that is next of his blood, to reveal her turpitude. I the Lord.

text.NRSV: You shall not uncover the nakedness of your father, which is the nakedness of your mother; she is your mother, you shall not uncover her nakedness.
text.Rotherham: The shame of thy father, even the shame of thy mother, shalt thou not uncover,—thy mother, she is, thou shalt not uncover her shame.
text.KJV: The nakedness of thy father, or the nakedness of thy mother, shalt thou not uncover: she is thy mother; thou shalt not uncover her nakedness.
text.NABRE: You shall not disgrace your father by having intercourse with your mother. She is your own mother; you shall not have intercourse with her.
text.DRB: The turpitude of thy father, and the turpitude of thy mother thou shalt not discover: she is thy mother, thou shalt not reveal her turpitude.

text.NRSV: You shall not uncover the nakedness of your father’s wife; it is the nakedness of your father.
text.Rotherham: The shame of thy father’s wife, shalt thou not uncover,—thy father’s shame, it is.
text.KJV: The nakedness of thy father’s wife shalt thou not uncover: it is thy father’s nakedness.
text.NABRE: You shall not have intercourse with your father’s wife, for that would be a disgrace to your father.
text.DRB: The turpitude of thy father's wife thou shalt not discover: for it is the turpitude of thy father:

text.NRSV: You shall not uncover the nakedness of your sister, your father’s daughter or your mother’s daughter, whether born at home or born abroad.
text.Rotherham: The shame of thy sister daughter of thy father or daughter of thy mother, born at home or born abroad, thou shalt not uncover their shame.
text.KJV: The nakedness of thy sister, the daughter of thy father, or daughter of thy mother, whether she be born at home, or born abroad, even their nakedness thou shalt not uncover.
text.NABRE: You shall not have intercourse with your sister, your father’s daughter or your mother’s daughter, whether she was born in your own household or born elsewhere.
text.DRB: The turpitude of thy sister by father, or by mother, which was born at home or abroad, thou shalt not reveal.

text.NRSV: You shall not uncover the nakedness of your son’s daughter or of your daughter’s daughter, for their nakedness is your own nakedness.
text.Rotherham: The shame of the daughter of thy son or the daughter of thy daughter, thou shalt not uncover their shame,—for, thine own shame, they are.
text.KJV: The nakedness of thy son’s daughter, or of thy daughter’s daughter, even their nakedness thou shalt not uncover: for theirs is thine own nakedness.
text.NABRE: You shall not have intercourse with your son’s daughter or with your daughter’s daughter, for that would be a disgrace to you.
text.DRB: The turpitude of thy son's daughter or of thy niece by the daughter, thou shalt not reveal: because it is thy turpitude.

text.NRSV: You shall not uncover the nakedness of your father’s wife’s daughter, begotten by your father, since she is your sister.
text.Rotherham: The shame of the daughter of thy father’s wife, born to thy father, she being, thy sister, thou shalt not uncover her shame.
text.KJV: The nakedness of thy father’s wife’s daughter, begotten of thy father, she is thy sister, thou shalt not uncover her nakedness.
text.NABRE: You shall not have intercourse with the daughter whom your father’s wife bore to him in his household, since she, too, is your sister.
text.DRB: The turpitude of thy father's wife's daughter, which she bare to thy father, and is thy sister, thou shalt not reveal.

text.NRSV: You shall not uncover the nakedness of your father’s sister; she is your father’s flesh.
text.Rotherham: The shame of thy father’s sister, shalt thou not uncover,—thy father’s near of kin, she is.
text.KJV: Thou shalt not uncover the nakedness of thy father’s sister: she is thy father’s near kinswoman.
text.NABRE: You shall not have intercourse with your father’s sister, since she is your father’s relative.
text.DRB: The turpitude of thy father's sister thou shalt not discover: because she is the flesh of thy father.

text.NRSV: You shall not uncover the nakedness of your mother’s sister, for she is your mother’s flesh.
text.Rotherham: The shame of thy mother’s sister, shalt thou not uncover,—for, thy mother’s near of kin, she is.
text.KJV: Thou shalt not uncover the nakedness of thy mother’s sister: for she is thy mother’s near kinswoman.
text.NABRE: You shall not have intercourse with your mother’s sister, since she is your mother’s relative.
text.DRB: The turpitude of thy mother's sister thou shalt not reveal, because she is of the flesh of thy mother.

text.NRSV: You shall not uncover the nakedness of your father’s brother, that is, you shall not approach his wife; she is your aunt.
text.Rotherham: The shame of thy father’s brother, shalt thou not uncover unto his wife, shalt thou not approach, thine aunt, she is.
text.KJV: Thou shalt not uncover the nakedness of thy father’s brother, thou shalt not approach to his wife: she is thine aunt.
text.NABRE: You shall not disgrace your father’s brother by having sexual relations with his wife, since she, too, is your aunt.
text.DRB: The turpitude of thy father's brother thou shalt not reveal, neither shall thou approach to his wife, who is joined to thee by affinity.

text.NRSV: You shall not uncover the nakedness of your daughter-in-law: she is your son’s wife; you shall not uncover her nakedness.
text.Rotherham: The shame of thy daughter-in-law, shalt thou not uncover,—thy son’s wife, she is, thou shalt not uncover her shame.
text.KJV: Thou shalt not uncover the nakedness of thy daughter in law: she is thy son’s wife; thou shalt not uncover her nakedness.
text.NABRE: You shall not have intercourse with your daughter-in-law; she is your son’s wife; you shall not have intercourse with her.
text.DRB: The turpitude of thy daughter in law thou shalt not reveal, because she is thy son's wife, neither shalt thou discover her ignominy.

text.NRSV: You shall not uncover the nakedness of your brother’s wife; it is your brother’s nakedness.
text.Rotherham: The shame of thy brother’s wife, shalt thou not uncover,—the shame of thy brother, it is.
text.KJV: Thou shalt not uncover the nakedness of thy brother’s wife: it is thy brother’s nakedness.
text.NABRE: You shall not have intercourse with your brother’s wife; that would be a disgrace to your brother.
text.DRB: The turpitude of thy brother's wife thou shalt not reveal: because it is the turpitude of thy brother.

text.NRSV: You shall not uncover the nakedness of a woman and her daughter, and you shall not take her son’s daughter or her daughter’s daughter to uncover her nakedness; they are your flesh; it is depravity.
text.Rotherham: The shame of a woman, and of her daughter, shalt thou not uncover,—neither the daughter of her son nor the daughter of her daughter, shalt thou take, to uncover her shame, near of kin, they are, wickedness, it is.
text.KJV: Thou shalt not uncover the nakedness of a woman and her daughter, neither shalt thou take her son’s daughter, or her daughter’s daughter, to uncover her nakedness; for they are her near kinswomen: it is wickedness.
text.NABRE: You shall not have intercourse with a woman and also with her daughter, nor shall you marry and have intercourse with her son’s daughter or her daughter’s daughter; they are related to her. This would be shameful.
text.DRB: The turpitude of thy wife, and her daughter thou shalt not reveal. Her son's daughter, and her daughter's daughter, thou shalt not take, to reveal her ignominy: because they are her flesh, and such copulation is incest.

text.NRSV: And you shall not take a woman as a rival to her sister, uncovering her nakedness while her sister is still alive.
text.Rotherham: And, a woman unto her sister, shalt thou not take,—to cause rivalry, by uncovering her shame besides her own while she is living.
text.KJV: Neither shalt thou take a wife to her sister, to vex her, to uncover her nakedness, beside the other in her life time.
text.NABRE: While your wife is still living you shall not marry her sister as her rival and have intercourse with her.
text.DRB: Thou shalt not take thy wife's sister for an harlot, to vex her withal, neither shalt thou reveal her turpitude, while she is yet living.

text.NRSV: You shall not approach a woman to uncover her nakedness while she is in her menstrual uncleanness.
text.Rotherham: And unto a woman during her removal for uncleanness, shalt thou not approach, to uncover her shame.
text.KJV: Also thou shalt not approach unto a woman to uncover her nakedness, as long as she is put apart for her uncleanness.
text.NABRE: You shall not approach a woman to have intercourse with her while she is in her menstrual uncleanness.
text.DRB: To a woman, having her flowers, thou shalt not approach, neither shalt thou reveal her turpitude.

text.NRSV: You shall not have sexual relations with your kinsman’s wife, and defile yourself with her.
text.Rotherham: And of the wife of thy neighbour, shalt thou not have carnal knowledge,—to commit uncleanness with her.
text.KJV: Moreover thou shalt not lie carnally with thy neighbour’s wife, to defile thyself with her.
text.NABRE: You shall not have sexual relations with your neighbor’s wife, defiling yourself with her.
text.DRB: With thy neighbour's wife thou shalt not company, nor be polluted with commixtion of seed.

text.NRSV: You shall not give any of your offspring to sacrifice them to Molech, and so profane the name of your God: I am the Lord.
text.Rotherham: And none of thy seed, shalt thou deliver up, to cause to pass through to Molech,—that thou profane not the name of thy God, I, am Yahweh.
text.KJV: And thou shalt not let any of thy seed pass through the fire to Molech, neither shalt thou profane the name of thy God: I am the Lord.
text.NABRE: You shall not offer any of your offspring for immolation to Molech, thus profaning the name of your God. I am the Lord.
text.DRB: Of thy seed thou shalt not give to be consecrated to the idol Moloch, nor pollute the name of thy God: I the Lord.

text.NRSV: You shall not lie with a male as with a woman; it is an abomination.
text.Rotherham: And, with mankind, shalt thou not lie as with womankind,—an abomination, it is.
text.KJV: Thou shalt not lie with mankind, as with womankind: it is abomination.
text.NABRE: You shall not lie with a male as with a woman; such a thing is an abomination.
text.DRB: Company not with mankind, as with womankind, because it is abomination.

text.NRSV: You shall not have sexual relations with any animal and defile yourself with it, nor shall any woman give herself to an animal to have sexual relations with it: it is perversion.
text.Rotherham: And of no beast, shalt thou have carnal knowledge to commit uncleanness therewith,—neither shall a woman present herself to a beast to couch down thereto,—confusion, it is.
text.KJV: Neither shalt thou lie with any beast to defile thyself therewith: neither shall any woman stand before a beast to lie down thereto: it is confusion.
text.NABRE: You shall not have sexual relations with an animal, defiling yourself with it; nor shall a woman set herself in front of an animal to mate with it; that is perverse.
text.DRB: With no beast shalt thou company, neither shalt thou be polluted with it. A woman shall not lie down to a beast, nor company with it: because it is an heinous fact.

text.NRSV: Do not defile yourselves in any of these ways, for by all these practices the nations I am casting out before you have defiled themselves.
text.Rotherham: Do not make yourselves unclean in any of these things,—For in all these things, have the nations made themselves unclean, whom, I, am sending out from before you.
text.KJV: Defile not ye yourselves in any of these things: for in all these the nations are defiled which I cast out before you:
text.NABRE: Do not defile yourselves by any of these things, because by them the nations whom I am driving out of your way have defiled themselves.
text.DRB: Neither be ye polluted in any of the things wherewith all the nations have been contaminated, which I will cast out before your sight,

text.NRSV: Thus the land became defiled; and I punished it for its iniquity, and the land vomited out its inhabitants.
text.Rotherham: Therefore hath the land become unclean, and I have visited the iniquity thereof upon it,—and the land hath vomited her inhabitants.
text.KJV: And the land is defiled: therefore I do visit the iniquity thereof upon it, and the land itself vomiteth out her inhabitants.
text.NABRE: And so the land has become defiled, and I have punished it for its wickedness, and the land has vomited out its inhabitants.
text.DRB: and wherewith the land is polluted: whose abominations I will visit, that it vomit out the inhabitants thereof.

text.NRSV: But you shall keep my statutes and my ordinances and commit none of these abominations, either the citizen or the alien who resides among you
text.Rotherham: Ye, therefore, shall observe my statutes and my regulations, and have nothing to do with any of these abominations,—whether the home-born, or the sojourner that sojourneth in your midst;
text.KJV: Ye shall therefore keep my statutes and my judgments, and shall not commit any of these abominations; neither any of your own nation, nor any stranger that sojourneth among you:
text.NABRE: You, however, must keep my statutes and decrees, avoiding all these abominations, both the natives and the aliens resident among you—
text.DRB: Keep my ordinances and judgments, and do not any of these abominations, as well the same countryman as the stranger that sojourneth with you.

text.NRSV: (for the inhabitants of the land, who were before you, committed all of these abominations, and the land became defiled);
text.Rotherham: For all these abominations, have the men of the land done, who were before you; And so the land hath become unclean:
text.KJV: (For all these abominations have the men of the land done, which were before you, and the land is defiled;)
text.NABRE: because the previous inhabitants did all these abominations and the land became defiled;
text.DRB: For all these execrable things did the inhabitants of the land, that have been before you, and have polluted it.

text.NRSV: otherwise the land will vomit you out for defiling it, as it vomited out the nation that was before you.
text.Rotherham: So shall the land not vomit you, through your making it unclean,—as it vomited the nation which was before you.
text.KJV: That the land spue not you out also, when ye defile it, as it spued out the nations that were before you.
text.NABRE: otherwise the land will vomit you out also for having defiled it, just as it vomited out the nations before you.
text.DRB: Beware therefore lest in like manner it vomit out you also, when you shall do the like things, as it vomited out the nation that was before you.

text.NRSV: For whoever commits any of these abominations shall be cut off from their people.
text.Rotherham: For whosoever shall have anything to do with any of these abominations, the persons who have, shall be cut off, out of the midst of their people.
text.KJV: For whosoever shall commit any of these abominations, even the souls that commit them shall be cut off from among their people.
text.NABRE: For whoever does any of these abominations shall be cut off from the people.
text.DRB: Every soul, that shall do any of these abominations, shall perish from the midst of his people,

text.NRSV: So keep my charge not to commit any of these abominations that were done before you, and not to defile yourselves by them: I am the Lord your God.
text.Rotherham: Therefore shall ye keep my charge, So that ye may have nothing to do with any of the abominable statutes with which they have had to do before you, So shall ye not make yourselves unclean thereby,—I—Yahweh, am your God.
text.KJV: Therefore shall ye keep mine ordinance, that ye commit not any one of these abominable customs, which were committed before you, and that ye defile not yourselves therein: I am the Lord your God.
text.NABRE: Heed my charge, then, not to observe the abominable customs that have been observed before your time, and thus become impure by them. I, the Lord, am your God.
text.DRB: Keep my commandments. Do not the things which they have done, that have been before you, and be not polluted in them: I the Lord your God.

text.NRSV: The Lord spoke to Moses, saying:
text.Rotherham: And Yahweh spake unto Moses, saying:
text.KJV: And the Lord spake unto Moses, saying,
text.NABRE: The Lord said to Moses:
text.DRB: Our Lord spake to Moyses, saying:

text.NRSV: Speak to all the congregation of the people of Israel and say to them: You shall be holy, for I the Lord your God am holy.
text.Rotherham: Speak unto all the assembly of the sons of Israel and thou shalt say unto them—Holy, shall ye be,—For holy, am I—Yahweh—your God
text.KJV: Speak unto all the congregation of the children of Israel, and say unto them, Ye shall be holy: for I the Lord your God am holy.
text.NABRE: Speak to the whole Israelite community and tell them: Be holy, for I, the Lord your God, am holy.
text.DRB: Speak to all the assembly of the children of Israel, and thou shalt say to them: Be ye holy, because I the Lord your God am holy.

text.NRSV: You shall each revere your mother and father, and you shall keep my sabbaths: I am the Lord your God.
text.Rotherham: Ye shall, every one, revere, his father and his mother, And my sabbaths, shall ye observe,—I—Yahweh, am your God.
text.KJV: Ye shall fear every man his mother, and his father, and keep my sabbaths: I am the Lord your God.
text.NABRE: Each of you revere your mother and father, and keep my sabbaths. I, the Lord, am your God.
text.DRB: Let every one fear his father, and mother. Keep my Sabbaths. I the Lord your God.

text.NRSV: Do not turn to idols or make cast images for yourselves: I am the Lord your God.
text.Rotherham: Do not turn unto things of nought, And, molten gods, shall ye not make to yourselves,—I—Yahweh, am your God.
text.KJV: Turn ye not unto idols, nor make to yourselves molten gods: I am the Lord your God.
text.NABRE: Do not turn aside to idols, nor make molten gods for yourselves. I, the Lord, am your God.
text.DRB: Turn not yourselves to idols, neither make you to yourselves molten gods. I the Lord your God.

text.NRSV: When you offer a sacrifice of well-being to the Lord, offer it in such a way that it is acceptable in your behalf.
text.Rotherham: And when ye would offer a peace-offering unto Yahweh, ye shall offer it that ye may be accepted.
text.KJV: And if ye offer a sacrifice of peace offerings unto the Lord, ye shall offer it at your own will.
text.NABRE: When you sacrifice your communion sacrifice to the Lord, you shall sacrifice it so that it is acceptable on your behalf.
text.DRB: If ye immolate an host of pacifics to the Lord, that it may be placable,

text.NRSV: It shall be eaten on the same day you offer it, or on the next day; and anything left over until the third day shall be consumed in fire.
text.Rotherham: On the day, ye offer it, shall it be eaten, and on the morrow,—but, that which remaineth until the third day, in fire, shall be consumed.
text.KJV: It shall be eaten the same day ye offer it, and on the morrow: and if ought remain until the third day, it shall be burnt in the fire.
text.NABRE: It must be eaten on the day of your sacrifice or on the following day. Whatever is left over until the third day shall be burned in fire.
text.DRB: that day wherein it is immolated, shall you eat it, and the next day: and whatsoever shall be left until the third day, you shall burn with fire.

text.NRSV: If it is eaten at all on the third day, it is an abomination; it will not be acceptable.
text.Rotherham: And, if it, be eaten, on the third day, unclean, it is it shall not be accepted,
text.KJV: And if it be eaten at all on the third day, it is abominable; it shall not be accepted.
text.NABRE: If any of it is eaten on the third day, it will be a desecrated offering and not be accepted;
text.DRB: If after two days any man eat thereof, he shall be profane, and guilty of impiety:

text.NRSV: All who eat it shall be subject to punishment, because they have profaned what is holy to the Lord; and any such person shall be cut off from the people.
text.Rotherham: But he that eateth it, his iniquity, shall bear, because that which had been hallowed unto Yahweh, hath he profaned,—so then that person shall be cut off from among his kinsfolk.
text.KJV: Therefore every one that eateth it shall bear his iniquity, because he hath profaned the hallowed thing of the Lord: and that soul shall be cut off from among his people.
text.NABRE: whoever eats of it then shall bear the penalty for having profaned what is sacred to the Lord. Such a one shall be cut off from the people.
text.DRB: and shall bear his iniquity, because he hath polluted the holy of the Lord, and that soul shall perish out of his people.

text.NRSV: When you reap the harvest of your land, you shall not reap to the very edges of your field, or gather the gleanings of your harvest.
text.Rotherham: And when ye reap the harvest of your land, thou shalt not wholly clear the border of thy field, in reaping,—nor shalt thou gather up the gleaning of thy harvest.
text.KJV: And when ye reap the harvest of your land, thou shalt not wholly reap the corners of thy field, neither shalt thou gather the gleanings of thy harvest.
text.NABRE: When you reap the harvest of your land, you shall not be so thorough that you reap the field to its very edge, nor shall you gather the gleanings of your harvest.
text.DRB: And when thou reapest the corn of thy land, thou shalt not shear the face of the earth to the very ground: neither shalt thou gather the ears that remain.

text.NRSV: You shall not strip your vineyard bare, or gather the fallen grapes of your vineyard; you shall leave them for the poor and the alien: I am the Lord your God.
text.Rotherham: And, thy vineyard, shalt thou not go over again, nor gather, every single grape: for the poor and for the sojourner, shalt thou leave them, I—Yahweh, am your God.
text.KJV: And thou shalt not glean thy vineyard, neither shalt thou gather every grape of thy vineyard; thou shalt leave them for the poor and stranger: I am the Lord your God.
text.NABRE: Likewise, you shall not pick your vineyard bare, nor gather up the grapes that have fallen. These things you shall leave for the poor and the alien. I, the Lord, am your God.
text.DRB: Neither in thy vineyard shalt thou gather the clusters and grapes that fall down, but shall leave them to the poor, and the strangers to take. I the Lord your God.

text.NRSV: You shall not steal; you shall not deal falsely; and you shall not lie to one another.
text.Rotherham: Ye shall not steal,—nor deceive nor lie one man to another;
text.KJV: Ye shall not steal, neither deal falsely, neither lie one to another.
text.NABRE: You shall not steal. You shall not deceive or speak falsely to one another.
text.DRB: You shall not commit theft. You shall not lie, neither shall any man deceive his neighbour.

text.NRSV: And you shall not swear falsely by my name, profaning the name of your God: I am the Lord.
text.Rotherham: Nor swear by my name falsely,—And so profane the name of thy God: I, am Yahweh.
text.KJV: And ye shall not swear by my name falsely, neither shalt thou profane the name of thy God: I am the Lord.
text.NABRE: You shall not swear falsely by my name, thus profaning the name of your God. I am the Lord.
text.DRB: Thou shalt not forswear thyself in my name, nor pollute the name of thy God. I the Lord.

text.NRSV: You shall not defraud your neighbor; you shall not steal; and you shall not keep for yourself the wages of a laborer until morning.
text.Rotherham: Thou shalt not oppress thy neighbour nor rob [him],—The wages of him that is hired shall not tarry with thee, until the morning.
text.KJV: Thou shalt not defraud thy neighbour, neither rob him: the wages of him that is hired shall not abide with thee all night until the morning.
text.NABRE: You shall not exploit your neighbor. You shall not commit robbery. You shall not withhold overnight the wages of your laborer.
text.DRB: Thou shalt not calumniate thy neighbour, nor oppress him by violence. The work of thy hireling shall not abide with thee until morning.

text.NRSV: You shall not revile the deaf or put a stumbling block before the blind; you shall fear your God: I am the Lord.
text.Rotherham: Thou shalt not curse the deaf, Nor, before the blind, place a stumbling-block,—So shalt thou stand in awe of thy God I, am Yahweh.
text.KJV: Thou shalt not curse the deaf, nor put a stumblingblock before the blind, but shalt fear thy God: I am the Lord.
text.NABRE: You shall not insult the deaf, or put a stumbling block in front of the blind, but you shall fear your God. I am the Lord.
text.DRB: Thou shalt not speak evil of the deaf man, nor put a stumbling block before the blind: but thou shalt fear the Lord thy God, because I am the Lord.

text.NRSV: You shall not render an unjust judgment; you shall not be partial to the poor or defer to the great: with justice you shall judge your neighbor.
text.Rotherham: Ye shall not act perversely in giving judgment, Thou shalt neither respect the person of the poor, Nor prefer the person of the great,—In righteousness, shalt thou judge thy neighbour.
text.KJV: Ye shall do no unrighteousness in judgment: thou shalt not respect the person of the poor, nor honour the person of the mighty: but in righteousness shalt thou judge thy neighbour.
text.NABRE: You shall not act dishonestly in rendering judgment. Show neither partiality to the weak nor deference to the mighty, but judge your neighbor justly.
text.DRB: Thou shalt not do that which is unjust, nor judge unjustly. Consider not the person of a poor man, neither honour thou the countenance of him that is mighty. Judge justly to thy neighbour.

text.NRSV: You shall not go around as a slanderer among your people, and you shall not profit by the blood of your neighbor: I am the Lord.
text.Rotherham: Thou shalt not go about talebearing, among thy people Thou shalt not stand by, over the blood of thy neighbour: I, am Yahweh.
text.KJV: Thou shalt not go up and down as a talebearer among thy people: neither shalt thou stand against the blood of thy neighbour: I am the Lord.
text.NABRE: You shall not go about spreading slander among your people; nor shall you stand by idly when your neighbor’s life is at stake. I am the Lord.
text.DRB: Thou shalt not be a criminator, nor a whisperer among the people. Thou shalt not stand against the blood of thy neighbour. I the Lord.

text.NRSV: You shall not hate in your heart anyone of your kin; you shall reprove your neighbor, or you will incur guilt yourself.
text.Rotherham: Thou shalt not hate thy brother in thy heart,—Thou shalt, faithfully reprove, thy neighbour, and not countenance him in sin:
text.KJV: Thou shalt not hate thy brother in thine heart: thou shalt in any wise rebuke thy neighbour, and not suffer sin upon him.
text.NABRE: You shall not hate any of your kindred in your heart. Reprove your neighbor openly so that you do not incur sin because of that person.
text.DRB: Thou shalt not hate thy brother in thy heart, but control him openly, lest thou incur sin through him.

text.NRSV: You shall not take vengeance or bear a grudge against any of your people, but you shall love your neighbor as yourself: I am the Lord.
text.Rotherham: Thou shalt not take vengeance neither shalt thou cherish anger against the sons of thy people, So shalt thou love thy neighbour as thyself,—I, am Yahweh.
text.KJV: Thou shalt not avenge, nor bear any grudge against the children of thy people, but thou shalt love thy neighbour as thyself: I am the Lord.
text.NABRE: Take no revenge and cherish no grudge against your own people. You shall love your neighbor as yourself. I am the Lord.
text.DRB: Seek not revenge, nor be mindful of the injury of thy citizens. Thou shalt love thy friend as thyself. I the Lord.

text.NRSV: You shall keep my statutes. You shall not let your animals breed with a different kind; you shall not sow your field with two kinds of seed; nor shall you put on a garment made of two different materials.
text.Rotherham: My statutes, shall ye observe, Thy beasts, shalt thou not cause to breed in two kinds, Thy field, shalt thou not sow with two sorts of seed,—And a garment woven of diverse threads, shalt thou not suffer to come upon thee.
text.KJV: Ye shall keep my statutes. Thou shalt not let thy cattle gender with a diverse kind: thou shalt not sow thy field with mingled seed: neither shall a garment mingled of linen and woollen come upon thee.
text.NABRE: Keep my statutes: do not breed any of your domestic animals with others of a different species; do not sow a field of yours with two different kinds of seed; and do not put on a garment woven with two different kinds of thread.
text.DRB: Keep ye my laws. Thou shalt not make thy cattle to gender with the beasts of another kind. Thy field thou shalt not sow with diverse seed. A garment that is woven of two sorts, thou shalt not put on.

text.NRSV: If a man has sexual relations with a woman who is a slave, designated for another man but not ransomed or given her freedom, an inquiry shall be held. They shall not be put to death, since she has not been freed;
text.Rotherham: And whosoever lieth carnally with a woman, she being a bondmaid, acquired for a husband, and neither, redeemed, nor, freedom, given her, inquisition, shall be made they shall not be put to death, because she was not free;
text.KJV: And whosoever lieth carnally with a woman, that is a bondmaid, betrothed to an husband, and not at all redeemed, nor freedom given her; she shall be scourged; they shall not be put to death, because she was not free.
text.NABRE: If a man has sexual relations with a female slave who has been acquired by another man but has not yet been redeemed or given her freedom, an investigation shall be made. They shall not be put to death, because she has not been freed.
text.DRB: If a man lie with a woman by carnal copulation, that is a bondwoman also marriable, & yet not redeemed with a price, nor made free, both shall be beaten, and they shall not die, because she was not free.

text.NRSV: but he shall bring a guilt offering for himself to the Lord, at the entrance of the tent of meeting, a ram as guilt offering.
text.Rotherham: but he shall bring in his guilt-bearer unto Yahweh, unto the entrance of the tent of meeting,—even a ram as a guilt-bearer;
text.KJV: And he shall bring his trespass offering unto the Lord, unto the door of the tabernacle of the congregation, even a ram for a trespass offering.
text.NABRE: The man shall bring to the entrance of the tent of meeting as his reparation to the Lord a ram as a reparation offering.
text.DRB: And for his offence he shall offer to the Lord, at the door of the tabernacle of testimony a ram:

text.NRSV: And the priest shall make atonement for him with the ram of guilt offering before the Lord for his sin that he committed; and the sin he committed shall be forgiven him.
text.Rotherham: and the priest shall put a propitiatory-covering over him with the guilt-bearing ram before Yahweh, on account of his sin which he hath committed,—so shall he have forgiveness, from his sin which he hath committed.
text.KJV: And the priest shall make an atonement for him with the ram of the trespass offering before the Lord for his sin which he hath done: and the sin which he hath done shall be forgiven him.
text.NABRE: With the ram of the reparation offering the priest shall make atonement before the Lord for the wrong the man has committed, so that he will be forgiven for the wrong he has committed.
text.DRB: and the Priest shall pray for him, and for his sin before the Lord, and he shall become propitious to him again, and the sin shall be forgiven.

text.NRSV: When you come into the land and plant all kinds of trees for food, then you shall regard their fruit as forbidden; three years it shall be forbidden to you, it must not be eaten.
text.Rotherham: And when ye come into the land, and plant any manner of fruit-tree, then shall ye count as uncircumcised the fruit thereof, three years, shall it be to you as if unclean, it shall not be eaten.
text.KJV: And when ye shall come into the land, and shall have planted all manner of trees for food, then ye shall count the fruit thereof as uncircumcised: three years shall it be as uncircumcised unto you: it shall not be eaten of.
text.NABRE: When you come into the land and plant any fruit tree there, first look upon its fruit as if it were uncircumcised. For three years, it shall be uncircumcised for you; it may not be eaten.
text.DRB: When you shall be entered into the land, and have planted in it fruit trees, you shall take away the prepuces of them: the fruits that come forth shall be unclean to you, neither shall you eat of them.

text.NRSV: In the fourth year all their fruit shall be set apart for rejoicing in the Lord.
text.Rotherham: But, in the fourth year, all the fruit thereof shall be hallowed for a festival of thanksgiving unto Yahweh.
text.KJV: But in the fourth year all the fruit thereof shall be holy to praise the Lord withal.
text.NABRE: In the fourth year, however, all of its fruit shall be dedicated to the Lord in joyous celebration.
text.DRB: But in the fourth year, all their fruit shall be sanctified, laudable to the Lord.

text.NRSV: But in the fifth year you may eat of their fruit, that their yield may be increased for you: I am the Lord your God.
text.Rotherham: And in the fifth year, shall ye eat the fruit thereof, that it may enrich you with its increase,—I—Yahweh, am your God.
text.KJV: And in the fifth year shall ye eat of the fruit thereof, that it may yield unto you the increase thereof: I am the Lord your God.
text.NABRE: Not until the fifth year may you eat its fruit, to increase the yield for you. I, the Lord, am your God.
text.DRB: And the fifth year you shall eat the fruits, gathering the offspring that they bring forth. I the Lord your God.

text.NRSV: You shall not eat anything with its blood. You shall not practice augury or witchcraft.
text.Rotherham: Ye shall eat nothing with the blood thereof,—Ye shall not practise divination neither shall ye use magic.
text.KJV: Ye shall not eat any thing with the blood: neither shall ye use enchantment, nor observe times.
text.NABRE: Do not eat anything with the blood still in it. Do not recite charms or practice soothsaying.
text.DRB: You shall not eat with blood. You shall not divine, nor observe dreams.

text.NRSV: You shall not round off the hair on your temples or mar the edges of your beard.
text.Rotherham: Ye shall not shave in a circle around your head,—nor shalt thou disfigure the fringe of thy beard.
text.KJV: Ye shall not round the corners of your heads, neither shalt thou mar the corners of thy beard.
text.NABRE: Do not clip your hair at the temples, nor spoil the edges of your beard.
text.DRB: Neither shall you cut your hair roundwise: nor shave your beard.

text.NRSV: You shall not make any gashes in your flesh for the dead or tattoo any marks upon you: I am the Lord.
text.Rotherham: Cuttings for a dead person, shall ye not make in your flesh. And punctures in your persons, shall ye not print,—I, am Yahweh.
text.KJV: Ye shall not make any cuttings in your flesh for the dead, nor print any marks upon you: I am the Lord.
text.NABRE: Do not lacerate your bodies for the dead, and do not tattoo yourselves. I am the Lord.
text.DRB: And for the dead you shall not cut your flesh, neither shall you make in yourselves any figures or marks. I the Lord.

text.NRSV: Do not profane your daughter by making her a prostitute, that the land not fall into whoredom and the land become full of depravity.
text.Rotherham: Do not profane thy daughter by causing her to be unchaste,—lest the land fall to unchastity, and so the land be filled with wickedness.
text.KJV: Do not prostitute thy daughter, to cause her to be a whore; lest the land fall to whoredom, and the land become full of wickedness.
text.NABRE: You shall not degrade your daughter by making a prostitute of her; otherwise the land will prostitute itself and become full of lewdness.
text.DRB: Make not thy daughter a common strumpet, lest the land be contamined and filled with wickedness.

text.NRSV: You shall keep my sabbaths and reverence my sanctuary: I am the Lord.
text.Rotherham: My sabbaths, shall ye observe, And my sanctuary, shall ye revere,—I, am Yahweh.
text.KJV: Ye shall keep my sabbaths, and reverence my sanctuary: I am the Lord.
text.NABRE: Keep my sabbaths, and reverence my sanctuary. I am the Lord.
text.DRB: Keep ye my Sabbaths, & fear my Sanctuary. I the Lord.

text.NRSV: Do not turn to mediums or wizards; do not seek them out, to be defiled by them: I am the Lord your God.
text.Rotherham: Do not turn unto mediums, Nor, for oracles, make search, To render yourselves unclean with them, I—Yahweh, am your God.
text.KJV: Regard not them that have familiar spirits, neither seek after wizards, to be defiled by them: I am the Lord your God.
text.NABRE: Do not turn to ghosts or consult spirits, by which you will be defiled. I, the Lord, am your God.
text.DRB: Decline not to magicians, neither ask any thing of soothsayers, to be polluted by them. I the Lord your God.

text.NRSV: You shall rise before the aged, and defer to the old; and you shall fear your God: I am the Lord.
text.Rotherham: Before a hoary head, shalt thou rise up, And shalt honour the presence of an elder,—So shalt thou stand in awe of thy God—I, am Yahweh.
text.KJV: Thou shalt rise up before the hoary head, and honour the face of the old man, and fear thy God: I am the Lord.
text.NABRE: Stand up in the presence of the aged, show respect for the old, and fear your God. I am the Lord.
text.DRB: Before the hoar head rise up, and honour the person of an old man: and fear the Lord thy God. I am the Lord.

text.NRSV: When an alien resides with you in your land, you shall not oppress the alien.
text.Rotherham: And, when there sojourneth with thee a sojourner in your land, ye shall not oppress him:
text.KJV: And if a stranger sojourn with thee in your land, ye shall not vex him.
text.NABRE: When an alien resides with you in your land, do not mistreat such a one.
text.DRB: If a stranger dwell in your land, and abide among you, do not upbraid him:

text.NRSV: The alien who resides with you shall be to you as the citizen among you; you shall love the alien as yourself, for you were aliens in the land of Egypt: I am the Lord your God.
text.Rotherham: As one home born from among yourselves, shall be unto you the sojourner who sojourneth with you, So shalt thou love him as thyself, For, sojourners, became ye, in the land of Egypt,—I—Yahweh, am your God.
text.KJV: But the stranger that dwelleth with you shall be unto you as one born among you, and thou shalt love him as thyself; for ye were strangers in the land of Egypt: I am the Lord your God.
text.NABRE: You shall treat the alien who resides with you no differently than the natives born among you; you shall love the alien as yourself; for you too were once aliens in the land of Egypt. I, the Lord, am your God.
text.DRB: but let him be among you as the same country man: and you shall love him as yourselves: for you also have been strangers in the Land of Ægypt. I the Lord your God.

text.NRSV: You shall not cheat in measuring length, weight, or quantity.
text.Rotherham: Ye shall not act perversely in giving judgment,—in measures of extent, in weights in measures of capacity:
text.KJV: Ye shall do no unrighteousness in judgment, in meteyard, in weight, or in measure.
text.NABRE: Do not act dishonestly in using measures of length or weight or capacity.
text.DRB: Do not any unjust thing in judgment, in rule, in weight, or measure.

text.NRSV: You shall have honest balances, honest weights, an honest ephah, and an honest hin: I am the Lord your God, who brought you out of the land of Egypt.
text.Rotherham: Just balances, just weights, a just ephah and a just hin, shall ye have,—I—Yahweh, am your God, who brought you forth out of the land of Egypt.
text.KJV: Just balances, just weights, a just ephah, and a just hin, shall ye have: I am the Lord your God, which brought you out of the land of Egypt.
text.NABRE: You shall have a true scale and true weights, an honest ephah and an honest hin. I, the Lord, am your God, who brought you out of the land of Egypt.
text.DRB: Let the balance be just, and the weights equal, the bushel just, and the sextary equal. I the Lord your God, that brought you out of the Land of Ægypt.

text.NRSV: You shall keep all my statutes and all my ordinances, and observe them: I am the Lord.
text.Rotherham: Therefore shall ye observe all my statutes and all my regulations and do them,—I, am Yahweh.
text.KJV: Therefore shall ye observe all my statutes, and all my judgments, and do them: I am the Lord.
text.NABRE: Be careful, then, to observe all my statutes and decrees. I am the Lord.
text.DRB: Keep all my precepts, and all my judgements, and do them. I the Lord.

text.NRSV: The Lord spoke to Moses, saying:
text.Rotherham: And Yahweh spake unto Moses, saying:
text.KJV: And the Lord spake unto Moses, saying,
text.NABRE: The Lord said to Moses:
text.DRB: And our Lord spake to Moyses, saying:

text.NRSV: Say further to the people of Israel: Any of the people of Israel, or of the aliens who reside in Israel, who give any of their offspring to Molech shall be put to death; the people of the land shall stone them to death.
text.Rotherham: Unto the sons of Israel, therefore shalt thou say, What man soever, there may be of the sons of Israel, or of the sojourners that sojourn in Israel, that giveth of his seed unto Molech, he shall, surely be put to death,—the people of the land shall stone him with stones;
text.KJV: Again, thou shalt say to the children of Israel, Whosoever he be of the children of Israel, or of the strangers that sojourn in Israel, that giveth any of his seed unto Molech; he shall surely be put to death: the people of the land shall stone him with stones.
text.NABRE: Tell the Israelites: Anyone, whether an Israelite or an alien residing in Israel, who gives offspring to Molech shall be put to death. The people of the land shall stone that person.
text.DRB: These things thou shalt speak to the children of Israel: If any man of the children of Israel, & of the strangers, that dwell in Israel, give of his seed to the idol Moloch, dying let him die: the people of the land shall stone him.

text.NRSV: I myself will set my face against them, and will cut them off from the people, because they have given of their offspring to Molech, defiling my sanctuary and profaning my holy name.
text.Rotherham: I, also, will set my face against that man, and will cut him off out of the midst of his people,—because of his seed, hath he given unto Molech, seeing that he hath made unclean my sanctuary, even to the extent of profaning my holy name.
text.KJV: And I will set my face against that man, and will cut him off from among his people; because he hath given of his seed unto Molech, to defile my sanctuary, and to profane my holy name.
text.NABRE: I myself will turn against and cut off that individual from among the people; for in the giving of offspring to Molech, my sanctuary was defiled and my holy name was profaned.
text.DRB: And I will set my face against him: and will cut him off from the midst of his people, because he hath given of his seed to Moloch, and hath contaminated my Sanctuary, and polluted my holy name.

text.NRSV: And if the people of the land should ever hide their eyes from those persons, when they give of their offspring to Molech, and do not put them to death,
text.Rotherham: But, if the people of the land, do even hide, their eyes from that man, when he giveth of his seed unto Molech,—so as not to put him to death,
text.KJV: And if the people of the land do any ways hide their eyes from the man, when he giveth of his seed unto Molech, and kill him not:
text.NABRE: If the people of the land condone the giving of offspring to Molech, by failing to put the wrongdoer to death,
text.DRB: And if the people of the land neglecting, and as it were little esteeming my commandment, let alone the man that hath given of his seed to Moloch, and will not kill him:

text.NRSV: I myself will set my face against them and against their family, and will cut them off from their people, them and all who follow them in prostituting themselves to Molech.
text.Rotherham: then will I myself, set my face against that man, and against his family,—and will cut him off, and all that follow unchastely after him—in going unchastely after Molech—out of the midst of their people.
text.KJV: Then I will set my face against that man, and against his family, and will cut him off, and all that go a whoring after him, to commit whoredom with Molech, from among their people.
text.NABRE: I myself will turn against that individual and his or her family, and I will cut off from their people both the wrongdoer and all who follow this person by prostituting themselves with Molech.
text.DRB: I will set my face upon that man, & his kindred, and will cut off both him, and all that consented with him, to commit fornication with Moloch, out of the midst of their people.

text.NRSV: If any turn to mediums and wizards, prostituting themselves to them, I will set my face against them, and will cut them off from the people.
text.Rotherham: The person also that turneth unto mediums and unto oracles, in going unchastely after them, then will I set my face against that person, and will cut him off out of the midst of his people.
text.KJV: And the soul that turneth after such as have familiar spirits, and after wizards, to go a whoring after them, I will even set my face against that soul, and will cut him off from among his people.
text.NABRE: Should anyone turn to ghosts and spirits and prostitute oneself with them, I will turn against that person and cut such a one off from among the people.
text.DRB: The soul that shall decline to Magicians and soothsayers, and shall commit fornication with them, I will set my face against it, and destroy it out of the midst of his people.

text.NRSV: Consecrate yourselves therefore, and be holy; for I am the Lord your God.
text.Rotherham: Therefore shall ye hallow yourselves and be holy,—Because I—Yahweh, am your God.
text.KJV: Sanctify yourselves therefore, and be ye holy: for I am the Lord your God.
text.NABRE: Sanctify yourselves, then, and be holy; for I, the Lord, your God, am holy.
text.DRB: Sanctify yourselves, and be holy, because I am the Lord your God.

text.NRSV: Keep my statutes, and observe them; I am the Lord; I sanctify you.
text.Rotherham: Therefore shall ye observe my statutes, and do them,—I—Yahweh, am he that is hallowing you.
text.KJV: And ye shall keep my statutes, and do them: I am the Lord which sanctify you.
text.NABRE: Be careful, therefore, to observe my statutes. I, the Lord, make you holy.
text.DRB: Keep my precepts, & do them. I the Lord that sanctify you.

text.NRSV: All who curse father or mother shall be put to death; having cursed father or mother, their blood is upon them.
text.Rotherham: When, any man whatsoever, curseth his father or his mother, he shall surely be put to death,—his father or his mother, hath he cursed, his blood, shall be upon himself.
text.KJV: For every one that curseth his father or his mother shall be surely put to death: he hath cursed his father or his mother; his blood shall be upon him.
text.NABRE: Anyone who curses father or mother shall be put to death; and having cursed father or mother, such a one will bear the bloodguilt.
text.DRB: He that curseth his father, or mother, dying let him die: he hath cursed father, and mother, his blood be upon him.

text.NRSV: If a man commits adultery with the wife of his neighbor, both the adulterer and the adulteress shall be put to death.
text.Rotherham: And, any man, who committeth adultery with the wife of any other man, he that committeth adultery with the wife of his neighbour shall surely be put to death—the adulterer, and the adulteress.
text.KJV: And the man that committeth adultery with another man’s wife, even he that committeth adultery with his neighbour’s wife, the adulterer and the adulteress shall surely be put to death.
text.NABRE: If a man commits adultery with his neighbor’s wife, both the adulterer and the adulteress shall be put to death.
text.DRB: If any man commit adultery with another man's wife, and commit adultery with his neighbour's wife, dying let them die, both the adulterer and the adultress.

text.NRSV: The man who lies with his father’s wife has uncovered his father’s nakedness; both of them shall be put to death; their blood is upon them.
text.Rotherham: And any man who lieth with his father’s wife, the shame of his father, hath uncovered,—they both shall, surely be put to death—their blood shall be upon themselves.
text.KJV: And the man that lieth with his father’s wife hath uncovered his father’s nakedness: both of them shall surely be put to death; their blood shall be upon them.
text.NABRE: If a man disgraces his father by lying with his father’s wife, the two of them shall be put to death; their bloodguilt is upon them.
text.DRB: He that lieth with his stepmother, and revealeth the ignominy of his father, dying let both die: their blood be upon them.

text.NRSV: If a man lies with his daughter-in-law, both of them shall be put to death; they have committed perversion, their blood is upon them.
text.Rotherham: And any man who lieth with his daughter-in-law, they both shall, surely be put to death, confusion, have they wrought—their blood shall be upon themselves.
text.KJV: And if a man lie with his daughter in law, both of them shall surely be put to death: they have wrought confusion; their blood shall be upon them.
text.NABRE: If a man lies with his daughter-in-law, both of them shall be put to death; they have done what is perverse; their bloodguilt is upon them.
text.DRB: If any man lie with his daughter in law, let both die, because they have done an heinous fact: their blood be upon them.

text.NRSV: If a man lies with a male as with a woman, both of them have committed an abomination; they shall be put to death; their blood is upon them.
text.Rotherham: And any man who lieth with mankind as with womankind, an abomination, have both of them wrought,—they shall, surely be put to death,—their blood, shall be upon themselves.
text.KJV: If a man also lie with mankind, as he lieth with a woman, both of them have committed an abomination: they shall surely be put to death; their blood shall be upon them.
text.NABRE: If a man lies with a male as with a woman, they have committed an abomination; the two of them shall be put to death; their bloodguilt is upon them.
text.DRB: He that lieth with man as if he should company with woman, both have committed abomination, dying let them die: their blood be upon them.

text.NRSV: If a man takes a wife and her mother also, it is depravity; they shall be burned to death, both he and they, that there be no depravity among you.
text.Rotherham: And, any man, who taketh a woman and her mother, wickedness, it is,—in fire, shall both he and they be consumed, that wickedness be not in your midst.
text.KJV: And if a man take a wife and her mother, it is wickedness: they shall be burnt with fire, both he and they; that there be no wickedness among you.
text.NABRE: If a man marries a woman and her mother also, that is shameful conduct; the man and the two women as well shall be burned to death, so that shamefulness may not be found among you.
text.DRB: He that besides his wife the daughter, marrieth her mother, hath done wickedness: he shall burn alive with them, neither shall there so great abomination remain in the midst of you.

text.NRSV: If a man has sexual relations with an animal, he shall be put to death; and you shall kill the animal.
text.Rotherham: And, any man, who hath carnal knowledge of a beast, shall, surely be put to death,—and, the beast, shall ye slay.
text.KJV: And if a man lie with a beast, he shall surely be put to death: and ye shall slay the beast.
text.NABRE: If a man has sexual relations with an animal, the man shall be put to death, and you shall kill the animal.
text.DRB: He that shall company with beast & cattle, dying let him die: the beast also do ye kill.

text.NRSV: If a woman approaches any animal and has sexual relations with it, you shall kill the woman and the animal; they shall be put to death, their blood is upon them.
text.Rotherham: And, a woman, who approacheth unto any beast, to couch down thereto, then shalt thou slay the woman and the beast,—they shall surely be put to death,—their blood, shall be upon themselves.
text.KJV: And if a woman approach unto any beast, and lie down thereto, thou shalt kill the woman, and the beast: they shall surely be put to death; their blood shall be upon them.
text.NABRE: If a woman goes up to any animal to mate with it, you shall kill the woman and the animal; they shall both be put to death; their bloodguilt is upon them.
text.DRB: The woman that shall lie under any beast, shall be killed together with the same: their blood be upon them.

text.NRSV: If a man takes his sister, a daughter of his father or a daughter of his mother, and sees her nakedness, and she sees his nakedness, it is a disgrace, and they shall be cut off in the sight of their people; he has uncovered his sister’s nakedness, he shall bear his iniquity.
text.Rotherham: And, any man, who taketh his sister—his father’s daughter or his mother’s daughter, and vieweth her shame, and, she, vieweth his shame, a disgrace, it is,—they shall therefore be cut off in the sight of the sons of their people,—the shame of his sister, hath he uncovered—his iniquity, shall he bear.
text.KJV: And if a man shall take his sister, his father’s daughter, or his mother’s daughter, and see her nakedness, and she see his nakedness; it is a wicked thing; and they shall be cut off in the sight of their people: he hath uncovered his sister’s nakedness; he shall bear his iniquity.
text.NABRE: If a man marries his sister, his father’s daughter or his mother’s daughter, and they have intercourse with each other, that is disgraceful; they shall be publicly cut off from the people; the man shall bear the penalty of having had intercourse with his own sister.
text.DRB: He that taketh his sister the daughter of his father, or the daughter of his mother, & seeth her turpitude, & she beholdeth her brother's ignominy, they have committed a shameful thing: they shall be slain in the sight of their people, because they have revealed one another's turpitude, & they shall bear their iniquity.

text.NRSV: If a man lies with a woman having her sickness and uncovers her nakedness, he has laid bare her flow and she has laid bare her flow of blood; both of them shall be cut off from their people.
text.Rotherham: And, any man, who lieth with a woman having her sickness, and uncovereth her shame, her fountain, hath he exposed, she, also hath uncovered her fountain of blood,—they shall therefore both be cut off out of the midst of their people.
text.KJV: And if a man shall lie with a woman having her sickness, and shall uncover her nakedness; he hath discovered her fountain, and she hath uncovered the fountain of her blood: and both of them shall be cut off from among their people.
text.NABRE: If a man lies with a woman during her menstrual period and has intercourse with her, he has laid bare the source of her flow and she has uncovered it. The two of them shall be cut off from the people.
text.DRB: He that companieth with a woman in her menstrual flux, & revealeth her turpitude, & she openeth the fountain of her blood, both shall be destroyed out of the midst of their people.

text.NRSV: You shall not uncover the nakedness of your mother’s sister or of your father’s sister, for that would uncover one’s own flesh; they shall bear their iniquity.
text.Rotherham: And the shame of thy mother’s sister, or of thy father’s sister, shalt thou not uncover,—for his near of kin, hath he exposed—their iniquity, shall they bear.
text.KJV: And thou shalt not uncover the nakedness of thy mother’s sister, nor of thy father’s sister: for he uncovereth his near kin: they shall bear their iniquity.
text.NABRE: You shall not have intercourse with your mother’s sister or your father’s sister, because that dishonors one’s own flesh; they shall bear their penalty.
text.DRB: The turpitude of thy aunt by thy mother, and of thy aunt by thy father, thou shalt not discover: he that doth this, hath disclosed the ignominy of his flesh, both shall bear their iniquity.

text.NRSV: If a man lies with his uncle’s wife, he has uncovered his uncle’s nakedness; they shall bear their sin, they shall die childless.
text.Rotherham: And, any man, who lieth with his uncle’s wife, the shame of his uncle, hath he uncovered,—their sin, shall they bear—childless, shall they die.
text.KJV: And if a man shall lie with his uncle’s wife, he hath uncovered his uncle’s nakedness: they shall bear their sin; they shall die childless.
text.NABRE: If a man lies with his uncle’s wife, he disgraces his uncle; they shall bear the penalty; they shall die childless.
text.DRB: He that companieth with the wife of his uncle by the father, or of his uncle by the mother, & revealeth the ignominy of his kindred, both shall bear their sin: without children they shall die.

text.NRSV: If a man takes his brother’s wife, it is impurity; he has uncovered his brother’s nakedness; they shall be childless.
text.Rotherham: And, any man, who taketh his brother’s wife, impurity, it is,—the shame of his brother, hath he uncovered—childless, shall they remain.
text.KJV: And if a man shall take his brother’s wife, it is an unclean thing: he hath uncovered his brother’s nakedness; they shall be childless.
text.NABRE: If a man takes his brother’s wife, it is severe defilement and he has disgraced his brother; they shall be childless.
text.DRB: He that marrieth his brother's wife, doth an unlawful thing, he hath revealed his brother's turpitude: they shall be without children.

text.NRSV: You shall keep all my statutes and all my ordinances, and observe them, so that the land to which I bring you to settle in does not vomit you out.
text.Rotherham: Therefore shall ye observe all my statutes and all my regulations, and do them,—So shall the land whereinto I am bringing you to dwell therein, not vomit you forth;
text.KJV: Ye shall therefore keep all my statutes, and all my judgments, and do them: that the land, whither I bring you to dwell therein, spue you not out.
text.NABRE: Be careful to observe all my statutes and all my decrees; otherwise the land where I am bringing you to dwell will vomit you out.
text.DRB: Keep my laws, and judgements, and do them: lest the land which you shall enter into and inhabit, vomit out you also.

text.NRSV: You shall not follow the practices of the nation that I am driving out before you. Because they did all these things, I abhorred them.
text.Rotherham: So shall ye not walk in the—statutes of the nations, which I am casting out from before you,—For all these things, had they done, Therefore I abhorred them;
text.KJV: And ye shall not walk in the manners of the nation, which I cast out before you: for they committed all these things, and therefore I abhorred them.
text.NABRE: Do not conform, therefore, to the customs of the nations whom I am driving out of your way, because all these things that they have done have filled me with disgust for them.
text.DRB: Walk not in the ordinances of the nations, which I will expel before you. For all these things have they done, and I have abhorred them.

text.NRSV: But I have said to you: You shall inherit their land, and I will give it to you to possess, a land flowing with milk and honey. I am the Lord your God; I have separated you from the peoples.
text.Rotherham: And said unto you—Ye, shall possess their soil, Yea, I myself, will give it you to possess it, A land flowing with milk and honey,—I—Yahweh, am your God, who have distinguished you from the peoples;
text.KJV: But I have said unto you, Ye shall inherit their land, and I will give it unto you to possess it, a land that floweth with milk and honey: I am the Lord your God, which have separated you from other people.
text.NABRE: But to you I have said: You shall take possession of their land. I am giving it to you to possess, a land flowing with milk and honey. I, the Lord, am your God, who have set you apart from other peoples.
text.DRB: But to you I speak: Possess their land, which I will give you for an inheritance, a land flowing with milk and honey. I the Lord your God, that have separated you from other peoples.

text.NRSV: You shall therefore make a distinction between the clean animal and the unclean, and between the unclean bird and the clean; you shall not bring abomination on yourselves by animal or by bird or by anything with which the ground teems, which I have set apart for you to hold unclean.
text.Rotherham: Therefore shall ye make a distinction—Between the clean beasts, and the unclean,—And between the unclean birds and the clean,—So shall ye not make your persons abominable with beast, or with bird or with anything which creepeth upon the ground, which I have distinguished for you, as unclean.
text.KJV: Ye shall therefore put difference between clean beasts and unclean, and between unclean fowls and clean: and ye shall not make your souls abominable by beast, or by fowl, or by any manner of living thing that creepeth on the ground, which I have separated from you as unclean.
text.NABRE: You, too, must set apart, then, the clean animals from the unclean, and the clean birds from the unclean, so that you do not make yourselves detestable through any beast or bird or any creature which creeps on the ground that I have set apart for you as unclean.
text.DRB: Therefore do you also separate the clean beast, from the unclean, and the clean fowl from the unclean: pollute not your souls in beasts, and birds, and all things that move on the earth, and which I have shewed unto you to be polluted.

text.NRSV: You shall be holy to me; for I the Lord am holy, and I have separated you from the other peoples to be mine.
text.Rotherham: Therefore shall ye be unto me, holy persons, for, holy, am, I—Yahweh,—Therefore have I distinguished you from the peoples, that ye may be mine.
text.KJV: And ye shall be holy unto me: for I the Lord am holy, and have severed you from other people, that ye should be mine.
text.NABRE: To me, therefore, you shall be holy; for I, the Lord, am holy, and I have set you apart from other peoples to be my own.
text.DRB: You shall be holy unto me, because I the Lord am holy, and I have separated you from other peoples, that you should be mine.

text.NRSV: A man or a woman who is a medium or a wizard shall be put to death; they shall be stoned to death, their blood is upon them.
text.Rotherham: And as touching man or woman, when there shall be in them a familiar spirit, or the spirit of an oracle, they shall, surely be put to death,—with stones, shall they be stoned—their blood, shall be upon themselves.
text.KJV: A man also or woman that hath a familiar spirit, or that is a wizard, shall surely be put to death: they shall stone them with stones: their blood shall be upon them.
text.NABRE: A man or a woman who acts as a medium or clairvoyant shall be put to death. They shall be stoned to death; their bloodguilt is upon them.
text.DRB: Man, or woman, in whom is a pythonical or divining spirit, dying let them die: they shall stone them: their blood be upon them.

text.NRSV: The Lord said to Moses: Speak to the priests, the sons of Aaron, and say to them: No one shall defile himself for any dead person among his kin,
text.Rotherham: And Yahweh said unto Moses, Say unto the priests, the sons of Aaron,—yea thou shalt say unto them, For a dead person, shall no one make himself unclean among his people:
text.KJV: And the Lord said unto Moses, Speak unto the priests the sons of Aaron, and say unto them, There shall none be defiled for the dead among his people:
text.NABRE: The Lord said to Moses: Speak to the priests, Aaron’s sons, and tell them: None of you shall make himself unclean for any dead person among his kindred,
text.DRB: Our Lord said also to Moyses: Speak to the Priests the sons of Aaron, and thou shalt say to them: Let not a priest be contaminated in the deaths of his citizens,

text.NRSV: except for his nearest kin: his mother, his father, his son, his daughter, his brother;
text.Rotherham: Saving for his kin, that are near unto him, for his mother or for his father, or for his son or for his daughter, or for his brother;
text.KJV: But for his kin, that is near unto him, that is, for his mother, and for his father, and for his son, and for his daughter, and for his brother,
text.NABRE: except for his nearest relatives, his mother or father, his son or daughter, his brother
text.DRB: but only in his kin, and nigh of blood, that is to say, upon his father and mother, and son, and daughter, brother also,

text.NRSV: likewise, for a virgin sister, close to him because she has had no husband, he may defile himself for her.
text.Rotherham: or for his sister, a virgin who is near unto him, who belongeth not unto a husband,—for her, he may make himself unclean:
text.KJV: And for his sister a virgin, that is nigh unto him, which hath had no husband; for her may he be defiled.
text.NABRE: or his unmarried sister, who is of his own family while she remains single; for these he may make himself unclean.
text.DRB: and sister, being a virgin which hath not been married to a husband:

text.NRSV: But he shall not defile himself as a husband among his people and so profane himself.
text.Rotherham: He shall not make himself unclean being a chief among his people,—by profaning himself:
text.KJV: But he shall not defile himself, being a chief man among his people, to profane himself.
text.NABRE: But as a husband among his kindred he shall not make himself unclean and thus profane himself.
text.DRB: but neither in the Prince of his people shall he be contaminated.

text.NRSV: They shall not make bald spots upon their heads, or shave off the edges of their beards, or make any gashes in their flesh.
text.Rotherham: They shall not make a baldness behind in their head, And the border of their beard, shall they not cut off,—And, in their flesh, shall they not make incisions.
text.KJV: They shall not make baldness upon their head, neither shall they shave off the corner of their beard, nor make any cuttings in their flesh.
text.NABRE: The priests shall not make bald the crown of their head, nor shave the edges of their beard, nor lacerate their body.
text.DRB: Neither shall they shave their head nor beard, nor make incisions in their flesh.

text.NRSV: They shall be holy to their God, and not profane the name of their God; for they offer the Lord’s offerings by fire, the food of their God, and therefore they shall be holy.
text.Rotherham: Holy persons, shall they be unto their God, and not profane the name of their God,—for, the altar-flames of Yahweh, the food of their God, do they bring near—Therefore shall they be holy.
text.KJV: They shall be holy unto their God, and not profane the name of their God: for the offerings of the Lord made by fire, and the bread of their God, they do offer: therefore they shall be holy.
text.NABRE: They shall be holy to their God, and shall not profane their God’s name, since they offer the oblations of the Lord, the food of their God; so they must be holy.
text.DRB: They shall be holy to their God, and shall not pollute his name: for the burnt sacrifice of the Lord, and breads of their God do they offer, and therefore they shall be holy.

text.NRSV: They shall not marry a prostitute or a woman who has been defiled; neither shall they marry a woman divorced from her husband. For they are holy to their God,
text.Rotherham: A woman that is unchaste or dishonoured, shall they not take, And a woman divorced from her husband, shall they not take,—For holy, he is unto his God.
text.KJV: They shall not take a wife that is a whore, or profane; neither shall they take a woman put away from her husband: for he is holy unto his God.
text.NABRE: A priest shall not marry a woman debased by prostitution, nor a woman who has been divorced by her husband; for the priest is holy to his God.
text.DRB: Awhore and a vile strumpet he shall not take to wife, nor her, that is put away from her husband: because they are consecrated to their God,

text.NRSV: and you shall treat them as holy, since they offer the food of your God; they shall be holy to you, for I the Lord, who sanctify you, am holy.
text.Rotherham: Therefore shalt thou hold him as holy, for the food of thy God, doth he bring near,—Holy, shall he be to thee, For, holy, am I—Yahweh, who am making you holy.
text.KJV: Thou shalt sanctify him therefore; for he offereth the bread of thy God: he shall be holy unto thee: for I the Lord, which sanctify you, am holy.
text.NABRE: Honor him as holy for he offers the food of your God; he shall be holy to you, because I, the Lord, am holy who make you holy.
text.DRB: and offer the breads of proposition. Be they holy therefore, because I also am holy, the Lord, that sanctify them.

text.NRSV: When the daughter of a priest profanes herself through prostitution, she profanes her father; she shall be burned to death.
text.Rotherham: And, the daughter of any priest, when she profaneth herself by unchastity, Her father, she, doth profane, with fire, shall she be consumed.
text.KJV: And the daughter of any priest, if she profane herself by playing the whore, she profaneth her father: she shall be burnt with fire.
text.NABRE: If a priest’s daughter debases herself by prostitution, she thereby debases her father; she shall be burned with fire.
text.DRB: The daughter of a Priest, if she be taken in whoredom, & dishonour the name of her father, shall be burnt with fire.

text.NRSV: The priest who is exalted above his fellows, on whose head the anointing oil has been poured and who has been consecrated to wear the vestments, shall not dishevel his hair, nor tear his vestments.
text.Rotherham: And, as for the high priest from among his brethren upon whose head is poured the anointing oil, and who is installed, by putting on the garments, His head, shall he not bare, And, his garments, shall he not rend;
text.KJV: And he that is the high priest among his brethren, upon whose head the anointing oil was poured, and that is consecrated to put on the garments, shall not uncover his head, nor rend his clothes;
text.NABRE: The most exalted of the priests, upon whose head the anointing oil has been poured and who has been ordained to wear the special vestments, shall not dishevel his hair or rend his garments,
text.DRB: The Grand Bishop, that is to say the Priest that is greatest among his brethren, upon whose head hath been poured the oil of unction, and whose hands were consecrated in priesthood, and who was revested with the holy vestments, shall not uncover his head, he shall not rent his garments:

text.NRSV: He shall not go where there is a dead body; he shall not defile himself even for his father or mother.
text.Rotherham: And, unto no persons of the dead, shall he go in,—For his father or for his mother, shall he not make himself unclean;
text.KJV: Neither shall he go in to any dead body, nor defile himself for his father, or for his mother;
text.NABRE: nor shall he go near any dead person. Not even for his father or mother may he thus become unclean;
text.DRB: and to no dead person shall he enter in at all. Upon his father also, and mother shall he not be contaminated.

text.NRSV: He shall not go outside the sanctuary and thus profane the sanctuary of his God; for the consecration of the anointing oil of his God is upon him: I am the Lord.
text.Rotherham: And, out of the sanctuary, shall he not go forth, so shall he not profane the sanctuary of his God,—for the consecration of the anointing oil of his God is upon him—I, am Yahweh.
text.KJV: Neither shall he go out of the sanctuary, nor profane the sanctuary of his God; for the crown of the anointing oil of his God is upon him: I am the Lord.
text.NABRE: nor shall he leave the sanctuary and profane the sanctuary of his God, for the consecration of the anointing oil of his God is upon him. I am the Lord.
text.DRB: Neither shall he go forth out of the holy places, lest he pollute the Sanctuary of the Lord, because the oil of the holy unction of his God is upon him. I the Lord.

text.NRSV: He shall marry only a woman who is a virgin.
text.Rotherham: But, he, shall take a woman in her virginity:
text.KJV: And he shall take a wife in her virginity.
text.NABRE: He shall marry only a woman who is a virgin.
text.DRB: He shall take a virgin unto his wife:

text.NRSV: A widow, or a divorced woman, or a woman who has been defiled, a prostitute, these he shall not marry. He shall marry a virgin of his own kin,
text.Rotherham: A widow or a divorced woman or one dishonoured—an unchaste woman, these, shall he not take, But, a virgin from among his own kinsfolk, shall he take to wife;
text.KJV: A widow, or a divorced woman, or profane, or an harlot, these shall he not take: but he shall take a virgin of his own people to wife.
text.NABRE: He shall not marry a widow or a woman who has been divorced or one who has been debased by prostitution, but only a virgin, taken from his kindred, he shall marry,
text.DRB: but a widow and her that is put away, and a filth, and a whore he shall not take, but a maid of his own people:
