text.NRSV: that he may not profane his offspring among his kin; for I am the Lord; I sanctify him.
text.Rotherham: So shall he not profane his seed, among his kinsfolk,—For I—Yahweh, do hallow him.
text.KJV: Neither shall he profane his seed among his people: for I the Lord do sanctify him.
text.NABRE: so that he not profane his offspring among his kindred. I, the Lord, make him holy. Priestly Blemishes.
text.DRB: that he mingle not the stock of his kindred with the common people of his nation: because I am the Lord that sanctify him.

text.NRSV: The Lord spoke to Moses, saying:
text.Rotherham: And Yahweh spake unto Moses, saying:
text.KJV: And the Lord spake unto Moses, saying,
text.NABRE: The Lord said to Moses:
text.DRB: And our Lord spake to Moyses, saying:

text.NRSV: Speak to Aaron and say: No one of your offspring throughout their generations who has a blemish may approach to offer the food of his God.
text.Rotherham: Speak unto Aaron, saying,—No man of thy seed, unto their generations in whom shall be any blemish, shall come near, to present the food of his God.
text.KJV: Speak unto Aaron, saying, Whosoever he be of thy seed in their generations that hath any blemish, let him not approach to offer the bread of his God.
text.NABRE: Say to Aaron: None of your descendants, throughout their generations, who has any blemish shall come forward to offer the food of his God.
text.DRB: Speak to Aaron: the man of thy seed throughout their families, that hath a blemish, shall not offer breads to his God,

text.NRSV: For no one who has a blemish shall draw near, one who is blind or lame, or one who has a mutilated face or a limb too long,
text.Rotherham: Surely, no man in whom is any blemish, shall come near,—No man who is blind or lame, or hath a flat nose, or is lanky;
text.KJV: For whatsoever man he be that hath a blemish, he shall not approach: a blind man, or a lame, or he that hath a flat nose, or any thing superfluous,
text.NABRE: Anyone who has any of the following blemishes may not come forward: he who is blind, or lame, or who has a split lip, or a limb too long,
text.DRB: neither shall he approach to his ministry: If he be blind, if lame, if he have a little, or a great, or a crooked nose,

text.NRSV: or one who has a broken foot or a broken hand,
text.Rotherham: nor any man who hath a broken foot,—or a broken hand;
text.KJV: Or a man that is brokenfooted, or brokenhanded,
text.NABRE: or a broken leg or arm,
text.DRB: if his foot be broken, if his hand,

text.NRSV: or a hunchback, or a dwarf, or a man with a blemish in his eyes or an itching disease or scabs or crushed testicles.
text.Rotherham: or is hump-backed or a dwarf, or hath defective vision,—or hath scurvy or scab, or is crushed in the stones:
text.KJV: Or crookbackt, or a dwarf, or that hath a blemish in his eye, or be scurvy, or scabbed, or hath his stones broken;
text.NABRE: or who is a hunchback or dwarf or has a growth in the eye, or who is afflicted with sores, scabs, or crushed testicles.
text.DRB: if he be crook backed, or blear-eyed, or have a pearl in his eye, or a continual scab, or dry scurf in his body, or be burnt.

text.NRSV: No descendant of Aaron the priest who has a blemish shall come near to offer the Lord’s offerings by fire; since he has a blemish, he shall not come near to offer the food of his God.
text.Rotherham: No, man in whom is a blemish of the seed of Aaron the priest, shall approach, to bring near the altar-flames of Yahweh,—a blemish, is in him, the food of his God, shall he not approach to bring near:
text.KJV: No man that hath a blemish of the seed of Aaron the priest shall come nigh to offer the offerings of the Lord made by fire: he hath a blemish; he shall not come nigh to offer the bread of his God.
text.NABRE: No descendant of Aaron the priest who has any such blemish may draw near to offer the oblations of the Lord; on account of his blemish he may not draw near to offer the food of his God.
text.DRB: Every one that hath a blemish of the seed of Aaron the Priest, shall not approach to offer the hosts to the Lord, nor the breads to his God.

text.NRSV: He may eat the food of his God, of the most holy as well as of the holy,
text.Rotherham: Of the food of his God, both of the most holy,—and the holy, may he eat:
text.KJV: He shall eat the bread of his God, both of the most holy, and of the holy.
text.NABRE: He may, however, eat the food of his God: of the most sacred as well as sacred offerings.
text.DRB: He shall eat notwithstanding of the breads that are offered in the Sanctuary,

text.NRSV: but he shall not come near the curtain or approach the altar, because he has a blemish, that he may not profane my sanctuaries; for I am the Lord; I sanctify them.
text.Rotherham: Only, unto the veil, shall he not come in and unto the altar, shall he not approach—because, a blemish, is in him, so shall he not profane my sanctuaries, For, I—Yahweh, am hallowing them.
text.KJV: Only he shall not go in unto the vail, nor come nigh unto the altar, because he hath a blemish; that he profane not my sanctuaries: for I the Lord do sanctify them.
text.NABRE: Only, he may not enter through the veil nor draw near to the altar on account of his blemish; he shall not profane my sacred precincts, for it is I, the Lord, who make them holy.
text.DRB: yet so that he enter not within the veil, or approach the altar, because he hath a blemish, and he must not contaminate my Sanctuary. I the Lord that sanctify them.

text.NRSV: Thus Moses spoke to Aaron and to his sons and to all the people of Israel.
text.Rotherham: And Moses spake [these things] unto Aaron, and unto his sons,—and unto all the sons of Israel.
text.KJV: And Moses told it unto Aaron, and to his sons, and unto all the children of Israel.
text.NABRE: Moses, therefore, told this to Aaron and his sons and to all the Israelites.
text.DRB: Moyses therefore spake to Aaron, and to his sons, and to all Israel, all things that had been commanded him.

text.NRSV: The Lord spoke to Moses, saying:
text.Rotherham: And Yahweh spake unto Moses, saying:
text.KJV: And the Lord spake unto Moses, saying,
text.NABRE: The Lord said to Moses:
text.DRB: Our Lord also spake to Moyses, saying:

text.NRSV: Direct Aaron and his sons to deal carefully with the sacred donations of the people of Israel, which they dedicate to me, so that they may not profane my holy name; I am the Lord.
text.Rotherham: Speak unto Aaron, and unto his sons, That they hold sacredly aloof from the holy things of the sons of Israel, so as not to profane my holy name,—in the things which they are hallowing unto me—I, am Yahweh.
text.KJV: Speak unto Aaron and to his sons, that they separate themselves from the holy things of the children of Israel, and that they profane not my holy name in those things which they hallow unto me: I am the Lord.
text.NABRE: Tell Aaron and his sons to treat with respect the sacred offerings which the Israelites consecrate to me; otherwise they will profane my holy name. I am the Lord.
text.DRB: speak to Aaron and to his sons, that they beware of those that are the consecrated things of the children of Israel, and contaminate not the name of the things sanctified to me, which they offer. I the Lord.

text.NRSV: Say to them: If anyone among all your offspring throughout your generations comes near the sacred donations, which the people of Israel dedicate to the Lord, while he is in a state of uncleanness, that person shall be cut off from my presence: I am the Lord.
text.Rotherham: Say unto them—Unto your generations, as touching any man who cometh near from among any of your seed, unto the hallowed things which the sons of Israel may hallow unto Yahweh, while his uncleanness is upon him, then shall that person be cut off from before me—I, am Yahweh.
text.KJV: Say unto them, Whosoever he be of all your seed among your generations, that goeth unto the holy things, which the children of Israel hallow unto the Lord, having his uncleanness upon him, that soul shall be cut off from my presence: I am the Lord.
text.NABRE: Tell them: If any one of you, or of your descendants in any future generation, dares, while he is in a state of uncleanness, to draw near the sacred offerings which the Israelites consecrate to the Lord, such a one shall be cut off from my presence. I am the Lord.
text.DRB: Say to them, and to their posterity: Every man of your stock, that approacheth to those things that are consecrated, & which the children of Israel have offered to the Lord, in whom there is uncleanness, shall perish before the Lord. I am the Lord.

text.NRSV: No one of Aaron’s offspring who has a leprous disease or suffers a discharge may eat of the sacred donations until he is clean. Whoever touches anything made unclean by a corpse or a man who has had an emission of semen,
text.Rotherham: No man soever of the seed of Aaron who is a leper, or hath a flux, shall eat of the holy things, until he be clean,—And as for him who toucheth anything that is unclean by the dead, or a man whose seed goeth from him;
text.KJV: What man soever of the seed of Aaron is a leper, or hath a running issue; he shall not eat of the holy things, until he be clean. And whoso toucheth any thing that is unclean by the dead, or a man whose seed goeth from him;
text.NABRE: No descendant of Aaron who is stricken with a scaly infection, or who suffers from a genital discharge, may eat of the sacred offerings, until he again becomes clean. Moreover, if anyone touches a person who has become unclean by contact with a corpse, or if anyone has had an emission of semen,
text.DRB: The man of the seed of Aaron that is a leper, or hath a flux of seed shall not eat of those things that are sanctified to me until he be healed. He that toucheth a thing unclean by occasion of that is dead, and he from whom issueth seed as it were of copulation,

text.NRSV: and whoever touches any swarming thing by which he may be made unclean or any human being by whom he may be made unclean—whatever his uncleanness may be—
text.Rotherham: or a man who toucheth any creeping thing, which is unclean to him,—or [toucheth] any human being who hath uncleanness, to the extent of any thing that maketh him unclean,
text.KJV: Or whosoever toucheth any creeping thing, whereby he may be made unclean, or a man of whom he may take uncleanness, whatsoever uncleanness he hath;
text.NABRE: or if anyone touches any swarming creature whose uncleanness is contagious or any person whose uncleanness, of whatever kind it may be, is contagious—
text.DRB: and he that toucheth a creeping beast, and whatsoever unclean thing, the touching whereof is filthy,

text.NRSV: the person who touches any such shall be unclean until evening and shall not eat of the sacred donations unless he has washed his body in water.
text.Rotherham: any person who toucheth any such, shall then be unclean until the evening,—and shall not eat of the hallowed things, unless he bathe his flesh in water.
text.KJV: The soul which hath touched any such shall be unclean until even, and shall not eat of the holy things, unless he wash his flesh with water.
text.NABRE: the one who touches such as these shall be unclean until evening and may not eat of the sacred portions until he has first bathed his body in water.
text.DRB: shall be unclean until even, and shall not eat those things that are sanctified: but when he hath washed his flesh with water,

text.NRSV: When the sun sets he shall be clean; and afterward he may eat of the sacred donations, for they are his food.
text.Rotherham: When the sun tooth in, then is he clean,—and thereafter, he may eat of the hallowed things, for, his food, it is.
text.KJV: And when the sun is down, he shall be clean, and shall afterward eat of the holy things; because it is his food.
text.NABRE: Then, when the sun sets, he shall be clean. Only then may he eat of the sacred offerings, for they are his food.
text.DRB: and the sun is down, then being cleansed he shall eat of the sanctified things, because it is his meat.

text.NRSV: That which died or was torn by wild animals he shall not eat, becoming unclean by it: I am the Lord.
text.Rotherham: That which dieth of itself or is torn in pieces, shall he not eat to make himself unclean thereby,—I, am Yahweh.
text.KJV: That which dieth of itself, or is torn with beasts, he shall not eat to defile himself therewith: I am the Lord.
text.NABRE: He shall not make himself unclean by eating of any animal that has died of itself or has been killed by wild beasts. I am the Lord.
text.DRB: Carrion and that which was taken of a beast, they shall not eat, nor be polluted in them. I am the Lord.

text.NRSV: They shall keep my charge, so that they may not incur guilt and die in the sanctuary for having profaned it: I am the Lord; I sanctify them.
text.Rotherham: Therefore shall they keep my charge, and shall not bear, for it, sin, and die therein, when they profane it,—I—Yahweh, am hallowing them.
text.KJV: They shall therefore keep mine ordinance, lest they bear sin for it, and die therefore, if they profane it: I the Lord do sanctify them.
text.NABRE: They shall keep my charge so that they will not bear the punishment in this matter and die for their profanation. I am the Lord who makes them holy.
text.DRB: Let them keep my precepts, that they be not subject to sin, and die in the Sanctuary, when they shall have polluted it. I the Lord that sanctify them.

text.NRSV: No lay person shall eat of the sacred donations. No bound or hired servant of the priest shall eat of the sacred donations;
text.Rotherham: And, no stranger, shall eat what is hallowed,—neither, one who dwelleth with a priest nor a hireling shall eat what is hallowed.
text.KJV: There shall no stranger eat of the holy thing: a sojourner of the priest, or an hired servant, shall not eat of the holy thing.
text.NABRE: Neither an unauthorized person nor a priest’s tenant or laborer may eat of any sacred offering.
text.DRB: No stranger shall eat of the sanctified things, the Priest's guest and hireling shall not eat of them.

text.NRSV: but if a priest acquires anyone by purchase, the person may eat of them; and those that are born in his house may eat of his food.
text.Rotherham: But when, a priest, buyeth any person, as the purchase of his silver, he may eat thereof,—and, the children of his household, they, may eat of his food.
text.KJV: But if the priest buy any soul with his money, he shall eat of it, and he that is born in his house: they shall eat of his meat.
text.NABRE: But a slave whom a priest acquires by purchase or who is born in his house may eat of his food.
text.DRB: But whom the Priest hath bought, and he that is his servant born in his house, these shall eat of them.

text.NRSV: If a priest’s daughter marries a layman, she shall not eat of the offering of the sacred donations;
text.Rotherham: And, when, a priest’s daughter, belongeth to a husband, who is a stranger, she, of the heave-offering of the hallowed things, may not eat.
text.KJV: If the priest’s daughter also be married unto a stranger, she may not eat of an offering of the holy things.
text.NABRE: A priest’s daughter who is married to an unauthorized person may not eat of the sacred contributions.
text.DRB: If the daughter of a Priest be married to any of the people: of those things that are sanctified, and of the first fruits she shall not eat.

text.NRSV: but if a priest’s daughter is widowed or divorced, without offspring, and returns to her father’s house, as in her youth, she may eat of her father’s food. No lay person shall eat of it.
text.Rotherham: But, when a priest’s daughter, cometh to be a widow or divorced and hath no seed, and so she returneth unto the house of her father, as in her youth, of the food of her father, she may eat,—but no stranger, shall eat thereof.
text.KJV: But if the priest’s daughter be a widow, or divorced, and have no child, and is returned unto her father’s house, as in her youth, she shall eat of her father’s meat: but there shall no stranger eat thereof.
text.NABRE: But if a priest’s daughter is widowed or divorced and, having no children, returns to her father’s house, she may then eat of her father’s food as in her youth. No unauthorized person, however, may eat of it.
text.DRB: But if she be a widow, or put away, and without children return to her father's house: as she was wont being a maid, she shall be fed with her father's meats. No stranger hath power to eat of them.

text.NRSV: If a man eats of the sacred donation unintentionally, he shall add one-fifth of its value to it, and give the sacred donation to the priest.
text.Rotherham: And, when, any man, eateth what is hallowed, by mistake, then shall he add the fifth part of it thereunto, and give unto the priest the hallowed thing:
text.KJV: And if a man eat of the holy thing unwittingly, then he shall put the fifth part thereof unto it, and shall give it unto the priest with the holy thing.
text.NABRE: If such a one eats of a sacred offering through inadvertence, that person shall make restitution to the priest for the sacred offering, with an increment of one fifth of the amount.
text.DRB: He that eateth of the sanctified things by ignorance, shall add the fifth part with that which he did eat, and shall give it to the Priest into the Sanctuary.

text.NRSV: No one shall profane the sacred donations of the people of Israel, which they offer to the Lord,
text.Rotherham: So shall they not profane the hallowed things of the sons of Israel,—which they heave up unto Yahweh;
text.KJV: And they shall not profane the holy things of the children of Israel, which they offer unto the Lord;
text.NABRE: The priests shall not allow the sacred offerings which the Israelites contribute to the Lord to be profaned
text.DRB: Neither shall they contaminate the sanctified things of the children of Israel, which they offer to the Lord:

text.NRSV: causing them to bear guilt requiring a guilt offering, by eating their sacred donations: for I am the Lord; I sanctify them.
text.Rotherham: nor cause them to bear guilty iniquity, when they eat their hallowed things,—For, I—Yahweh, am hallowing them.
text.KJV: Or suffer them to bear the iniquity of trespass, when they eat their holy things: for I the Lord do sanctify them.
text.NABRE: nor make them incur a penalty when they eat their sacred offerings. For I, the Lord, make them holy.
text.DRB: lest perhaps they sustain the iniquity of their offence, when they shall have eaten the sanctified things. I the Lord that sanctify them.

text.NRSV: The Lord spoke to Moses, saying:
text.Rotherham: And Yahweh spake unto Moses, saying:
text.KJV: And the Lord spake unto Moses, saying,
text.NABRE: Unacceptable Victims. The Lord said to Moses:
text.DRB: And our Lord spake to Moyses, saying:

text.NRSV: Speak to Aaron and his sons and all the people of Israel and say to them: When anyone of the house of Israel or of the aliens residing in Israel presents an offering, whether in payment of a vow or as a freewill offering that is offered to the Lord as a burnt offering,
text.Rotherham: Speak unto Aaron, and unto his sons, and unto all the sons of Israel, and thou shalt say unto them—What man, soever, of the house of Israel, or of the sojourners in Israel, may bring near his oblation, as regardeth any of their vows, or any of their freewill offerings which they may bring near unto Yahweh, as an ascending-sacrifice,
text.KJV: Speak unto Aaron, and to his sons, and unto all the children of Israel, and say unto them, Whatsoever he be of the house of Israel, or of the strangers in Israel, that will offer his oblation for all his vows, and for all his freewill offerings, which they will offer unto the Lord for a burnt offering;
text.NABRE: Speak to Aaron and his sons and to all the Israelites, and tell them: When anyone of the house of Israel, or any alien residing in Israel, who presents an offering, brings a burnt offering as a votive offering or as a voluntary offering to the Lord,
text.DRB: Speak to Aaron, and to his sons, and to all the children of Israel, and thou shalt say to them: The man of the house of Israel, and of the strangers which dwell with you, that offereth his oblation, either paying his vows, or offering of his own accord, whatsoever that be which he presenteth for an holocaust of the Lord,

text.NRSV: to be acceptable in your behalf it shall be a male without blemish, of the cattle or the sheep or the goats.
text.Rotherham: that ye may be accepted, [it must be] a male without defect, of the beeves, or of the sheep, or of the goats:
text.KJV: Ye shall offer at your own will a male without blemish, of the beeves, of the sheep, or of the goats.
text.NABRE: if it is to be acceptable for you, it must be an unblemished male of the herd, of the sheep or of the goats.
text.DRB: to be offered by you, it shall be a male without spot, of beefs, & muttons, & of goats.

text.NRSV: You shall not offer anything that has a blemish, for it will not be acceptable in your behalf.
text.Rotherham: whatsoever hath in it a blemish, shall ye not bring near,—for it shall not be accepted for you.
text.KJV: But whatsoever hath a blemish, that shall ye not offer: for it shall not be acceptable for you.
text.NABRE: You shall not offer one that has any blemish, for such a one would not be acceptable on your behalf.
text.DRB: If it have a blemish, you shall not offer it, neither shall it be acceptable.

text.NRSV: When anyone offers a sacrifice of well-being to the Lord, in fulfillment of a vow or as a freewill offering, from the herd or from the flock, to be acceptable it must be perfect; there shall be no blemish in it.
text.Rotherham: And, whosoever would bring near a peace-offering unto Yahweh, to consecrate a vow or as a freewill-offering with a bullock or a sheep, without defect, shall it be, to be accepted, no, blemish, shall be therein.
text.KJV: And whosoever offereth a sacrifice of peace offerings unto the Lord to accomplish his vow, or a freewill offering in beeves or sheep, it shall be perfect to be accepted; there shall be no blemish therein.
text.NABRE: When anyone presents a communion sacrifice to the Lord from the herd or the flock in fulfillment of a vow, or as a voluntary offering, if it is to find acceptance, it must be unblemished; it shall not have any blemish.
text.DRB: The man that offereth a victim of pacifics to the Lord, either paying his vows, or offering of his own accord, as well of beefs as of muttons, shall offer it without blemish, that it may be acceptable: there shall be no blemish in it.

text.NRSV: Anything blind, or injured, or maimed, or having a wen, or mangy, or scabbed—you shall not offer to the Lord or put any of them on the altar as offerings by fire to the Lord.
text.Rotherham: Blind, or broken, or rent, or having a running sore, or scurvy, or scab, ye shall not bring these near unto Yahweh,—and no, altar-flame, shall ye present therefrom upon the altar, unto Yahweh.
text.KJV: Blind, or broken, or maimed, or having a wen, or scurvy, or scabbed, ye shall not offer these unto the Lord, nor make an offering by fire of them upon the altar unto the Lord.
text.NABRE: One that is blind or lame or maimed, or one that has running lesions or sores or scabs, you shall not offer to the Lord; do not put such an animal on the altar as an oblation to the Lord.
text.DRB: If it be blind, if it be broken, if it have a scar, if blisters, or a scab, or a dry scurf: you shall not offer them to the Lord, nor burn of them upon the Lord's altar.

text.NRSV: An ox or a lamb that has a limb too long or too short you may present for a freewill offering; but it will not be accepted for a vow.
text.Rotherham: Whether an ox or a lamb long or short limb, as a free-will offering, thou mayest offer it, but, for a vow, it shall not be accepted.
text.KJV: Either a bullock or a lamb that hath any thing superfluous or lacking in his parts, that mayest thou offer for a freewill offering; but for a vow it shall not be accepted.
text.NABRE: An ox or a sheep that has a leg that is too long or is stunted you may indeed present as a voluntary offering, but it will not be acceptable as a votive offering.
text.DRB: An ox and a sheep, having the ear and the tail cut off, thou mayst offer voluntarily, but a vow cannot be payed of them.

text.NRSV: Any animal that has its testicles bruised or crushed or torn or cut, you shall not offer to the Lord; such you shall not do within your land,
text.Rotherham: But that which is bruised in the stones or broken therein, or torn or cut, shall ye neither bring ear unto Yahweh, nor on your own land, shall ye offer.
text.KJV: Ye shall not offer unto the Lord that which is bruised, or crushed, or broken, or cut; neither shall ye make any offering thereof in your land.
text.NABRE: One that has its testicles bruised or crushed or torn out or cut off you shall not offer to the Lord. You shall neither do this in your own land
text.DRB: No beast that hath the stones bruised, or crushed, or cut and taken away, shall you offer to the Lord, & in your land make not this at all.

text.NRSV: nor shall you accept any such animals from a foreigner to offer as food to your God; since they are mutilated, with a blemish in them, they shall not be accepted in your behalf.
text.Rotherham: Even at the hand of the son of a stranger, shall ye not bring near the food of your God of any of these,—for, their damage is in them, a blemish, is in them, they shall not be accepted for you.
text.KJV: Neither from a stranger’s hand shall ye offer the bread of your God of any of these; because their corruption is in them, and blemishes be in them: they shall not be accepted for you.
text.NABRE: nor receive from a foreigner any such animals to offer up as the food of your God; since they are deformed or blemished, they will not be acceptable on your behalf.
text.DRB: Of the hand of a stranger you shall not offer breads to your God, and what other thing soever he would give: because they are all corrupted & blemished: you shall not receive them.

text.NRSV: The Lord spoke to Moses, saying:
text.Rotherham: And Yahweh spake unto Moses, saying:—
text.KJV: And the Lord spake unto Moses, saying,
text.NABRE: The Lord said to Moses:
text.DRB: And our Lord spake to Moyses, saying:

text.NRSV: When an ox or a sheep or a goat is born, it shall remain seven days with its mother, and from the eighth day on it shall be acceptable as the Lord’s offering by fire.
text.Rotherham: When, a beeve or a sheep or a goat, is brought forth, then shall it be seven days under its dam,—and, from the eighth day and onwards, it shall be accepted as an oblation of an altar-flame unto Yahweh,
text.KJV: When a bullock, or a sheep, or a goat, is brought forth, then it shall be seven days under the dam; and from the eighth day and thenceforth it shall be accepted for an offering made by fire unto the Lord.
text.NABRE: When an ox or a lamb or a goat is born, it shall remain with its mother for seven days; only from the eighth day onward will it be acceptable, to be offered as an oblation to the Lord.
text.DRB: An ox, a sheep, and a goat, when they are brought forth, shall be seven days under the udder of their dam: but the eighth day and so forward they may be offered to the Lord.

text.NRSV: But you shall not slaughter, from the herd or the flock, an animal with its young on the same day.
text.Rotherham: Whether cow or ewe—it and its young, shall ye not slaughter on one day.
text.KJV: And whether it be cow or ewe, ye shall not kill it and her young both in one day.
text.NABRE: You shall not slaughter an ox or a sheep on one and the same day with its young.
text.DRB: Whether it be a beef, or a sheep, they shall not be immolated in one day with their young ones.

text.NRSV: When you sacrifice a thanksgiving offering to the Lord, you shall sacrifice it so that it may be acceptable in your behalf.
text.Rotherham: And when ye would offer a sacrifice of thanksgiving unto Yahweh, for your acceptance, shall ye offer it.
text.KJV: And when ye will offer a sacrifice of thanksgiving unto the Lord, offer it at your own will.
text.NABRE: Whenever you offer a thanksgiving sacrifice to the Lord, so offer it that it may be acceptable on your behalf;
text.DRB: If you immolate an host for thanks-giving to the Lord, that he may be placable,

text.NRSV: It shall be eaten on the same day; you shall not leave any of it until morning: I am the Lord.
text.Rotherham: On the same day, shall it be eaten, ye shall not leave thereof until morning,—I, am Yahweh.
text.KJV: On the same day it shall be eaten up; ye shall leave none of it until the morrow: I am the Lord.
text.NABRE: it must be eaten on the same day; none of it shall be left over until morning. I am the Lord.
text.DRB: the same day you shall eat it, there shall not ought remain until the morning of the next day. I the Lord.

text.NRSV: Thus you shall keep my commandments and observe them: I am the Lord.
text.Rotherham: So then ye shall observe my commandments, and do them,—I, am Yahweh.
text.KJV: Therefore shall ye keep my commandments, and do them: I am the Lord.
text.NABRE: Be careful to observe my commandments. I am the Lord.
text.DRB: Keep my commandments, & do them. I the Lord.

text.NRSV: You shall not profane my holy name, that I may be sanctified among the people of Israel: I am the Lord; I sanctify you,
text.Rotherham: So shall ye not profane my holy name, So shall I be hallowed in the midst of the sons of Israel,—I, am Yahweh, who am hallowing you;
text.KJV: Neither shall ye profane my holy name; but I will be hallowed among the children of Israel: I am the Lord which hallow you,
text.NABRE: Do not profane my holy name, that in the midst of the Israelites I may be hallowed. I, the Lord, make you holy,
text.DRB: Pollute not my holy name that I may be sanctified in the midst of the children of Israel. I the Lord that sanctify you,

text.NRSV: I who brought you out of the land of Egypt to be your God: I am the Lord.
text.Rotherham: Who brought you forth out of the land of Egypt, that I might become your God,—I, am Yahweh.
text.KJV: That brought you out of the land of Egypt, to be your God: I am the Lord.
text.NABRE: who led you out of the land of Egypt to be your God. I am the Lord.
text.DRB: and brought you out of the Land of Ægypt, that I might be your God. I the Lord.

text.NRSV: The Lord spoke to Moses, saying:
text.Rotherham: And Yahweh spake unto Moses, saying:
text.KJV: And the Lord spake unto Moses, saying,
text.NABRE: The Lord said to Moses:
text.DRB: And our Lord spake to Moyses saying:

text.NRSV: Speak to the people of Israel and say to them: These are the appointed festivals of the Lord that you shall proclaim as holy convocations, my appointed festivals.
text.Rotherham: Speak unto the sons of Israel and thou shalt say unto them, As for the appointed seasons of Yahweh, which ye shall proclaim as holy convocations, these, are they—my appointed seasons:—
text.KJV: Speak unto the children of Israel, and say unto them, Concerning the feasts of the Lord, which ye shall proclaim to be holy convocations, even these are my feasts.
text.NABRE: Speak to the Israelites and tell them: The following are the festivals of the Lord, which you shall declare holy days. These are my festivals:
text.DRB: Speak to the children of Israel, and thou shalt say to them: These are the festivities of our Lord, which you shall call holy.

text.NRSV: Six days shall work be done; but the seventh day is a sabbath of complete rest, a holy convocation; you shall do no work: it is a sabbath to the Lord throughout your settlements.
text.Rotherham: Six days, shall work be done, but, on the seventh day, shall there be a sabbath of sacred rest a holy convocation, no work, shall ye do,—a sabbath, shall it be unto Yahweh, in all your dwellings.
text.KJV: Six days shall work be done: but the seventh day is the sabbath of rest, an holy convocation; ye shall do no work therein: it is the sabbath of the Lord in all your dwellings.
text.NABRE: For six days work may be done; but the seventh day is a sabbath of complete rest, a declared holy day; you shall do no work. It is the Lord ’s sabbath wherever you dwell.
text.DRB: Six days ye shall do work: the seventh day, because it is the rest of the sabbath, shall be called holy. No work shall you do in it: it is the Sabbath of the Lord in all your habitations.

text.NRSV: These are the appointed festivals of the Lord, the holy convocations, which you shall celebrate at the time appointed for them.
text.Rotherham: These, are the appointed seasons of Yahweh, holy convocations,—which ye shall proclaim in their appointed season:—
text.KJV: These are the feasts of the Lord, even holy convocations, which ye shall proclaim in their seasons.
text.NABRE: Passover. These are the festivals of the Lord, holy days which you shall declare at their proper time.
text.DRB: These therefore are the holy festivities of the Lord, which you must celebrate in their times.

text.NRSV: In the first month, on the fourteenth day of the month, at twilight, there shall be a passover offering to the Lord,
text.Rotherham: In the first month, on the fourteenth of the month, between the evenings, is a Passover unto Yahweh;
text.KJV: In the fourteenth day of the first month at even is the Lord’s passover.
text.NABRE: The Passover of the Lord falls on the fourteenth day of the first month, at the evening twilight.
text.DRB: The first month, the fourteenth day of the month at even, is the Phase of the Lord:

text.NRSV: and on the fifteenth day of the same month is the festival of unleavened bread to the Lord; seven days you shall eat unleavened bread.
text.Rotherham: and on the fifteenth day of this month, is the festival of unleavened cakes unto Yahweh,—seven days—unleavened cakes, shall ye eat.
text.KJV: And on the fifteenth day of the same month is the feast of unleavened bread unto the Lord: seven days ye must eat unleavened bread.
text.NABRE: The fifteenth day of this month is the Lord ’s feast of Unleavened Bread. For seven days you shall eat unleavened bread.
text.DRB: and the fifteenth day of this month is the solemnity of the Azymes of the Lord. Seven days shall you eat azymes.

text.NRSV: On the first day you shall have a holy convocation; you shall not work at your occupations.
text.Rotherham: On the first day—a holy convocation, shall there be to you: no laborious work, shall ye do.
text.KJV: In the first day ye shall have an holy convocation: ye shall do no servile work therein.
text.NABRE: On the first of these days you will have a declared holy day; you shall do no heavy work.
text.DRB: The first day shall be most solemn unto you, & holy: no servile work shall you do in it:

text.NRSV: For seven days you shall present the Lord’s offerings by fire; on the seventh day there shall be a holy convocation: you shall not work at your occupations.
text.Rotherham: So then ye shall bring near an altar-flame unto Yahweh, seven days,—on the seventh day, [shall there be] a holy convocation, no laborious work, shall ye do.
text.KJV: But ye shall offer an offering made by fire unto the Lord seven days: in the seventh day is an holy convocation: ye shall do no servile work therein.
text.NABRE: On each of the seven days you shall offer an oblation to the Lord. Then on the seventh day you will have a declared holy day; you shall do no heavy work.
text.DRB: but you shall offer sacrifice in fire to the Lord seven days, but the seventh day shall be more solemn, & more holy: and you shall do no servile work in it.

text.NRSV: The Lord spoke to Moses:
text.Rotherham: And Yahweh spake unto Moses saying:
text.KJV: And the Lord spake unto Moses, saying,
text.NABRE: The Lord said to Moses:
text.DRB: And our Lord spake to Moyses, saying:

text.NRSV: Speak to the people of Israel and say to them: When you enter the land that I am giving you and you reap its harvest, you shall bring the sheaf of the first fruits of your harvest to the priest.
text.Rotherham: Speak unto the sons of Israel, and thou shalt say unto them:—When ye enter into the land which, I, am giving you, and ye reap the harvest thereof, then shall ye bring in a sheaf of the first-ripe corn of your harvest, unto the priest;
text.KJV: Speak unto the children of Israel, and say unto them, When ye be come into the land which I give unto you, and shall reap the harvest thereof, then ye shall bring a sheaf of the firstfruits of your harvest unto the priest:
text.NABRE: Speak to the Israelites and tell them: When you come into the land which I am giving you, and reap its harvest, you shall bring the first sheaf of your harvest to the priest,
text.DRB: Speak to the children of Israel, and thou shalt say to them. When you shall be entered into the land, which I will give you, and shall reap your corn, you shall bring sheaves of ears, the first fruits of your harvest to the Priest:

text.NRSV: He shall raise the sheaf before the Lord, that you may find acceptance; on the day after the sabbath the priest shall raise it.
text.Rotherham: and he shall wave the sheaf before Yahweh, that ye may be accepted,—on the morrow of the sabbath, shall the priest wave it.
text.KJV: And he shall wave the sheaf before the Lord, to be accepted for you: on the morrow after the sabbath the priest shall wave it.
text.NABRE: who shall elevate the sheaf before the Lord that it may be acceptable on your behalf. On the day after the sabbath the priest shall do this.
text.DRB: who shall elevate the bundle before the Lord, the next day after the Sabbath, that it may be acceptable for you, and shall sanctify it.

text.NRSV: On the day when you raise the sheaf, you shall offer a lamb a year old, without blemish, as a burnt offering to the Lord.
text.Rotherham: And ye shall offer on the day when ye wave the sheaf, a he-lamb without defect, the finest of its year for an ascending-sacrifice unto Yahweh;
text.KJV: And ye shall offer that day when ye wave the sheaf an he lamb without blemish of the first year for a burnt offering unto the Lord.
text.NABRE: On this day, when your sheaf is elevated, you shall offer to the Lord for a burnt offering an unblemished yearling lamb.
text.DRB: And in the self-same day that the sheaf is consecrated, shall be killed a lamb without spot of a year old for an holocaust of the Lord.

text.NRSV: And the grain offering with it shall be two-tenths of an ephah of choice flour mixed with oil, an offering by fire of pleasing odor to the Lord; and the drink offering with it shall be of wine, one-fourth of a hin.
text.Rotherham: and the, meal-offering thereof shall be two-tenth parts of fine meal overflowed with oil, as an altar-flame unto Yahweh a satisfying odour,—and, the drink-offering thereof, [shall be] mine, the fourth of a hin.
text.KJV: And the meat offering thereof shall be two tenth deals of fine flour mingled with oil, an offering made by fire unto the Lord for a sweet savour: and the drink offering thereof shall be of wine, the fourth part of an hin.
text.NABRE: Its grain offering shall be two tenths of an ephah of bran flour mixed with oil, as a sweet-smelling oblation to the Lord; and its libation shall be a fourth of a hin of wine.
text.DRB: And the libaments shall be offered with it, two tenths of flour tempered with oil, for a burnt sacrifice of the Lord, and a most sweet odour: libaments also of wine, the fourth part of an hin.

text.NRSV: You shall eat no bread or parched grain or fresh ears until that very day, until you have brought the offering of your God: it is a statute forever throughout your generations in all your settlements.
text.Rotherham: And neither bread, nor roasted corn, nor garden-land grain, shall ye eat, until this selfsame day, until ye have brought in the oblation of your God,—an age-abiding statute unto your generations, in all your dwellings.
text.KJV: And ye shall eat neither bread, nor parched corn, nor green ears, until the selfsame day that ye have brought an offering unto your God: it shall be a statute for ever throughout your generations in all your dwellings.
text.NABRE: You shall not eat any bread or roasted grain or fresh kernels until this day, when you bring the offering for your God. This shall be a perpetual statute throughout your generations wherever you dwell.
text.DRB: Bread, and fried barley, & frumenty, you shall not eat of the corn, until the day that you offer thereof to your God. It is a precept for ever in your generations, and all your habitations.

text.NRSV: And from the day after the sabbath, from the day on which you bring the sheaf of the elevation offering, you shall count off seven weeks; they shall be complete.
text.Rotherham: Then shall ye keep count to yourselves from the morrow of the sabbath, from the day ye brought in the wave sheaf,—seven sabbaths complete, shall there be:
text.KJV: And ye shall count unto you from the morrow after the sabbath, from the day that ye brought the sheaf of the wave offering; seven sabbaths shall be complete:
text.NABRE: Pentecost. Beginning with the day after the sabbath, the day on which you bring the sheaf for elevation, you shall count seven full weeks;
text.DRB: You shall number therefore from the morrow after the Sabbath, wherein you did offer the sheaf of the first fruits, seven full weeks,

text.NRSV: You shall count until the day after the seventh sabbath, fifty days; then you shall present an offering of new grain to the Lord.
text.Rotherham: until on the morrow of the seventh sabbath, ye count fifty days,—then shall ye bring near a new meal-offering unto Yahweh.
text.KJV: Even unto the morrow after the seventh sabbath shall ye number fifty days; and ye shall offer a new meat offering unto the Lord.
text.NABRE: you shall count to the day after the seventh week, fifty days. Then you shall present a new grain offering to the Lord.
text.DRB: unto the morrow after the seventh week be expired, that is to say fifty days, and so you shall offer a new sacrifice to the Lord,

text.NRSV: You shall bring from your settlements two loaves of bread as an elevation offering, each made of two-tenths of an ephah; they shall be of choice flour, baked with leaven, as first fruits to the Lord.
text.Rotherham: Out of your dwellings, shall ye bring in two wave loaves of two-tenths of an ephah, of fine meal, shall they be, with leaven, shall they be baked,—first-fruits unto Yahweh.
text.KJV: Ye shall bring out of your habitations two wave loaves of two tenth deals: they shall be of fine flour; they shall be baken with leaven; they are the firstfruits unto the Lord.
text.NABRE: For the elevated offering of your first-ripened fruits to the Lord, you shall bring with you from wherever you live two loaves of bread made of two tenths of an ephah of bran flour and baked with leaven.
text.DRB: out of all your habitations, two loaves of first fruits, of two tenths of flour leavened, the which you shall bake for the first fruits of the Lord.

text.NRSV: You shall present with the bread seven lambs a year old without blemish, one young bull, and two rams; they shall be a burnt offering to the Lord, along with their grain offering and their drink offerings, an offering by fire of pleasing odor to the Lord.
text.Rotherham: Then shall ye bring near with the bread seven he-lambs without defect of the first year, and one young bullock, and two rams,—they shall be an ascending-sacrifice unto Yahweh, with their meal-offering, and their drink-offerings, an altar-flame of satisfying odour unto Yahweh.
text.KJV: And ye shall offer with the bread seven lambs without blemish of the first year, and one young bullock, and two rams: they shall be for a burnt offering unto the Lord, with their meat offering, and their drink offerings, even an offering made by fire, of sweet savour unto the Lord.
text.NABRE: Besides the bread, you shall offer to the Lord a burnt offering of seven unblemished yearling lambs, one bull of the herd, and two rams, along with their grain offering and libations, as a sweet-smelling oblation to the Lord.
text.DRB: And you shall offer with the breads seven lambs without spot of a year old, and one calf from the herd, & two rams, and they shall be for an holocaust with their libaments, for a most sweet odour to the Lord.

text.NRSV: You shall also offer one male goat for a sin offering, and two male lambs a year old as a sacrifice of well-being.
text.Rotherham: Then shall ye offer one he-goat, as a sin-bearer,—and two he-lambs, of the first year, as a peace-offering.
text.KJV: Then ye shall sacrifice one kid of the goats for a sin offering, and two lambs of the first year for a sacrifice of peace offerings.
text.NABRE: One male goat shall be sacrificed as a purification offering, and two yearling lambs as a communion sacrifice.
text.DRB: You shall make a buck goat also for sin, and two lambs of a year old for hosts of pacifics.

text.NRSV: The priest shall raise them with the bread of the first fruits as an elevation offering before the Lord, together with the two lambs; they shall be holy to the Lord for the priest.
text.Rotherham: And the priest shall wave them, with the firstfruits’ bread, as a wave-offering before Yahweh, with the two he-lambs,—holy, shall they be unto Yahweh, for the priest.
text.KJV: And the priest shall wave them with the bread of the firstfruits for a wave offering before the Lord, with the two lambs: they shall be holy to the Lord for the priest.
text.NABRE: The priest shall elevate them—that is, the two lambs—with the bread of the first-ripened fruits as an elevated offering before the Lord; these shall be sacred to the Lord and belong to the priest.
text.DRB: And when the Priest hath elevated them with the breads of the first fruits before the Lord, they shall turn to his use.

text.NRSV: On that same day you shall make proclamation; you shall hold a holy convocation; you shall not work at your occupations. This is a statute forever in all your settlements throughout your generations.
text.Rotherham: And ye shall make proclamation on this self-same day—a holy convocation, shall it be unto you; no laborious work, shall ye do,—an age-abiding statute in all your dwellings, unto your generations.
text.KJV: And ye shall proclaim on the selfsame day, that it may be an holy convocation unto you: ye shall do no servile work therein: it shall be a statute for ever in all your dwellings throughout your generations.
text.NABRE: On this same day you shall make a proclamation: there shall be a declared holy day for you; no heavy work may be done. This shall be a perpetual statute through all your generations wherever you dwell.
text.DRB: And you shall call this day most solemn, and most holy: no servile work shall you do in it. It shall be an everlasting ordinance in all your habitations, and generations.

text.NRSV: When you reap the harvest of your land, you shall not reap to the very edges of your field, or gather the gleanings of your harvest; you shall leave them for the poor and for the alien: I am the Lord your God.
text.Rotherham: And, when ye reap the harvest of your land, thou shalt not wholly clear the border of thy field when thou reapest, and, the gleanings of thy field, shalt thou not glean,—for the poor and for the sojourner, shalt thou leave them. I—Yahweh, am your God.
text.KJV: And when ye reap the harvest of your land, thou shalt not make clean riddance of the corners of thy field when thou reapest, neither shalt thou gather any gleaning of thy harvest: thou shalt leave them unto the poor, and to the stranger: I am the Lord your God.
text.NABRE: When you reap the harvest of your land, you shall not be so thorough that you reap the field to its very edge, nor shall you gather the gleanings of your harvest. These things you shall leave for the poor and the alien. I, the Lord, am your God.
text.DRB: And after you reap the corn of your land, you shall not cut it to the very ground: neither shall you gather the ears that remain, but you shall let them alone for the poor & for strangers. I am the Lord your God.

text.NRSV: The Lord spoke to Moses, saying:
text.Rotherham: And Yahweh spake unto Moses, saying:
text.KJV: And the Lord spake unto Moses, saying,
text.NABRE: New Year’s Day. The Lord said to Moses:
text.DRB: And our Lord spake to Moyses, saying:

text.NRSV: Speak to the people of Israel, saying: In the seventh month, on the first day of the month, you shall observe a day of complete rest, a holy convocation commemorated with trumpet blasts.
text.Rotherham: Speak unto the sons of Israel, saying:—In the seventh month on the first of the month, shall there be unto you a sacred rest, a trumpet-blowing commemoration a holy convocation:
text.KJV: Speak unto the children of Israel, saying, In the seventh month, in the first day of the month, shall ye have a sabbath, a memorial of blowing of trumpets, an holy convocation.
text.NABRE: Tell the Israelites: On the first day of the seventh month you will have a sabbath rest, with trumpet blasts as a reminder, a declared holy day;
text.DRB: Speak to the children of Israel: The seventh month, on the first day of the month, shall be a Sabbath, a memorial, by sounding of trumpets, and shall be called holy:

text.NRSV: You shall not work at your occupations; and you shall present the Lord’s offering by fire.
text.Rotherham: no laborious work, shall ye do,—but ye shall bring near an altar-flame unto Yahweh.
text.KJV: Ye shall do no servile work therein: but ye shall offer an offering made by fire unto the Lord.
text.NABRE: you shall do no heavy work, and you shall offer an oblation to the Lord.
text.DRB: no servile work shall you do in it, and you shall offer holocaust to the Lord.

text.NRSV: The Lord spoke to Moses, saying:
text.Rotherham: And Yahweh spake unto Moses, saying,
text.KJV: And the Lord spake unto Moses, saying,
text.NABRE: The Day of Atonement. The Lord said to Moses:
text.DRB: And our Lord spake to Moyses, saying:

text.NRSV: Now, the tenth day of this seventh month is the day of atonement; it shall be a holy convocation for you: you shall deny yourselves and present the Lord’s offering by fire;
text.Rotherham: Surely on the tenth of this seventh month, is, the Day of Propitiation, a holy convocation, shall it be to you, therefore shall ye humble your souls,—and bring near an altar-flame unto Yahweh.
text.KJV: Also on the tenth day of this seventh month there shall be a day of atonement: it shall be an holy convocation unto you; and ye shall afflict your souls, and offer an offering made by fire unto the Lord.
text.NABRE: Now the tenth day of this seventh month is the Day of Atonement. You will have a declared holy day. You shall humble yourselves and offer an oblation to the Lord.
text.DRB: upon the tenth day of this seventh month shall be the day of expiations most solemn, and it shall be called holy: and you shall afflict your souls in it, and shall offer holocaust to the Lord.

text.NRSV: you shall do no work during that entire day; for it is a day of atonement, to make atonement on your behalf before the Lord your God.
text.Rotherham: And, no work, shall ye do, on this selfsame day,—for a day of propitiation, it is, to put a propitiatory-covering over you before Yahweh your God.
text.KJV: And ye shall do no work in that same day: for it is a day of atonement, to make an atonement for you before the Lord your God.
text.NABRE: On this day you shall not do any work, because it is the Day of Atonement, when atonement is made for you before the Lord, your God.
text.DRB: No servile work shall you do the time of this day: because it is a day of propitiation, that the Lord your God may become propitious unto you.

text.NRSV: For any person who is not denied in self on that very day shall be cut off from the people.
text.Rotherham: For, whosoever be the person that shall not be humbled on this selfsame day, then shall he be cut off from among his kinsfolk.
text.KJV: For whatsoever soul it be that shall not be afflicted in that same day, he shall be cut off from among his people.
text.NABRE: Those who do not humble themselves on this day shall be cut off from the people.
text.DRB: Every soul, that is not afflicted this day, shall perish out of his people:

text.NRSV: And any person who does any work during that entire day, such a person I will destroy from the midst of the people.
text.Rotherham: And, whosoever be the person that shall do any work on this selfsame day, then will I destroy that person from the midst of his people.
text.KJV: And whatsoever soul it be that doeth any work in that same day, the same soul will I destroy from among his people.
text.NABRE: If anyone does any work on this day, I will remove that person from the midst of the people.
text.DRB: and which shall do any work, the same will I destroy out of his people.

text.NRSV: You shall do no work: it is a statute forever throughout your generations in all your settlements.
text.Rotherham: No work, shall ye do,—an age-abiding statute to your generations, in all your dwellings.
text.KJV: Ye shall do no manner of work: it shall be a statute for ever throughout your generations in all your dwellings.
text.NABRE: You shall do no work; this is a perpetual statute throughout your generations wherever you dwell;
text.DRB: No work therefore shall you do in it: it shall be an everlasting ordinance unto you in all your generations, and habitations.

text.NRSV: It shall be to you a sabbath of complete rest, and you shall deny yourselves; on the ninth day of the month at evening, from evening to evening you shall keep your sabbath.
text.Rotherham: A sabbath of sacred rest, shall it be unto you, so shall ye humble your souls,—on the ninth of the month in the evening, from evening to evening, shall ye keep your sabbath.
text.KJV: It shall be unto you a sabbath of rest, and ye shall afflict your souls: in the ninth day of the month at even, from even unto even, shall ye celebrate your sabbath.
text.NABRE: it is a sabbath of complete rest for you. You shall humble yourselves. Beginning on the evening of the ninth of the month, you shall keep your sabbath from evening to evening.
text.DRB: It is a Sabbath of resting, and you shall afflict your souls the ninth day of the month: from even until even you shall celebrate your sabbaths.

text.NRSV: The Lord spoke to Moses, saying:
text.Rotherham: And Yahweh spake unto Moses, saying:
text.KJV: And the Lord spake unto Moses, saying,
text.NABRE: The Feast of Booths. The Lord said to Moses:
text.DRB: And our Lord spake to Moyses, saying:

text.NRSV: Speak to the people of Israel, saying: On the fifteenth day of this seventh month, and lasting seven days, there shall be the festival of booths to the Lord.
text.Rotherham: Speak unto the sons of Israel, saying:—On the fifteenth day of this seventh month, shall be the festival of booths, for seven days, unto Yahweh.
text.KJV: Speak unto the children of Israel, saying, The fifteenth day of this seventh month shall be the feast of tabernacles for seven days unto the Lord.
text.NABRE: Tell the Israelites: The fifteenth day of this seventh month is the Lord ’s feast of Booths, which shall continue for seven days.
text.DRB: Speak to the children of Israel: From the fifteenth day of this seventh month, shall be the festivity of tabernacles seven days to the Lord.

text.NRSV: The first day shall be a holy convocation; you shall not work at your occupations.
text.Rotherham: On the first day, [shall be] a holy convocation,—no laborious work, shall ye do.
text.KJV: On the first day shall be an holy convocation: ye shall do no servile work therein.
text.NABRE: On the first day, a declared holy day, you shall do no heavy work.
text.DRB: The first day shall be called most solemn and most holy: no servile work shall you do in it. And seven days you shall offer holocausts to the Lord.

text.NRSV: Seven days you shall present the Lord’s offerings by fire; on the eighth day you shall observe a holy convocation and present the Lord’s offerings by fire; it is a solemn assembly; you shall not work at your occupations.
text.Rotherham: Seven days, shall ye bring near an altar-flame unto Yahweh: on the eighth day—a holy convocation, shall there be unto you so shall ye bring near an altar-flame unto Yahweh—the closing of the feast, it is, no laborious work, shall ye do.
text.KJV: Seven days ye shall offer an offering made by fire unto the Lord: on the eighth day shall be an holy convocation unto you; and ye shall offer an offering made by fire unto the Lord: it is a solemn assembly; and ye shall do no servile work therein.
text.NABRE: For seven days you shall offer an oblation to the Lord, and on the eighth day you will have a declared holy day. You shall offer an oblation to the Lord. It is the festival closing. You shall do no heavy work.
text.DRB: The eighth day also shall be most solemn and most holy, and you shall offer holocausts to the Lord: for it is of assembly and collection: no servile work shall you do in it.

text.NRSV: These are the appointed festivals of the Lord, which you shall celebrate as times of holy convocation, for presenting to the Lord offerings by fire—burnt offerings and grain offerings, sacrifices and drink offerings, each on its proper day—
text.Rotherham: These, are the appointed seasons of Yahweh, which ye shall proclaim, as holy convocations,—for bringing near as an altar-flame unto Yahweh, an ascending-sacrifice, and a meal-offering, a sacrifice and a drink-offering, each day’s appointment on its own day:—
text.KJV: These are the feasts of the Lord, which ye shall proclaim to be holy convocations, to offer an offering made by fire unto the Lord, a burnt offering, and a meat offering, a sacrifice, and drink offerings, every thing upon his day:
text.NABRE: These, therefore, are the festivals of the Lord which you shall declare holy days, in order to offer as an oblation to the Lord burnt offerings and grain offerings, sacrifices and libations, as prescribed for each day,
text.DRB: These are the festivities of the Lord, which you shall call most solemn and most holy, and shall offer in them oblations to the Lord, holocausts and libaments according to the rite of every day:

text.NRSV: apart from the sabbaths of the Lord, and apart from your gifts, and apart from all your votive offerings, and apart from all your freewill offerings, which you give to the Lord.
text.Rotherham: besides the sabbaths of Yahweh, and besides your presents, and besides all your vows, and besides all your freewill offerings which ye shall give unto Yahweh.
text.KJV: Beside the sabbaths of the Lord, and beside your gifts, and beside all your vows, and beside all your freewill offerings, which ye give unto the Lord.
text.NABRE: in addition to the Lord ’s sabbaths, your donations, your various votive offerings, and the voluntary offerings that you present to the Lord.
text.DRB: beside the sabbaths of the Lord, and your gifts and those that you shall offer by vow, or which you shall give to the Lord voluntarily.

text.NRSV: Now, the fifteenth day of the seventh month, when you have gathered in the produce of the land, you shall keep the festival of the Lord, lasting seven days; a complete rest on the first day, and a complete rest on the eighth day.
text.Rotherham: Surely, on the fifteenth day of the seventh month, when ye have gathered in the yield of the land, shall ye celebrate the festival of Yahweh seven days,—on the first day, a sacred rest, and, on the eighth day, a sacred rest.
text.KJV: Also in the fifteenth day of the seventh month, when ye have gathered in the fruit of the land, ye shall keep a feast unto the Lord seven days: on the first day shall be a sabbath, and on the eighth day shall be a sabbath.
text.NABRE: On the fifteenth day, then, of the seventh month, when you have gathered in the produce of the land, you shall celebrate the feast of the Lord for a whole week. The first and the eighth day shall be days of rest.
text.DRB: Therefore from the fifteenth day of the seventh month, when you shall have gathered all the fruits of your land, you shall celebrate the festivity of the Lord seven days, on the first day and the eighth shall be a sabbath, that is rest.

text.NRSV: On the first day you shall take the fruit of majestic trees, branches of palm trees, boughs of leafy trees, and willows of the brook; and you shall rejoice before the Lord your God for seven days.
text.Rotherham: So then ye shall take you, on the first day, boughs of goodly trees, branches, of palm-trees, and boughs of thick trees, and poplars of the ravine,—and shall rejoice before Yahweh your God, seven days.
text.KJV: And ye shall take you on the first day the boughs of goodly trees, branches of palm trees, and the boughs of thick trees, and willows of the brook; and ye shall rejoice before the Lord your God seven days.
text.NABRE: On the first day you shall gather fruit of majestic trees, branches of palms, and boughs of leafy trees and valley willows. Then for a week you shall make merry before the Lord, your God.
text.DRB: And you shall take to you the first day the fruits of a most fair tree, and the branches of palms, and boughs of the tree with thick leaves, and willows of the brook, and you shall rejoice before the Lord your God.

text.NRSV: You shall keep it as a festival to the Lord seven days in the year; you shall keep it in the seventh month as a statute forever throughout your generations.
text.Rotherham: So shall ye celebrate it as a festival unto Yahweh, seven days in the year,—a statute age-abiding, to your generations. In the seventh month, shall ye celebrate it;
text.KJV: And ye shall keep it a feast unto the Lord seven days in the year. It shall be a statute for ever in your generations: ye shall celebrate it in the seventh month.
text.NABRE: You shall keep this feast of the Lord for one whole week in the year. By perpetual statute throughout your generations in the seventh month of the year, you shall keep it.
text.DRB: And you shall celebrate the solemnity thereof seven days in the year. It shall be an everlasting ordinance in your generations. The seventh month shall you celebrate the festivity,

text.NRSV: You shall live in booths for seven days; all citizens in Israel shall live in booths,
text.Rotherham: In booths, shall ye dwell seven days;—All the home-born in Israel, shall dwell in booths:
text.KJV: Ye shall dwell in booths seven days; all that are Israelites born shall dwell in booths:
text.NABRE: You shall dwell in booths for seven days; every native-born Israelite shall dwell in booths,
text.DRB: and shall dwell in bowers seven days, every one that is of the stock of Israel shall abide in tabernacles:

text.NRSV: so that your generations may know that I made the people of Israel live in booths when I brought them out of the land of Egypt: I am the Lord your God.
text.Rotherham: To the end that your generations may know, that, in booths, made I the sons of Israel to dwell, when I brought them forth out of the land of Egypt,—I—Yahweh, am your God.
text.KJV: That your generations may know that I made the children of Israel to dwell in booths, when I brought them out of the land of Egypt: I am the Lord your God.
text.NABRE: that your descendants may realize that, when I led the Israelites out of the land of Egypt, I made them dwell in booths. I, the Lord, am your God.
text.DRB: that your posterity may learn that I made the children of Israel to dwell in tabernacles, when I brought them out of the Land of Ægypt. I the Lord your God.

text.NRSV: Thus Moses declared to the people of Israel the appointed festivals of the Lord.
text.Rotherham: So then Moses declared, the appointed seasons of Yahweh, unto the sons of Israel.
text.KJV: And Moses declared unto the children of Israel the feasts of the Lord.
text.NABRE: Thus did Moses announce to the Israelites the festivals of the Lord.
text.DRB: And Moyses spake concerning the solemnities of our Lord to the children of Israel.

text.NRSV: The Lord spoke to Moses, saying:
text.Rotherham: And Yahweh spake unto Moses, saying:
text.KJV: And the Lord spake unto Moses, saying,
text.NABRE: The Lord said to Moses:
text.DRB: And our Lord spake to Moyses, saying:

text.NRSV: Command the people of Israel to bring you pure oil of beaten olives for the lamp, that a light may be kept burning regularly.
text.Rotherham: Command the sons of Israel, That they bring unto thee—pure oil olive, beaten, for giving light,—to cause the lamp to burn up continually.
text.KJV: Command the children of Israel, that they bring unto thee pure oil olive beaten for the light, to cause the lamps to burn continually.
text.NABRE: Order the Israelites to bring you clear oil of crushed olives for the light, so that you may keep the lamp burning regularly.
text.DRB: Command the children of Israel, that they bring unto thee oil of olives most pure, and clear, to furnish the lamps continually,

text.NRSV: Aaron shall set it up in the tent of meeting, outside the curtain of the covenant, to burn from evening to morning before the Lord regularly; it shall be a statute forever throughout your generations.
text.Rotherham: Outside the veil of the testimony, in the tent of meeting, shall Aaron order it, from evening until morning, before Yahweh continually,—a statute age-abiding, to your generations.
text.KJV: Without the vail of the testimony, in the tabernacle of the congregation, shall Aaron order it from the evening unto the morning before the Lord continually: it shall be a statute for ever in your generations.
text.NABRE: In the tent of meeting, outside the veil that hangs in front of the covenant, Aaron shall set up the lamp to burn before the Lord regularly, from evening till morning, by a perpetual statute throughout your generations.
text.DRB: without the veil of the testimony in the tabernacle of covenant. And Aaron shall set them from even until morning before the Lord, by a perpetual service and rite in your generations.

text.NRSV: He shall set up the lamps on the lampstand of pure gold before the Lord regularly.
text.Rotherham: Upon the pure lampstand, shall he order the lamps,—before Yahweh, continually.
text.KJV: He shall order the lamps upon the pure candlestick before the Lord continually.
text.NABRE: He shall set up the lamps on the pure gold menorah to burn regularly before the Lord. The Showbread.
text.DRB: Upon the candle stick most clean shall they be put always in the sight of the Lord.

text.NRSV: You shall take choice flour, and bake twelve loaves of it; two-tenths of an ephah shall be in each loaf.
text.Rotherham: And thou shalt take fine meal, and bake it, in twelve cakes,—of two-tenths, shall each cake be.
text.KJV: And thou shalt take fine flour, and bake twelve cakes thereof: two tenth deals shall be in one cake.
text.NABRE: You shall take bran flour and bake it into twelve cakes, using two tenths of an ephah of flour for each cake.
text.DRB: Thou shalt take also flour, & shalt bake thereof twelve breads, which shall have every one two tenths:

text.NRSV: You shall place them in two rows, six in a row, on the table of pure gold.
text.Rotherham: And thou shalt set them in two rows, six in a row,—upon the pure table, before Yahweh.
text.KJV: And thou shalt set them in two rows, six on a row, upon the pure table before the Lord.
text.NABRE: These you shall place in two piles, six in each pile, on the pure gold table before the Lord.
text.DRB: which thou shalt set six one against another upon the most clean table before the Lord,

text.NRSV: You shall put pure frankincense with each row, to be a token offering for the bread, as an offering by fire to the Lord.
text.Rotherham: And thou shalt put upon each row pure frankincense,—so shall it belong unto the bread as a memorial, an altar-flame unto Yahweh.
text.KJV: And thou shalt put pure frankincense upon each row, that it may be on the bread for a memorial, even an offering made by fire unto the Lord.
text.NABRE: With each pile put some pure frankincense, which shall serve as an oblation to the Lord, a token of the bread offering.
text.DRB: and thou shalt put upon them the clearest frankincense, that the bread may be for a monument of the oblation of the Lord.

text.NRSV: Every sabbath day Aaron shall set them in order before the Lord regularly as a commitment of the people of Israel, as a covenant forever.
text.Rotherham: Sabbath day by sabbath day, shall he order it before Yahweh continually,—from the sons of Israel as an age-abiding covenant:
text.KJV: Every sabbath he shall set it in order before the Lord continually, being taken from the children of Israel by an everlasting covenant.
text.NABRE: Regularly on each sabbath day the bread shall be set out before the Lord on behalf of the Israelites by an everlasting covenant.
text.DRB: Every sabbath they shall be changed before the Lord, received of the children of Israel by an everlasting covenant:

text.NRSV: They shall be for Aaron and his descendants, who shall eat them in a holy place, for they are most holy portions for him from the offerings by fire to the Lord, a perpetual due.
text.Rotherham: so shall it be for Aaron and for his sons, and they shall eat it in a holy place,—for most holy, shall it be unto him from among the altar-flames of Yahweh, a statute age-abiding.
text.KJV: And it shall be Aaron’s and his sons’; and they shall eat it in the holy place: for it is most holy unto him of the offerings of the Lord made by fire by a perpetual statute.
text.NABRE: It shall belong to Aaron and his sons, who must eat it in a sacred place, since it is most sacred, his as a perpetual due from the oblations to the Lord. Punishment of Blasphemy.
text.DRB: and they shall be Aaron's and his sons', that they may eat them in a holy place: because it is most Holy of the sacrifices of the Lord by a perpetual right.

text.NRSV: A man whose mother was an Israelite and whose father was an Egyptian came out among the people of Israel; and the Israelite woman’s son and a certain Israelite began fighting in the camp.
text.Rotherham: And forth went the son of a woman of Israel—he being the son of a man of Egypt—in the midst of the sons of Israel;
text.KJV: And the son of an Israelitish woman, whose father was an Egyptian, went out among the children of Israel: and this son of the Israelitish woman and a man of Israel strove together in the camp;
text.NABRE: A man born of an Israelite mother and an Egyptian father went out among the Israelites, and in the camp a fight broke out between the son of the Israelite woman and an Israelite man.
text.DRB: And behold there went forth the son of a woman of Israel, whom she had born of an Ægyptian among the children of Israel, and fell at words in the camp with a man of Israel.

text.NRSV: The Israelite woman’s son blasphemed the Name in a curse. And they brought him to Moses—his mother’s name was Shelomith daughter of Dibri, of the tribe of Dan—
text.Rotherham: and the son of the woman of Israel, and a man of Israel, reviled one another in the camp. And the son of the Israelitish woman uttered contemptuously the Name, and reviled. So they brought him in unto Moses,—now, the name of his mother, was Shelomith, daughter of Dibri, of the tribe of Dan;
text.KJV: And the Israelitish woman’s son blasphemed the name of the Lord, and cursed. And they brought him unto Moses: (and his mother’s name was Shelomith, the daughter of Dibri, of the tribe of Dan:)
text.NABRE: The son of the Israelite woman uttered the Lord ’s name in a curse and blasphemed. So he was brought to Moses—now his mother’s name was Shelomith, daughter of Dibri, of the tribe of Dan—
text.DRB: And when he had blasphemed the name, and had cursed it, he was brought to Moyses: (And his mother was called Salumith, the daughter of Dabri of the tribe of Dan.)

text.NRSV: and they put him in custody, until the decision of the Lord should be made clear to them.
text.Rotherham: and they put him in ward,—that a clear answer might be given to them at the mouth of Yahweh.
text.KJV: And they put him in ward, that the mind of the Lord might be shewed them.
text.NABRE: and he was kept in custody till a decision from the Lord should settle the case for them.
text.DRB: And they did cast him into prison, till they might know what our Lord would command.

text.NRSV: The Lord said to Moses, saying:
text.Rotherham: Then spake Yahweh unto Moses, saying:
text.KJV: And the Lord spake unto Moses, saying,
text.NABRE: The Lord then said to Moses:
text.DRB: Who spake to Moyses,

text.NRSV: Take the blasphemer outside the camp; and let all who were within hearing lay their hands on his head, and let the whole congregation stone him.
text.Rotherham: Bring forth him that reviled unto the outside of the camp, then shall all that heard [him] lean their hands upon his head,—and all the assembly shall stone him.
text.KJV: Bring forth him that hath cursed without the camp; and let all that heard him lay their hands upon his head, and let all the congregation stone him.
text.NABRE: Take the blasphemer outside the camp, and when all who heard him have laid their hands on his head, let the whole community stone him.
text.DRB: saying: Bring forth the blasphemer without the camp, and let all that heard him put their hands upon his head, and let all the people stone him.

text.NRSV: And speak to the people of Israel, saying: Anyone who curses God shall bear the sin.
text.Rotherham: And, unto the sons of Israel, shalt thou speak saying,—What man soever curseth his God shall bear his sin.
text.KJV: And thou shalt speak unto the children of Israel, saying, Whosoever curseth his God shall bear his sin.
text.NABRE: Tell the Israelites: Anyone who blasphemes God shall bear the penalty;
text.DRB: And to the children of Israel thou shalt speak: The man that curseth his God shall bear his sin:

text.NRSV: One who blasphemes the name of the Lord shall be put to death; the whole congregation shall stone the blasphemer. Aliens as well as citizens, when they blaspheme the Name, shall be put to death.
text.Rotherham: And, he that contemptuously uttereth the name of Yahweh, shall be, surely put to death, all the assembly shall, surely stone, him,—as the sojourner so the home-born, when he contemptuously uttereth the Name, he shall be put to death.
text.KJV: And he that blasphemeth the name of the Lord, he shall surely be put to death, and all the congregation shall certainly stone him: as well the stranger, as he that is born in the land, when he blasphemeth the name of the Lord, shall be put to death.
text.NABRE: whoever utters the name of the Lord in a curse shall be put to death. The whole community shall stone that person; alien and native-born alike must be put to death for uttering the Lord ’s name in a curse.
text.DRB: and he that blasphemeth the name of the Lord, dying let him die: all the multitude of the people shall stone him, whether he be a natural, or stranger. He that blasphemeth the name of the Lord, dying let him die.

text.NRSV: Anyone who kills a human being shall be put to death.
text.Rotherham: And, when, any man, by smiting taketh the life of any human being, he shall be surely put to death,
text.KJV: And he that killeth any man shall surely be put to death.
text.NABRE: Whoever takes the life of any human being shall be put to death;
text.DRB: He that striketh, and killeth a man, dying let him die.

text.NRSV: Anyone who kills an animal shall make restitution for it, life for life.
text.Rotherham: And he that by smiting taketh away the life of a beast, shall make it good,—life for life.
text.KJV: And he that killeth a beast shall make it good; beast for beast.
text.NABRE: whoever takes the life of an animal shall make restitution of another animal, life for a life.
text.DRB: He that striketh a beast, shall render one for it, that is to say, soul for soul.

text.NRSV: Anyone who maims another shall suffer the same injury in return:
text.Rotherham: And, when, any man, inflicteth a blemish upon his neighbour, as he hath done, so, shall it be done to him:
text.KJV: And if a man cause a blemish in his neighbour; as he hath done, so shall it be done to him;
text.NABRE: Anyone who inflicts a permanent injury on his or her neighbor shall receive the same in return:
text.DRB: He that giveth any of his neighbours a blemish, as he did, so shall it be done to him:

text.NRSV: fracture for fracture, eye for eye, tooth for tooth; the injury inflicted is the injury to be suffered.
text.Rotherham: fracture for fracture, eye for eye, tooth for tooth, as he inflicteth a blemish upon a human being, so, shall one be inflicted upon him.
text.KJV: Breach for breach, eye for eye, tooth for tooth: as he hath caused a blemish in a man, so shall it be done to him again.
text.NABRE: fracture for fracture, eye for eye, tooth for tooth. The same injury that one gives another shall be inflicted in return.
text.DRB: fracture for fracture, eye for eye, tooth for tooth he shall restore. What blemish he gave, the like shall he be compelled to sustain.

text.NRSV: One who kills an animal shall make restitution for it; but one who kills a human being shall be put to death.
text.Rotherham: And he that killeth a beast, shall make it good,-and he that killeth a human being, shall be put to death.
text.KJV: And he that killeth a beast, he shall restore it: and he that killeth a man, he shall be put to death.
text.NABRE: Whoever takes the life of an animal shall make restitution, but whoever takes a human life shall be put to death.
text.DRB: He that striketh a beast, shall render another. He that striketh a man, shall be punished.

text.NRSV: You shall have one law for the alien and for the citizen: for I am the Lord your God.
text.Rotherham: One rule, shall ye have, as the sojourner, so the home-born, shall be,—For, I—Yahweh, am your God.
text.KJV: Ye shall have one manner of law, as well for the stranger, as for one of your own country: for I am the Lord your God.
text.NABRE: You shall have but one rule, for alien and native-born alike. I, the Lord, am your God.
text.DRB: Let there be equal judgment among you, whether a stranger, or a natural sin: because I am the Lord your God.

text.NRSV: Moses spoke thus to the people of Israel; and they took the blasphemer outside the camp, and stoned him to death. The people of Israel did as the Lord had commanded Moses.
text.Rotherham: So then Moses spake unto the sons of Israel, and they took forth the reviler, unto the outside of the camp, and stoned him with stones. Thus, the sons of Israel did, as Yahweh commanded Moses.
text.KJV: And Moses spake to the children of Israel, that they should bring forth him that had cursed out of the camp, and stone him with stones. And the children of Israel did as the Lord commanded Moses.
text.NABRE: When Moses told this to the Israelites, they took the blasphemer outside the camp and stoned him; they did just as the Lord commanded Moses.
text.DRB: And Moyses spake to the children of Israel: and they brought him forth that had blasphemed, without the camp, and they stoned him. And the children of Israel did as our Lord had commanded Moyses.

text.NRSV: The Lord spoke to Moses on Mount Sinai, saying:
text.Rotherham: And Yahweh spake unto Moses in Mount Sinai, saying—
text.KJV: And the Lord spake unto Moses in mount Sinai, saying,
text.NABRE: The Lord said to Moses on Mount Sinai:
text.DRB: And our Lord spake to Moyses in the mount Sinai, saying:

text.NRSV: Speak to the people of Israel and say to them: When you enter the land that I am giving you, the land shall observe a sabbath for the Lord.
text.Rotherham: Speak unto the sons of Israel, and thou shalt say unto them:—When ye enter into the land which, I, am giving you, then shall the land keep a sabbath unto Yahweh.
