text.NRSV: If it concerns an animal that may be brought as an offering to the Lord, any such that may be given to the Lord shall be holy.
text.Rotherham: And if it be a beast whereof men may bring near an oblation unto Yahweh, all that whereof aught is given to Yahweh, shall be holy.
text.KJV: And if it be a beast, whereof men bring an offering unto the Lord, all that any man giveth of such unto the Lord shall be holy.
text.NABRE: If the offering vowed to the Lord is an animal that may be sacrificed, every such animal given to the Lord becomes sacred.
text.DRB: But the beast, that may be immolated to the Lord, if a man do vow it, shall be holy,

text.NRSV: Another shall not be exchanged or substituted for it, either good for bad or bad for good; and if one animal is substituted for another, both that one and its substitute shall be holy.
text.Rotherham: He shall not alter it, nor change it—good for bad or bad for good,—but if he, do, change beast for beast, then shall, both it and what was given in exchange for it be holy.
text.KJV: He shall not alter it, nor change it, a good for a bad, or a bad for a good: and if he shall at all change beast for beast, then it and the exchange thereof shall be holy.
text.NABRE: The offerer shall not substitute or exchange another for it, either a worse or a better one. If the offerer exchanges one animal in place of another, both the original and its substitute shall become sacred.
text.DRB: and cannot be changed, that is to say, neither a better for a bad, nor a worse for a good, and if he change it, both itself that was changed, and that for the which it was changed, shall be consecrated to the Lord.

text.NRSV: If it concerns any unclean animal that may not be brought as an offering to the Lord, the animal shall be presented before the priest.
text.Rotherham: And, if it be any unclean beast whereof men may not bring near an oblation to Yahweh, then shall he present the beast before the priest:
text.KJV: And if it be any unclean beast, of which they do not offer a sacrifice unto the Lord, then he shall present the beast before the priest:
text.NABRE: If any unclean animal which is unfit for sacrifice to the Lord is vowed, it must be set before the priest,
text.DRB: The unclean beast, which cannot be immolated to the Lord, if any man vow it shall be brought before the Priest.

text.NRSV: The priest shall assess it: whether good or bad, according to the assessment of the priest, so it shall be.
text.Rotherham: and the priest shall estimate it, whether it is good or bad,—according to thine estimate, O priest, so, shall it be.
text.KJV: And the priest shall value it, whether it be good or bad: as thou valuest it, who art the priest, so shall it be.
text.NABRE: who shall determine its value in keeping with its good or bad qualities, and the value set by the priest shall stand.
text.DRB: Who judging whether it be good or evil, shall set the price:

text.NRSV: But if it is to be redeemed, one-fifth must be added to the assessment.
text.Rotherham: But if he should please to redeem, it, then shall he add the fifth part thereof unto thine estimate.
text.KJV: But if he will at all redeem it, then he shall add a fifth part thereof unto thy estimation.
text.NABRE: If the offerer wishes to redeem the animal, the person shall pay one fifth more than this valuation.
text.DRB: which if he that offereth will give, he shall add above the estimation the fifth part.

text.NRSV: If a person consecrates a house to the Lord, the priest shall assess it: whether good or bad, as the priest assesses it, so it shall stand.
text.Rotherham: And, when, any man, would hallow his house to be holy unto Yahweh, then shall the priest estimate it, whether it is good or bad,—as the priest shall estimate it, so, shall it stand.
text.KJV: And when a man shall sanctify his house to be holy unto the Lord, then the priest shall estimate it, whether it be good or bad: as the priest shall estimate it, so shall it stand.
text.NABRE: When someone dedicates a house as sacred to the Lord, the priest shall determine its value in keeping with its good or bad qualities, and the value set by the priest shall stand.
text.DRB: If a man vow his house, and sanctify it to the Lord, the Priest shall consider it, whether it be good or bad, and according to the price which he shall appoint, it shall be sold.

text.NRSV: And if the one who consecrates the house wishes to redeem it, one-fifth shall be added to its assessed value, and it shall revert to the original owner.
text.Rotherham: But, if he that halloweth it, would redeem his house, then shall he add the fifth part of the silver of thine estimate thereunto and it shall be his.
text.KJV: And if he that sanctified it will redeem his house, then he shall add the fifth part of the money of thy estimation unto it, and it shall be his.
text.NABRE: A person dedicating a house who then wishes to redeem it shall pay one fifth more than the price thus established, and then it will again belong to that individual.
text.DRB: but if he that had vowed will redeem it, he shall give the fifth part of the estimation besides, and shall have the house.

text.NRSV: If a person consecrates to the Lord any inherited landholding, its assessment shall be in accordance with its seed requirements: fifty shekels of silver to a homer of barley seed.
text.Rotherham: And if of the field of his possession any man would hallow unto Yahweh, then shall thine estimate be according to the seed thereof,—the seed of a homer of barley, at fifty shekels of silver.
text.KJV: And if a man shall sanctify unto the Lord some part of a field of his possession, then thy estimation shall be according to the seed thereof: an homer of barley seed shall be valued at fifty shekels of silver.
text.NABRE: If someone dedicates to the Lord a portion of hereditary land, its valuation shall be made according to the amount of seed required to sow it, the acreage sown with a homer of barley seed being valued at fifty silver shekels.
text.DRB: And if he vow the field of his possession, and consecrate it to the Lord, the price shall be esteemed according to the measure of the seed. If the ground be sowed with thirty bushels of barley, let it be sold for fifty sicles of silver.

text.NRSV: If the person consecrates the field as of the year of jubilee, that assessment shall stand;
text.Rotherham: If, from the year of jubilee, he would hallow his field, according to thine estimate, shall it stand.
text.KJV: If he sanctify his field from the year of jubile, according to thy estimation it shall stand.
text.NABRE: If the dedication of a field is made at the beginning of a jubilee period, the full valuation shall hold;
text.DRB: If he vow his field immediately from the year of Jubilee, that is beginning, how much it can be worth, at so much it shall be esteemed:

text.NRSV: but if the field is consecrated after the jubilee, the priest shall compute the price for it according to the years that remain until the year of jubilee, and the assessment shall be reduced.
text.Rotherham: But if after the jubilee he would hallow his field, then shall the priest reckon to him the silver, according to the years that remain, until the year of the jubilee,—and it shall be abated from thine estimate.
text.KJV: But if he sanctify his field after the jubile, then the priest shall reckon unto him the money according to the years that remain, even unto the year of the jubile, and it shall be abated from thy estimation.
text.NABRE: but if it is some time after this, the priest shall estimate its money value according to the number of years left until the next jubilee year, with a corresponding reduction on the valuation.
text.DRB: but if some time after, the Priest shall account the money according to the number of years that remain until the Jubilee, and there shall be diminished of the price.

text.NRSV: And if the one who consecrates the field wishes to redeem it, then one-fifth shall be added to its assessed value, and it shall revert to the original owner;
text.Rotherham: But, if he that hath hallowed it should be pleased to redeem, the field, then shall he add the fifth part of the silver of thine estimate thereunto and it shall be assured to him.
text.KJV: And if he that sanctified the field will in any wise redeem it, then he shall add the fifth part of the money of thy estimation unto it, and it shall be assured to him.
text.NABRE: A person dedicating a field who then wishes to redeem it shall pay one fifth more than the price thus established, and so reclaim it.
text.DRB: And if he that had vowed will redeem his field, he shall add the fifth part of the esteemed money, and shall possess it.

text.NRSV: but if the field is not redeemed, or if it has been sold to someone else, it shall no longer be redeemable.
text.Rotherham: But if he will not redeem the field, but have sold the field to another man, it shall be redeemable no longer;
text.KJV: And if he will not redeem the field, or if he have sold the field to another man, it shall not be redeemed any more.
text.NABRE: If, instead of redeeming such a field, one sells it to another, it may no longer be redeemed;
text.DRB: And if he will not redeem it, but it be sold to any other man, he that had vowed it can redeem it no more:

text.NRSV: But when the field is released in the jubilee, it shall be holy to the Lord as a devoted field; it becomes the priest’s holding.
text.Rotherham: so shall the field, when it goeth out in the jubilee, be holy unto Yahweh as a devoted, field,—to the priest, shall belong the possession thereof.
text.KJV: But the field, when it goeth out in the jubile, shall be holy unto the Lord, as a field devoted; the possession thereof shall be the priest’s.
text.NABRE: but at the jubilee it shall be released as sacred to the Lord; like a field that is put under the ban, it shall become priestly property.
text.DRB: for when the day of Jubilee cometh, it shall be sanctified to the Lord, and the possession consecrated pertaineth to the right of the Priests.

text.NRSV: If someone consecrates to the Lord a field that has been purchased, which is not a part of the ancestral holding,
text.Rotherham: If, however, a field that he hath bought, which is not of the fields of his possession, he would hallow unto Yahweh,
text.KJV: And if a man sanctify unto the Lord a field which he hath bought, which is not of the fields of his possession;
text.NABRE: If someone dedicates to the Lord a field that was purchased and was not part of hereditary property,
text.DRB: If the field be bought, and being not of his ancestors' possession be sanctified to the Lord,

text.NRSV: the priest shall compute for it the proportionate assessment up to the year of jubilee, and the person shall pay the assessment as of that day, as a holy thing to the Lord.
text.Rotherham: then shall the priest reckon to him the amount of thine estimate until the year of the jubilee,—and he shall give thine estimate, in that day, as holy unto Yahweh.
text.KJV: Then the priest shall reckon unto him the worth of thy estimation, even unto the year of the jubile: and he shall give thine estimation in that day, as a holy thing unto the Lord.
text.NABRE: the priest shall compute its value in proportion to the number of years until the next jubilee, and on the same day the person shall pay the price thus established, a sacred donation to the Lord;
text.DRB: the Priest shall account the price according to the number of years, unto the jubilee: and he that had vowed shall give that to the Lord.

text.NRSV: In the year of jubilee the field shall return to the one from whom it was bought, whose holding the land is.
text.Rotherham: In the year of the jubilee, shall the field return unto him from whom he bought it to him whose it was as a possession in the land.
text.KJV: In the year of the jubile the field shall return unto him of whom it was bought, even to him to whom the possession of the land did belong.
text.NABRE: at the jubilee the field shall revert to the hereditary owner of this land from whom it had been purchased.
text.DRB: But in the Jubilee, it shall return to the former owner that sold it and had it in the lot of his possession.

text.NRSV: All assessments shall be by the sanctuary shekel: twenty gerahs shall make a shekel.
text.Rotherham: And, every estimate of thine, shall be by the holy shekel,—twenty gerahs, make the shekel.
text.KJV: And all thy estimations shall be according to the shekel of the sanctuary: twenty gerahs shall be the shekel.
text.NABRE: Every valuation shall be made according to the standard of the sanctuary shekel. There are twenty gerahs to the shekel.
text.DRB: All estimation shall be weighed by the sicle of the sanctuary. A sicle hath twenty oboles.

text.NRSV: A firstling of animals, however, which as a firstling belongs to the Lord, cannot be consecrated by anyone; whether ox or sheep, it is the Lord’s.
text.Rotherham: Only the firstling which is born a firstling to Yahweh, among beasts, no man shall hallow it,—whether ox or sheep, unto Yahweh, it belongeth.
text.KJV: Only the firstling of the beasts, which should be the Lord’s firstling, no man shall sanctify it; whether it be ox, or sheep: it is the Lord’s.
text.NABRE: Irredeemable Offerings. Note that a firstborn animal, which as such already belongs to the Lord, may not be dedicated. Whether an ox or a sheep, it is the Lord ’s.
text.DRB: The first born, which pertain to the Lord, no man may sanctify and vow: whether it be ox, or sheep, they are the Lord's.

text.NRSV: If it is an unclean animal, it shall be ransomed at its assessment, with one-fifth added; if it is not redeemed, it shall be sold at its assessment.
text.Rotherham: But, if it be among the beasts that are unclean, then shall he ransom it by thine estimate, and add the fifth part of it thereunto,—but, if it is not redeemed, then shall it be sold by thine estimate.
text.KJV: And if it be of an unclean beast, then he shall redeem it according to thine estimation, and shall add a fifth part of it thereto: or if it be not redeemed, then it shall be sold according to thy estimation.
text.NABRE: But if it is an unclean animal, it may be redeemed by paying one fifth more than its value. If it is not redeemed, it shall be sold at its value.
text.DRB: And if it be an unclean beast, he that offereth it shall redeem it, according to thy estimation, and shall add the fifth part of the price. If he will not redeem it, it shall be sold to another for how much soever it was esteemed by thee.

text.NRSV: Nothing that a person owns that has been devoted to destruction for the Lord, be it human or animal, or inherited landholding, may be sold or redeemed; every devoted thing is most holy to the Lord.
text.Rotherham: Only no devoted thing which any man shall devote unto Yahweh, of all that belongeth to him,—of man or beast, or of the field of his possession, shall be either sold or redeemed,—as to every devoted thing, most holy, it is unto Yahweh.
text.KJV: Notwithstanding no devoted thing, that a man shall devote unto the Lord of all that he hath, both of man and beast, and of the field of his possession, shall be sold or redeemed: every devoted thing is most holy unto the Lord.
text.NABRE: Note, also, that any possession which someone puts under the ban for the Lord, whether it is a human being, an animal, or a hereditary field, shall be neither sold nor redeemed; everything that is put under the ban becomes most holy to the Lord.
text.DRB: Any thing that is consecrated to the Lord, whether it be man, or beast, or field, shall not be sold, neither can it be redeemed. Whatsoever is once consecrated, shall be holy of holies to the Lord.

text.NRSV: No human beings who have been devoted to destruction can be ransomed; they shall be put to death.
text.Rotherham: As touching any one devoted, who may be devoted from among men he shall not be ransomed,—he must be, surely put to death.
text.KJV: None devoted, which shall be devoted of men, shall be redeemed; but shall surely be put to death.
text.NABRE: All human beings that are put under the ban cannot be redeemed; they must be put to death.
text.DRB: And any consecration, that is offered of a man, shall not be redeemed, but dying shall die.

text.NRSV: All tithes from the land, whether the seed from the ground or the fruit from the tree, are the Lord’s; they are holy to the Lord.
text.Rotherham: And, as for all the tithe of the land whether of the seed of the land, [or] of the fruit of the trees, unto Yahweh, it belongeth,—as something holy unto Yahweh.
text.KJV: And all the tithe of the land, whether of the seed of the land, or of the fruit of the tree, is the Lord’s: it is holy unto the Lord.
text.NABRE: All tithes of the land, whether in grain from the fields or in fruit from the trees, belong to the Lord; they are sacred to the Lord.
text.DRB: All tithes of the land, whether of corn, or of the fruits of trees, are the Lord's, and are sanctified to him.

text.NRSV: If persons wish to redeem any of their tithes, they must add one-fifth to them.
text.Rotherham: But, if any man should be pleased, to redeem, aught of his tithes, the fifth part thereof, shall he add thereunto.
text.KJV: And if a man will at all redeem ought of his tithes, he shall add thereto the fifth part thereof.
text.NABRE: If someone wishes to redeem any of the tithes, the person shall pay one fifth more than their value.
text.DRB: And if any man will redeem his tithes, he shall add the fifth part of them.

text.NRSV: All tithes of herd and flock, every tenth one that passes under the shepherd’s staff, shall be holy to the Lord.
text.Rotherham: And, as for all the tithe of herd and flock, all that passeth under the rod, the tenth, shall be holy unto Yahweh.
text.KJV: And concerning the tithe of the herd, or of the flock, even of whatsoever passeth under the rod, the tenth shall be holy unto the Lord.
text.NABRE: The tithes of the herd and the flock, every tenth animal that passes under the herdsman’s rod, shall be sacred to the Lord.
text.DRB: Of all the tithes of oxen, and sheep, and goats, that pass under the sheep-herd's rod, every tenth that cometh shall be sanctified to the Lord.

text.NRSV: Let no one inquire whether it is good or bad, or make substitution for it; if one makes substitution for it, then both it and the substitute shall be holy and cannot be redeemed.
text.Rotherham: He shall not examine whether it be good or bad neither shall he change it,—or, if he do in anywise change, it, then shall both, it and what is given in exchange for it, be holy, it shall not be redeemed.
text.KJV: He shall not search whether it be good or bad, neither shall he change it: and if he change it at all, then both it and the change thereof shall be holy; it shall not be redeemed.
text.NABRE: It shall not matter whether good ones or bad ones are thus chosen, and no exchange may be made. If any exchange is made, both the original animal and its substitute become sacred and cannot be redeemed.
text.DRB: It shall not be chosen neither good nor bad, neither shall it be changed for another. If any man change it, both that which was changed, and that for the which it was changed, shall be sanctified to the Lord, and shall not be redeemed.

text.NRSV: These are the commandments that the Lord gave to Moses for the people of Israel on Mount Sinai.
text.Rotherham: These are the commandments which Yahweh commanded Moses for the sons of Israel,—in Mount Sinai.
text.KJV: These are the commandments, which the Lord commanded Moses for the children of Israel in mount Sinai.
text.NABRE: These are the commandments which the Lord gave Moses on Mount Sinai for the Israelites.
text.DRB: These are the precepts, which our Lord commanded Moyses unto the children of Israel in the mount Sinai.

6.25: Tell Aaron and his sons: This is the ritual for the purification offering. At the place where the burnt offering is slaughtered, there also, before the Lord, shall the purification offering be slaughtered. It is most holy.
6.26: The priest who offers the purification offering shall eat of it; it shall be eaten in a sacred place, in the court of the tent of meeting.
6.27: Whatever touches its flesh becomes holy. If any of its blood spatters on a garment, the stained part must be washed in a sacred place.
6.28: A clay vessel in which it has been boiled shall be broken; if it is boiled in a copper vessel, this shall be scoured afterward and rinsed with water.
6.29: Every male of the priestly line may eat it. It is most holy.
6.30: But no purification offering of which some blood has been brought into the tent of meeting to make atonement in the sanctuary shall be eaten; it must be burned with fire.
